summary: signature audit should infer prototype candidates from localized argument mismatches
body: Validation at 8f973c5bd: 60 signature functions, 547 findings, 467 localized, 0 patch candidates. Rebucket counts included 139 signature-call-type/argument-presence/prototype-candidate-missing and 2 signature-call-type/argument-width/width-prototype-candidate-missing. This is the most promising remaining signature-specific source-lever path after register-source cascades were separated out. Governance: Reusable class: localized argument-presence and width prototype inference Applies to: top-60 signature-call-type sample after #431: 139 argument-presence prototype-candidate-missing findings and 2 argument-width width-prototype-candidate-missing findings Source-actionable output: For localized argument-presence/argument-width findings, inspect the source call expression, visible callee declaration/definition, and expected/current ABI argument registers to propose bounded prototype or callsite cast/type candidates, with validation scores and affected caller/callee blast radius. Stop condition: On the top-60 signature sample, localized argument-presence/width findings either produce validated candidate patches or narrower reasons such as variadic call, external prototype unavailable, macro-generated call, or unsafe global prototype change. Existing workflow failed: After #431, debug suggest signatures localizes callsites but rebuckets argument-presence and argument-width findings to prototype-candidate-missing instead of proposing any source candidate; the signature bucket still has zero patch candidates in the sample.

All 1327 actions rebucketed to structural-reconstruction/call-source-localization/call-not-localized. This is now the narrowest blocker before signature-call-type can become a source-lever campaign. Existing workflow failed: After #429/#430, debug suggest signatures correctly classifies findings but every action in the top-60 sample rebuckets to call-not-localized, including argument-width cases such as fn_8019F9C4; without source call-site localization the tool cannot produce bounded source patches.

summary: signature audit should turn repeated findings into patchable levers or rebuckets
body: Initial validation of #428 shows the tool is a useful classifier but not yet a campaign-ready source-lever generator. The dominant output is audit-only call-target-shape mismatch, often caused by shifted call offsets or upstream structural/register cascades rather than a concrete signature/prototype fix. Add aggregation and source localization so repeated findings either become bounded candidate patches or drive taxonomy rebucketing out of signature-call-type. Governance: Reusable class: signature-call-type source-lever generation and rebucketing Applies to: top-60 signature-call-type sample: 1327 findings, 0 patch candidates; 1132 call-target-shape-audit and 195 call-argument-type-audit actions Source-actionable output: For repeated call-target-shape and call-argument findings, emit either localized source candidate patches with validation scores or explicit no-source-lever rebucket reasons such as call-offset-shift, register-source-cascade, width/prototype candidate missing, or structural red herring. Stop condition: On the same top-60 signature-call-type sample, the tool either produces at least one validated source candidate or classifies each audit-only finding into a concrete non-actionable/rebucket reason that campaign agents can use to stop. Existing workflow failed: After #428, debug suggest signatures classified 1327 findings across the top 60 signature rows but every action had patch=None; even medium argument-width cases such as fn_8019F9C4 only report generic audit the source argument type guidance.
resolution_note: fixed in 14341a8614c9476460dc657b57970dbf38e01234: signature auditor now emits validation-aware summary stop conditions and concrete rebucket reasons for audit-only findings

summary: signature bucket needs ABI/typeflow source-lever audit
body: Proposed pivot: build a read-only then sandbox-validating signature audit that maps call-prep diffs to source-level type/prototype levers. It should parse checkdiff around bl sites, classify ext/clrlwi/lwz/lfs/mr/argument-register differences, correlate with current prototypes/field types/casts, aggregate across callsites, and emit bounded candidate patches only for low-blast-radius cases first (same-TU static helpers, leaf helpers, local casts). Governance: Reusable class: signature-call-type ABI/typeflow mismatch diagnosis Applies to: signature-call-type bucket: 297 rows Source-actionable output: ranked prototype/type/cast/source-site candidates with affected callsites, confidence, and sandbox validation results Stop condition: on a representative same-TU/static-helper sample, either produce retained source improvements or prove the bucket should be rebucketed into manual/type-model ceilings Existing workflow failed: debug suggest casts emitted no bounded source candidates in the signature-call-type triage sample; current taxonomy labels 297 rows but does not identify prototype, argument width, return type, field type, or callsite cast levers
resolution_note: Fixed in d9a197178: added debug suggest signatures to audit checkdiff call-prep signature/type mismatches, map them to source call/prototype/cast actions, and optionally validate one-line cast-removal candidates through temp-object checkdiff scoring.

summary: post-sync stale build/GALE01/config.json causes phantom 'Missing configuration'
body: After tools/workflow/sync-upstream.sh resets master to a restructured upstream, the dtk-generated build/GALE01/config.json from the PRE-sync build is left in place. tools/project.py:load_build_config (line 400) only regenerates config.json when the dtk *version* changes; a same-version stale config.json is read as-is. Its unit list still references removed/renamed source (e.g. melee/it/it_266F.c after upstream's it_266F split), so generate_build_ninja(add_unit) prints 'Missing configuration for melee/it/it_266F.c' and the resulting build.ninja is wrong. Repro: sync across an upstream commit that splits/renames a TU, then run python configure.py without clearing build/. Workaround: rm -f build/GALE01/config.json && python configure.py (forces dtk to regenerate units from current config.yml). Suggested fix: sync-upstream.sh should rm -f build/<VERSION>/config.json (and ideally stale build/<VERSION>/asm orphans) as a post-sync step, OR load_build_config should invalidate config.json when its referenced unit set diverges from config.libs. Related to #426 (both are post-sync staleness).
resolution_note: Fixed in 75a59dd38: sync-upstream removes stale build/*/config.json after sync.

summary: sync-upstream.sh blindly overlays configure.py, clobbering upstream restructuring
body: tools/workflow/sync-upstream.sh restores the fork tooling overlay by checking out the PRE-SYNC master's version of each TOOLING_PATH on top of a fresh 'git reset --hard upstream/master'. configure.py is in TOOLING_PATHS, so the WHOLE fork configure.py is restored, silently reverting every change upstream made to it since the merge-base. Observed after syncing master from upstream (merge-base 9cabdce8b -> upstream 3d97e6756, 20 commits): our overlaid configure.py was missing upstream's file-split restructuring (it_266F split into itspawn/itdraw/itdrop/itgroundcoll/it_3F14/..., ft_0C31 split, ftCrazyHand split into ~35 ftCh_*.c, hsd_3B5C.c addition) and reverted ~12 Object() match-status markers (lbarchive Matching->Equivalent, plbonuslib/grheal/grzebesroute/vi0402/itbombhei/... Matching->NonMatching). The source tree (correctly reset to upstream) no longer matched the build config -> 'Missing configuration for melee/it/it_266F.c' on configure, build broken. Root cause: configure.py is NOT pure fork tooling; it is a hybrid of fork customizations (here: --no-require-protos + require_protos default, wibo_tag bump, _purge_wrong_arch_wibo helper) and upstream-owned content (the entire config.libs Object() source list + match markers). Blind overlay loses the upstream-owned half. Fix options: (a) Do NOT overlay configure.py wholesale; instead take upstream's configure.py and re-apply ONLY the fork customizations (3-way merge, or maintain the fork bits as a patch / clearly-delimited block). (b) Split fork-only config into a separate imported module that upstream's configure.py sources, so the overlay only restores that module. (c) After overlay, 'git merge-file' / apply a saved configure.py.fork.patch and fail loudly on conflict. Workaround used this time: git checkout upstream/master -- configure.py, then manually re-applied the 3 fork customizations.
resolution_note: Fixed in 75a59dd38: sync-upstream now keeps upstream configure.py as base and reapplies only fork configure deltas with anchor checks.

summary: permuter randomizer mutates only func_name fn, not injected same-TU callee body (blocks 'permute helper to fix caller')
body: SIBLING of #424. #424 fixes the SCORE (inject callee body so base.o inlines); THIS is the next gap: even then the randomizer only touches func_name. src candidate.py:83 extract_fn(self.ast,self.fn_name)->randomizer.randomize(self.ast,self.fn). So 'permute the shared helper to shift its inline and improve the caller' is impossible. Repro: hsd_3AA7.c fn_803AC7DC(98.5)/fn_803AC6B8(97.7) both walled SOLELY on inlined fn_803AC634 file-0 {sector_size,usable,remaining} reg 3-cycle; dump local --force-phys <force-phys-from-diff vec> --force-phys-fn fn_803AC7DC --diff => full byte MATCH (C shape provably exists) but the only lever (helper body) is unreachable to the randomizer; 8 hand variants all (neutral,neutral)|(break,break). GOTCHA also seen: AC7DC bootstrap wrote a broken objdump_command 'melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump'; the working one is /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump -dr -EB -mpowerpc -M broadway. Proposed: settings.toml randomize_funcs=[caller,callee] (or randomize all top-level FuncDefs whose body is present) while scoring func_name; document in bootstrap output. See memory cardstate_iter13_inlined_helper_coloring_ceiling.md. Governance: Reusable class: permuter-randomizer-scope: to fix a caller walled on an inlined same-TU helper, the randomizer must be able to mutate the helper body, not just func_name Applies to: fn_803AC7DC, fn_803AC6B8 (hsd_3AA7.c, both walled on inlined fn_803AC634 file-0 coloring); any caller walled on an inlined same-TU helper's codegen Source-actionable output: a randomized base.c where the injected inlinable-callee body is also mutated (caller still scored), surfacing helper-source shapes that shift the inlined coloring Stop condition: permuter run with both caller+injected-callee mutated finds a sub-base candidate that checkdiff confirms improves the caller, OR exhausts N iters proving the helper-source is a true ceiling Existing workflow failed: bootstrap -f fn_803AC6B8 + inject fn_803AC634 body (#424 workaround): base score now meaningful (310) but randomizer (candidate.py:83 extract_fn(func_name)) mutates AC6B8 ONLY, never the injected helper body; 3000-4263 iters floored at base; func_name=fn_803AC634 just scores the 100% standalone helper
resolution_note: Fixed in decomp-permuter bd47946 and 876a94f plus melee master 94930bf95 and 9474a49af: added settings.toml randomize_funcs support, scoped Candidate/Permuter randomization, remote payload propagation, and bootstrap wiring that writes randomize_funcs for injected same-TU inline helpers while keeping pre-existing user settings unless --force is used. Verified with decomp-permuter unittest/mypy/cargo checks, melee-agent pytest/compile checks, and installed-entrypoint bootstrap smoke.

summary: permuter bootstrap forward-declares same-TU inlinable callees instead of injecting bodies
body: Workaround used: paste the callee's full body into base.c replacing the forward decl -> base.o inlines (0 bl) -> score drops to the real residual. Fix heuristic: if target.o's FN has no 'bl <callee>' but the source TU defines <callee>, inject that definition into base.c during bootstrap. fn_803ACD58 (no inlinable same-TU callee) bootstrapped correctly and the permuter cracked a real lever (inline i*ss + decl reorders, 97.9->99.7), confirming the tool works when base.c is faithful. Governance: Reusable class: permuter-setup-fidelity: base.c must reproduce production-TU inlining or the score is meaningless Applies to: fn_803AC7DC, fn_803AC6B8, fn_803ACC0C (hsd_3AA7.c) and any function that inlines a same-TU callee Source-actionable output: a corrected nonmatchings/FN/base.c where same-TU inlinable callees are DEFINED (not forward-declared) so base.o inlines them like production Stop condition: base.o for FN has zero 'bl <same-TU-inlinable-callee>' relocs absent from target.o; base permuter score == checkdiff residual (e.g. fn_803ACD58 250 ~= 97.9%) Existing workflow failed: 'debug permute bootstrap -f fn_803AC7DC' emitted base.c that only forward-declares fn_803AC634; base.o had 7x 'bl fn_803AC634' while target.o inlines it; base score 20625 reflected call-vs-inline gap not the ~63-reg residual
resolution_note: Fixed in 670a92873: debug permute bootstrap now injects same-TU callees absent from target call edges as inline definitions before the target, follows transitive missing callees, preserves live bl/b/R_PPC_REL24 callees, and removes stale base.o after patching base.c.

summary: classify and triage named-BSS versus .bss.0 anchor residuals
body: Governance: Reusable class: BSS section-anchor residuals where current source relocates through .bss.0 but target uses a named object such as lbl_80472ED8 Applies to: fn_80181C80, hsd_80393760, hsd_80392E80, hsd_80392CCC Source-actionable output: checkdiff/taxonomy verdict that separates source-fixable named-BSS declaration/overlay candidates from compiler-internal .bss.0 anchor ceilings, with candidate named-object/addend mapping and real-tree validation when source-fixable Stop condition: for each named-BSS/.bss.0 residual, either a validated source edit reaches match/improvement or the tool labels it BSS-anchor ceiling so campaigns stop probing source churn Existing workflow failed: small-pattern audit of fn_80181C80 showed target relocs to lbl_80472ED8+small offsets while current uses ...bss.0+large offsets; naming the aggregate struct did not change score or relocs. particle.c contains multiple TODOs explicitly capped by .bss.0 relocation symbols. Resolved #375 covers sdata2/data name-magic source declarations but not BSS anchors.
resolution_note: Fixed in ebbcfb5f0621b06ea9bdc54cd3c107fc64b7a8c1: checkdiff now detects named-BSS versus ...bss.N relocation ceilings, name-magic blocks source probes on BSS anchors, and taxonomy queues label these as bss-section-anchor-ceiling.

summary: harvest --taxonomy-dir rejects taxonomy root with queues subdir
body: Campaign codex/indexed-pointer-campaign-20260605-040814: the requested preview command used --taxonomy-dir /Users/mike/code/melee/build/function-taxonomy and failed with 'harvest queue is missing: /Users/mike/code/melee/build/function-taxonomy/indexed-struct-pointer.tsv'. The current artifact layout has the queue at /Users/mike/code/melee/build/function-taxonomy/queues/indexed-struct-pointer.tsv, and rerunning with --taxonomy-dir /Users/mike/code/melee/build/function-taxonomy/queues succeeded. summary: debug search structure should implement statement-order axis for source-order levers
body: Campaign evidence from codex/hsd3aa7-structure-campaign-20260605-0240: current debug search structure axes (case-order/control-flow/decl-order) had near-zero actionable yield on hsd_3AA7.c, while the retained improvement for hsd_803AC558 came from a hand-applied statement-order/source-order lever: splitting size=(size<<8)|p[3] into in-place size<<=8; size|=p[3] improved 82.84% -> 88.76% by preventing MWCC rlwimi fusion and emitting target slwi+or. This is not covered by the resolved #416 implementation, which only has future-axis markers for statement-order. Reusable class: data-flow-safe statement/source-order search inside a function, including expression split vs fused update, queue-index advance before/after copy, snapshot/local ordering, and similar source-order levers that preserve semantics but alter MWCC codegen. Applies to: hsd_803AC558, fn_803AC168, fn_803ADE4C, fn_803B21E8, plus structural/control-flow-source-shape rows where previous wins came from hand-discovered source ordering. Source-actionable output: ranked candidate source diffs generated through the post-#419 non-mutating output-dir path, with checkdiff delta, touched statement span, and enough context for an agent to apply/verify a retained source edit. Stop condition: if no statement-order candidate improves score after enumerating bounded safe swaps/splits, classify the residual as not addressable by statement-order and let the campaign move on. Existing workflow failed: #416 was resolved with case-order/control-flow/decl-order and future-axis markers, but this campaign found those implemented axes had approximately zero actionable yield on hsd_3AA7.c; the only retained source improvement came from a hand statement-order lever the tool could not search.
resolution_note: fixed in 7b214615008d78aeff06afe8ce9965237457c558: debug search structure now includes a source-only statement-order axis with split/fuse shift-or candidates, conservative local-scalar adjacent swaps, candidate diffs/touched spans, and candidates-generated stop conditions; verified with search tests and hsd_803AC558 CLI smoke

summary: MWCC pointer-offset const-fold: source base+var_off+const always lowers to base+(var_off+const) [addi var,const then add base] but some targets emit (base+var_off)+const [add base,var then addi result,const]; no C expression shape controls it
body: hsd_3AA7.c fn_803ACFC0 (90.6 pct): the plain state->x0 + hdr_offset accesses (no extra const) match the target; the +const ones (memcpy +0x20, memset +psize+0x20, AC3F8-arg +0x13) diverge because MWCC folds hdr_offset+const into one addi then adds x0, while target computes add(x0,hdr_offset) then addi(+const). Explicit u8* hdr = state->x0 + hdr_offset reproduces the association BUT caches the base, collapsing the targets per-access x0 re-reads (-8 pct). Likely a backend address-reassociation pass with no C lever; needs force-proof of reachability like the disp-form #410. Governance: Reusable class: pointer-offset constant reassociation / address-mode selection Applies to: fn_803ACFC0, fn_803B1F78 Source-actionable output: a diagnostic that proves whether the (base+var)+const form is reachable from any C, OR a source-shape recommendation; currently all 5 tested forms re-fold or collapse the targets per-access base re-reads Stop condition: no source form changes the lowering after the 5 tested expression shapes Existing workflow failed: inspect diagnose = NO FAST TRANSFORM (casts clean, all decl-orders <=0); manual expression reshaping no-op; sibling/adjacent to #410 disp-form selection
resolution_note: fixed in 49ea3e9b79d381d235141b093e1c973b299b4cfc: debug inspect diagnose now reports pointer_offset_reassociation only when source, expected ASM, and current pcdump agree on supported call consumer/constant; verified with focused tests, full debug CLI tests, and fn_803ACFC0 JSON/text smoke

summary: debug search structure mutates live working-tree source in place; concurrent build/checkdiff (or a backgrounded run) races it and can leave the source corrupted + stray pcdump_*.txt
body: REPRO/EVIDENCE: Ran 'melee-agent debug search structure -f hsd_803AC558 ... &' in the BACKGROUND, then immediately ran 'python tools/checkdiff.py hsd_803AC558' in the foreground. checkdiff's ninja build FAILED with an expression-syntax error and the working-tree src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c was left CORRUPTED at line 3855 (inside fn_803B1338, NOT the -f target hsd_803AC558) with invalid partial control-flow transform text: } else } else switch (state->x28[file_id]) { } else case 2: { ... } else break; A stray pcdump_56348_1780653464085.txt artifact was also left in the repo root. 'git checkout -- src/.../hsd_3AA7.c' + rm restored a clean tree. ISOLATION (controlled): (1) a SINGLE foreground search rolls back cleanly even when an axis hits --timeout (verified: control-flow --timeout 8 on fn_803B1338 -> git status clean, line 3855 intact). (2) a SINGLE foreground hsd_803AC558 control-flow search -> clean tree, no stray pcdump. So the corruption requires CONCURRENCY: the search mutates the shared live source file IN PLACE to evaluate each variant, so any concurrent ninja/checkdiff (or a second overlapping search) observes/compiles a half-applied variant, and an interrupted save/restore can persist the corruption. IMPACT: silently breaks subsequent builds/checkdiffs until manual git checkout; the botched site can be in a DIFFERENT function than -f, so it is easy to misattribute. Campaign-blocking if a backgrounded search is used. SUGGESTED FIX: evaluate variants against a temp copy / scratch worktree instead of mutating the live source; and/or take an exclusive lock + refuse to run if the target source is dirty or another search is active; clean up pcdump_*.txt on exit. At minimum, document that 'debug search structure' must run exclusively (foreground, no concurrent build/checkdiff). Note: the if-equality-to-single-case-switch operator also emitted syntactically invalid C for the chained 'if(==1){}else if(==2){}' at fn_803B1338:3851 — worth a scoping/validity check independent of the concurrency bug.
resolution_note: fixed in 1d9850d9a: debug search structure now generates decl-order/control-flow/case-order candidates from source into the output directory by default instead of invoking live-tree mutating compile/scoring axes; smoke verified hsd_803AC558 leaves git status unchanged and creates no root pcdump_*.txt

summary: disp-form rollback IS reachable from C (corrects #410): inline base cast + per-loop snap/saved locals close fn_803ADE4C+fn_803B21E8 to 100%
body: CORRECTION to #410 (resolved as 'UNREACHABLE from C') — the disp-form rollback store in hsd_3AA7.c IS reachable from source for low-register-pressure functions. Two stacked levers, both committed (branch worktree-agent-a9c4e2fef2c875673, commits 11ea20338 + 092cbf3ac): LEVER 1 (flips stwx -> disp-form): replace the CACHED base access 'CardBufEntry* entries=(CardBufEntry*)hsd_804D1138; ... entries[saved].x10=0;' with the cast INLINED at the access site: '((CardBufEntry*)hsd_804D1138)[saved].x10=0;'. With the base cached in a callee-save local MWCC strength-reduces the element address and folds the field offset into the scaled index (stwx); with the cast inlined MWCC re-derives the element pointer and selects 'add base,scaled; stw 0x10(ptr)' = retail disp-form. NOTE: #410 tested 'CardBufEntry* e=&entries[saved]; e->x10' (pointer-temp) and found it strength-reduces back — TRUE, but that is the SAME strength reduction the cached-base form triggers; the fix is the OPPOSITE (don't name the base, inline the constant cast). This is the analogue of the switch_case dispatcher LEVER 2 'inline ptr->field, no cached local'. LEVER 2 (fixes the residual snap/index register swap -> 100%): a function with N rollback loops sharing ONE snap/ONE saved local colors the snapshot/index pair inconsistently between loops (loop1 snap=r6,idx=r7 vs retail r7,r6). Give EACH loop its OWN locals (snap1/saved1, snap2/saved2, …, last loop keeps snap/saved). #410 listed 'separate snap2/saved2 locals' as a FAILED form — but that was tested as a SECOND-pass without LEVER 1 in place and as a single split, not per-loop. With Lever 1's disp-form already matching, per-loop locals close it. mwcc-debug 'inspect diagnose' decl-order sweep MISSES this (it permutes the shared decl, never splits one local into N). RESULTS: fn_803ADE4C 96.1->100% (2 loops), fn_803B21E8 94.8->100% (3 loops). Byte-identical via dtk disasm. TU 18 fns@100% (was 16). PRESSURE GATE (Lever 1 is NOT universally TU-wide): regresses high-param functions — fn_803B26CC (5 params) 92.3->89.6, fn_803AE7F8 (5 params) 73.6->70.8. In low-pressure fns the re-materialized base CSEs into the single callee-save r31 (no redundant load); in 5-param fns there is no free callee-save so the inline cast emits a redundant lis/addi base in the loop, costing more than the disp gain. The 5 parallel-owned builders all have the disp-form wall but are high-pressure -> need a pressure-reducing companion. ASK: (a) mwcc-debug 'diagnose' should add a 'split shared loop-carried local into per-loop copies' transform (currently only permutes decl order). (b) The disp-vs-stwx selection could be a recognized class with the inline-base-cast hint surfaced. #410's 'unreachable' verdict should be reopened/amended to 'reachable for low-pressure rollback fns via inline-base-cast + per-loop locals; pressure-gated'.
resolution_note: fixed in db3b7d629: debug inspect diagnose now recognizes disp-form rollback source-shape hints, distinguishes cached-base recommendations from already-inlined stores, gates per-loop split hints on repeated self-updating rollback index locals, and reports pressure-gated JSON/text guidance

summary: Structure permuter (debug search structure): systematically permute semantics-preserving SOURCE structure (case-order/decl-order/stmt-order/loop-shape/inline-boundary) and rank by checkdiff delta - the high-yield search axis decomp-permuter doesn't cover
body: THE RECURRING FAILURE MODE THIS WOULD FIX: across 8 rounds on hsd_3AA7.c, the dominant way functions got UNSTUCK was discovering a SOURCE-STRUCTURE lever that had been misdiagnosed as a "backend/ig-order ceiling" and banked. Every one was found by HAND, slowly, and several sat undiscovered for many rounds: - hsd_803AAA48 dispatcher 28.6->77.6% (+49!) from ONE lever: reorder switch cases to the target's physical block order (MWCC emits case bodies in source-textual order). It had been banked as a "diffuse -145-instruction inline long-tail ceiling". - fn_803ADF90 et al: control-flow-source-shape (hoist helper out of loop, explicit if/else vs boolean cast, statement/snapshot/flag hoist) - banked as "CEILING-INLINE/frame" for 2 rounds first. - fn_803ADE4C: snapshot-hoist (statement order). fn_803B1338: switch/case + missing async layer. These are all SEMANTICS-PRESERVING SOURCE-STRUCTURE permutations whose effect on codegen is hard to predict by eye but trivial to MEASURE. ASK (ambitious, generalizable): a STRUCTURE permuter - `debug search structure -f <fn>` (or `permute-structure`) - the structural counterpart to decomp-permuter (which permutes registers/expressions). It systematically generates SEMANTICS-PRESERVING source-structure variants and measures checkdiff delta for each, surfacing the ones that reduce the diff. Axes to permute: - switch/case statement order (the +49% lever) - local declaration order - statement order within a basic block (where data-flow-safe) - loop termination/shape: i<N vs i!=N vs down-count (drives MWCC unroll), pow2 vs exact-divisor - helper inline-vs-outline: `inline` keyword on a global helper, #pragma inline_depth(N) - branch idiom: explicit if/else vs boolean expression; ternary vs if - cache-vs-recompute a helper result / pointer base It would have found the dispatcher lever in minutes instead of 7 rounds. OUTPUT (source-actionable): a ranked list of structural transforms with the resulting checkdiff %/diff-line delta and the exact source change, e.g. "reorder cases to [0,5,11,...] -> 33.4%->77.5%", "declare flag at fn scope + init first -> +4.2%", "i!=N termination -> exact-divisor unroll". NOT A FORCE KNOB: it permutes LEGAL, SEMANTICS-PRESERVING SOURCE STRUCTURE and emits a SOURCE diff the agent reviews - the opposite of forcing a backend register assignment. Critically, per near100_buckets_ceiling_dominated_census, register-source-search is ~0 yield; STRUCTURE-search has PROVEN, large yield this campaign (the dispatcher alone). This is the high-yield search axis the permuter does NOT cover. GENERALITY: the control-flow-source-shape class is a named checkdiff classification; this applies to the 784 project-wide <90% reconstruction functions + ANY near-match currently banked as "backend ceiling" without a structure search having been run (i.e. most of them). It also operationalizes the lead directive to "question claimed walls": run the structure search before banking anything as a ceiling. RELATION TO #402: #402 (control-flow-shape suggester) SUGGESTS a transform from a single diff read; this SEARCHES the structural space and MEASURES - they compose (suggester proposes candidates, searcher validates/ranks). STOP CONDITION: when no structural permutation reduces the diff, the residual is genuinely backend (register/selection/coalescing) and the existing register-tiebreak/schedule/frame tools apply - and you can bank the ceiling with confidence instead of guessing. Governance: Reusable class: control-flow-source-shape structural search (case-order/decl-order/statement-order/loop-shape/inline-boundary) Applies to: every near-match currently banked as a backend ceiling without a structure search; the 784 project-wide <90% reconstruction functions; acutely hsd_3AA7.c's builders + dispatcher where hand-found structure levers gave +49% (dispatcher) and +8-26% (builders) Source-actionable output: ranked list of semantics-preserving source-structure transforms with resulting checkdiff delta + the exact source edit (reorder cases to [..], declare X first, i!=N termination, inline helper Y) - agent applies & verifies Stop condition: when no structural permutation reduces the diff: residual is genuinely backend (register/selection) - bank the ceiling with confidence; existing register-tiebreak/schedule/frame tools apply Existing workflow failed: agents find these by hand and miss them for many rounds (dispatcher switch-order undiscovered 7 rounds); decomp-permuter permutes registers/expressions NOT control-flow/declaration structure; #402 suggests-from-one-diff but does not search/measure the structural space
resolution_note: fixed in 0f4a9660d: added debug search structure with decl-order/control-flow orchestration, conservative case-order source probes, ranked JSON/text output, future-axis markers, and safety blockers

summary: function taxonomy inventory worker pool hung during full regeneration
body: Campaign report from /Users/mike/code/melee on 2026-06-05: after rebucketing signature-call-type, inline-boundary, and known-small-pattern advisory rows, a full taxonomy regeneration hung in the worker pool after about 13 minutes and was interrupted. The campaign only mechanically refreshed ignored local queue labels to validate preview behavior; it did not get a clean full regeneration. This blocks reliable next-campaign queue selection because current queue function membership may be stale even when labels are updated. Repro context: run full taxonomy regeneration from /Users/mike/code/melee, likely python tools/function_taxonomy_inventory.py --workers 4; observed hang in worker pool with no completed fresh taxonomy output after ~13 minutes. Expected: either complete successfully, emit progress/timeout diagnostics per worker, or fail with the specific function/worker that hung so campaigns can exclude or fix it.
resolution_note: fixed in 0d24e4db3: taxonomy inventory now bounds checkdiff and decl-orders worker subprocesses with CLI-tunable timeouts, records checkdiff timeouts as per-function error rows, and keeps full regeneration from hanging silently on an unbounded worker command

summary: validate_symbol_renames false-positive on removing a local var whose TYPE name ends in State/Data/Info/etc
body: validate_symbol_renames (tools/melee-agent/src/hooks/validate_commit.py:799) flags a Bad rename when a commit REMOVES a local variable declaration whose TYPE matches the descriptive-name regex (e.g. 'CardState* state = cmd->state;') and, in the same diff, ADDS lines that happen to contain pre-existing address-based fn_* call names (because nearby code was reformatted/moved). Repro: hsd_3AA7.c hsd_803AAA48 inline refactor removed ~14 'CardState* state' locals (converting state->X to cmd->state->X) and rewrote case 17, so fn_803AC2A4/fn_803ACF30/fn_803AC258 (already unnamed in the codebase, NOT renamed) appear on added lines. Hook reports: removed ['CardState'], added ['fn_803AC2A4',...]. But CardState the TYPE is still used 33x in the file and nothing was renamed. Suggestion: exclude a 'removed' descriptive name if it still appears anywhere in the post-commit file (grep the working tree), or only consider names that are SYMBOLS (function/global identifiers in symbols.txt) rather than struct type names, or require the removed descriptive name and added address name to be on positionally-corresponding lines. Worktree agent-acd2658bd138811c2 on branch worktree-agent-acd2658bd138811c2.
resolution_note: fixed in e7664652c: validate_symbol_renames now filters removed descriptive tokens that are C type references, with regressions for the CardState local-declaration false positive and real descriptive-symbol replacements

summary: Frame tooling needs a MATCH-RELEVANCE signal: distinguish frame deltas that gate the byte-match from offset-only/match-neutral ones (chasing a match-neutral delta cost ~3 rounds)
body: SUPERSEDES the premise of #406 and lead-note #407 (and aligns with #412): there is NO cmd-buffer coalescing; the builders' frame delta is object-size stride padding that does not gate the match. Do NOT build an 'anti-coalesce operator' (#406) — there's nothing to de-coalesce. THE GENERALIZABLE GAP: frame-reservations / suggest frame / target derive --frame-from-checkdiff give an EXACT frame target and a delta, but no signal whether that delta is MATCH-GATING or MATCH-NEUTRAL. checkdiff already knows which diff lines are pure stack-slot-offset noise (they don't move fuzzy-match) vs structural. The tool should cross that in and label each frame residual: 'MATCH-NEUTRAL: this frame delta is offset-only, closing it will not yield match=true — the real residual is <register/selection/inline class>'. VALUE: this single signal would have redirected the hsd_3AA7.c builder campaign 3 rounds earlier from the (dead) frame-overlay theory to the real walls (ig-order register cascade + disp-form address-mode selection, both backend/no-C-lever). It generalizes to every frame-reservations run: agents currently cannot tell a real frame wall (e.g. gm_801A9DD0's mistyped-int +8 home, which IS match-gating) from a match-neutral stride artifact. NOT a force knob — it's a classification/relevance signal on existing diagnostic output. Real-tree validation: fn_803AE7F8 s32 cmd[10] frame delta 56->24, match% 73.6 FLAT (committed evidence on branch); forced-concurrency stride unchanged at 36B. Governance: Reusable class: frame-delta-match-relevance Applies to: every function frame-reservations/suggest-frame/target-derive runs on; acutely the hsd_3AA7.c builder family fn_803AE7F8/AF3F0/B0120/ADF90/B1338 + any near-match with a stack-slot-only residual Source-actionable output: a verdict per frame delta: MATCH-GATING (closing it is necessary for match=true) vs MATCH-NEUTRAL (offset-only stack-slot lines that do not change fuzzy-match / byte-match) — so an agent knows whether to invest in the frame at all Stop condition: stop when the tool labels the residual MATCH-NEUTRAL (move to the real wall) or when match=true Existing workflow failed: frame-reservations reports a 56-byte delta + 'non-overlap mismatch' on the builders, which is (a) mislabeled (stride 40B-vs-36B is not overlap; both layouts have 7 distinct buffers) and (b) MATCH-NEUTRAL (s32 cmd[10] reproduces the 40B stride, shrinks the delta 56->24, match% FLAT 73.6%). Three rounds chased this frame-overlay/coalescing/build-batching theory before iteration-6 disasm proved no coalescing exists and the stride is pure object size + match-irrelevant. The tool's 'non-overlap mismatch' verdict actively misled the campaign.
resolution_note: fixed in ba7c5b458: frame taxonomy/stuck/inventory now emits frame_match_relevance with match-neutral same-frame stack-slot residuals, match-gating pure reservations, and unknown for size/alignment or frame-movement-only evidence

summary: CardState cmd-buffer class: #406 'overlapped/coalesce' framing is WRONG; buffers are distinct, residual is 40-vs-36 stride and is MATCH-NEUTRAL
body: SIBLING/CORRECTION to #406 (round-6, fn_803AE7F8, cleanest 56B-delta builder). #406 says 'MINE=36B overlapped' and asks for an anti-coalesce operator. Disasm proves this is FACTUALLY WRONG: 1. NO COALESCING/OVERLAP: dtk elf disasm of MINE .o, mapped all 7 'addi r3,r1,off' cmd-buffer bases before each 'bl fn_803AC168'. The 7 cmd[9] buffers are DISTINCT and NON-OVERLAPPING in BOTH ref and mine (each base+36 <= next base). REF stride = uniform 40 (0x5c,0x84,0xac,0xd4,0xfc, all 0x28 apart); MINE stride = tight 36 (gap 0). The difference is per-buffer PADDING (40 vs 36), not overlap. 2. BUILD-BATCHING HYPOTHESIS REFUTED (the untested round-6 hypothesis): restructured the write-loop erase+write pair to build-both-then-push-both, then forced GENUINE concurrency (re-read cmdE[8] + re-push cmdE AFTER cmdW's push, so cmdE is provably live across cmdW). Buffers STAYED 36 apart (0xc0/0x9c), frame unchanged. Concurrency does NOT drive 40B stride. MWCC packs concurrently-live same-size cmd[9] buffers at 36B just like sequential ones. 3. 40B STRIDE IS OBJECT-SIZE: 's32 cmd[10]' (40B) reproduces 40B stride, shrinks frame delta 56->24 (frame-reservations 1216->1248 vs target 1272). But match% stays FLAT 73.6%. The frame/stride is MATCH-NEUTRAL: the ~18 checkdiff 'stack-slot' lines are pure offset differences that do not gate fuzzy-match. 4. frame-reservations 'non-overlap mismatch' verdict is MISLEADING: it flags the 36-vs-40 STRIDE difference as 'overlap' when neither layout overlaps. ACTION: revise the #406 governance — the class is NOT anti-coalesce; an alignment/stride operator would close the frame metric but NOT match%. The real match walls for these builders are (a) register cascade (ig-order ceiling: verify_failed lands r16 vs ref r18, decl-order sweep + force-iter-first + verify_failed-first-decl all no-op) and (b) disp-form rollback (#410, selection not coloring: element-pointer form strength-reduces back to stwx, force-iter-first does not flip). Round-6 verdict: builders are a backend ceiling for MATCH; the cmd-buffer frame layout is a real-but-match-irrelevant artifact.
resolution_note: fixed in a04bb6b05: preserved size/alignment frame causes through taxonomy, added CardState distinct 40-vs-36 stride negative regression, and corrected stale #406/#407 docs so distinct non-overlapping cmd buffers are not treated as overlap/coalescing

summary: Frame tooling: cmd-buffer 8-align/non-overlap class has correct target but no working source lever
body: 5 CardState builders walled on frame-overlay. REF=40B 8-aligned non-overlapping cmd[9] stack buffers (17 distinct slots in B1338); MINE=36B mixed-align overlapped. ADF90 additionally needs 2 callee-saves (stmw r15 vs r17), a register-coloring cascade (diagnose: NO FAST TRANSFORM, spilled call-return copy r112=fn_803AC634->file_blocks, all 14 decl-orders no-op). The frame tooling end-to-end (dump setup/local on macOS, frame-reservations, target derive --frame-from-checkdiff, score-source, suggest frame, first-divergence --frame) all WORK and correctly TARGET the residual; the gap is purely the missing source/backend lever for buffer alignment+overlap. See also bug #405 (PAD_STACK probe placed amid C89 decls). Worktree agent-a5a44aba10d1443d6, baseline 49ccaf507. Governance: Reusable class: frame-overlay: 8-aligned non-overlapping stack-buffer reservation (CARD cmd[9] builder family) Applies to: fn_803ADF90, fn_803AF3F0, fn_803AE7F8, fn_803B0120, fn_803B1338 (hsd_3AA7.c); generalizes to any TU whose target reserves 8-aligned/non-overlapping repeated small stack buffers Source-actionable output: a probe operator (or backend diagnostic) that controls per-buffer stack-slot alignment/stride OR prevents MWCC from coalescing mutually-exclusive-branch buffers, so the agent can emit the exact 40-byte 8-aligned non-overlapping buffer layout the frame target already specifies Stop condition: score-source frame distance reaches 0 OR checkdiff match=true for >=1 of the 5 fns; if no probe makes the buffer stride 40/8-aligned after the operator exists, record force-frame-not-reached as a backend ceiling Existing workflow failed: frame-reservations + target derive --frame-from-checkdiff give the EXACT target (B1338 1040/stmw r15/full offset map) but suggest frame + frame-transform-search only offer PAD_STACK (flat low reservation: B1338 760->872, score 560->476, match% FLAT 70.74) and decl-order/block-scope/temp-introduction operators - NONE change buffer stride/alignment/overlap. Manually tested cmd[10], cmd[10]+cmd[9]=0, union{f64;s32[10]}, ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN(8), function-scope distinct buffers, loop-scope dead buffers, address-escaping/volatile dead local, block_map[64]->[83]: ALL no-op on stride/match. MWCC coalesces the buffers into 36B 4-aligned slots regardless of C source shape.
resolution_note: superseded/corrected in a04bb6b05: #412 refuted the anti-coalesce premise; tooling now classifies the cmd-buffer residual as size/alignment/stride guidance, suppresses distinct-home concurrent-lifetime suggestions, and leaves the remaining match wall to backend/match-relevance tracking

summary: rollback-loop add+stw-disp vs addi+stwx is unreachable instruction-selection (hsd_3AA7 CardState family)
body: In src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c the CardState snap/saved rollback loop (entries[saved].x10 = 0; saved=(saved+1)%128;) is compiled by retail as 'mulli; addi+1; srawi; add r4,base,r4; addze; stw rZ,0x10(r4)' (form a real element pointer + displacement store). EVERY current build of this TU instead emits 'mulli; addi+1; srawi; addi r4,r4,16; addze; stwx rZ,base,r4' (indexed store). This is pure instruction-SELECTION downstream of register allocation/pressure: the retail build keeps the stored 0 in a callee-save (e.g. fn_803ADF90 uses 'stw r19,0x10(r4)' with r19=0 callee-saved) which drives the disp-form; the smaller cleanup functions keep 0 volatile -> stwx. CHARACTERIZED AS UNREACHABLE from C: tested 5 source forms (explicit CardBufEntry* e=&entries[saved]; (&entries[saved])->x10; *(s32*)((u8*)entries+saved*36+0x10); shared s32 zero live across the call; separate snap2/saved2 locals) -- ALL robustly produce stwx. force-iter-first leaves it unchanged (selection, not coloring). Even the 'gold template' fn_803ADF90 fails to reproduce the disp-form in the current build (its rollback uses 'addi r4,r4,16; stwx r19,r26,r4' vs retail 'add r4,r25,r4; stw r19,0x10(r4)'). Affects fn_803ADE4C(96.1%), fn_803B21E8(94.8%), fn_803B26CC(92.3%) and the banked builders. ASK: a backend probe/diagnostic to (a) confirm whether the disp-form is reachable by forcing the store-value to a callee-save and (b) recognize 'array-element-store addressing-mode (add+disp vs addi+stwx)' as a selection class distinct from coloring, so agents stop grinding register tools on it. Sibling of mwcc_ignode_ordering_ceiling. Worktree: agent-ab5b8d347beb3198a.
resolution_note: fixed in 624cebb63: checkdiff now classifies the CardState rollback-loop add+stw-displacement vs addi+stwx residual as backend_ceiling subclass=array-element-store-addressing-mode, with reason text explicitly calling it addressing-mode instruction selection and not register coloring. Added positive/negative regressions; ran test_checkdiff_stack_diagnostics.py (18 passed), compileall tools/checkdiff.py, diff-check, and a direct classifier smoke.

summary: permute triage (without --apply-best) leaves working-tree source staged/dirty instead of restoring
body: 2026-06-04: ran 'debug permute triage <dir> -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C --top 10' (NO --apply-best). Per skill docs source should be restored unless --apply-best. After it completed, src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c was left MODIFIED with a staged permuter candidate (new_var/new_var2 artifacts, reformatted braces) in mnDiagram_8023FA6C. git status showed 'M'. Had to git checkout HEAD to recover. Risk: silently committing a permuter candidate (often functionally broken - the staged one had a hoisted p-- breaking the insertion-shift loop). Triage must restore base on exit when --apply-best is absent.
resolution_note: verified current debug permute triage restore behavior and locked it with regression test in 4b57a4869. Added a command-level test for an improving candidate without --apply-best in text mode; triage reports WIN but leaves the real source byte-for-byte original. Ran pytest -k debug_permute_triage: 11 passed, plus pre-commit checks.

summary: Lead reframe of #406: the cmd-buffer-coalescing residual has an UNTESTED source-lever path (control-flow lifetime) — pursue that before any backend force-frame operator
body: Lead steering on #406 to keep it gate-compliant (source-lever path required; not a force knob). ROOT CAUSE (from the frame round): the 5 builders' cmd[9] buffers COALESCE because MWCC liveness proves them NON-CONCURRENT — they sit in mutually-exclusive if(arg)/else branches, so they share stack slots (36B/overlapped), while the original keeps 17 DISTINCT 40B/8-aligned slots. The frame agent exhaustively tested DECLARATION levers (cmd[10], unions, ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN, 15 distinct vars, dead/volatile/address-escaped locals) — all coalesce. Correct conclusion: declaration cannot defeat coalescing. BUT the coalescing root is LIFETIME, not declaration — and the SOURCE-LEVER path is UNTESTED: a CONTROL-FLOW restructure that makes the cmd buffers CONCURRENTLY LIVE so MWCC cannot prove them non-concurrent (e.g. build multiple cmd buffers before consuming/pushing them, or any shape where buffer lifetimes genuinely overlap). The original's 17 distinct slots imply its buffers were NOT in mutually-exclusive, sequentially-consumed branches. This is a control-flow-source-shape problem (ties directly to #402), NOT a frame-declaration problem. REFRAME #406: - PRIMARY ask (source-lever, gate-compliant): recognize cmd-buffer-coalescing/overlap as a control-flow-source-shape SUB-CLASS and have the #402 control-flow-shape suggester detect 'target keeps N distinct same-size buffers, current coalesces M<N' and suggest the concurrent-lifetime restructure. target->score path already exists (frame-reservations + target derive give the exact 40B/8-align/17-slot goal); the missing piece is the SOURCE transform suggestion, not a backend knob. - A backend force-frame/anti-coalesce operator is acceptable ONLY as a reachability-CONFIRMATION tool (analogous to force-phys: prove the layout is reachable), NEVER as the match mechanism. Do NOT build it as a standalone force knob (per the no-force-knob discipline / near100_buckets_ceiling_dominated_census). ACTION BEFORE BACKEND TOOLING: a dedicated builder re-attack must TEST the control-flow-lifetime source hypothesis (concurrent buffer lifetimes). Until that's tried and fails, the 5 builders are BANKED-WITH-OPEN-HYPOTHESIS, not declared ceilings. This avoids the premature-ceiling trap an independent review already caught us in twice this batch (fn_803ADF90, fn_803ADE4C).
resolution_note: fixed in 6152b3d8/a61256864: debug suggest control-flow-shape now emits a conservative concurrent-buffer-lifetime source hypothesis from ASM stack-home evidence, with copyable source-first follow-ups and regressions for missing-call, partial-extraction, stale-call-home, and no-repeated-offset false positives. #406 remains open for actual source-probe/frame-distance success or backend-ceiling recording.

summary: frame-transform-search PAD_STACK probe inserts amid C89 decls -> malformed-source compile fail
body: debug mutate frame-transform-search --operator frame-reservation-pad generates a PAD_STACK(N) probe that is inserted in the MIDDLE of the C89 declaration block (between 'CardBufEntry* entries=...' and 's32 block_map[64];' in fn_803ADF90), e.g. line 1692. PAD_STACK expands to a 'do{...}while(0)' statement, so placing it amid declarations is a C89 syntax error -> the probe compiles with exit 3 ('function not emitted in pcdump') and is reported status=malformed-source, so the operator silently does nothing. It DOES work when the function's declarations happen to end cleanly before the first statement (fn_803B1338: PAD_STACK landed after the last decl 'u8* fdata;' and moved frame 760->872, verdict partial-source-reachable-frame-transform). FIX: the frame-reservation-pad operator must insert PAD_STACK at the FIRST-STATEMENT position (after ALL leading declarations in the function/block), not after an arbitrary decl. Repro: melee-agent debug mutate frame-transform-search -f fn_803ADF90 --operator frame-reservation-pad --frame-reservation-bytes 80 --compile-probes --json (worktree agent-a5a44aba10d1443d6, hsd_3AA7.c @ 49ccaf507).
resolution_note: fixed in 89fa52e17: frame-reservation PAD_STACK probes now treat C89 array declarations as leading locals, so PAD_STACK inserts after the full declaration block; frame-reservation-pad-stack compile probe for fn_803ADF90 now reports ok

summary: debug suggest control-flow-shape: rank source transforms for the control-flow-source-shape class (call-hoist / branch-idiom / loop-peel / walk-shape / missing-call-layer) from target-vs-built ASM diff -- no pcdump needed
body: COMMAND CONTEXT: agents repeatedly hit checkdiff class "control-flow-source-shape" (the dominant reconstruction lever) with NO suggester for it. The debug suggest suite has: frame, casts, register-tiebreak, coalesce, schedule, inlines -- but nothing for control-flow source shape. OBSERVED (the manual workflow that works but is slow/ad-hoc): to crack a control-flow-source-shape function, an agent must hand-diff the target ASM (melee-agent extract get <fn> --full) against the built .o (dtk elf disasm build/GALE01/src/<unit>.o), write a custom per-function bl-counter, and eyeball branch shapes -- then reason out the source transform. Our recon round built /tmp/callprofile.py by hand; an independent codex review re-derived the same analysis manually. Worse: without this tool we MISLABELED fn_803ADF90/AF3F0/AE7F8/B0120 as "CEILING-INLINE" (premature ceiling) when checkdiff actually reports them control-flow-source-shape with concrete untested transforms. CONCRETE EVIDENCE (fn_803ADF90, 78%, class=control-flow-source-shape): the actionable transforms a tool should surface, each tied to an observed target-vs-current ASM divergence: - call-hoist: fn_803AC7DC is called INSIDE the loop condition (source for(i=0;i<fn_803AC7DC(arg0);i++)) but target calls it ONCE before the loop then uses mtctr -> "cache the helper result before the loop". (The codebase caches it everywhere else.) - branch-idiom: target busy-test emits cmpwi/bne + li1/li0 (explicit if/else 0-1) vs current subfic/cntlzw/srwi (boolean cast) -> "rewrite as explicit if/else". - walk/rollback shape: target stores via base+index*36+0x10 direct array walk vs current routes through a different array pointer via an inlined helper -> "write the direct pointer-walk". The frame-size + callee-save-set delta (target stmw r15 / 17 regs vs current stmw r18 / 14) is DOWNSTREAM of these shape fixes, not an independent wall. EXPECTED TOOL (MVP, heuristic): debug suggest control-flow-shape -f <fn> -> reads target ASM (extract get) + built .o, outputs a RANKED list of source transforms with the specific ASM evidence for each: (1) call-profile delta annotated in-loop vs out-of-loop (hoist candidates), (2) branch-idiom classification (boolean-cast vs explicit if/else; cmp/blt vs bdnz loop termination), (3) induction/walk-pointer shape (indexed lwzx vs hoisted element pointer; base+stride*i+off vs array[i][f]), (4) first-iteration peel / pass-unroll detection (instruction-before-loop-header matching loop-body-start), (5) missing/extra call layers (e.g. fn_803B1338: 0 vs 17 fn_803AC168 calls = an absent async builder layer). KEY ADVANTAGE: this is ASM-diff-driven (target asm vs built .o), so unlike frame/coalesce/schedule suggesters it needs NO pcdump cache and NO debug-compiler setup -- zero setup gate, immediately usable in any worktree. EXPECTED TOOL (AMBITIOUS, the big version): a CFG-aligned reconstruction recommender -- align the target's basic-block/loop/branch CFG against the current source's CFG, identify each structural divergence (extra/missing block, call placement, loop trip-count/peel, branch nesting, walk shape), map each to a source transform with a confidence score + ASM evidence, and optionally emit a candidate source patch the agent tests. This automates exactly what a human decompiler does on this class. NOT A FORCE KNOB: it never forces a register/coalesce/phys assignment. It reads target+current ASM, identifies STRUCTURAL divergence, and outputs SOURCE-level hypotheses the agent reasons about and tests. (Per near100_buckets_ceiling_dominated_census: register-source-search is ~0 yield; this is the opposite -- it targets the source-addressable structural class.) BLOCKED WORK / VALUE: control-flow-source-shape is the highest-confidence reconstruction lever (the <90% bands are 784 of 1593 unmatched project-wide; this class is a primary sub-population). In hsd_3AA7.c alone it applies to fn_803ADF90/AF3F0/AE7F8/B0120/B1338 (5 functions, builder family sharing one template). EMPIRICAL VALIDATION IN FLIGHT: a matching agent is right now applying these exact transforms to the builder family; will append the closed-count as proof of source-actionability. Governance: Reusable class: control-flow-source-shape Applies to: control-flow-source-shape class: hsd_3AA7.c builder family fn_803ADF90/AF3F0/AE7F8/B0120/fn_803B1338 + the 784 <90% reconstruction-band functions project-wide where this class dominates Source-actionable output: ranked source transforms each tied to an ASM divergence: hoist helper out of loop, boolean-cast->explicit if/else, indexed-walk vs hoisted-pointer, first-iteration peel/unroll, absent call-layer -- agent applies & tests Stop condition: stop when checkdiff class != control-flow-source-shape (residual has moved to register/frame/schedule, handled by existing suggesters) Existing workflow failed: no debug suggest subcommand covers this class (suite = frame/casts/register-tiebreak/coalesce/schedule/inlines); agents hand-diff extract-get vs dtk-disasm + custom bl-counter and mislabel results as CEILING-INLINE
resolution_note: fixed in 7b62482eb: add debug suggest control-flow-shape from checkdiff ASM with ranked source-transform evidence

summary: harvest filters need preview counts before running batches
body: Campaign evidence from /Users/mike/code/melee on 2026-06-04: updated --where filter tooling worked, but two guarded campaign batches produced zero-row ledgers because filter criteria drifted from the current taxonomy values. structural-reconstruction with --where source_actionability=structural-rebuild --where headline_tool=control-flow-shape-search processed 0 rows; the queue still has 213 structural-rebuild/control-flow-source-shape rows, but headline_tool is now extract-opseq-xrefs. stack-local-layout with --where source_actionability=source-probe processed 0 rows because the current queue now has 59 generator-gated and 6 current-tools rows, no source-probe rows. summary: mwcc-debug inspect diagnose VERDICT says 'WIN AVAILABLE' when cast auto-verify shows all false positives (+0.00%)
body: 2026-06-04: 'inspect diagnose mnDiagram_80242C0C' printed '== VERDICT: WIN AVAILABLE ==' driven by 2 HIGH-severity casts. But the cast-audit detail (when fully printed) says: 'line 2611: (f32) fighter_id drop test: 94.44% -> 94.44% (+0.00%, false positive)' for BOTH casts. Applying the suggested drop = exactly 0.00% change (confirmed via checkdiff). The VERDICT header should be NO FAST TRANSFORM (or 'casts found but all false-positive') when every auto-verified transform is +0.00%. As-is it misleads agents into applying neutral edits. The grep-friendly summary line ('WIN AVAILABLE') contradicts the detail body.
resolution_note: verified in d68c987c946534d0e751f7a641ae997c82c8ba11: current master gates WIN AVAILABLE on strictly positive verified cast deltas and adds a regression that +0.00% cast drops report false positive / NO FAST TRANSFORM FOUND

summary: Stranded-match recovery: 50 'match:' commits live in unmerged agent worktrees; ~12 are functions STILL unmatched in master — verified work being lost. Build a recover+verify+surface tool
body: DISCOVERY (this session): while doing manual TU completion I found plAttack_8003759C had a verified 100% match (702d724e5) stranded in another worktree, never merged. A scan (git log --all --grep='match:' --not upstream/master) found 50 such commits; intersecting their function names with the current unmatched taxonomy yields 12 functions that are verified-elsewhere but still unmatched in master. With 58 active worktrees and no auto-integration, verified matches are being lost at scale. CAVEATS the tool must handle (learned recovering plAttack_8003759C): - Base drift: the worktree commit's parent may differ from the current fork (plAttack: worktree had loops at i=0, my fork at i=2; cherry-pick conflicted). Must 3-way apply and RE-VERIFY with checkdiff, not trust the old 100% claim. - Upstream dupes: --not upstream/master excludes the COMMIT, not the FUNCTION; a function may already be matched in upstream via a different merged commit. Verify the function is actually unmatched in the live fork (and re-check upstream) before counting it. - Multiple competing commits per function (dedup): plAttack_8003759C x2, ftCo_80093790 x3, un_802FE260 x3, grIceMt_801FA0BC x3 — pick the one that re-verifies to match=true. - Sibling/data dependencies: some matches (e.g. ftNn_Init_80123954 per the sdata2-pool-flush class) depend on a sibling being matched; recovery may need the sibling commit too. Companion to #386 (the global-install clobber) — both stem from 58 isolated worktrees with no cross-worktree state sharing. This tool turns that liability into a recoverable asset. Governance: Reusable class: verified match commits stranded in abandoned/unmerged agent worktrees (never PR'd to master), recoverable by re-apply+checkdiff Applies to: 12 recoverable now (still in unmatched taxonomy + have a worktree match commit): fn_803ACF30, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, ftCo_8009C744, ftNn_Init_80123954, grGreatBay_801F60C4, grIceMt_801FA0BC, grPushOn_80218888, plAttack_8003759C(RECOVERED 2de5c51fb), pl_80040688, pl_800407C8, un_802FE260; 50 total stranded match: commits across worktrees Source-actionable output: a 'melee-agent recover-matches' tool: (1) git log --all --grep='match:' --not upstream/master to enumerate stranded match commits; (2) parse function name(s); (3) FILTER to functions still unmatched in the CURRENT fork (checkdiff != match) AND not already matched in upstream via another commit; (4) for each, apply the worktree commit's file change (cherry-pick or 3-way) — note base may differ so re-verify; (5) run checkdiff; (6) keep only match=true, drop redundant/regressed; (7) emit a batched, dedup'd list for one PR. Dedup needed: many functions have 2-3 competing worktree commits (e.g. plAttack_8003759C x2, ftCo_80093790 x3). Stop condition: Drain the recoverable set: each function either recovered (checkdiff match=true, committed) or confirmed-redundant (already matched in upstream) or confirmed-stale (no longer applies). Re-run opportunistically as worktrees accumulate; stop a given run when 0 new recoverable functions remain. Existing workflow failed: No current mechanism surfaces matches committed in one agent worktree to other agents or to master; they require manual PR. Proof: 702d724e5 'match: plAttack_8003759C 100%' sat unmerged in worktree-agent-ae48cd30 while the function stayed unmatched in master; I only found it by chance via a MEMORY note, then recovered it by hand (cherry-pick conflicted because the worktree base had i=0 vs my fork's i=2; needed re-verification).
resolution_note: Fixed in edbfcb1f2: audit recover-matches now supports --verify and --apply. It dedupes stranded match commits by function, transfers only the named function body from candidate commits, re-verifies with checkdiff --format json --no-fingerprint, preserves unrelated dirty work during verify, rejects dirty tracked worktrees for --apply, and reports verified/applied/not_match/apply_failed/skipped_after_verified rows. Verified tests/test_audit_recover_matches.py + test_audit_net_new.py (14 passed), compileall src/cli/audit.py, help/read-only/verify-zero smokes, and live --verify --limit 3 preserving status.

summary: checkdiff: fuzzy match% can RISE while opcode-similarity CRASHES when removing locally-redundant instructions from an inline-boundary-dominated function; opcode-similarity should be the authoritative structural-progress signal
body: OBSERVED in hsd_3AA7.c: applying the (correct) cmd-buffer no-zero-init lever to fn_803AF3F0 took fuzzy match 48.2->52.9% but opcode similarity 34.8->2.8% (line delta 23->115); fn_803AE7F8 13.6->4.3%. These functions are EXPECTED-larger (target inlines fn_803ACC0C/AC634 my code calls) AND have mis-reconstructed control flow (EXPECTED 8x fn_803AC168 vs my 4-5x, plus a spurious fn_803ACB74). Removing correct-but-redundant instructions shifts the stream so the positional aligner collapses, yet fuzzy-match (byte-bag) rewards it. REQUEST: (1) when fuzzy% rises but opcode-similarity drops by a large margin, checkdiff should WARN that this is likely false progress (the report.json TU% uses fuzzy and would mislead). (2) The existing 'current omits that call and is larger' line is a GOOD gate signal for the no-zero-init lever; consider surfacing a 'current-larger vs expected-larger' verdict explicitly so agents know whether instruction-removal levers are safe. (3) Consider an opcode-similarity-delta guard in the [REPEAT]/regression telemetry.
resolution_note: Fixed in ddd2174c81d3c22c4ae5ec4e618882ef98e2995c: checkdiff now warns when fuzzy match rises while opcode similarity collapses and line/hunk shape regresses, and inline-boundary artifacts expose size_verdict=current-larger. Verified tests/test_checkdiff_stack_diagnostics.py (16 passed) and compileall tools/checkdiff.py.

summary: mismatch-db candidate: per-iteration struct-field reload coalescing into one callee-save (model with for-condition comma assignment); plus inverse-CSE-rematerialize wall characterization
body: TWO findings from hsd_3AA7.c last-mile work: (A) LEVER (fn_803ACD58 97.3->97.9, byte-identical structure): when the target RELOADS a struct field every loop iteration (e.g. state->x8 reloaded in the for-condition each iter) and REUSES that one load in the body (i*x8 + the CARDRead size arg coalesced into ONE callee-save), a plain 'offset = i*state->x8; CARDRead(...,state->x8,...)' reloads x8 twice (extra instr). FIX: 'for(i=0; size=state->x8, i < (bound using size)/size; i++){ ... use size ... }' -- the comma operator makes 'size' a single per-iteration reload shared by condition AND body, coalescing to the callee-save the target uses. Residual after = ig_idx callee-save numbering only. NOTE: this is a recurring CardState shape; candidate mismatch-db pattern 'field reloaded-per-iter + reused-in-body -> for-condition comma local'. (B) CEILING (fn_803AC168 91.7%, banked): target indexes hsd_804D1148[hsd_804D7980] (read index) AND keeps read_idx (=hsd_804D7980) separately in a callee-save across OSRestoreInterrupts -> it REMATERIALIZES the non-volatile global read for the array index. ANY C form indexing by hsd_804D7980 or read_idx makes MWCC CSE the two reads (drops to 86.1%, register cascade). Current source dodges by indexing via the VOLATILE hsd_804D7984 (no CSE) but reads the wrong slot. This is the inverse of [[cse_rematerialize_divide_ceiling]] (target rematerializes, source CSEs); no C lever found. Just confirming the class extends to 'non-volatile global read that the target reloads but source CSEs'.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc8bb6b8c4ce37526bfda63cce7cc7a31e2c0803: added searchable mismatch-db entries loop-field-reload-comma-assignment and inverse-cse-rematerialized-global-read, with persisted DB migration, regression tests preserving user provenance, and live CLI search smokes.

summary: Lead gate-review of #391: ACCEPTED — generalizable source-transform op, not a force knob
body: Lead sign-off on #391 (cmd-buffer / sparse-scratch-array-field-write auto-suggest). GATE VERDICT: ACCEPTED. Satisfies the generality gate on all axes: - Reusable class named (cmd-buffer-no-zero-init), applies to 3 functions + 20+ call sites + 1 matched confirming sibling (fn_803AC258) — well over the 3+ threshold. - Source-actionable output is concrete (recognize 's32 cmd[N]={0}' feeding a type-dispatched consumer; suggest sparse field-write decoded from stw OFFs before the bl). - Has a precise, non-trivial stop-condition (only when checkdiff reports current-larger; do NOT apply when expected-larger/inline-boundary-dominated) — this is directly powered by the classification refinement in #392, so #391 and #392 should be resolved together. - It is a SOURCE-TRANSFORM op, explicitly NOT a per-function register force knob. This is exactly the class of generalizable tooling we want; the same campaign deliberately rejected force-only knobs. Verified empirically in-tree: the lever's three functions are committed (fn_803B0E9C 47.5->73.5, fn_803ADF90 57.4->65.6, fn_803AD16C 59.2->68.6) on the integration branch. Recommend prioritizing #391+#392 as a pair.
resolution_note: Addressed with #391 fix in dc8bb6b8c4ce37526bfda63cce7cc7a31e2c0803: accepted sparse scratch array source-transform pattern is now searchable and migrated into existing mismatch databases. #392 remains separate for checkdiff false-progress warning.

summary: harvest needs first-class queue filters for source-actionable campaigns
body: Campaign evidence from /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/a55e/melee on 2026-06-04: raw top-of-queue harvest sampling mostly measured queue hygiene instead of harness ROI. stack-local-layout top-5 produced unsupported-harness x4 + one cache error, but the regenerated queue has only 8 current-tools rows and 154 source-probe rows buried below generator-gated/ceiling rows. structural-reconstruction top-5 produced unsupported-harness x5 because raw ordering hit backend-ceiling/direct-inspection rows, despite the same queue containing 213 source_actionability=structural-rebuild rows with headline_tool=control-flow-shape-search. indexed-struct-pointer produced real negative evidence, but that required reading ledger details after the raw batch. Current workaround is manually creating filtered TSVs or taxonomy dirs before running harvest. Requested feature: add repeatable harvest filters such as --where source_actionability=current-tools, --where headline_tool=control-flow-shape-search, and/or --exclude-source-actionability backend-ceiling,generator-gated,diagnostic-only; record active filters in the ledger so harvest summarize can distinguish filtered ROI from raw queue noise. Governance: Reusable class: filtered taxonomy harvest by source_actionability/headline_tool/frame_closability_tier Applies to: stack-local-layout current-tools rows: it_3F14_Logic7_PickedUp, it_3F14_Logic7_DmgReceived, mnCount_8025092C, ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_1_Anim, ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_0_Anim, it_3F14_Logic7_Dropped, lb_80014638, it_802A013C, structural-reconstruction structural-rebuild rows: 213 rows including gm_801BE638, un_80305058, fn_8001E910, fn_8019A71C, it_802B64FC, grOnett_801E40E4, gm_801A4014, ft_80089B08, campaign hygiene for excluding backend-ceiling/generator-gated/diagnostic-only rows before small harvest batches Source-actionable output: harvest can run small batches only over source-actionable rows, producing ledgers whose blockers reflect harness/source-lever ROI instead of unsupported raw queue ordering Stop condition: Stop when agents can run filtered harvest batches without hand-built TSVs and the ledger records the filter criteria; verify with stack-local-layout current-tools and structural-reconstruction structural-rebuild batches where unsupported-harness from excluded rows is absent. Existing workflow failed: Raw harvest batches from the latest campaign: stack-local-layout --limit 5 => unsupported-harness x4 plus pcdump cache error; structural-reconstruction --limit 5 --compose => unsupported-harness x5; this led to a misleading recommendation to stop buckets that actually contain source-actionable rows.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc8bb6b8c4ce37526bfda63cce7cc7a31e2c0803: added first-class harvest queue filters (--where FIELD=VALUE and --exclude-source-actionability), filter metadata in ledgers and summarize rollups, with tests. Verified tests/test_harvest.py (78 passed), help smoke, --limit 0 metadata smoke, and real filtered structural-reconstruction queue smoke avoiding excluded unsupported-harness rows.

summary: mismatch-db pattern: fixed-size scratch array passed to type-dispatched consumer must NOT be zero-initialized (={0}); write only the fields the target stores
body: PROVEN: fn_803B0E9C 47.5->73.5% (line delta 80->4), fn_803ADF90 57.4->65.6%, fn_803AD16C 59.2->68.6% (opcode sim 20->62%). Field sets vary by cmd[0] type (e.g. type 4 = cmd[0,1,2,4,5,6,7,8] leaving cmd[3] dirty; type 6 = cmd[0,1] only). Recorded to memory cardstate_cmd_buffer_no_zero_init_lever.md. This is a SOURCE-TRANSFORM pattern (not a register force knob). Governance: Reusable class: cmd-buffer-no-zero-init / sparse-scratch-array-field-write Applies to: hsd_3AA7.c fn_803AC168 builders: fn_803B0E9C, fn_803ADF90, fn_803AD16C (20+ call sites); fn_803AC258 (matched, confirms pattern) Source-actionable output: Recognize 's32 cmd[N] = {0}' (or compound literal) feeding a type-dispatched consumer where the .o only writes a subset of fields; suggest: drop ={0}, declare bare array, write exactly the stored field set (incl. explicit zeros) decoded from stw rN,OFF(base) before the consumer bl. Even when all N are written, ={0} emits a separate zeroing block + overwrites (15 vs 9 stores). Stop condition: Only when checkdiff reports 'current ... is larger' (current-larger). If EXPECTED is larger (inline-boundary-dominated, e.g. fn_803AF3F0/AE7F8/B0120), the lever misaligns: opcode-similarity crashes while fuzzy-match rises -> do NOT apply. Existing workflow failed: m2c reconstructs fixed-size cmd records as zero-initialized; no existing op detects sparse-field-write intent. Manual decode per cmd-type works but is tedious across 20+ sites.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc8bb6b8c4ce37526bfda63cce7cc7a31e2c0803: added searchable mismatch-db pattern sparse-scratch-array-no-zero-init to sample entries and persisted DB migration, including source-transform guidance, current-larger caveat, provenance for fn_803B0E9C/fn_803ADF90/fn_803AD16C, regression tests, and live CLI search smoke.

summary: control-flow-shape-search missing operator: if(x==C){body} that emits non-inverted beq-body/b-skip should be modeled as single-case switch (closed fn_8019A71C the harness gave up on)
body: DIAGNOSIS: fn_8019A71C had a single residual instruction — expected compiles the leading 'if (*state == 0x13) { fn_8019B458(state); }' as a NON-inverted branch to the body (cmpwi; beq .body; b .skip; .body: ...; .skip:) while a plain if() emits the inverted skip (cmpwi; bne .skip; ...). MWCC emits the non-inverted branch-to-case-body layout for a single-case switch. Rewriting the if as 'switch (*state) { case 0x13: fn_8019B458(state); break; }' reproduced the exact shape -> 100% instruction-identical (committed 2e4e46f51, pre-commit Match-regressions passed). INCIDENCE NOTE (for the resolver's sweep): the 'expected N / current N-1 / branch shape differs' signature is NECESSARY but not SUFFICIENT for this lever — checked 2 sibling rows: grOnett_801E40E4 is a 4-byte offset shift from a different inserted instruction (same mnemonics), gm_801BE638 is CSE-vs-rematerialize on Player_GetRemainingHP. So the operator should (a) detect a leading 'if (x == constant) { body }' whose expected diff shows beq-to-body+b vs our bne, then (b) try the switch rewrite. Pairs well with the planned structural harness. Governance: Reusable class: non-inverted leading equality branch: expected 'beq body; b skip' (2 branches) vs our inverted 'bne skip' (1 branch); source lever = single-case switch not if Applies to: structural-reconstruction branch-or-control-flow-shape rows whose residual is 'expected N, current N-1' + 'branch shape differs before downstream operand' localized to a leading if(x==C){body}; PROVEN on fn_8019A71C (gm_18A5.c) 98.4%->100% instruction-identical, commit 2e4e46f51 Source-actionable output: new control-flow-shape-search operator 'if-equality-to-single-case-switch' that rewrites 'if (x == C) { body }' -> 'switch (x) { case C: body; break; }', compiles, scores with checkdiff, validates match=true; complements existing early-guard-return/guard-shape/ternary ops which do NOT cover this Stop condition: Ship when the operator auto-closes fn_8019A71C in a harvest dry-run AND a sweep of the 213 control-flow-shape rows reports how many additional rows it closes (even if 0 beyond fn_8019A71C, keep the operator + log incidence). Do not expand scope to multi-case or relational (>,>=) switches in this issue. Existing workflow failed: control-flow-shape-search (#378) dry-run returned no_match on fn_8019A71C (blocker no-control-flow-shape-candidate); its operators early-guard-return/guard-shape/ternary-to-if-else/if-else-to-ternary/bool-condition-spelling do not try if->switch, so it cannot produce the non-inverted branch shape
resolution_note: Fixed in 1ee931a7205d952109697d0c93a2a349285a484d: added local if-equality-to-single-case-switch control-flow-shape operator with scoped single-case switch rendering and safety tests. Verified fn_8019A71C validates at 100%. Incidence sweep considered 213 branch/control-flow rows; regex prefilter found 184 possible rows, actual generator scanned 177 with 15 probe rows, score pass validated 1 row (fn_8019A71C) and 0 additional rows; 7 actual-scan timeouts and 6 missing-source rows recorded in build/harvest/if-equality-switch-*.json.

summary: Sharpen #387/#386: isolated-worktree 'cd <main-repo> && ninja' silently builds in MAIN worktree — cost one agent ~1hr; actionable fix inside
body: Strengthens #387 (and root-cause #386) with concrete cost + a fix direction, from a 5-agent parallel batch. CONCRETE COST: one teammate lost ~1hr to flip-flopping diffs (a function reading 100% then 75% on alternate checkdiff runs) because its build/checkdiff ran in the SHARED main worktree (/Users/mike/code/melee) where another agent was editing the same file. The confusion looked like a tooling bug but was cross-worktree interference. EXACT TRIGGER: agents (often following skill examples) run 'cd /Users/mike/code/melee && ninja ...' or 'cd <main-repo> && python tools/checkdiff.py ...'. checkdiff/ninja then resolve ROOT to the main worktree, not the agent's isolated worktree, so edits in the isolated worktree are invisible and builds race other agents. ACTIONABLE FIXES (any of): 1. Skill/command examples must NOT hardcode 'cd /Users/mike/code/melee'; an isolated agent's cwd is already its worktree — run build/checkdiff with no cd. 2. checkdiff.py / melee-agent could warn (or refuse) when invoked with a cwd whose repo root differs from an env-provided expected worktree, or when GIT_WORK_TREE/cwd mismatch. 3. isolation-worktree creation should auto-run worktree-doctor --fix (symlink orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol): ALL 5 agents this batch hit the missing-DOL symlink (only .gitkeep present) and had to symlink it manually before building. BATCH ROI TALLY for this papercut class: 5/5 agents hit missing-DOL; >=2/5 hit the cd-to-main bleed; 1 lost ~1hr. High aggregate cost for parallel-agent workflows. Same single-global-install root as #386.
resolution_note: Fixed in 4675e3d9f: tools/checkdiff.py now refuses to run when MELEE_AGENT_EXPECTED_WORKTREE or GIT_WORK_TREE points at a different repo root, and CLAUDE/AGENTS build guidance now warns isolated agents not to cd into /Users/mike/code/melee before build/checkdiff.

summary: melee-agent editable install is a SINGLE global package shared across 58 worktrees — every 'pip install -e' repoints it, clobbering all other concurrent agents mid-sweep
body: ROOT CAUSE: 'pip install -e tools/melee-agent' installs ONE editable distribution into the global site-packages (/opt/homebrew/lib/python3.11/site-packages) with a single console-script entry point + __editable__ finder pointing at ONE worktree's tools/melee-agent. There are currently 58 worktrees (.claude/worktrees/* + .codex/worktrees/*), many with a live agent. Whenever ANY agent runs 'pip install -e' (the documented per-sweep workaround) it REPOINTS that single global install to its own worktree, silently changing every other agent's 'melee-agent' to run different code. IMPACT: - Non-determinism: a command's behavior (which harnesses exist, which resolver patches are live) depends on whoever reinstalled last. Results are not reproducible. - Correctness risk: an agent can unknowingly run ANOTHER worktree's half-edited/mid-commit code, producing wrong harvest/checkdiff/score results that then get committed as 'matches'. - Wasted cycles + reinstall-war: agents reinstall defensively before every sweep and STILL get clobbered partway through. - The design intent of editable install (resolver commits to tools/melee-agent are 'auto-live' without reinstall) is GOOD and must be preserved — the bug is that 'live' is shared globally instead of per-worktree. PREFERRED FIX (worktree-resolving launcher): make 'melee-agent' a thin shim that, at invocation, finds the git toplevel of CWD, prepends <toplevel>/tools/melee-agent/src (or the package root) to sys.path, and runs the CLI from there. Then every worktree's invocation uses its OWN code, edits stay live (editable semantics preserved per-worktree), and NO global pip state is touched -> the 58-worktree clobber surface collapses to zero. 'python -m src.cli' already resolves CWD-relative (pyproject.toml + src/cli present), so the shim is mostly plumbing + PATH. ALT: per-worktree venv (heavier; each agent uses <worktree>/.venv/bin/melee-agent). STOPGAP (ship immediately regardless): on every run, if the resolved package dir != CWD-worktree's tools/melee-agent, print a one-line stderr WARNING with both paths so agents detect the clobber instantly instead of debugging 'vanished' commands.
