Governance: Reusable class: shared global editable install repointed across concurrent agent worktrees (non-deterministic CLI: vanished harnesses, wrong/half-edited code, reinstall-war) Applies to: ALL melee-agent CLI commands run by concurrent agents in separate worktrees; observed this session the global 'Editable project location' flipped agent-a65e23063 -> practical-kilby-2ffbd7 -> /Users/mike/code/melee (main) within minutes; prior sessions: frame-transform-search 'vanished' when repointed to .codex/worktrees/7f1a Source-actionable output: a worktree-resolving 'melee-agent' launcher (resolve tools/melee-agent from the invoking CWD's git toplevel and run python -m src.cli with that on sys.path[0], so each invocation uses ITS OWN worktree code with zero global state) OR a per-worktree venv bootstrap; PLUS a cheap stopgap: melee-agent warns on every run when its resolved package dir != the CWD worktree's tools/melee-agent Stop condition: Done when two agents in different worktrees run melee-agent concurrently for >=1h without either reinstalling and without observing the other's code/harnesses (verify: each worktree's 'melee-agent debug mutate --help' lists only ITS registered harnesses regardless of what another agent does). If CWD-resolution is infeasible, ship the warn-on-mismatch stopgap + document a per-worktree venv workflow and update CLAUDE.md/MEMORY to stop advising 'pip install -e before each sweep'. Existing workflow failed: Current workaround (memory: 'pip install -e tools/melee-agent from your worktree, re-check before each sweep') does NOT hold: the install is global, so any other agent's reinstall clobbers it again mid-sweep; this session it was repointed away from my worktree at least twice between my own reinstalls.
resolution_note: Fixed in 9d14b71195b11ed9377769998a6e293ebd37133f: console script now targets a worktree-resolving src.launcher, doctor verifies/upgrades old entrypoints, and /opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent was refreshed from /Users/mike/code/melee.

summary: Re #386: editable-install path bleed manifested as builds running in a SIBLING worktree (2/5 agents, parallel batch)
body: Concrete repro/ROI evidence for #386 from a 5-agent parallel decomp batch (each agent in its own isolation worktree under .claude/worktrees/agent-*). OBSERVED: 2 of 5 agents reported their initial 'python configure.py && ninja -j3 build/GALE01/report.json' / checkdiff build resolved to a SIBLING worktree (mystifying-lewin-0b1904, the lead's worktree) instead of their own agent-* worktree. One agent: 'Initial build accidentally ran in a sibling worktree due to absolute-path skills; corrected by symlinking the DOL and running worktree-doctor --fix in my actual worktree.' Another hit missing base-DOL in the fresh worktree, also fixed via worktree-doctor --fix. IMPACT: wasted setup time per agent; risk of cross-worktree build/report.json races when N agents build concurrently. The lead's worktree source stayed clean (only gitignored build/ artifacts were touched), so no source clobber this time, but report.json/.o races are possible. WORKAROUND THAT WORKED: worktree-doctor.py --fix (symlinks base DOL) in the agent's real worktree, then re-run build from cwd. EXPECTED: a fresh isolation worktree should be build-ready (base DOL + generated headers present or auto-symlinked) and all build/checkdiff/melee-agent invocations should resolve ROOT from cwd, never a sibling worktree. Consider having isolation-worktree creation run worktree-doctor --fix automatically. This is the same single-global-editable-install root cause as #386; filing as a note so the resolver has the sibling-worktree-build symptom + repro.
resolution_note: Same root cause as #386; fixed in 9d14b71195b11ed9377769998a6e293ebd37133f by making the global entrypoint resolve src.cli from the invoking git worktree instead of the last editable install path.

summary: data-symbol harvest auto-yield = 0/25 (deepening 0/124): bucket is section-anchor/whole-TU-.data-layout dominated, NOT float-naming — settle anchor C-fixability before building more harness
body: CENSUS RESULT (2026-06-04): ran resolved #375 name-magic-source-declarations harness over top-25 data-symbol-relocation rows (all 99.9x%). 0 matched. Corroboration: user's name-magic-data-deepening matched 0/124. The 'highest-yield 110 near-100 easy wins' framing (binary_wide_harvest_map memory) is REFUTED for current tooling. WHY: the float/@N case the harness handles is already absent in these high-% rows; the residual keeping match=false is dominated (~68% of sample) by the section-anchor case: expected names a .data symbol (e.g. mn_803EAE68) but our build emits R_PPC_ADDR16_HA/LO ...data.N. mn_803EAE68 is ALREADY a named static array (mnmain.c:62) and the function STILL emits ...data.0 -> lever is NOT 'declare the name', it is whole-TU .data object ORDER / split placement (cf. sdata2-pool-flush-sibling class). 3 rows were correctly auto-flagged sdata2-pool-order-dependent (the #375 caveat works). DECISION GATE before building: empirically test on mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter whether the section-anchor can be flipped from C (reorder .data defs off split-0, or model a neighbor blob). checkdiff is the oracle (this row does NOT auto-resolve to match=true, unlike the benign anchor class). Outcome A fixable -> whole-TU data-layout-reorder harness + sibling-pool-order resolver (match controlling sibling first; can cascade per TU). Outcome B ceiling -> re-bucket out of current-tools-data-symbol. Either way one verdict redirects ~110 near-100 rows. Governance: Reusable class: data-symbol section-anchor (R_PPC_ADDR16_HA/LO ...data.N vs named .data symbol) + whole-TU .data layout / sibling-pool-order Applies to: data-symbol-relocation bucket top-25 (0 closed): ~17 section-anchor (mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter, fn_80186080, grGreatBay_801F499C, ifStatus_802F7134, grBigBlue_801E59F8, grHomeRun_8021C82C, grOldPupupu_802108B4, grPura_80211E08, gmClassic_801B2D54, mpGetSpeed, gm_801A9B30_OnEnter, grGreens_80213524), 3 pool-order (fn_801981A0, grCastle_801CD4D0, grCorneria_801DD534), 3 ambiguous-value (HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtr, HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr, grCorneria_801E1BF0), 3 unsupported-site (HSD_TExpSchedule, it_802A850C, Exception_ReportStackTrace) Source-actionable output: A verdict on section-anchor C-fixability + either a data-layout-reorder/sibling-pool-order harness spec (if fixable) or a re-bucket of section-anchor rows into a ceiling tier (if not) Stop condition: If mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter section-anchor (...data.0 vs mn_803EAE68) cannot be flipped to the named reloc from C after <=3 levers (.data def reorder, neighbor-struct model, controlling-sibling match), declare section-anchor a link-layout ceiling, re-bucket those rows out of current-tools-data-symbol, and do NOT build a data-layout harness. If it CAN be flipped, build the harness; stop when it auto-closes >=10 data-symbol rows or exhausts the 132-row bucket. Existing workflow failed: melee-agent harvest data-symbol-relocation (name-magic-source-declarations harness #375): 0/25 closed; blockers no-name-magic-candidate 9, raw-diff-no-supported-data-symbol-pair 7, sdata2-pool-order-dependent 3, ambiguous-sdata2-value 3, unsupported-source-site 3
resolution_note: Fixed in 7864412d45c9dd45d168cf238dd6ce26caa04602: mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter decision gate shows section-anchor HA/LO is source-fixable via static-to-global, and harvest now re-buckets residual cases as section-anchor-source-fixable-residual instead of generic no-name-magic-candidate.

summary: permute remote status 'best score' is windowed/latest-iteration, not global-min — non-monotonic, blocks convergence/crackability assessment
body: OBSERVED: 'remote status' prints a line 'best score: iteration N, K errors, score = S' but S is NOT the global minimum — it is non-monotonic across polls. REPRODUCER (same job, two polls minutes apart): fn_80169900: poll1 -> 'iteration 5726, score = 20'; poll2 -> 'iteration 7679, score = 1390' gmMainLib_8015D984: poll1 -> 'score = 45'; poll2 -> 'score = inf' GROUND TRUTH via 'remote tail fn_8001EBF0-...': every log line is the CURRENT iteration's random-mutation score (50,1155,740,170,50,50,255,...), bouncing off a floor of 50; status appears to grab a recent-window value, not the running best. IMPACT: cannot tell crackable (floor descending toward 0 / hit score==0) from ceiling (floor pinned at e.g. 50) from 'status' alone — defeats the crackability census this fleet is running. Must 'tail' and eyeball, which is unscalable across 9+ jobs. ASK: (1) parse the full remote permuter.log for the true global-min score and report 'best (global-min): S @iterN' separately from 'latest: S'; (2) detect+flag score==0 (match found) and whether decomp-permuter saved an output candidate; (3) add 'permute remote triage' that summarizes all local jobs as one table: fn | iters | global-min | latest | match? | verdict(descending/plateau/ceiling). This makes the register-crackability census a single command instead of 9 tails.
resolution_note: Fixed in cab8540702cced814ffa61577bca5d4459adf3aa: remote status now parses full permuter.log for global-min/latest scores, reports match/output flags, and adds debug permute remote triage.

summary: Build control-flow-shape source-transform harness for structural-reconstruction/branch-shape (213 fns)
body: branch-or-control-flow-shape is the largest structural sub-class (213). The fixes are a known, finite pattern library (mismatch-db): insert/remove a redundant null-guard to add/kill a dead beq; invert ternary to if/else; hoist an early return; fold bool/==0. Build a harness like frame-transform-search/coalesce-search that enumerates these pattern transforms against the diff, compiles, scores via checkdiff, validates, and applies. Seed from the mismatch-db entries. High population, semi-mechanical. Governance: Reusable class: structural-reconstruction branch-or-control-flow-shape: apply mismatch-db control-flow source rewrites + validate Applies to: the 213 branch-or-control-flow-shape rows (147 of 376 structural are >=95%); plus 153 opcode-sequence-diff rows Source-actionable output: for a branch-shape mismatch, generate candidate control-flow source rewrites from the mismatch-db pattern library (redundant null-guard dead-beq; ternary <-> if/else inversion; early-return restructure; bool / ==0 folds; defensive-guard wrap), compile, score with checkdiff, validate match=true, apply Stop condition: either a validated control-flow rewrite reaching match=true, or no pattern-library transform improves the branch shape for that function (defer to manual) Existing workflow failed: next_command for these 213 is 'extract get + checkdiff' (manual inspection only). The mismatch-db ALREADY encodes the control-flow patterns (dead_beq_via_redundant_null_guards, ternary-to-if-else-inversion, etc.) but no tool APPLIES + validates them -- agents hand-rewrite one at a time. Frame got frame-transform-search; register got coalesce-search/select-order-search; control-flow-shape (the single largest sub-class) has no search harness.
resolution_note: fixed in 1a22ef5a1: added control-flow-shape probe generator, debug mutate control-flow-shape-search, harvest/taxonomy routing, and verified on master after #375/#382 integration

summary: name-magic source harness (#375)/compose emits 'extern' INTO the .c — violates convention, pre-commit blocks it; must emit to a header
body: harvest data-symbol-relocation --compose --apply applied to mpGetSpeed: inserted 'extern volatile f32 mpLib_804D8050;' directly into src/melee/mp/mplib.c (line ~4022) plus 'speed->z = 0.0F' -> 'speed->z = mpLib_804D8050'. This VIOLATES the codebase convention (CLAUDE.md: keep externs in .h/.static.h, not .c). The pre-commit 'Extern declarations' check BLOCKS new .c externs -- verified earlier on grGreatBay_801F499C where the externs had to be moved to grgreatbay.h to commit (a1125f217). REQUIREMENT for #375: the name-magic source harness must emit the extern declarations to the function's HEADER (e.g. mplib.h), not inline in the .c. Secondary: the same apply also did 'static struct mpLib_803BF248_t_x4 mpLib_803BD3D8 = {' -> removed 'static' (a separate data-symbol naming change) and only reached 99.26% (sub-100, not a full match), so the partial is not committable regardless. Net composition gauge on data-symbol top-8: 0 full matches (4 blocked, 3 no_match, 1 sub-100 partial) -- these composites have ceiling layers (slot-placement/register) no harness closes. Please ensure #375 places externs in a header before marking done.
resolution_note: fixed in ab87281e1: name-magic source probes now carry externs as header declarations, generated variants retain .h candidates, and scorer/harvest apply and roll back source+header together including compose partial layers

summary: Build name-magic SOURCE-declaration auto-harvest harness for data-symbol-relocation (132 fns, mostly ~100%, anon-vs-named relocs)
body: data-symbol-relocation is the highest-yield unmatched class: 132 fns, 110 at >=99%, byte-identical but match=false because source emits anonymous symbols (floats interned as @N in sdata2; data referenced via ...data.N+offset section anchors) where the original used NAMED symbols. The name-magic mapping is already computable (checkdiff resolves to 100% via it; verify-name-magic lists values + bias suggestions). Build a harness analogous to frame-transform-search that: (1) reads verify-name-magic + checkdiff expected reloc names to map each anonymous symbol to its named target; (2) GENERATES the source declarations (proven patterns: volatile named float extern to defeat constant-fold; named data extern + field reference replacing section-anchor+offset; named int-to-float bias symbols); (3) compiles, scores with checkdiff, validates match=true; (4) applies. Caveat to handle: .sdata2/.data pool ORDER can depend on earlier unmatched siblings in the TU (see the sdata2 pool-flush class) -- detect and report that as a blocker rather than a false fail. This single harness unblocks ~110 near-100% functions. Governance: Reusable class: data-symbol-relocation: anonymous .sdata2/@N literals + section-anchor relocs to named-symbol source declarations Applies to: the 132 data-symbol-relocation rows (110 at >=99%, 36 at >=99.9%); reproducer mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter (3 diffs: @267->mn_804DBDA8 float 0.0, plus ...data.0->mn_803EAE68 named data x2) Source-actionable output: generated source declarations that make the function a REAL byte-match: named float/double externs with anti-constant-fold (volatile) replacing @N sdata2 literals; named data-symbol externs + references replacing ...data.N+offset section-anchor relocs; int-to-float bias named symbols (verify-name-magic already maps @377->s32=NAMED, @834->u32=NAMED). Compile + validate via checkdiff, then apply. Stop condition: for a data-symbol-relocation fn at ~100% whose only diff is anonymous-vs-named relocs, either a validated source-declaration set reaching match=true, or an explicit blocker named (e.g. .sdata2 pool order depends on an unmatched sibling function in the same TU) Existing workflow failed: verify-name-magic lists anonymous .sdata2 values and suggests int-to-float bias mappings; name-magic --map REWRITES THE .O (verification-only, NOT shippable source); checkdiff --no-name-magic shows the expected named symbol. But NO tool generates the SOURCE declarations/references to make it a real match -- agents hand-declare named float externs / model data blobs per function, 132 times. Frame got frame-transform-search (generate->compile->score->validate); data-symbol-relocation (the single largest near-100% class) has only advisory/verification tools.
resolution_note: fixed in 42786eda1: added name-magic source declaration probe generation, debug mutate name-magic-source-declarations scoring with checkdiff --no-name-magic, and data-symbol-relocation harvest registration/apply/rollback gates

summary: harvest driver should COMPOSE multiple harnesses per function until match=true — single-bucket harvest only closes single-layer fns, but unmatched fns are mostly composite
body: Key strategic finding from running the landed harnesses: the remaining ~1100 unmatched functions are unmatched BECAUSE they are composite (multi-layer). Each per-category harness (#375 data-symbol, #376/#381 indexed-struct, #378 control-flow, frame) fixes ONE layer; applied alone it only closes the rare single-layer function in its bucket. To harvest at scale, the #379 driver must COMPOSE: for each function, apply every applicable harness (data-symbol → indexed-struct → frame → register → control-flow), re-running checkdiff classification between steps, until match=true or no harness helps. Commit full matches; per layers-of-blockers, optionally commit a verified correct intermediate layer and report the residual. This is the difference between harvesting single-layer stragglers (few) and the composite majority (most of the 1100). Builds on #379/#380. Governance: Reusable class: composite-function harvest: iterate/compose layer-fix harnesses (data-symbol + indexed-struct + frame + register + control-flow) per function until match=true Applies to: composite unmatched fns: fn_80186F6C (indexed-struct + 36 data-relocs + frame), mnCharSel_8025D1C4 (indexed-struct + 7 data-relocs + register), grGreatBay_801F499C (data-symbol + slot-placement) Source-actionable output: harvest driver applies ALL applicable registered harnesses to a function in sequence, re-classifying after each layer-fix, validating match=true at the end; per the layers-of-blockers policy, keep a correct layer-fix even if the function does not yet fully match, and report the remaining residual layer Stop condition: a function reaches match=true via composed layer-fixes, or no registered harness improves it further (then report the residual layer/blocker) Existing workflow failed: the per-bucket harvest (#379) applies ONE harness per function. But unmatched fns are mostly COMPOSITE (multiple residual layers): indexed-struct-pointer fns are ALSO data-symbol + frame + register. A single harness fixes one layer but never reaches match=true — verified: indexed-struct harvest on the top-15 bucket rows = 0 full matches (6 no_match because the scalar-split fixes the indexed-struct layer but data-symbol/register layers remain; 9 blocked). Single-layer fns (which one harness CAN close) are rare in the unmatched set — those were matched long ago. So the high-yield path is COMPOSITION.
resolution_note: fixed in 77da349ea: harvest --compose now runs ordered registered harness layers, preserves verified sub-100 layer improvements with strict JSON checkdiff/rollback, records per-layer ledger details, and supports name-magic harvest selection

summary: indexed-struct-search harness (#376) blocks all canonical rows — looks for explicit source pointer but materialization is MWCC-internal CSE of arr[i].field
body: melee-agent harvest indexed-struct-pointer --apply blocked all 15 first-bucket rows with indexed-struct-hint-unavailable, INCLUDING the canonical fn_80186F6C and mnCharSel_8025D1C4 that checkdiff --compact clearly flags as indexed-struct-pointer-materialization WITH the rewrite hint. ROOT CAUSE: the harness _indexed_struct_pointer_candidates (pressure_explorer.py:2785) scans SOURCE for an explicit element-pointer (T* p = &arr[i]; p->field) to rewrite. But the source is ALREADY direct array.base+offset: fn_80186F6C uses lbl_803D9498[lbl_804D6618.x0].x0 / .x4 / .x8 ... repeatedly. MWCC materializes the shared element pointer ITSELF by CSE-ing &array[index] across the repeated accesses -- there is NO explicit source pointer to rewrite, so the harness finds 0 candidates and blocks every row. The ACTUAL lever (per checkdiff's own hint) is 'split the first field into a scalar local' to break MWCC's CSE of the index expression while keeping later accesses as array.base+offset. FIX: implement the scalar-split (or index-local) transform that discourages MWCC's CSE materialization, and/or consume checkdiff's asm indexed-struct hint to locate the materialized site -- do not require an explicit source pointer. Net harvest this run: 0/15 (all blocked); harness is currently non-functional for the common indexed-struct pattern.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc6522d0d: indexed-struct-search now detects repeated direct array[index].field accesses and emits C89-compatible scalar-split probes; verified on fn_80186F6C and mnCharSel_8025D1C4.

summary: Build indexed-struct-pointer source-transform harness: element-pointer materialization to direct array.base+offset (46 fns)
body: indexed-struct-pointer-materialization is a mechanically-defined class: source materializes T* p = &base[i] and accesses p->field, where the original kept array.base+byte/index offset. checkdiff prints the precise rewrite. Build a harness like frame-transform-search that: (1) parses the checkdiff indexed-struct hint + source to locate the element-pointer; (2) generates the AST rewrite (scalar-local first field; direct base[i].field afterwards); (3) compiles, scores with checkdiff, validates; (4) applies. __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY (100%, bytes already match) is the canonical first target. 46 functions; clean mechanical yield. Governance: Reusable class: indexed-struct-pointer-materialization: live element-pointer (T* p = &base[i]; p->field) rewritten to direct base[i].field / array.base+offset Applies to: the 46 indexed-struct-pointer rows (4 at >=99%); reproducer __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY at 100.00% (24 indexed load/stores, current materializes 9 element pointers) Source-actionable output: detect a materialized per-element pointer held live across field accesses/calls/loops and rewrite later p->field accesses to direct base[i].field (array.base+offset) expressions, splitting the first field into a scalar local per the checkdiff hint; compile + validate via checkdiff, apply Stop condition: for an indexed-struct-pointer fn whose checkdiff hint names an element-pointer materialization, either a validated rewrite reaching match=true, or a named blocker (the pointer is genuinely required live) Existing workflow failed: checkdiff already EMITS the exact transform (split first field to scalar, keep accesses as array.base+offset, avoid a live per-element pointer across calls/loops) but NO tool applies + validates it. Agents hand-rewrite per function, 46 times. Frame got frame-transform-search; this mechanical pointer-shape class has only the advisory hint.
resolution_note: Fixed in 13b3605000ea33a9e02dc467e7c2d65dfc4de78c: added debug mutate indexed-struct-search, conservative indexed pointer probe generation with safety blockers, harvest indexed-struct-pointer registration, stable blocker propagation, apply rollback coverage, and committed spec/plan artifacts.

summary: harvest driver (#379) re-processes already-matched functions from stale queue; applies redundant probes (churn) and lacks a match-regression guard
body: melee-agent harvest stack-local-layout --apply re-processed it_3F14_Logic7_PickedUp and it_3F14_Logic7_DmgReceived (BOTH already match=true 100% via committed PAD_STACK) and applied a redundant block-scope wrapper on top (counted as status=applied) because the census queue queues/stack-local-layout.tsv pre-dates the matches. No regression (they stay matched) but it is churn and mislabels already-matched fns as fresh applies. FIXES: (1) before processing each row, run checkdiff and SKIP if already match=true (or refresh the queue from current build/GALE01/report.json); (2) add a post-apply match-regression guard (like the pre-commit Match-regressions hook) so --apply can never touch/regress an existing match in the same file; (3) report 'already-matched' as a distinct ledger status, not 'applied'. Observed ledger on a 20-row stack-local-layout run: applied=2 (both already-matched churn), no_match=5 (composite frame-too-small that do not close), unsupported=13 (reorder-gated-362 slot-placement, no registered harness). Net NEW matches from the driver run: 0.
resolution_note: fixed in c8c839a75: harvest --apply now preflights rows with checkdiff and records already-matched instead of running harness/apply; apply also rolls back if a previously matched same-file function regresses

summary: Unify per-category auto-harvest behind a single melee-agent harvest <work_bucket> driver + common harness contract
body: Frame proved the loop: census tier -> generate -> compile -> score -> validate -> apply -> commit. Generalize it: define a common harness CONTRACT (each category harness takes a function and returns a validated source transform or a named blocker) and a melee-agent harvest <work_bucket> driver that reads the closability tier, dispatches to the right harness, applies validated closes (--apply), and emits a yield ledger. Then adding a category = registering its harness. Composes #375 (data-symbol), #376 (indexed-struct), #378 (control-flow), frame-transform-search, coalesce-search. This is the orchestration layer that turns the per-class harnesses into an overnight binary-wide harvest. Governance: Reusable class: cross-category auto-harvest orchestration / common harness contract Applies to: all work_buckets with a validate-harness: frame (frame-transform-search), register-allocator (coalesce-search/select-order-search), data-symbol-relocation (#375), indexed-struct-pointer (#376), control-flow-shape (#378) Source-actionable output: a melee-agent harvest <work_bucket> [--apply] [--min-match N] driver that reads the census tier, dispatches each function to its category validate-harness via a common contract (input: function; output: {validated source transform | named blocker}), applies validated closes, and reports a per-tier yield ledger Stop condition: harvest <bucket> sweeps the census tier and returns, per function, either an applied/validated match or a named blocker; works for >=2 categories sharing the contract Existing workflow failed: each category harness is a separate one-off command (frame-transform-search, coalesce-search, future #375/#376/#378) with bespoke output; an agent must hand-loop per category. There is no single sweep driver or common harness output contract, so harvesting any category at scale is manual orchestration (as done by hand this session for frame: 8-function sweep, hand-apply, commit).
resolution_note: fixed in 47ee857335ea168bfbbd26f4cab20403e5fc014e: added root melee-agent harvest <work_bucket> driver, common harness contract, versioned yield ledger, safe apply/rollback, CLI tests, and installed CLI smoke coverage

summary: frame-transform-search dematerialize (#369) misses the dominant frame-too-large culprit: address-taken / multi-use locals
body: Harvest finding 2026-06-04: the current-tools-padstack (frame-too-small) tier auto-closes via the #366 PAD_STACK generator (DmgReceived 99.94->100 this session). But the frame-too-large tier (gen-gated-366, 19 rows) yields ~0 clean closes because #369 frame-local-dematerialize only handles safe one-use scalar/pointer locals; the real culprits are address-taken locals (itSeakneedlethrown @365 is an address-taken 8-byte float-pair passed to a fill function) or multi-use locals, where dematerialize correctly declines. Several gen-gated-366 rows are also MISCLASSIFIED: their actual frame delta is 0 (frame already correct) with a non-frame residual (mnItemSw_8023453C, un_803053C4 at frame=expected) -- the checkdiff-only tier over-counts frame-too-large. Asks: (1) extend dematerialize to address-taken (escape-analysis: if &local only feeds a non-escaping callee) and multi-use cases; (2) tighten the checkdiff-only tier so frame=expected rows are not tagged gen-gated-366/frame-size. Builds on #366/#369. Governance: Reusable class: frame-too-large dematerialize for address-taken / multi-use locals Applies to: gen-gated-366 frame-too-large rows; ~17 of 19 swept returned no-safe-lever/inconclusive (itSeakneedlethrown @365 address-taken float-pair, mpJointListAdd, ftCo_800AC5A0, mpCheckFloor, ftAction_80072CD8, Ground_801C4FAC) Source-actionable output: validated source transforms that shrink the frame when the over-reserved slot is an address-taken or multi-use local (e.g. prove the taken address does not escape and convert to register-resident values, or split/narrow the object), not only safe one-use scalar/pointer locals Stop condition: for a frame-too-large divergence whose culprit is address-taken/multi-use, either a validated shrink transform or an explicit non-reachable verdict naming why Existing workflow failed: frame-transform-search PAD_STACK closes frame-too-SMALL cleanly (PickedUp, DmgReceived = 100% this session), but a 19-function gen-gated-366 (frame-too-large) sweep produced 0 clean closes: #369 dematerialize only fires on safe ONE-USE scalar/pointer locals and reports no-safe-semantic-lever for the address-taken/multi-use culprits that dominate this tier
resolution_note: fixed in 02642fb6d: same-frame stack-layout rows now use structured frame data and route to lifetime-layout instead of frame-too-large/gen-gated-366; frame-local-dematerialize no-safe statuses now name address-taken and multi-use local blockers without generating unsafe source rewrites

summary: Refine signature-call-type classifier: 297-row class is mostly red-herring (suggest-casts finds nothing); split out true cast-fixable subset
body: The signature-call-type bucket (297, 2nd largest) over-attributes to signature/cast when the real cause is downstream. Validated: ftCo_800CF6E8 suggest-casts is empty; the diff is structural+data+frame. Refine classify_bucket to (1) run/precompute suggest-casts and keep signature-call-type ONLY when a medium+ cast exists; (2) otherwise re-bucket by the dominant residual (frame-reservation gap -> stack-local-layout; high reloc count -> data-symbol-relocation; line-count/call-shape with inline markers -> inline-boundary). This prevents agents from burning cycles on 297 dead-end next_commands. Companion to the divide-rematerialize / prologue-mr red-herring findings. Governance: Reusable class: signature-call-type classification accuracy: separate true cast-fixable from symptom-misclassified call-shape diffs Applies to: the 297 signature-call-type rows; ftCo_800CF6E8 (suggest casts finds 0 medium+ casts; real diff = +-4 lines + 578 reloc + 32 register + frame PAD_STACK) Source-actionable output: reclassify signature-call-type rows: surface the subset where suggest casts actually finds a medium+ cast (the true cast-fixable harvest), and re-bucket the rest by dominant residual (frame / inline / data-symbol / structural) so the next_command points at the real lever, not a dead-end suggest-casts Stop condition: rows where suggest casts finds 0 casts are no longer labeled cast-fixable; each is re-bucketed by its dominant residual signal Existing workflow failed: census next_command for all 297 is 'debug suggest casts && checkdiff', but suggest casts finds NO casts on the top rows (ftCo_800CF6E8: No casts at severity>=medium) and the real diff is a multi-residual structural mismatch. classification=signature-type-mismatch is a known red-herring (call-shape diff is a downstream symptom, e.g. +1 instr shifting bl offsets). Agents follow next_command and find nothing actionable.
resolution_note: Fixed in 30f35af5f: function taxonomy now runs a source cast audit for signature-type-mismatch rows, keeps signature-call-type only when medium/high cast evidence exists, exposes cast counts in artifacts, and reroutes zero-cast red herrings by residual frame/data/inline/structural signals. Verified ftCo_800CF6E8 has 0 cast warnings and now routes to stack-local-layout/frame-size-delta.

summary: function-taxonomy: sub-categorize stack-local-layout by frame-divergence CAUSE + closability tier (wire #360 attribution into classifier + digest + dashboard)
body: Now that the frame attribution stack exists (#358 frame_allocation_trace, #359 expected reconstruction, #360 first-divergence cause + attributed-object + reachable/ceiling verdict), the taxonomy should sub-categorize stack-local-layout by actual CAUSE and CLOSABILITY, not just direction. Promote #360 outputs into the taxonomy records, the stuck-static-focus digest (debug.py), and the dashboard (function_taxonomy_dashboard.py + template). Sub-categories proven this session: (1) frame-size/pure-reservation [no access origin] = CLOSABLE NOW via PAD_STACK(delta) — it_3F14_Logic7_PickedUp matched 100 percent, Dropped peeled the frame layer; (2) frame-size/lifetime-or-ordering-shift [named object tmp/sp18/spawn/item_attrs+12] = gated on #366 materialize/lifetime generator (PAD_STACK only nudges, e.g. mnCount 99.92->99.94); (3) frame-too-large = gated on #366 remove-slot/FP-hoist generator; (4) same-frame-stack-slot-placement = gated on #362 assignment-order reorder lever; (5) ceiling/unresolved-attribution = bank. The routing work (resolved #356) should consume the closability tier to choose next_command. DESIGN NOTE: cause/object/verdict come from the pcdump-based attribution; either run it for the bounded stack-local-layout subset during generation (~62 rows, affordable) or derive a pcdump-free pure-reservation approximation from checkdiff no-access-origin reasons. Builds on #356/#358/#360/#362/#366. Governance: Reusable class: stack-local-layout sub-categorization by frame-divergence cause + closability tier Applies to: all ~62 stack-local-layout rows, frame-size/pure-reservation (PickedUp, Dropped), frame-size/lifetime-or-ordering-shift (mnCount tmp, ftKb item_attrs+12, it_802B1DF8 spawn), same-frame-stack-slot-placement (AXDriver_8038BF6C) Source-actionable output: taxonomy records + stuck-static-focus digest + dashboard rows that carry, per stack-local-layout function: the frame-divergence CAUSE (pure-reservation / lifetime-or-ordering-shift / stack-object-offset-shift / slot-placement), the attributed source object, the source-reachable/ceiling/unresolved verdict, and a CLOSABILITY TIER (current-tools-padstack | gen-gated-366 | reorder-gated-362 | ceiling) — with a next_command matching the tier and dashboard grouping/filtering by tier Stop condition: every stack-local-layout row carries cause+verdict+closability-tier; the dashboard can filter to current-tools-ready rows; pure-reservation rows are labeled current-tools-padstack and lifetime-shift/too-large rows are labeled gen-gated-366 Existing workflow failed: function_taxonomy_inventory.py classify_bucket only splits frame DIRECTION (frame-too-large/small/delta) and same-frame-stack-slot-placement; it does not capture the frame-divergence CAUSE, the attributed source object, or the reachable/ceiling verdict that #360 now produces. So the dashboard cannot distinguish rows closable NOW (PickedUp/Dropped pure-reservation pattern, PAD_STACK) from rows gated on the #366 generators or the #362 reorder lever, and an agent must run inspect frame-reservations per function to learn which is which.
resolution_note: Fixed in 44002c048: added stack-frame closability taxonomy, pcdump-backed frame attribution in inventory rows, tiered stuck digest routing, dashboard closability filters/columns, and executable current-tools frame-transform commands.

summary: Frame allocator under-reserves spill slots below inlined struct (AbsorbDesc) vs retail
body: ftCo_GuardReflect_Anim (src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.c): with #pragma inline_depth(3) + PAD_STACK(8) the function is byte-identical to the shipped binary EXCEPT the inlined ftCo_80092450 AbsorbDesc is placed at sp+0x18 vs expected sp+0x24 (off by 12 bytes / 3 words). The expected reserves a larger UNUSED spill region below the named/inlined struct; my compile reserves less. Opcode sequence, registers, and schedule all match (checkdiff class=stack-slot-layout, 99.95%). Reproducers in same TU (all same AbsorbDesc-placement class): - ftCo_80093BC0 99.91% (AbsorbDesc 0x10 vs 0x14, off by 4) - ftCo_80092908 99.79% (off by 4) - ftCo_GuardReflect_Anim 99.95% (off by 12) CONTRAST: ftCo_800924C0 and ftCo_80093A50 inline the SAME ftCo_80092450 and place the AbsorbDesc correctly at 100%. So it is context-dependent allocator spill-reservation, not a systematic wibo bug. No C-source lever found: PAD_STACK / u8[N] / Vec3+(void)& all land ABOVE the inlined struct (named-local region) and never grow the low spill region. mwcc-debug has no frame model so inspect can't see it. This is a concrete candidate for the proposed Tier-8 frame/local-area layer (#329/#330): the residual is purely reserved-but-unused spill bytes below an inlined address-taken struct.
resolution_note: Fixed in cdb11bac4: checkdiff now recognizes PAD_STACK-assisted same-frame stack-slot-localizer cases where expected stack locals remain higher as a reserved-unused-low-spill-region ceiling, suppressing generic natural frame-reservation advice and exposing closability_tier=ceiling/source_guidance=reserved-low-spill-ceiling. Verified with pytest tools/melee-agent/tests/test_checkdiff_stack_diagnostics.py -q; current ftCo_GuardReflect_Anim --no-build smoke runs but current object is in a broader signature mismatch state, not the reported near-match shape.

summary: name-magic value-collision: duplicate-valued .sdata2 magic constants map to wrong symbol, spurious checkdiff mismatch on byte-perfect functions
body: REPRO: gm/gm_1832.c fn_80188EE8 is byte-perfect (verified: dtk disasm of build/GALE01/src vs build/GALE01/obj .o shows 195 instruction rows, all 50 byte diffs are R_PPC_REL24/ADDR16/SDA21 relocation PLACEHOLDERS; normalized mnemonic diff is EMPTY; every reloc target matches the reference by VALUE in identical ORDER). Yet checkdiff reports match=false 89.87%. ROOT CAUSE: tools/melee-agent/src/mwcc_debug/o_rewriter.py find_named_sdata2_symbols_by_value() uses out.setdefault(value, name) (line ~197), and suggest_name_magic_map() maps each anon @N to a named symbol purely by 8-byte VALUE. This TU has DUPLICATE-VALUED magic constants from an FP-constant-pool flush: lbl_804DA598==lbl_804DA600 (both 0x4330000000000000, unsigned int->float bias) and lbl_804DA560==lbl_804DA610 (both 0x4330000080000000, signed bias). The reference fn_80188EE8 references lbl_804DA600 + lbl_804DA610 (the post-flush duplicates), but name-magic renames our @889/@672 to the FIRST symtab alias lbl_804DA598/lbl_804DA560, producing a spurious reloc-NAME mismatch even though the resolved addresses/values are correct and the .text is identical. The comment at o_rewriter.py:161-162 ('magic values are globally unique bit patterns, so matching by value is unambiguous') is FALSE when a TU has pool-flush duplicates. A single global @N->name rename cannot satisfy functions that reference different same-value aliases. FIX (positional, per-relocation-site): when the target .o has multiple named symbols sharing a magic value, resolve each anon @N REFERENCE to the named symbol the TARGET object references at the SAME instruction offset (relocation site), not the global value-first symbol. Equivalently: checkdiff should treat two SDA21 relocations whose targets hold identical .sdata2 bytes as a match (value-equivalence on the reloc target, not name-equivalence). This is the same class as the known '.sdata2 pool flush sibling blocks checkdiff' note. IMPACT: any byte-perfect function in a TU that has pool-flush-duplicated magic constants will under-report (here 89.87% instead of match=true). Triggered by surrounding unmatched/banked functions (fn_80188738/910/B3C, issue #367) whose codegen forces the pool flush.
resolution_note: Fixed in 16630e57a: value-based name-magic now abstains on duplicate-valued .sdata2 symbols, and checkdiff canonicalizes relocation annotations for 8-byte .sdata2 aliases proven to have identical backing bytes. Verified with pytest tools/melee-agent/tests/test_checkdiff_name_magic.py tools/melee-agent/tests/test_mwcc_debug_o_rewriter.py -q and a fn_80188EE8 JSON smoke showing duplicate magic relocs normalized to __sdata2_value_* tokens (remaining mismatch was unrelated source/codegen divergence in current object).

summary: frame-transform-search needs safe semantic source levers for -8/no-pad frame-size shrink cases
body: Follow-up from issue #366. The safe fix adds delta-aware PAD_STACK actuation and false-ceiling gating, but -8/no-explicit-PAD_STACK cases still need semantic local materialize/dematerialize source levers. An attempted regex dematerializer was rejected in review because it could rewrite lvalues, duplicate side-effectful initializers, and cross control-flow boundaries. Governance: Reusable class: safe semantic local materialize/dematerialize frame-size transforms with AST/source side-effect and control-flow guards Applies to: -8 frame-size divergences with no explicit PAD_STACK to shrink Source-actionable output: compiled+scored source variants that remove or relocate simple local stack homes without changing semantics, with provenance identifying the local and initializer/assignment pattern Stop condition: for an attributed -N frame-size divergence with no explicit PAD_STACK to shrink, frame-transform-search generates at least one safe semantic source candidate or reports no-safe-semantic-lever instead of a ceiling Existing workflow failed: PAD_STACK cannot shrink frames when there is no existing explicit pad, while generic lifetime probes do not change frame size; unsafe regex dematerialization can produce false source-reachable candidates
resolution_note: fixed in c3ea4f764: frame-transform-search now emits guarded frame-local-dematerialize probes for safe one-use scalar/pointer locals, preserves semantic lever status through frame-reservations validation, and reports no-safe-semantic-lever for shrink cases with no safe semantic candidate

summary: debug dump remote fails when Windows checkout is detached before pcdump generation
body: Command: melee-agent debug dump remote src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c Exit code: 70 Observed: remote run_pcdump.ps1 attempted git pull --rebase --autostash in C:\Users\mikes\code\melee, but the checkout is not currently on a branch, so git pull refused to choose a branch and the dump produced 0 bytes. Impact: blocked live diagnose smoke for gm_1832/#367 after local pcdump cache was missing. checkdiff final-asm smokes still verified the #367 classifier for fn_80188738/fn_80188910/fn_80188B3C.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1746b2461: run_pcdump.ps1 detects detached default checkouts and uses fetch/reset to origin/<branch> instead of branchless git pull; remote script deployed and gm_1832.c dump smoke passed.

summary: Stale 'debug verify-with-name-magic' command referenced in docs/memory; name-magic is now auto-applied by checkdiff
body: OBSERVED: 'melee-agent debug verify-with-name-magic -f FN' (referenced in mismatch-db/anon-magic workflow docs and agent memory) is not a valid subcommand. 'melee-agent debug --help' lists: diff-schedule, coalesce-search, select-order-search, dump, inspect, target, suggest, mutate, intervene, permute, util, search. No name-magic verb. EXPECTED: either the command exists, or docs point to the current path. ACTUAL CURRENT BEHAVIOR: name-magic is auto-applied by checkdiff every run (tools/checkdiff.py apply_name_magic_if_available -> mwcc_debug.o_rewriter.apply_name_magic_auto; disable via checkdiff --no-name-magic). Removed in the 2026-05-21 mwcc-debug-cli-reorg. BLOCKED: an if2fc93 teammate looked for the command on a reloc-name residual (un_802FE260/fn_802FDA78); worked around it since checkdiff auto-resolves @N. Low-severity papercut; fix = update mismatch-db/anon-magic doc + note name-magic is automatic.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1746b2461: stale bare verify-with-name-magic references now point to debug util verify-name-magic, with docs/source regression coverage.

summary: mwcc-debug: no source lever for CSE-vs-rematerialize divide divergence (val/100 recompute) in gm_1832 digit twins
body: CLASS: CSE/rematerialization divergence — the target .o recomputes an integer divide-by-constant that our compile common-subexpression-eliminates (CSE). REPRODUCER (3 byte-identical-to-upstream twins in src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c): fn_80188910 94.89%, fn_80188738 93.98%, fn_80188B3C 94.22% All three are the same digit-extraction shape: if ((val / 100) != 0) HSD_JObjReqAnimAll(j0, (f32)(val / 100)); else HSD_JObjReqAnimAll(j0, 10.0f); THE DIVERGENCE (identical in all 3, block 1 only; rest is byte-identical modulo link-resolved .sdata2 names): EXPECTED keeps the mulhw result in a surviving reg (r4) and RECOMPUTES the quotient in the THEN block: +0d0 mulhw r4,r0,r31 +0d4 srawi r0,r4,5 ; +0d8 srwi ; +0dc add. ; +0e0 beq +0e4 srawi r0,r4,5 <-- RECOMPUTE from r4 +0ec srwi r4,r0,31 ; +0f4 add r0,r0,r4 ; +0f8 xoris (int->float) OURS computes the quotient once (mulhw r0; ...; add. r0=val/100) and REUSES r0 directly at the THEN xoris (full CSE) -> 3 fewer instructions. pcdump (build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/gm/gm_1832.txt) confirms it is an IR/global-optimization decision, NOT register coloring: B23: mulhw r60; srawi r64; rlwinm r62; add r35,r64,r62 ; bt eq B27 B24: xoris r65,r35,0x8000 <-- B24 REUSES r35 (the B23 quotient) The optimizer propagated the quotient (r35) across the branch; the target build did not (it shared only the multiply and rematerialized the quotient). CONTRAST: sibling fn_80188D3C (MATCHED 100%) single-computes because its hundreds=val/100 has MULTIPLE uses (hundreds*100 in block 2) so it is kept in a callee-saved reg (r30). The twins use val%100 (not (val/100)*100), so val/100 is a single-use transient — the target rematerializes it; our compile CSEs it. EXISTING WORKFLOW FAILED: - inspect ceiling: "NO FAST TRANSFORM FOUND" (cast audit none; decl-order promote i/val both +0.00%). - suggest inlines --seed-source repeated: only function-extraction candidates, none address in-block CSE. - Source levers tried, all no-op (still 94.89%, same fingerprint): (1) redundant (s32) cast on the value division; (2) fresh local `s32 h = val/100;` inside the THEN block; MWCC CSE is value-based, re-merges every variant. - force-phys / force-iter are register-only and structurally CANNOT add the 3 rematerialization instructions (this is an instruction-COUNT delta, below the allocator). So the standard force-target "is it reachable" probe is N/A. - Source is byte-identical to upstream/master, which is ALSO unmatched at 94.89% — i.e. no known C form reproduces the target's non-CSE codegen. REQUEST: a source-actionable lever (or a definitive "intrinsic, no C lever" verdict) for "target rematerializes a divide/expr that our compile CSEs across an adjacent condition->value use." Ideally: detect the shared-multiply + duplicated-quotient target shape, and either (a) emit a ranked source transform that suppresses the condition<->value quotient CSE, or (b) classify it as an intrinsic value-numbering ceiling so matchers bank it instead of grinding. STOP CONDITION: no candidate source transform raises any of the 3 twins above their baseline (94.89/93.98/94.22) after the search, OR a verdict that the non-CSE shape is unreachable from C for this idiom. Governance: Reusable class: CSE/rematerialization: target recomputes a divide-by-constant our compile CSEs across an adjacent condition->value use Applies to: fn_80188910,fn_80188738,fn_80188B3C Source-actionable output: ranked source transforms that suppress the condition<->value quotient CSE, or an intrinsic-value-numbering-ceiling verdict Stop condition: no candidate raises any twin above baseline (94.89/93.98/94.22), or a verdict that the non-CSE shape is unreachable from C for this idiom Existing workflow failed: inspect ceiling NO FAST TRANSFORM; decl-order/cast/fresh-local/suggest-inlines all no-op; force-* is register-only and cannot add the 3 rematerialization instructions; source is byte-identical to upstream which is also stuck at 94.89%
resolution_note: fixed in 59aba8cc0f: debug inspect diagnose and tools/checkdiff now classify the signed magic divide CSE-vs-rematerialized quotient pattern as an intrinsic value-numbering/backend ceiling; verified on fn_80188738, fn_80188910, and fn_80188B3C checkdiff smokes

summary: frame-transform-search: frame-SIZE operators never generate candidates (even when forced) — only generic lifetime probes fire, so it ceilings 100% of frame-size functions
body: The diagnosis/attribution stack (#358/#359/#360) is excellent and validated: on itSeakneedlethrown it correctly identifies @365 as an address-taken 8-byte float-pair local, cause lifetime-or-ordering-shift, verdict source-reachable-candidate. But the ACTUATION generator behind frame-transform-search (#361) only emits the 2 generic lifetime operators; the 4 frame-size operators are listed in the operator filter but generate nothing, even when explicitly forced. Net effect: 0/5 frame-size functions closed, all ceiling. Note the internal contradiction to fix: attribution says source-reachable while the search says ceiling, purely because no generator exists for the needed transform -- the reachable verdict overpromises vs actuation. Also notable: all 5 observed deltas are exactly +/-8 bytes (a coherent one-8-byte-slot cause class: held-FP-const/double home or one extra/missing aligned slot) -- a tractable first target for the generators. The suggest-frame derive-target/score-source path VALIDATES candidates but does not GENERATE them, so closing still requires hand-written source today. Ask: implement the frame-size source-variant generators and wire them into the --compile-probes search path. Builds on #361 (harness landed); delivers #357's intended outcome. Governance: Reusable class: frame-size source-transform generation (add/remove an 8-byte slot, FP-const home, PAD_STACK) Applies to: the 24 frame-size stack-local-layout rows; reproducers it_3F14_Logic7_PickedUp (too-small +8), itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll (too-large -8, address-taken float pair @365), mpJointListAdd (32 vs 24), ftCo_800AC5A0 (56 vs 48) Source-actionable output: compiled+scored source variants that actually change the reserved frame size by the attributed delta (PAD_STACK add/remove for pure-reservation deltas; FP-const hoist/split for held-FP-const homes; materialize/dematerialize an attributed local), wired into frame-transform-search --compile-probes so a frame-size function can validate-close instead of always ceiling Stop condition: for an attributed +/-N frame-size divergence the search generates and scores at least one frame-size-CHANGING candidate; a ceiling verdict is only emitted after a real frame-size transform was tried and failed Existing workflow failed: frame-transform-search (#361) derivation/attribution/scoring/ceiling logic all work, but across 5 frame-size functions it generated ONLY block-scope and declaration-use-distance (generic lifetime probes that cannot change frame size). The advertised frame-size operators (frame-reservation-pad-stack, frame-split-fp-const-lifetime, frame-direct-literal-at-final-fp-call, frame-magic-scratch-relocation) produced ZERO candidates even when forced via --operator, so every frame-size function verdicts bounded-frame-transform-ceiling without a real frame-size transform ever being attempted.
resolution_note: Fixed safe core in ba473a11dd912e7c61973f6ea81acc6e356e3b19: frame-transform-search derives signed frame deltas, generates delta-aware frame-reservation PAD_STACK probes through --compile-probes/--operator, and only emits frame-transform ceilings after a frame-size-capable probe was actually measured. Semantic -8/no-pad local materialize/dematerialize source levers are tracked separately in follow-up issue #369.

summary: checkdiff: add upfront 'backend-ceiling' classification (byte-identical mnemonics + register-rotation/coalesce) so target-selection deprioritizes unmatchable-from-C residuals
body: Heuristics computable from checkdiff's existing asm diff WITHOUT mwcc-debug at triage: (a) ref_mnemonics==our_mnemonics AND all line diffs are register-token-only swaps => pure coloring rotation (e.g. ftCo_80090594 4-way GPR rotation, HSD_QuatLib_8037EC4C f3/f4<->f5/f6); (b) |ref_mnemonics|-|our_mnemonics|==1 where the extra is a coalescible mr/addi AND the only call-shape delta is self-rel bl-offset shift => coalesce ceiling (fn_80175D34, grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0); (c) cross-ref checkdiff's own REPEAT ledger: 3+ attempts at one fingerprint with byte-identical mnemonics => ceiling. Emit primary='backend-ceiling' (+subclass coloring-rotation|coalesce|li-hoist) with confidence. ROI evidence = THIS batch (5/5 high-% picks were ceilings); also retro-confirmed by inspect-diagnose=NO FAST TRANSFORM on all of them. Pairs with bug #364 (the 1441 red herring is special-case (b)). Governance: not one-off (7+ named fns + documented family), closes a repeated mis-triage workflow failure, output is a source-actionable SELECTION decision (pick structural targets), not force-only. Governance: Reusable class: mwcc-coalesce-coloring-ceiling Applies to: fn_80175D34,HSD_QuatLib_8037EC4C,un_802FF620,ftCo_80090594,grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0,grZakoGenerator_801CAC14,grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 + documented ig_idx family (fn_80248A78,fn_802487A8) Source-actionable output: a cheap upfront primary-class label ('backend-ceiling') that routes target-selection AWAY from high-% register/coalesce ceilings and TOWARD lower-%/bodyless structural fns that yield real matches — a selection decision, not a force knob Stop condition: stop using once checkdiff emits the backend-ceiling label and selection routes around it; stop building if the heuristic can't separate ceiling from source-addressable without invoking mwcc-debug at triage time (too heavy) Existing workflow failed: current 'instruction-sequence'/'signature-type-mismatch' labels do NOT distinguish source-addressable from backend-ceiling; this batch picked 5 fns across 5 TUs purely by checkdiff class and ALL 5 were backend coalesce/coloring ceilings (0 matches, 4 banked walls)
resolution_note: fixed in 6cc9d8e8b: checkdiff now emits backend-ceiling with coloring-rotation/coalesce subclasses for cheap upfront target-selection triage

summary: checkdiff mislabels +/-1-instruction coalesce/scheduler ceilings as signature-type-mismatch (self-relative bl offset red herring)
body: checkdiff.py:1441: 'if ref_calls != our_calls: primary="signature-type-mismatch"'. When our body differs from expected by +/-1 instruction (a surviving/eliminated mr or addi from a coalesce/scheduler ceiling), every self-relative 'bl <fn+0xNN>' operand shifts, so ref_calls != our_calls even when there is NO prototype/return/inline-boundary difference. Observed: fn_80175D34 (gmresult.c, 94.05%) is byte-identical except one redundant prologue 'mr r30,r0' (a coalesce ceiling, regalloc identical, inspect stuck=no issue) yet is labeled signature-type-mismatch. Same false tell recurs across the ig_idx/coalesce-ceiling family (grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0 81.25%, ftCo_80090594 91.83%, un_802FF620 91.38%). Impact: mis-triages register/scheduler ceilings as source-addressable signature bugs, wasting target-selection and agent time. Expected: when ref_mnemonics differ only in COUNT and the call-shape delta is solely offset-labeled self-relative bls (no named-symbol call added/removed), do NOT emit signature-type-mismatch; prefer an instruction-count/scheduling label. Verified against checkdiff.py:1441 this session.
resolution_note: fixed in 6cc9d8e8b: checkdiff now detects single coalescible mr/addi deltas with self-relative bl offset churn as backend-ceiling/coalesce instead of signature-type-mismatch

summary: inspect analyze/simulate 'pass alignment failed' on valid cached pcdump
body: build/mwcc_debug_cache/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.txt (gen 2026-06-01, 13291 lines) contains a complete 'COLORGRAPH DECISIONS' block for HSD_QuatLib_8037EC4C (class=1, n_nodes=4, ig_idx 58..32 descending, physicals r0..r13/r31) and a correct 'AFTER REGISTER COLORING' asm body that byte-matches the live build. But 'debug inspect analyze <dump> -f HSD_QuatLib_8037EC4C' and 'debug inspect simulate ... -f ...' both print 'No virtual registers found (or pass alignment failed).' and exit nonzero. The COLORGRAPH/IG data is clearly present, so the analyze/simulate pass-pair detector (AFTER PEEPHOLE FORWARD -> AFTER REGISTER COLORING) is failing to align on this dump (possibly an older dump schema or the multi-emit duplicate-function blocks at lines 6997/7040/7090/7139/...). Repro: run either command against that dump. Impact: blocks the structured coloring-tiebreak analysis path; had to read COLORGRAPH DECISIONS manually. A clearer 'dump schema vN unsupported / regenerate' message (vs the generic 'no virtuals') would help.
resolution_note: fixed in 97e463424: inspect analyze now recovers FPR virtual mappings from f32+ pcdump registers; inspect simulate reports FPR-only dumps as GPR-simulator unsupported without a wrapped tooling failure

summary: Instrument MWCC stack-frame / local-area allocation pass (the colorgraph analog for the frame)
body: FOUNDATION for the whole frame-tooling stack. Locate the MWCC routine that lays out the local area and assigns stack homes (sizes the frame, places spills, address-taken locals, the callee-save block, alignment), and dump its decisions the way colorgraph dumps register coloring. Current dump local pcdump already exposes symbolic stack homes for some functions (see _pcdump_has_symbolic_stack_homes); realize that into a COMPLETE, ordered frame model. Everything downstream (expected-side reconstruction, frame first-divergence, source attribution, directed transforms, slot-placement order) depends on this visibility existing. Without it every frame tool is black-box r1-offset diffing. Reproducer itSeakneedlethrown: frame 80, saved r27-r31+f31, spills=32. Governance: Reusable class: stack-frame allocation observability Applies to: all stack-local-layout rows; reproducers itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll (frame 80 vs 72) and AXDriver_8038BF6C (192=192 wrong offsets) Source-actionable output: per-function dump of MWCC stack-home assignments: every stack object (saved-register block, spill slot, address-taken local, array/struct home, call-arg temp, alignment pad) with its size, assignment order, final r1 offset, and origin tag; the frame analog of the BEFORE/AFTER REGISTER COLORING dump Stop condition: the dump reproduces the function actual frame size and every r1 offset in the emitted asm; if the pass/symbol cannot be located, report which Existing workflow failed: mwcc-debug instruments register coloring (colorgraph) only; the frame/local-area allocator is a black box. checkdiff and inspect frame-reservations can only diff FINAL r1 offsets and report a byte delta plus unused ranges (e.g. +8B at 0x24-0x34) with no view of which objects occupy the frame or why it is that size.
resolution_note: fixed in 749c8ec50: added frame_allocation_trace with ordered final-layout stack objects, symbolic stack-home orders, validation coverage, allocator-pass not-located limitations, and CLI/JSON exposure

summary: Frame first-divergence with source attribution and a fixable-vs-ceiling verdict
body: Depends on #358 (current frame model) and #359 (expected model). Diff the two object lists to find the first divergence, then add the missing CROSS-PASS IDENTITY: link the front-end ObjObject / IR temp (mwcc-inspect territory: ENodes/ObjObjects) to the backend stack home (#358) so a slot NAMES a source variable. This is the register first_divergence analog for the frame and the piece that makes a frame finding source-actionable. Include the cause taxonomy (missing-inline / type-size / lifetime-across-call / address-taken / spill-from-pressure / alignment) and a ceiling detector for internal-tiebreak cases so agents stop grinding ceilings. Bridges mwcc-inspect and mwcc-debug. Governance: Reusable class: frame-size/local-area first-divergence plus source attribution Applies to: the 24 frame-size stack-local-layout rows; reproducer itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll (8 bytes too large) Source-actionable output: given the current frame model (#358) and reconstructed expected (#359), the FIRST diverging stack object: its kind (extra/missing spill, extra/missing saved reg, differently-sized array/struct, extra call-arg temp, alignment), the source object it traces to via cross-pass ObjObject<->stack-home identity, a CAUSE hypothesis (missing inline adds/removes a temp, wrong type size, lifetime spanning a call forces a home, address-taken, spill from pressure), and a verdict: source-reachable vs compiler-internal tiebreak (ceiling) Stop condition: emits an attributed first-divergence with a cause hypothesis and confidence; flags ceiling when the divergence is an internal ordering/tiebreak with no addressable source object Existing workflow failed: checkdiff reports frame 8 bytes larger plus shifted r1 offsets but cannot say WHICH object the 8 bytes are or WHAT source construct creates it; lifetime-layout lists saved/spill COUNTS but attributes no specific slot to a source variable and diagnoses no cause. Agents grind blind (decl-orders 0/24).
resolution_note: Implemented frame first-divergence source attribution in f49dfa1458ab509cf3b96ffe39f09caf74cf11a8: symbolic stack-home source objects now include per-symbol expected offsets, primary source object metadata, source-aware cause taxonomy, unresolved attribution dependency, and validated source-reachable/ceiling semantics. Verified with 129 affected tests plus frame-reservations CLI smokes.

summary: Directed frame-transform search and validate (the permuter for the frame)
body: This is the missing ENGINE behind #357. Instead of blind probes, take the attributed divergence + cause from #360 and synthesize the SPECIFIC transform class that addresses it, then compile and score against expected frame + checkdiff. Supersedes lifetime-layout probe set; fold in #355 fix. Depends on #360 (which depends on #358, #359). Delivers the outcome described in #357. Governance: Reusable class: frame-directed source-transform search Applies to: the 24 frame-size stack-local-layout rows; reproducer itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll Source-actionable output: ranked, compiled-and-scored source transforms targeted at the attributed divergence from #360: materialize/dematerialize a local (force/prevent a stack home), merge/split temps, extend/contract a lifetime across the divergence call, change a type size, force/prevent address-taking, add/remove a missing inline; each validated against expected frame size and checkdiff match Stop condition: no transform in a bounded, divergence-keyed candidate set reduces the frame-size delta toward 0 or improves checkdiff match (then report ceiling) Existing workflow failed: lifetime-layout --compile-probes generates a couple of BLIND probes (block-scope/early-guard/call-arg-tempization), observed 1 neutral (frame 80->80) and 1 broken (#355); decl-orders cannot change frame size (0/24). Neither is keyed on an attributed cause, so they are random rather than directed.
resolution_note: Implemented directed frame-transform search in 1d07d20bbe642dfc50700ee6b7daf9522f831198: added debug mutate frame-transform-search to derive frame divergence, generate directed source probes, compile/score candidates, retain source-actionable metadata, and report validated/ceiling stop conditions. Verified with focused pytest suite and CLI smokes.

summary: Stack-home assignment-order model plus reorder lever and internal-tiebreak ceiling detector (same-frame-slot-placement)
body: Covers the MAJORITY of the category (39/63) and is mechanistically DISTINCT from frame-size: the frame is the right SIZE but objects land at wrong OFFSETS because MWCC assigns homes in a different ORDER than the target. Needs #358 assignment-order dump + #359 expected-order inference + a reorder lever + ceiling detection. Likely surfaces a hard internal-tiebreak ceiling for many of these (be honest in the verdict) but today we cannot even distinguish fixable from ceiling. Depends on #358. Governance: Reusable class: same-frame stack-slot assignment-order modeling and reorder lever Applies to: the 39 same-frame-stack-slot-placement rows (majority of the category); reproducer AXDriver_8038BF6C (frame 192=192, wrong offsets) Source-actionable output: for correct-size/wrong-offset functions: MWCC stack-home ASSIGNMENT ORDER (from #358), the order implied by the target asm offsets (from #359), the permutation between them, the source lever that perturbs assignment order (first-use order / lifetime / decl order as proxies), and a verdict: source-reachable reorder vs compiler-internal tiebreak ceiling (the frame analog of the ig_idx-descending register ceiling) Stop condition: either a source reorder that aligns assignment order to expected (validated by checkdiff), or a ceiling verdict when the order is fixed by an internal tiebreak below C control Existing workflow failed: decl-orders (the current proxy) was 0/12 on this subcategory because stack-home placement follows lifetime/assignment-order, not declaration-statement order; there is no view of the actual assignment order to target.
resolution_note: fixed in f4a3eb5c7 and prior stack-home commits: frame-reservations now reports stack-home assignment order, expected target offset/order, permutation/cycles, source reorder probe levers/commands, and attaches validated source-reachable/partial/internal-tiebreak-ceiling verdicts from --probe-results-json

summary: build source-transform corpus plus active experiment engine
body: Follow-up to #348. The coverage matrix tells us which source regions/holders/transform families are relevant, but we also need a reusable corpus of semantics-preserving C transform families and a dynamic engine that instantiates them against a specific MWCC diagnostic. This avoids the chicken-and-egg problem: the tool does not need to know the winning edit in advance; it needs to map a proof vector to source roles, select bounded transform families, generate probes, real-tree validate, and record positive/negative evidence. Corpus sources should include retained historical matches, near-miss/generated candidates that moved target assignments, and failed campaigns like E7B4. Transform metadata should include preconditions, semantic-risk class, source-region selector, expected compiler effect, generated probe form, and observed outcomes. Example families: temp sink/hoist, condition split/merge, scoped alias, declaration/use boundary movement, loop index vs pointer-walk role split, helper inline/extract, helper argument shape, int vs s32 loop counter, reload branch scope, and lifetime preserve/shorten around calls/loops. Governance: Reusable class: Forced-state and coupled-allocator cases where diagnostics identify target roles but the stock-C source lever is unknown Applies to: ftCo_8009E7B4 Source-actionable output: A bounded set of generated source probes with source-region labels, transform-family labels, semantic-risk classification, per-target assignment movement, real-tree match result, and retained/negative-evidence ledger entries Stop condition: For a target diagnostic, the engine either finds a retained source improvement, exhausts applicable transform families with recorded evidence, or recommends a larger refactor with the exact source regions and uncovered transform classes Existing workflow failed: #247 added forced-state source search and #317/#348 diagnose/categorize coupled proof vectors, but E7B4 still requires manual invention of C levers; current output repeats generic advice such as interference insertion or simplify-order shift without a reusable transform corpus or dynamic instantiation/learning loop
resolution_note: fixed through transform-corpus plan-transforms engine, completed with 32fea6175: generated source-transform probes now validate into normalized evidence records with family/risk/source-region/target-assignment metadata plus retained/negative evidence counts for ledger consumption

summary: No source-actionable tool closes a frame-size delta (frame-too-large/small): diagnosis exists, transform does not
body: The diagnostic layer for frame-size residuals is strong: frame-reservations reports current vs expected frame, byte delta, and the differing unused ranges (itSeakneedlethrown: current unused 0x24-0x34=16B vs expected 0x24-0x2c=8B, so 8 extra reserved bytes). But NO tool turns that diagnosis into a source change. A frame-actionable tool would map the expected-vs-current unused-range delta to candidate source shapes (a missing materialized local/array, an address-taken temp the original needed for a stack slot, a lifetime that must span a call to claim a slot, or a spill-count difference) and rank/validate them against the expected frame + checkdiff match -- analogous to how force-phys/force-coalesce attack register coloring, but for the frame/local area. Until this exists, frame-too-large/small rows should be marked diagnostic-only/manual (see routing issue), not quick-wins. Related: #355 (one lifetime-layout probe class is broken), #354. Governance: Reusable class: frame-reservation size delta (frame-too-large/frame-too-small) source transform Applies to: 24 frame-size stack-local-layout rows; reproducer itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll (current frame=80, expected=72, +8B unused at 0x24-0x34, spills=32) Source-actionable output: ranked, validated source transforms (add/remove a real local array/struct or address-taken/volatile temp; extend or contract a local lifetime across a call; change spill count) that move MWCC's reserved frame toward the expected size and improve checkdiff match Stop condition: no transform in a bounded candidate set reduces the frame-size delta toward 0 or improves checkdiff match Existing workflow failed: inspect frame-reservations is read-only (pinpoints +8B unused at 0x24-0x34 vs expected 0x24-0x2c but gives no source lever); lifetime-layout --compile-probes produced 1 neutral probe (frame 80->80, no match) plus 1 broken probe (#355); decl-orders cannot change frame size (0/24 sweep); there is no --force-frame and the target schema has no frame field
resolution_note: fixed in a866bda84: frame-reservations now emits directed frame probe plans and --probe-results-json ranks/validates frame-transform variants against expected frame size, including validated, partial, and bounded-ceiling stop conditions

summary: Reconstruct the EXPECTED frame layout from target ASM (expected-side frame solver)
body: SHARED DEPENDENCY for any current-vs-expected frame comparison. Parse the target function prologue (stwu r1 frame size, callee-save stores) and every r1-relative access (offset, width from lwz/lhz/lbz/lfs/stfd/etc., stride in loops) to infer slot boundaries, the saved-register block, and occupied vs unused ranges. frame-reservations already computes unused ranges; formalize that into a full expected object map so first-divergence (next issue) can diff expected vs current object lists. Pairs with the frame-instrumentation issue (current side). Governance: Reusable class: expected-side frame reconstruction from target asm Applies to: all stack-local-layout rows; reproducer itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Coll (expected frame 72, expected unused 0x24-0x2c) Source-actionable output: a reconstructed expected stack-object/slot map (frame size, callee-save block, occupied vs unused ranges, inferred object boundaries from r1 access widths/strides) derived purely from the target function prologue plus all r1-relative accesses; the expected-side counterpart to the current-side model from the frame-instrumentation issue Stop condition: reconstructed expected frame size and occupied/unused ranges match what inspect frame-reservations already derives; object-boundary inference is best-effort and flagged where ambiguous Existing workflow failed: we have the target ASM but never the original source, so an expected pcdump can never be produced by recompilation. inspect frame-reservations already infers expected unused ranges from the asm (proving the data exists), but there is no structured expected object/slot model to diff a current-side model against.
resolution_note: fixed in a8ffa1b25: frame-reservations now exposes best-effort stack_objects and stack_object_map_status for expected and current frame models, reconstructing frame size, r1-access objects, ABI header, and unused gaps from target ASM; object-boundary confidence is explicit; verified with frame-reservation tests and CLI JSON smoke

summary: lifetime-layout: call-argument-tempization probe crashes pcdump validation (function not emitted, exit 3)
body: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f AXDriver_8038BF6C --compile-probes: the 'call-arg-tempization-0' probe applies a source transform whose recompiled pcdump omits the target function ('function AXDriver_8038BF6C not found in pcdump', exit 3), so the probe fails. The harness recovers (restores source, continues) but the probe class appears systematically broken for functions where tempizing a call arg changes emission. Either the transform is malformed or the pcdump lookup needs to tolerate the renamed/inlined emission. Observed alongside a neutral 'early-guard-return-0' probe (frame 192->192, match 99.57). Net: lifetime-layout produced no source-actionable win for this same-frame-stack-slot-placement function.
resolution_note: fixed in 4ac02b91c: call-argument-tempization now skips unbalanced nested-call matches such as AXDriver_8038BF6C's powf(...) inside HSD_SynthSFXSetPitchRatio(...), avoiding malformed source probes whose pcdump omits the target function; verified with failing regression, pressure explorer test slice, real AXDriver source probe generation, and CLI smoke

summary: stuck-static-focus next_steps ignores subcategory: headlines decl-orders for frame-size rows where it is categorically incapable
body: ROOT CAUSE: the stuck-static-focus digest routes next_steps off the pcdump-gated frame_residual_hint, not off the pcdump-FREE subcategory that the classifier already computes from checkdiff reasons (function_taxonomy_inventory.py:192-201 classify_bucket: stack-slot-layout->same-frame-stack-slot-placement; stack-layout+'frame reservation gap'+'too large/small'->frame-too-large/small). Because the taxonomy is generated --no-pcdump, frame_residual is null everywhere and the digest falls through to decl-orders for everything. decl-orders cannot change frame SIZE (verified: itSeakneedlethrown frame=80 vs expected 72, decl reorder improved regs to 99.93 but frame stayed 80) and is too coarse for slot-placement (0/12 in sweep). FIX (self-contained in debug.py digest): (1) derive a frame-size signal pcdump-free from checkdiff's reasons/frame-probe (same data classify_bucket uses) and populate digest['frame_residual'] from it; (2) when the residual is frame-size, headline 'melee-agent debug inspect frame-reservations -f FN' + suggest-frame and DO NOT headline decl-orders; (3) when same-frame-stack-slot-placement, headline 'lifetime-layout --compile-probes'; (4) demote decl-orders to a single '[~70s] cheap probe (often neutral on this class)'; (5) stop labeling frame-size rows as quick-wins in the dashboard/queue. Related: #354 (record best_decl_delta), #353 (decl-orders combined-declarator), #355 (lifetime-layout probe bug). Deeper tooling gap tracked separately. Governance: Reusable class: stack-local-layout next_steps routing by residual subcategory Applies to: all 62 stuck-static-focus stack-local-layout rows; 24 frame-size (frame-too-large/small/delta), 38 same-frame-stack-slot-placement Source-actionable output: next_steps that headline frame tools (inspect frame-reservations + suggest-frame) for frame-size rows and lifetime-layout --compile-probes for slot-placement rows; decl-orders demoted to a cheap optional probe; frame_residual populated pcdump-free Stop condition: no frame-size row headlines decl-orders; every stack-local-layout row's headline next_step matches its subcategory; frame_residual is non-null wherever checkdiff reports a frame-reservation gap Existing workflow failed: _detect_frame_residual_hint (debug.py:3479) is pcdump-gated; the taxonomy ran --no-pcdump so frame_residual is null for all 62 rows -> the frame branch (debug.py:8153) never fires -> the decl-orders step (gated only on candidate_count>0, debug.py:8163-8172) becomes the headline next_step for ALL rows including the 24 frame-size ones; a full 24-fn decl-orders sweep matched 0
resolution_note: fixed in 4f3b2f260: inspect stuck now reads checkdiff classification without pcdump, populates frame_residual for frame-size and same-frame stack-slot rows, headlines frame-reservation or lifetime-layout tools by subcategory, and demotes decl-orders to an optional cheap probe; verified with targeted CLI tests and real lb_80014638 stuck smoke

summary: function-taxonomy: decl_order_summary should record best-achievable match delta, not just candidate_count
body: Today the dashboard's stack-local-layout queue is selected/ranked partly on decl_order_summary.candidate_count, which only counts how many local-decl reorderings EXIST (search-space size), not whether any improves match. Empirically (2026-06-03) a sweep of all 24 rows with candidate_count in 1..4 found ZERO orderings reaching 100%. Proposal: when building taxonomy.records, actually run (or cache) the bounded decl-orders 'all' enumeration per candidate function and store best_decl_delta (pp), best_ordering (string), and evaluated_status (evaluated | build-failed-combined-declarator(#353) | no-freedom-init-dependency). Then the queue can rank by best_decl_delta and drop the dead rows. This turns a 24-function brute-force sweep into a precomputed, ranked, actionable list. Related: #353 (combined-declarator BUILD FAILED blocks evaluation for some rows). Governance: Reusable class: decl-order quick-win triage for stack-local-layout queue Applies to: stack-local-layout queue rows (primary=stack-slot-layout/stack-layout, match>=99) Source-actionable output: per-function best_decl_delta + winning ordering string (or 'unevaluated: <reason>'), so the queue ranks real decl-order wins Stop condition: every stack-local-layout queue row carries a computed best_decl_delta or an explicit unevaluated reason Existing workflow failed: candidate_count ranks by reorderable-decl count; a full 24-fn decl-orders sweep produced 0 wins -> count is not predictive of a win, so agents must brute-run decl-orders per function only to discover the queue is empty
resolution_note: fixed in 3b04d4289: taxonomy inventory now runs bounded decl-orders all evaluation for >=99 stack-local-layout records, stores decl_order_summary plus best delta/order/status fields in records and queue TSVs, and records no-freedom-init-dependency stop conditions; verified with unit tests, CLI smoke, and real lb_80014638 decl-orders evaluator smoke

summary: decl-orders: combined-declarator locals (int i,j,min; / Vec3 a,b,c;) make every reorder BUILD FAILED
body: melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders cannot evaluate any ordering for functions whose function-top locals share a comma-separated declaration. Repro: mnCount_8025092C has 'CountEntry entries[..]; CountEntry tmp; int i, j, min;' -> the tool lists i,j,min as separate locals and every promote/demote/swap reports BUILD FAILED (12/12). lb_80014638 has 'Vec3 sp30, sp24, sp18;' -> 6/6 BUILD FAILED. Root cause appears to be naive line/declarator surgery that splits a combined 'int i, j, min;' into invalid C. Fix direction: when moving a variable that is part of a comma-separated declarator list, rewrite it as its own correctly-typed declaration (preserving struct/array type) rather than editing the shared line. Separately, it_3F14_Logic7_DmgReceived BUILD FAILEDs for a DIFFERENT, legitimate reason: 'itDoseiAttributes* attr = ip->xC4_article_data->...;' depends on the prior 'Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);' -- the tool should detect init-dependencies and report 'skipped: attr depends on ip' instead of an opaque BUILD FAILED.
