Impact: blocks decl-order evaluation for a whole class of stack/local-layout functions in the >=99% quick-win queue (this batch). Suggest also surfacing the captured compiler error under BUILD FAILED for diagnosability.
resolution_note: fixed in 167a35893: decl-orders now splits comma-separated declaration lines into independent declarations before reordering, skips initializer dependency inversions with reasons, and real lb_80014638 swap smoke compiles/scores candidates instead of BUILD FAILED

summary: Force-phys register search is shallow + has no permuter scorer for phys-iter (Case-B dispense) targets
body: Follow-up to #343 (force-phys static scoring shipped, GOOD). On ft_800852B0 the search now scores correctly ('Target force-phys: 53:r4') but the candidate generation + depth are insufficient for a Case-B colorgraph-DISPENSE compiler-temp target. (1) SHALLOW STATIC: 13 candidates, insert-alias only at @0 (not 'immediately before the temp def' / lifetime-extension as the suggester describes); 0 flip ig53. (2) NO FORCE-PHYS PERMUTER: 'with-permuter'/setup-simplify-order-scorer use the SIMPLIFY-ORDER scorer, which aborts on phys-iter (-1 baseline); no colorgraph-dispense permuter scorer exists, so no broad permuter run can be scored by 'does ig53 reach r4'. NEEDED: position-aware + lifetime-extension interference candidates, AND a force-phys/dispense PERMUTER scorer (analog of the simplify-order scorer but for COLORGRAPH DECISIONS positions). ft_800852B0 may be a genuine ig-order ceiling, but without these we can't PROVE it the way the frame layer can. Governance: Reusable class: register Case-B/C colorgraph-dispense tiebreak on a compiler-temp (no source var bound) Applies to: ft_800852B0 ig53; generalizes to un_803147C4 (tylist swap), fn_802FA6C4 (ifstock 3-cycle), fn_80175A94 reg residual Source-actionable output: ranked, checkdiff-verified C-source edits (position-aware interference-insertion at the temp's def site; holder lifetime-extension) that flip the compiler-temp to the force-phys target register Stop condition: a candidate reaches force-phys target (ig53->r4) AND checkdiff match=true; OR a bounded force-phys-scored permuter run exhausts with zero candidates moving the dispense assignment -> rigorously-proven ig-order ceiling Existing workflow failed: mutate simplify-order --force-phys 53:4 = 0/13 shallow static candidates (insert-alias@0, decl-orders, type-change); simplify-order permuter scorer aborts on phys-iter target (baseline order all -1, 'Layer A cannot help'); byte-match permuter (prior agent, 1000+ local/1400 remote) found nothing but UB
resolution_note: Fixed in 12dae8fc071fd2c216a3df3190927b7b02e01946: simplify-order force-phys searches now include bounded holder-lifetime variants after later read sites, so ft_800852B0 no longer stops at the old 13 shallow static candidates. The force-phys permuter scorer path is present via setup-simplify-order-scorer --scorer-mode force-phys and debug target score-force-phys; smoke/tested with 18 ft_800852B0 candidates including holder-lifetime variants and scorer setup tests.

summary: force-phys-from-diff emits contradictory phys targets when expected/current have a frame-size delta (no anchor alignment)
body: COMMAND: debug target force-phys-from-diff on ifMagnify_802FC3C0 (frame 0x38 vs expected 0x30, delta=-8; 'debug suggest frame' confirms delta=-8, 'no unused-home signature'). OBSERVED: target derivation emits CONTRADICTORY phys assignments for the same virtual (ig35 wanted BOTH r31 and r30) because expected vs current asm are aligned on RAW stack offsets, which are shifted by the 8-byte frame phantom-gap. The whole register map is a consistent permutation (slot->r28 vs expected r27, base->r27 vs r31) driven by COLORGRAPH ig_idx-descending order, but the frame shift desyncs the diff alignment so force-phys-from-diff produces conflicting/unusable targets. EXPECTED: frame-aware alignment -- align expected vs current on call/reloc anchors (or normalize by the detected frame-size delta) before deriving per-virtual phys targets, so a frame-shifted-but-otherwise-permuted function yields a clean target set. BLOCKED: cannot get a usable force-phys target on the very common 'frame-shift + register-cascade' wall class. SECOND INDEPENDENT HIT this batch: tm-mplib saw the same family on mpLib_800534FC_Floor/mpJointUnhide/mpLib_80057BC0 (register-coloring cascades, 'NO FAST TRANSFORM'); memory records the same on the gr/ stage cluster and gm_801A9DD0/mnGallery frame walls. NAMED CLASS, 3+ fns. SOURCE-ACTIONABLE VALUE: a correct frame-aware target lets us decide reachable->permuter-candidate vs genuinely frame-locked->bank, instead of grinding blind. SIBLING: relates to #344 (force-phys search depth + missing phys-iter permuter scorer) -- same tool, adjacent gap; consider resolving together. Also adjacent to frame-layer gap (mwcc-debug has no frame model).
resolution_note: Fixed in 840cd3815b62d04ca549460bf1fa220a76df9baf. force-phys-from-diff now detects target/current frame-size deltas, aligns current stack-slot operands by the delta before pairing register-diff instructions, exposes frame_alignment metadata, and removes any IG with conflicting target phys assignments from the runnable force_phys/force_vector outputs. Verified on ifMagnify_802FC3C0: frame_delta=-8 applied and conflicted IGs are diagnostic-only, not exported.

summary: checkdiff name-magic does not resolve anonymous .data/.sdata string symbols (only .sdata2)
body: In mplib.c, checkdiff reports R_PPC_ADDR16_HA/LO reloc diffs '@411 vs lbl_803BF4B0' (.data, __FILE__ string) and R_PPC_EMB_SDA21 '@412 vs mpLib_804D3960' (.sdata, assert #cond "0"). Both anonymous locals resolve to the SAME link address as the named globals (verified: powerpc-eabi-objcopy --redefine-sym @411=lbl_803BF4B0 --redefine-sym @412=mpLib_804D3960 + --globalize, then checkdiff --no-build removes the diff, leaving only the real register diff). checkdiff's apply_name_magic_if_available only handles .sdata2 magic constants; collect_section_anchor_aliases only handles zero-size section anchors. Neither covers non-zero-size anonymous .data/.sdata STRING literals (LINEID_CHECK/HSD_ASSERTREPORT __FILE__ and #cond). Result: false-positive reloc-name diffs on every LINEID_CHECK function in mplib.c, obscuring the real residual. Expected: value/link-address-based resolution for .data/.sdata anonymous string objects co-located (by link address) with named symbols from symbols.txt, like the existing .sdata2 path. Blocks clean residual triage on mpLib_800534FC_Floor, mpLib_800536CC_Floor, mpLib_DrawSnapping, etc.
resolution_note: Fixed in 77b5cb8a9f94e8e5ca27e814b17962abe34ca2f9. checkdiff relocation normalization now maps local anonymous .data/.sdata @N string symbols to named peer-object globals, including the mpLib_800534FC_Floor @411/@412 case, so false reloc-name diffs are removed while real code diffs remain.

summary: coupled force-phys diagnosis needs concrete source-lever coverage matrix
body: E7B4 now has the right high-level diagnosis but still not the closer. Current diagnose --force-phys 0:58:4,0:34:30,0:56:29 identifies coupled volatile/reload and callee-save/lifetime clusters, then emits generic levers: interference insertion, simplify-order shift, targeted alias. Prior probes have already exhausted many versions of those families without source movement. Needed next layer: map each proof-vector virtual to concrete pcdump/source regions, candidate holder values, live-range edges, and transform-family coverage; then report which source-lever families were tried, which changed each target assignment, and which untried source regions remain. This should make the output source-actionable or explicitly prove transform-space exhaustion instead of repeating generic allocator advice. Governance: Reusable class: Coupled allocator proof vectors where force-phys byte-matches but stock C source levers are unknown Applies to: ftCo_8009E7B4 Source-actionable output: A source-region/holder/transform coverage matrix with concrete file spans, candidate edits or generated probes, per-target assignment effects, and remaining untried source-shape hypotheses Stop condition: For a target vector, every mapped cluster has either a retained source improvement, an exhausted transform family with recorded negative evidence, or a concrete larger-refactor recommendation with source regions Existing workflow failed: Resolved #317 diagnose recognizes E7B4 clusters but still emits generic levers; directed searches, b4 tree probes, flag probes, coalesce/select/lifetime probes, and remote permuters did not convert the proof vector into stock-C source
resolution_note: Added coupled force-phys source-lever coverage matrix in c1bd790de; diagnose --force-phys now maps proof clusters to source regions, target holders, transform-family status/evidence from attempts, expected assignment effects, and next probes in text and JSON. impact=diagnostic-only

summary: deep tooling campaigns need ROI and generality tracking
body: Independent audit feedback highlights that E7B4 has generated dozens of tooling issues and risks overfitting the hardest allocator cases. The attempts ledger records local progress, but there is no first-class ROI/generalization view per tooling feature: which later functions used it, whether it produced retained source changes, whether it only produced negative evidence, and whether it should be considered mature/closed. This is needed to decide whether an E7B4 blocker is worth a new general tool or should be treated as a one-function overfit and deferred. Governance: Reusable class: Portfolio discipline for deep mwcc-debug/decomp tooling campaigns Applies to: ftCo_8009E7B4 Source-actionable output: For each tooling issue/feature, a report linking downstream function uses, retained source wins, negative-evidence attempts, open follow-up coverage gaps, and a recommendation: keep investing, mature, or stop for this function Stop condition: A campaign can answer whether new tooling applies to 3+ functions or closes a repeated workflow failure, and can show retained-source ROI or a documented abandon/defer recommendation Existing workflow failed: Issue and attempt records exist separately, but E7B4 decisions still require manual reconstruction of 50+ issue outcomes and many neutral attempts; this makes overfit vs reusable-tool judgments ad hoc
resolution_note: Added issue campaign-report ROI/generalization reporting in 6279761f6; combines issue impact tags, governance applies-to functions, open gaps, and attempt-ledger retained/negative evidence into JSON/table recommendations. impact=diagnostic-only

summary: orphaned local wibo compile can remain unkillable in UE state
body: Found local orphan PID 24276 while checking stale ftCo_8009E7B4 permuter activity. Command was build/tools/wibo ... mwcceppc.exe -c src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c -o build/GALE01/src/melee/ft. It had PPID 1, STAT UE, start Mon Jun 1 21:49:51 2026, elapsed over 40h, and ~0 CPU. Both kill and kill -9 left it present. This can block local build resources and needs a detector/cleanup warning or wrapper timeout that prevents orphaned uninterruptible compiler processes.
resolution_note: Fixed in 4d9449905: added debug permute local-orphans to detect orphaned local wibo/MWCC compile processes, flag uninterruptible UE state, and print operator guidance when kill cannot clean them up.

summary: remote permuter tail -n dumps huge carriage-return progress stream instead of bounded lines
body: Command: melee-agent debug permute remote tail ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-143543 -n 80, also 184447 and 220047. Expected roughly 80 useful log lines. Actual output was a very large progress stream because decomp-permuter progress uses carriage returns/backspaces, so -n did not bound logical output. This makes checking stale remote jobs noisy and token-expensive. Please sanitize CR progress output or cap by bytes/time as well as newline count.
resolution_note: Fixed in 4d9449905: remote tail snapshots now fetch a bounded byte window and sanitize carriage-return progress streams into logical tail lines while preserving --follow streaming.

summary: remote permuter needs stale-job detection and cleanup affordance
body: While checking coder3, three ftCo_8009E7B4 jobs were still active after more than 24 hours: ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-143543, ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-184447, and ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-220047. Fetch/triage showed no candidate beat the retained 99.71% source. Governance: Reusable class: Remote permuter stale job and resource management for long-running function searches Applies to: ftCo_8009E7B4 Source-actionable output: Status reports wall time, latest output age, best/current score, owner/source, and a concrete stop/keep recommendation or TTL metadata Stop condition: Jobs older than a configured TTL or with no improving outputs for a configured duration are identified with one-command cleanup guidance Existing workflow failed: remote list/status/tail showed active jobs but did not expose age, productivity, ownership, or stale cleanup guidance; tail -n produced huge CR progress dumps
resolution_note: Fixed in 4d9449905: remote status now reports job wall age, remote log idle age, best-score hint, source metadata, and stop/cleanup guidance with configurable stale/idle thresholds.

summary: register-tiebreak directed search scores Case-B DISPENSE tiebreaks by simplify-order (inapplicable, baseline -1) instead of force-phys
body: Follow-up to #341 (register-tiebreak finder shipped + suggests levers + a directed search). On ft_800852B0 the finder works but the directed search it recommends can't make progress because it uses the WRONG objective: - first-divergence: ig53 Case B (colorgraph DISPENSE, iter 45), force-phys 53:4 PROVEN reachable. - 'suggest register-tiebreak' recommends 'mutate simplify-order --fn ft_800852B0 --want-late 53'. - Running it: 'Baseline order: -1,-1,-1...' (ig53 is NOT simplify-order-addressable — it's a phys-iter/COLORGRAPH-DECISIONS position, exactly the skill's 'Observed prefix all -1 => phys-iter, Layer A cannot help' case). It generated the RIGHT lever candidates (decl-orders, insert-alias unk0/pairs/i/new_var@0 = the interference-insertion lever, type-change) and compiled 13, but scored them by SIMPLIFY-ORDER distance -> 'No variants made progress beyond baseline'. It never tested whether any candidate makes ig53 actually reach r4. GAP: for a Case-B/C COLORGRAPH-DISPENSE divergence (simplify-order baseline all -1), the directed search must score candidates by the FORCE-PHYS objective (does ig53 reach r_target? verifiable via a forced-target reachability check, or just real-tree checkdiff match%), NOT simplify-order distance. And 'suggest register-tiebreak' should NOT route a Case-B dispense divergence to 'mutate simplify-order --want-late' (Layer A) — it should route to a force-phys/checkdiff-scored interference-insertion search. The interference-insertion lever (its own #1 suggestion) is the right one but is currently generated-then-mis-scored. Reproducer: ft_800852B0, ig53 Case B, force-phys 53:4 reachable; the search ('mutate simplify-order --want-late 53 --no-preserve-precolor') compiles 13 variants, 0 progress, because the metric is wrong for this divergence class.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0aa975a83be4fc30753e37e93210db333905b40a: register-tiebreak guidance now routes Case-B/C searches to --force-phys, and mutate simplify-order can score variants by force-phys assignment hits when simplify-order positions are unusable.

summary: mutate decl-orders / inspect diagnose: 'Could not find decl block' on functions with interspersed declarations
body: On ft_800852B0 (ft/ft_0852.c), 'inspect diagnose' step 2 and 'mutate decl-orders' both report 'Could not find decl block — skipping', so the decl-order lever is unavailable. Cause: the function's locals are INTERSPERSED, not a single top block — unk0/pairs declared+initialized at the top (with initializers referencing CostumeListsForeachCharacter), then 'int i; int new_var=0;', then 'int costume_idx' declared INSIDE the loop. The decl-block parser apparently looks for one contiguous uninitialized-decl block. FIX: handle interspersed/initialized decls + loop-scope decls (enumerate orderings of the function-scope decls that CAN legally move), or at least fall back gracefully + say which decls it could/couldn't reorder. This blocks a documented Tier-7b lever on exactly the register-tiebreak functions where it's most wanted.
resolution_note: Fixed in 9f5535d3c: diagnose/mutate decl-orders now use AST-backed scope maps and scope-aware reordering for struct-pointer initializers and loop-local decl visibility; covered by regression tests.

summary: Need a register-tiebreak SOURCE-LEVER finder for compiler-temp Case-B/C divergences (suggest + directed search)
body: Register analog of the frame source-lever gap (#334/#338/#339, which were built and cracked gm_801A9DD0). On ft_800852B0 (97.36%, body byte-identical) the register tooling DIAGNOSES + proves REACHABILITY but has no path to a source fix: - inspect analyze + first-divergence --force-phys 53:4: First divergence class 0 iter 45 ig_idx 53, baseline ig53->r3, target ig53->r4, cause=Case B. Lever (advisory): 'introduce interference with the holders of registers below r_target (r3), or move ig53 later in simplify order'. - force-phys 53:4 -> '--- MATCH ---' (reachable). - BUT 'ig 53 -> no source variable bound — likely a compiler temp' (it's the gFtDataList @ha load: 'B1: lis r53,HA(gFtDataList)'). So there's no source var to manipulate, and the generic lever doesn't translate to a concrete edit. - Existing source suggesters DON'T cover it: 'suggest coalesce --discover' = empty (not a coalesce); 'suggest inlines' = irrelevant helper-extraction; 'suggest casts' = none. 'inspect diagnose'/'mutate decl-orders' = 'Could not find decl block — skipping'. The obvious manual levers (move-gFtDataList-access, explicit-pointer-locals, decl-reorder) are exhausted (prior agent + permuter thousands of iters). NEEDED (mirror the frame layer): (a) 'suggest register-tiebreak -f FN --force-phys <ig:phys>' that, for a Case-B/C compiler-temp divergence, emits CONCRETE indirect source levers — interference insertion (extend a named holder's lifetime to occupy r_below at the temp's def), simplify-order shifts (relocate the defining expression of the temp's source symbol), targeted alias insertions — ranked; (b) a directed search (like 'mutate search' for frames) that materializes those as seeds and scores them against the force-phys target. The force-phys target is the objective; gFtDataList @ha ig53->r4 is the goal. Reproducer: ft_800852B0 (ft/ft_0852.c), force-phys 53:4 proves reachable.
resolution_note: Fixed first-pass feature in 9f5535d3c: added debug suggest register-tiebreak plus diagnose --force-phys guidance for compiler-temp Case-B/C targets, with concrete source levers and verification commands; covered by CLI regressions.

summary: suggest inlines accepts impossible helper slices containing return before live call
body: Command: melee-agent debug suggest inlines -f ftCo_8009E7B4. After master 109e8f4ee, the output marked void-helper candidates as accepted even though several source snippets contain 'return;' followed by 'ftCo_8009CB40(...)' or duplicate return/call slices from lines 817-825. This is misleading during E7B4 last-mile work because these are not natural extractable inline/helper bodies and can send agents toward unsafe probes. Please filter helper suggestions to contiguous executable regions that are control-flow valid, or downgrade them with an explicit invalid/control-flow-crossing reason.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2512d799eab6c8d3276506d64554535e055a4146: repeated void-helper span groups now reject return/break/continue control flow, preventing impossible helper slices such as return plus later ftCo_8009CB40 calls from being accepted.

summary: Frame search isn't frame-DIRECTED: mutate search uses generic seeds + byte-match permuter objective, ignores suggest-frame levers + the frame scorer
body: Follow-up to #338 — the expected-144 frame target now derives ('target derive --frame-from-checkdiff') and 'score-source' gives a real baseline frame-distance (34). But 'mutate search -f gm_801A9DD0 --target <144-target>' STILL can't move it, for two concrete reasons visible in the run: 1. SEEDS ARE GENERIC, not frame-derived: log says 'no symbol-bridge seed plans; using source-shape probe fallback' and the 7 seeds are adjacent-decl-swap / temp-introduction / type-width / declaration-use-distance / early-guard-return / block-scope / call-arg-tempization. NONE come from 'suggest frame' (which recommended suppress-unused-local-home = split-FP-const-lifetime / direct-literal-at-final-FP-call, and reduce-alignment-growth = move int->float magic-scratch lifetime). The suggester's levers are never turned into seeds. 2. PERMUTER OBJECTIVE ISN'T FRAME-AWARE: each seed 'permuting for 70s' returned 'no improvement after 0.1-0.3s' — it scores byte-match only, so a mutation that REDUCES frame-distance toward 144 (the 34->0 gradient the scorer now computes) but doesn't yet flip bytes looks like an instant dead-end and is abandoned. Net: 'No seed produced a permuter improvement.' NEEDED to close the loop: (a) 'mutate search' should generate FRAME-SPECIFIC seeds from 'suggest frame' (materialize split-FP-const-lifetime / direct-literal-at-call / magic-scratch-relocation as seed source variants); (b) blend the frame-distance score (the 34-baseline scorer) into the permuter's per-seed improvement objective so frame-reducing mutations are kept + explored, not abandoned. The scorer EXISTS and is aimed at 144 — it just isn't driving the seed generation or the search objective. Reproducer harness unchanged: gm_801A9DD0 (frame 152, score 34) -> expected 144 (mnGallery_80258A08 proves C-reachable, force-frame proves byte-reachable).
resolution_note: Fixed in 4887b6f3990b94411ba740062017d4def8e94d00: Tier 3 mutate search now prioritizes frame-directed seeds from frame targets and scores/retains seed-base frame improvements.

summary: mwcc-debug has NO stack-frame/local-area model — frame-only walls are invisible+unaddressable (Tier 8 needed)
body: EXPOSED via a single-function deep-dive on gm_801A9DD0 (gmopening, 99.60%, instruction-sequence byte-identical to target; ONLY diff is +8 stack frame: stwu -152/0x98 vs target 0x90). Clean reproducer: sibling mnGallery_80258A08 uses the IDENTICAL held-FP-const idiom and compiles to stwu -144/0x90 (matched). So the +8 is a real, isolated frame-allocation divergence (MWCC reserves an unused 4-byte stack home for the held-2.0f far_val local, never stored, padded to 8). GAP: the entire mwcc-debug tier stack models the REGISTER ALLOCATOR + SCHEDULER and is BLIND to the GENERATING-EPILOGUE/PROLOGUE (stack-frame/local-area) pass. Evidence, tool by tool: - inspect stuck -> 'Coloring summary ... Guidance issues: (none — no flagged issues)'. Frame not mentioned. - inspect diagnose -> runs cast-audit + 11 decl-order candidates (all register/source-shape), verdict 'NO FAST TRANSFORM FOUND', offers only force-phys/permuter. far_val/near_val promotion HURTS (-0.28%). - target derive -> emits 'virtuals: {32:30, 35:31, ...}' — a pure virtual->physical REGISTER map. The target schema has NO frame field, so a frame goal (0x90 / no unused home) is INEXPRESSIBLE -> can't be scored or permuter-optimized toward. - force surface (dump local) -> --force-phys (T5), --force-iter-first/--force-select-order/--force-coalesce (T6), --force-schedule (T7). NO --force-frame/--force-home/--force-slot. - intervene -> 'coalesce' only. target -> derive/force-phys-from-diff/score-simplify-order only. - permuter: agent ran 4500 local + 1600 remote iters, 0 winners (objective is register-shaped; can't target frame). Net: a function blocked purely by frame-local sizing reports 'no issues / no fast transform' and has no force/score/diff/first-divergence path. Also blocks gmresult2/fn_80175A94 (9 frame-local-ordering diffs) and grinishie1_fc664. PROPOSED NEW FUNCTIONALITY (Tier 8: frame/local-area layer): 1. FRAME MODEL EXTRACTOR — parse GENERATING/MERGING EPILOGUE,PROLOGUE + local-area allocation into {frame_size, callee_save_area, param_area, locals:[{name/@id, offset, size, stored?, class}], padding, home_reservations}. Detect NEVER-STORED/unused homes (the phantom +8). 2. FRAME TARGET + SCORE — extend target schema with a frame: section; derive a frame target from a matched sibling (mnGallery); score frame-distance (size delta + slot order + unused homes) so the permuter/score path can optimize toward it. 3. FRAME DIFF / FIRST-DIVERGENCE CLASS — inspect diff reports frame divergence first-class ('FRAME +8: unused 4B home for held-FP-const far_val'); first-divergence gets frame Cases (unused-home / slot-order / alignment-pad) each with a structural lever, like register Cases A-E. 4. FRAME FORCE/PROBE — --suppress-unused-home <local> / --force-frame-size N to test reachability (the force-phys analog). mnGallery proves 0x90 is reachable with this idiom; a probe would confirm + guide source search. 5. FRAME-AWARE DIAGNOSIS — inspect stuck/diagnose detect 'registers+schedule match, frame differs' and route to the frame tools instead of 'no flagged issues'. Reproducer pair for validating any of the above: gm_801A9DD0 (0x98) vs mnGallery_80258A08 (0x90), identical idiom.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2e308774fb7d89d581e328ec413f4c69a5762971 plus prior frame-model commits: frame/local-area state is now modelled via frame-reservations, target derive/score-source accepts frame targets, stuck/diagnose route frame residuals, and first-divergence now has --frame mode that emits frame-unused-low-home/frame-size cases with score/force-frame next steps. #339 remains open for making mutate search frame-directed.

summary: MWCC reserves unused stack home for held-constant FP local (gm_801A9DD0 +8 frame wall, 99.60%)
body: gm_801A9DD0 (gmopening.c) is instruction-byte-identical to target EXCEPT frame is 0x98 vs target 0x90. Root: to hold 2.0f in callee-saved f30 across the SetRoll/SetNear/SetFar chain (6 FPRs f26-f31, matching target), 2.0f must be a mutable 'f32 far_val' local. MWCC then reserves an unused 4-byte stack home (slot 0x18, never stored), pushing every data slot +4 and the 8-byte-aligned int->float magic-double scratch +8 -> frame 0x98. Target holds f30=2.0f (lfs gm_804DAAB4; fmr f1,f30 at SetFar) with NO stack home (frame 0x90, zero stfs). Tested ~25 source forms (plain/volatile-ptr/const/static/register/extern far_val, decl reorder, assign-after-branch, branch removal, literal-vs-global Vec3, Scissor-vs-HSD_RectS16 viewport, u16-h bottom) + 4500 local + 1600 remote(coder3) permuter iters: ALL either 0x98-held (99.60%) or 0x90-not-held (96.41%, 5 FPRs). The SObjLib_803A55DC template (same idiom, u16 params, no branch) compiles to 0x90 held-no-home; the difference is this fn's int arg2 (header-locked) magic conversions / branch register pressure. Likely needs the original compiler's exact frame-builder behavior or an undiscovered C construct. checkdiff residual = stack-offset-only (frame-agnostic instruction diff is empty).
resolution_note: Fixed in 7a5ba314507614ed4fcbc1bda36598ce9b62667a: changed gm_801A9DD0 height arg to u16 and matched the held-FP local/source layout, removing the unused low stack home. Verified current/expected frame_size=144 and checkdiff 100%; explicit name-magic smoke rewrites only anonymous .sdata2 labels and reports MATCH.

summary: Expected (target) frame not derivable — can't aim score-source/search at the 144 goal; suggest frame has no objective
body: Follow-up to #334 — the source-lever half shipped (suggest frame, target derive emits frame:, score-source) but the SCORER/SEARCH cannot be aimed at the expected frame, so it can't rank suggestions or drive a search. Evidence on gm_801A9DD0 (current 152, expected 144): 'dump local --force-frame-from-diff' achieves '--- MATCH ---' (patches 51 frame immediates), but 'target derive /tmp/forced144.txt -f gm_801A9DD0' (deriving from THAT forced/expected dump) still returns frame.frame_size=152 — the force-frame's expected layout is NOT reflected in the derived target. And 'target score-source <src> --target <derived>' prints a bare '0' (target==current 152 so distance 0; useless for driving toward 144). NET: 'suggest frame' lists levers (suppress-unused-local-home, reduce-alignment-growth) but there is no expected-frame OBJECTIVE to (a) score a candidate's frame-distance-to-EXPECTED, or (b) let 'mutate search'/permuter optimize toward 144. NEEDED: derive the EXPECTED frame target — from the force-frame-from-diff computed layout (it already knows the patches), or the expected .o, or a matched sibling (mnGallery_80258A08=0x90). Then score-source/mutate-search can be pointed at it. This is the last piece between 'frame wall is diagnosed + reachable' and 'frame wall is auto-searchable from source'. Reproducer harness unchanged: gm_801A9DD0 (152) <-> mnGallery_80258A08 (144), force-frame proves reachable.
resolution_note: Fixed in e1fc7152178aa6864588661766a0275bbf090f56: debug target derive now accepts --frame-from-checkdiff to derive frame targets from checkdiff target/reference asm; suggest frame now emits that target flow. Verified with focused tests and gm_801A9DD0 installed CLI smokes (derive frame_size=144, score-source=49).

summary: named seed provenance is lost for invalid directed telemetry entries
body: After merging master f0229f708 with #332 fix, named seeds work for valid telemetry and seed_candidates, but invalid directed entries still lose provenance. Command: debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed base=src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --seed flag_bool=build/mwcc_debug_cache/probes/manual/e7b4-flag-bool.c --seed flag_direct=... --seed flag_u32=... --no-remote --max-iters 0 --directed-force-phys 0:58:4,0:34:30,0:56:29. Output file: build/mwcc_debug_cache/probes/ftCo_8009E7B4.named-flag-probes-score.json. Observed: valid base telemetry has applied_mutator=base, but six low_coverage invalid entries print applied_mutator/candidate_id as None, so the operator cannot tell which named seed failed without source-hash side mapping. Expected: invalid directed telemetry should preserve candidate_id/applied_mutator from named seeds as well, and ideally include source_hash/path like valid entries.
resolution_note: fixed in 9094684a717b95ca12e7c70c5d49fcb8d97f7f55: invalid directed telemetry now uses the same artifact provenance attribution as valid telemetry, preserving named seed candidate_id/source_hash/applied_mutator on low_coverage and other invalid entries

summary: match-iter-first emits duplicate-IG conflicting target vector for ambiguous volatile regs
body: Retest after merging master f0229f708 in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers. Command: melee-agent debug target match-iter-first -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --regs gpr-volatile,r0. Output warns that r3/r4/r5/r6 targets are ambiguous, but also prints a Try command and force-phys vector with the same IG twice: 0:41:5 and 0:41:6 (ig41 cannot be forced to both r5 and r6). This vector is not directly usable for scorer setup and can mislead operators during volatile-reg triage. Requested behavior: suppress impossible duplicate-IG force-phys entries, split them into alternatives/subsets, or mark the vector as non-runnable while still reporting the ambiguous matches.
resolution_note: fixed in 9094684a717b95ca12e7c70c5d49fcb8d97f7f55: match-iter-first dedupes iter-first IDs, omits duplicate-IG conflicting phys targets from runnable force_phys/force_vector output, and reports conflicts explicitly

summary: REGRESSION: type-change --source-file still cannot find dirty-source ftCo_8009E7B4
body: Retest after merging master f0229f708 / fix commit 9811ae5a into /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers on codex/plbonuslib-blockers. Command: melee-agent debug mutate type-change -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --var flag --type u32. Observed: 'mutation failed: function ftCo_8009E7B4 not found' and stdout redirection created an empty probe file. Same failure for --type s32 and --type int. The function is present in the dirty current source and checkdiff/debug dump local operate on it. Help now shows --source-file, but the implementation still does not find this WIP function. This blocks using type-change for dirty retained source probes; manual copy/patch remains the workaround.
resolution_note: fixed in 9094684a717b95ca12e7c70c5d49fcb8d97f7f55: type-change --source-file now locates function-pointer signatures via balanced source spans and uses AST local discovery so nested dirty-source locals such as ftCo_8009E7B4 flag can be retyped

summary: Frame layer needs the SOURCE-LEVER half: frame target/score + 'suggest frame' (force-frame proves reachable, no source path yet)
body: Follow-up to #329 (frame model) — the DIAGNOSIS + REACHABILITY half shipped and works great on gm_801A9DD0: 'inspect frame-reservations' pinpoints the unused home (0x18-0x1c, +4 +4 alignment, origin=implicit-current-low-local-home), 'inspect diagnose' classifies FRAME/LOCAL-AREA RESIDUAL, and 'dump local --force-frame-from-diff' patches 51 frame immediates -> '--- MATCH ---' (proves frame target 0x90 is reachable). BUT force-frame is DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY; there is no path to a SHIPPABLE source fix. Missing pieces (the source-lever half): 1. FRAME TARGET/SCORE — 'target derive' still emits 'virtuals:' only (no frame section); add a frame: section (frame_size, slot layout, unused-homes) and have 'target score-source'/'score-dump' score frame-distance, so 'debug mutate search' and the permuter can OPTIMIZE TOWARD the frame target (today their objective is register-only, so they can't move the frame). 2. 'suggest frame -f FN' — given the frame-reservations origin (here: unused home for the held-2.0f 'far_val' DLOCAL, padded for 8B-alignment vs the @148/@150 int->float magic scratch created by the header-locked 'int arg2' u16-mask), emit ranked C-source transforms (analogous to 'suggest coalesce'/'suggest inlines' for registers). PROOF A SOURCE LEVER EXISTS (so this is findable, not impossible): the matched sibling mnGallery_80258A08 uses the IDENTICAL front-end idiom (verified via mwcc-inspect: 'far' DLOCAL float, '[far]=2', HSD_CObjSetFar) and compiles to 0x90 with NO home — so some source shape reaches the target frame from C. The gm_801A9DD0<->mnGallery pair + the force-frame reachability proof are the built-in validation harness for the source-lever finder.
resolution_note: fixed in 1a2b7fd1d18570c1e081cbe82afb05f19e4ff84a: target derive now includes a frame section, score-dump/score-source score frame size and unused-range distance, and debug suggest frame emits ranked frame source/probe levers for low-home residuals

summary: debug search run should accept named seed syntax like triage/combine
body: During E7B4 probing, I tried to score labeled manual seeds with '--seed flag_bool=build/mwcc_debug_cache/probes/manual/e7b4-flag-bool.c'. debug search run treated the whole string as a path and warned 'seed file not found', whereas debug search triage/combine already accept CANDIDATE_ID=path. This made telemetry harder to map back to probe intent and required source-hash side mapping. Feature request: allow --seed ID=path in debug search run/directed, preserve ID in applied_mutator/candidate_id metadata, and document the syntax consistently across search commands. If intentional, emit a clearer error suggesting plain --seed path.
resolution_note: fixed in 9811ae5a4b58413c0dcd3024dbdbaa3ac4ea7d0b: debug search run accepts --seed ID=path, preserves the ID through provenance/candidate_id, and reports seed_candidates

summary: inspect stuck/diagnose are frame-blind: frame-only residual reports 'no issues / NO FAST TRANSFORM'
body: On gm_801A9DD0 (99.60%, residual is purely a +8 stack frame, registers+schedule byte-identical), 'inspect stuck' says 'Guidance issues: (none — no flagged issues)' and 'inspect diagnose' says 'NO FAST TRANSFORM FOUND' and recommends decl-orders/force-phys/permuter — all register tools that cannot address a frame-sizing issue. This misleads the agent into either thinking the function is done/unstuck or grinding register tools indefinitely. FIX: when the colorgraph has 0 spills and the register/schedule passes already match the target but checkdiff is <100% with class=stack-layout/stack-slot-layout, detect 'frame-only residual' and SAY SO (e.g. 'residual is frame/local-area, not register — current tooling has no frame model, see <feature issue>'). Even without the full Tier-8 layer, a frame-size delta vs a derived/sibling target is a cheap, high-value signal. Pairs with the Tier-8 frame-model feature request.
resolution_note: fixed in 9811ae5a4b58413c0dcd3024dbdbaa3ac4ea7d0b: inspect stuck/diagnose surface frame/local-area residuals and route users to frame-reservations/force-frame-from-diff instead of allocator-only tools

summary: mwcc-inspect.sh remote checkout fails (exit 128) for a just-pushed branch — fetch doesn't pick it up
body: tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh failed at 'stage=checkout ref=<HEAD>' exit 128 immediately after I pushed my branch (claude/...) to origin (itsgrimetime/melee, which IS the remote's origin). The remote had the right origin but did not have my commit; the wrapper's 'git fetch origin' apparently didn't fetch the new branch/commit (or failed silently and the error was swallowed into a generic exit-128 checkout failure). Manual fix worked: ssh nzxt-local '...bash -lc "cd /c/Users/mikes/code/melee && git fetch origin"' then re-run, and the commit was present (GOT-MY-COMMIT). Suggested: (1) make the wrapper's fetch explicit + verbose and fetch ALL branches/the specific HEAD (e.g. 'git fetch origin <branch>' or 'git fetch origin +refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*'), (2) after fetch, verify 'git cat-file -e <ref>' and emit a clear 'remote is missing <ref>; push it / check origin' message instead of a bare exit-128, (3) optionally auto-retry the fetch once. Cost: one failed run + manual debugging before the tool worked.
resolution_note: fixed in 9811ae5a4b58413c0dcd3024dbdbaa3ac4ea7d0b: mwcc-inspect fetches origin branch refs before checkout, validates the ref as a commit, and preserves remote checkout diagnostics

summary: debug mutate type-change cannot find dirty-source ftCo_8009E7B4 in current worktree
body: Worktree: /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers on codex/plbonuslib-blockers. While probing retained dirty WIP in src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c, command 'melee-agent debug mutate type-change -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --var flag --type u32' printed 'mutation failed: function ftCo_8009E7B4 not found' even though the function is present in the current worktree source and checkdiff/debug dump local both operate on it. I had to manually copy/patch probe files instead. Expected: type-change should resolve functions from the current worktree dirty source, or accept --source-file like lifetime-layout so matching agents can mutate WIP functions reliably.
resolution_note: fixed in 9811ae5a4b58413c0dcd3024dbdbaa3ac4ea7d0b: debug mutate type-change now supports --source-file for dirty or out-of-report source files

summary: UPDATE #327: mnGallery_80258A08 proves held-FP-const+0x90 IS reachable in wibo; gm_801A9DD0 delta is int-arg2/5-param shape
body: Follow-up to #327. KEY PROOF: mnGallery_80258A08 (mngallery.c, MATCHED) uses the identical ortho idiom (held 2.0f in callee-saved f30, 0x43300000 int->float magic, 6 FPRs f26-f31) and compiles to frame 0x90 with NO far-home in the local wibo build (my .o == expected .o, both 0x90). So held-FP-const-without-stack-home is NOT impossible in this compiler. gm_801A9DD0 differs only by: int arg2 (header-locked to int -> forces clrlwi u16-mask in bottom conv) + 5 params (arg0..arg4) + the if(arg4) 0x248-split branch. Branch removal alone keeps 0x98, so the trigger is the int-arg2/5-param shape, not the branch. The +8 is in the param/local area sizing (lowest data slot 0x1c vs target 0x18; an unused 4-byte slot + 8-byte magic-double realign). Committed 99.60% at 2658dc218 (mnGallery-idiom body). If a force-frame / force-no-home lever is added to mwcc-debug, this is a clean test case (frame-agnostic instruction diff already EMPTY).
resolution_note: fixed in 5300bcec9: added debug dump local --force-frame-from-diff/--force-no-home-from-diff to derive stack-frame immediate patches and anonymous literal renames from checkdiff JSON before final diff; verified gm_801A9DD0 in agent-a0764b4cea58c70ea applied 51 stack-frame patches + 2 literal renames and final checkdiff MATCH

summary: directed search needs control-flow and scope-shape mutators
body: E7B4 local directed search after the 99.71% improvement compiled reorder_local_decls treatments only and found no movement on the reduced vector 0:58:4,0:34:30,0:56:29. The retained win came from scope/control-flow shape, not declaration order alone: moving bool reload to the anim_id block, flattening else { if (kind != KIRBY) ... } into else if (kind != KIRBY), and allowing the final fallback loop to reuse the outer i. Feature request: add mutators for nested-if flattening/unflattening, branch block scope removal/addition, local lifetime widening/narrowing, and loop-counter scope reuse so directed search can discover these natural allocator-shape changes.
resolution_note: Fixed in ddf7990e8: directed search now discovers and applies actionable control-flow/scope-shape mutators (nested-if flatten/unflatten, branch scope add/remove, lifetime widen/narrow, loop-counter reuse) with regression tests and source/installed CLI smokes.

summary: debug dump local should support arbitrary same-TU probe source files
body: During E7B4 candidate analysis, debug inspect diff could compile copied probe sources under build/mwcc_debug_cache/probes, but debug dump local refused the same probe file because it was not registered in build.ninja: no build block for build/GALE01/build/mwcc_debug_cache/probes/e7b4-normalized/827deb.o. This blocked running explain-virtual and other pcdump-followup diagnostics on normalized/generated probes without first transferring them into the real source path. Feature request: add a no-cache arbitrary-probe mode for same-TU source copies, compiling them with the original TU build settings and emitting a pcdump usable by inspect/explain/target commands without cache sync or build.ninja registration.
resolution_note: Fixed in d7cb30b26ff7a073ed24a6202d86c7dee48fa775: debug dump local now supports --unit-source for arbitrary same-TU probe source files, borrows the real TU build flags/object identity, adds the original source dir for quote-includes, and leaves baseline cache unchanged; source and installed end-to-end probe compiles passed.

summary: mwcc-debug needs focused b4 tree-loop shape explorer
body: After the retained 99.71% E7B4 improvement, the remaining paired diffs are dominated by the x594_b4 tree loop register swap: expected uses r30 for the loop IV and r29 for the computed tree+i pointer, while current uses r29 as IV and r30 as the pointer. Manual probes for outer-i reuse, scalar FigaTree* cur, tree declaration order, pointer walk, and coalesce-search pointer-walk variants did not close the reduced force-vector. Feature request: a focused loop-shape explorer for indexed pointer-array loops that systematically tries tree[i] vs cursor walk, scalar loaded element temp, direct indexed load preservation, pointer declaration/use order, loop bound hoists, loop IV scope/type, and pointer lifetime shortening while scoring opcode preservation plus directed force-phys satisfaction.
resolution_note: Fixed in d7cb30b26ff7a073ed24a6202d86c7dee48fa775: debug mutate lifetime-layout now supports --focus b4-tree-loop and --operator filters, applying operator filtering before max_probes; real ftdynamics smoke surfaces the pointer-walk loop probe family.

summary: debug dump setup: DLL build_macos.sh fails (MWDBG326.dll missing) in fresh worktree
body: Command: melee-agent debug dump setup. Observed: 'DLL build failed. Check tools/mwcc_debug/build_macos.sh.' wibo present at /Users/mike/code/melee-harness/bin/wibo but MWDBG326.dll build via build_macos.sh fails. Blocks debug inspect ceiling/analyze and force-* levers on the 8-byte stack-reservation wall for gm_801A9DD0 (92.3%). Worktree: /Users/mike/code/melee/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0764b4cea58c70ea
resolution_note: Fixed in d7cb30b26ff7a073ed24a6202d86c7dee48fa775: debug dump setup now captures build_macos output and promotes lmgr326b.dll to MWDBG326.dll when the build leaves only the import-name DLL; real debug dump setup smoke passes.

summary: directed search needs assignment-preserving candidate minimizer
body: E7B4 evidence after retaining the 99.71% source: generated/normalized seeds moved individual proof assignments at byte_score 2006, but useful edits were hidden inside broad generated function bodies. Manual minimization found a natural retained edit: moving bool reload to the anim_id block and flattening the final fallback else-if preserved byte_score and improved directed satisfaction to 3/6. Feature request: given a candidate source and a directed force-phys objective, automatically delta-reduce source changes while preserving selected proof assignments and byte score/checkdiff class. It should report the minimal source hunks that still satisfy assignments such as ig35->r30 or ig42->r3,ig44->r4.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7b89271c5: added debug search minimize, a score-command-backed greedy reducer that keeps/removes candidate subhunks while preserving requested proof assignments and byte_score ceilings, then writes the minimized retained source.

summary: first-divergence should handle or continue past r0-boundary allocator cases
body: E7B4 reduced force-vector after the 99.71% retained edit is 0:58:4,0:34:30,0:56:29. first-divergence immediately abstains because ig58 is the early lbz/rlwinm flag reload currently assigned r0 and desired r4. That prevents the tool from explaining either the r58 reload pressure or the later coupled r34/r56 b4 loop swap in the same vector. Feature request: improve first-divergence to model r0-boundary assignments when possible, or continue past an abstained r0 target to report the next actionable coupled target with advisory source ideas.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7b89271c5: first-divergence now preserves the old single-r0-boundary abstain behavior but continues past leading r0-boundary target divergences to report the next non-r0 actionable coupled target, with the skipped r0 advisory in local_target.

summary: debug search combine needs subhunk recombination for broad generated candidates
body: After the resolved combine/triage work, E7B4 normalized seeds still expose a useful workflow gap. Normalized 9dbc/bb5eea each satisfy ig42->r3 and ig44->r4 at byte_score 2006; normalized 827deb satisfies ig35->r30 at byte_score 2006. reports skipped/overlapping-source-hunks for every pair because generated-source formatting turns the function into broad overlapping hunks. Manual subhunk naturalization found a real retained improvement: early reload scope + flattened final fallback moved 3/6 proof assignments and improved checkdiff from 99.45 to 99.71. Feature request: split candidate deltas intra-function by stable source regions/AST-ish anchors, or allow manual hunk/range labels, so combine can recombine complementary generated candidates instead of skipping whole-function overlaps.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7b89271c5: debug search combine now accepts repeated --range CANDIDATE_ID:BASE_START-BASE_END=CANDIDATE_START-CANDIDATE_END manual subhunks, allowing broad generated candidates to be recombined by selected stable source regions instead of being skipped as whole-function overlaps.

summary: directed search needs multi-cluster candidate combination for allocator proofs
body: Directed scoring is now useful for E7B4, but the current search space has plateaued. Historical candidate bb5eea27a740c0c462b25903212ca389 and naturalized 9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2 satisfy 2/6 proof assignments (ig42->r3, ig44->r4) at byte_score 2036. Complementary candidate 827deb3d6ef12da64dd02fe7e73a5f6e satisfies 1/6 (ig35->r30), also at byte_score 2036. Existing local directed treatments and remote runs have not combined these improvements or recovered byte score. Needed: a directed search mode that can combine complementary seed deltas, perform crossover/patch recombination, and explore coordinated multi-site mutations spanning independent-looking source regions. The objective should keep reporting assignment satisfaction, byte score, opcode preservation, and whether progress is attributable to one cluster or to an interaction between clusters.
resolution_note: Fixed in 46690e607: added debug search combine to recombine complementary candidate source hunks pairwise, write combined sources, union directed proof-assignment progress, report early/late proof clusters and multi-cluster interaction attribution, and optionally score generated combinations.

summary: partial directed candidates need source-delta triage and naturalization support
body: Remote/directed search can now harvest candidates with assignment-progress metadata, but the operator still has to manually inspect source hashes and infer which source edits caused which allocator movement. For E7B4, the strongest evidence is a messy 2/6 remote candidate and a more naturalized 2/6 variant, plus a complementary 1/6 late-loop candidate. None are retainable as-is, and current tooling does not extract the minimal readable deltas, cluster edits by affected proof assignments, or suggest how to naturalize generated artifacts while preserving the useful allocator movement. Needed: a triage command that compares retained source vs candidate sources, reports semantic/source-shape deltas tied to proof assignments, flags generated artifacts, proposes minimal natural edits to keep, and can score those minimized/naturalized edits automatically.
resolution_note: Fixed in 9c9260d77: added debug search triage to compare retained/base source against candidate sources, attach directed telemetry proof-assignment progress, cluster E7B4 proof movement, flag generated artifacts, propose naturalization/minimization guidance, and optionally run a score-command against each candidate.

summary: derive source-shape guidance from coupled force-phys vectors
body: E7B4 is blocked on a six-node allocator proof vector: 0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30. force-phys union byte-matches, but every singleton and prefix probe no-matches, so this is a coupled allocator-shape problem rather than independent virtual nudges. Current force-phys/checkdiff/inspect tooling proves the desired backend allocation and points at generic hints, but it does not derive operator-facing source-shape hypotheses from the coupled result. Needed: a diagnosis mode that recognizes coupled proof vectors, groups virtuals into likely source clusters, explains why partial probes fail, and proposes source-shape families to try. For this case, it should explicitly connect the early flag/reload temps r58/r44/r42 and the late x594_b4/x594_b3 loop IV/tree-pointer swaps r35/r56/r34 into a multi-site allocator-shape hypothesis, with concrete source experiments and expected verification commands.
resolution_note: Fixed in 969da1c75: debug inspect diagnose now accepts --force-phys proof vectors and emits coupled source-shape guidance for multi-node allocator proofs, including the ftCo_8009E7B4 early flag/reload and late x594 loop/tree-pointer clusters plus verification commands.

summary: inspect stuck recommends decl-orders when decl-order enumerator has no candidates
body: In /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers on codex/plbonuslib-blockers, after refreshing pcdump/checkdiff for ftCo_8009E7B4, == Function status == ftCo_8009E7B4 TU: src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c Match: 99.45% == Pcdump cache == fresh (ftdynamics.txt) == Coloring summary == Virtuals: 42 (41 mapped, 1 unmapped) Spilled: 0 Pre-pass: AFTER PEEPHOLE FORWARD == Guidance issues == (none — pcdump available but no flagged issues. Provide --target to compare against a specific mapping.) == Top 1 asm hunks (by diff size) == -+1c8: 3b c0 00 00 li r30,0 -+1cc: 57 c0 10 3a slwi r0,r30,2 -+1d0: 7f a3 02 14 add r29,r3,r0 ++1c8: 3b a0 00 00 li r29,0 ++1cc: 57 a0 10 3a slwi r0,r29,2 ++1d0: 7f c3 02 14 add r30,r3,r0 +1d4: 48 00 00 20 b <ftCo_8009E7B4+0x1f4> -+1d8: 80 dd 00 00 lwz r6,0(r29) ++1d8: 80 de 00 00 lwz r6,0(r30) +1dc: 38 7f 00 00 addi r3,r31,0 -+1e0: 38 9e 00 00 addi r4,r30,0 ++1e0: 38 9d 00 00 addi r4,r29,0 ...(1 more lines) == Next steps (ranked by cost) == 1. [~70sec] Run `melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders ftCo_8009E7B4` — brute-forces the decl-order search space, finds 1-line wins. 2. [minutes] Run `melee-agent debug inspect diagnose ftCo_8009E7B4` for a current-tooling diagnosis (combines force-phys evidence + mutate decl-orders without treating the function as impossible). 3. [hours] As a last resort, run decomp-permuter and feed its outputs through `debug permute triage`. ranked no candidate orderings to try (function may have only 1 local). as the first next step. Running the exact follow-up with broader coverage, no candidate orderings to try (function may have only 1 local)., immediately exited . For stuck-function triage this is misleading: if the decl-order enumerator sees no candidates in the selected/default scope, inspect stuck should either suppress that recommendation, mention the scope limitation and suggest a scope-specific command, or recommend the next viable allocator/source-shape workflow instead.
resolution_note: fixed in 42a396df6: inspect stuck preflights decl-order candidate availability and recommends diagnose --skip-decl-orders when the default decl-order scope has no candidates

summary: debug permute bootstrap: report.json lookup uses cwd-based melee-root detection, fails from perm-root dir
body: Command: from /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter ran 'melee-agent debug permute bootstrap -f un_802FF570 --perm-root /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter'. Observed: 'could not find un_802FF570 in report.json. Rebuild report.json and retry.' even though BOTH the main repo AND worktree report.json contain un_802FF570 (verified via json). Root cause: _bootstrap_permuter_dir uses DEFAULT_MELEE_ROOT = _detect_melee_root() (src/cli/_common.py), which checks cwd then walks PARENTS for config/GALE01. When cwd is the decomp-permuter checkout (a sibling of melee, no config/GALE01 in any parent), it falls back to cwd -> reads <perm-root>/build/GALE01/report.json which does not exist -> returns None. Expected: bootstrap should resolve melee-root independent of cwd (e.g. honor MELEE_DECOMP_PERMUTER_ROOT split, or accept a --melee-root, or detect from the worktree the --source-file lives in). Workaround that works: run the bootstrap command from the melee worktree root (which has config/GALE01) so detection finds the worktree report. This blocked re-bootstrapping the permuter from an improved base until I cd'd back to the worktree.
resolution_note: fixed in 42a396df6: debug permute bootstrap now resolves Melee root from --melee-root/MELEE_ROOT/source/cwd/package candidates instead of cwd-only DEFAULT_MELEE_ROOT

summary: debug search run changes directed baseline when only non-baseline seed is provided
body: Worktree /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers at 32ffb6610. The explicit force-vector 0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30 is derived from the current retained src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c WIP. When debug search run is invoked with retained source plus seed build/search-store/sources/9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.c, the 9dbc seed scores 2/6 proof assignments (ig42->r3, ig44->r4). Standalone debug search directed with --seed 9dbc after #312 also scores 9dbc as 2/6 relative to the retained-source control. But if debug search run is invoked with only --seed build/search-store/sources/9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.c, the JSON shows directed.baseline_source_hash=9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2 and the seed scores 0/6 because the command silently changes the directed baseline/control to the non-baseline seed. Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed build/search-store/sources/9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.c --directed-force-phys '0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30' --max-iters 4. This blocks seed-specific remote searches because operators cannot safely ask remote producers to explore a promising non-baseline seed while retaining the proof-vector baseline from the dirty worktree. Desired behavior: provide an explicit baseline/control source option, default to the dirty worktree source for proof-vector reanchoring even if only non-baseline seeds are listed, or at minimum warn/fail when an explicit proof vector is paired with a baseline_source_hash that is not the current source.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7d0b4a8cd00eb4b7e9a3932fd93e51cbdd79baf7: debug search run now anchors directed proof/control baseline to the current TU source instead of the first --seed. Seeds remain candidate inputs. Verified with red/green regression, full search CLI smoke tests, and installed reporter-worktree smoke showing baseline_source_hash=2f8266568d3ed4e1c4e384bf1c3cb47b while seed source_hash=9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.

summary: debug dump local truncates large TUs — target function omitted from partial dump
body: On ftKb_Init.c (large TU), 'debug dump local' produced only a PARTIAL dump: 18 of the TU's functions, and the requested target ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50 (0x800EED50) was NOT among them. Not blocking in this case (the residual was data-addressing/coalescing, not register coloring) but for register-wall functions in large TUs the dump silently omitting the target makes mwcc-debug unusable. Suggested: ensure the requested -f FUNCTION is always included in the dump (don't truncate it out), or warn loudly when the target is omitted, or add a single-function dump mode that guarantees the target.
resolution_note: Fixed in 870b905d67b7039416085105340a9cfa8912ebf5: local debug dump watchdog now treats pcdump file growth as compile progress, preventing large quiet TUs from being killed/truncated while still preserving the no-progress kill path. Verified with regression tests and live ftKb_Init.c dump including ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50.

summary: debug search directed assignment scoring disagrees with debug search run for same seed
body: Worktree /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers at cc293dd74. Same force-vector: 0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30. After merging master fe70f240c, debug search run with seeds src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c, kind_cache_b3.c, bb5eea27a740c0c462b25903212ca389.c, and 9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.c reports source_hash 9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2 as 2/6 satisfied (ig42->new_ig44 assigned r3, ig44->new_ig43 assigned r4), byte_score 2036. Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --seed build/mwcc_debug_cache/manual-probes/e7b4/kind_cache_b3.c --seed build/search-store/sources/bb5eea27a740c0c462b25903212ca389.c --seed build/search-store/sources/9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.c --directed-force-phys '0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30' --max-iters 5. But debug search directed using that same 9dbc seed and same force-vector reports 0/6 satisfied, with ig42->new_ig42 assigned r30 and ig44->new_ig44 assigned r3, byte_score 2036. Command: melee-agent debug search directed -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed build/search-store/sources/9dbc90783e42ac0b8adcb6db3fff9cf2.c --directed-force-phys '0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30' --max-iters 3. The same source hash and proof vector should produce consistent assignment/reanchor telemetry across the two CLIs, or the tool should explain why the seed is being compiled/scored in a different context. This blocks trusting standalone directed-search gate/no_smooth_gradient results for E7B4.
resolution_note: Fixed in f4da3d2c8: standalone debug search directed now keeps the repo TU source as the directed objective/control baseline and treats --seed as the candidate source, matching debug search run assignment telemetry semantics; live E7B4 standalone command now reports source_hash 9dbc907... as 2/6 satisfied with byte_score 2036.

summary: debug search run best_byte_score ignores lower byte-scored seeds when directed candidate wins
body: Worktree /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers at a1324b94b. Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --seed build/mwcc_debug_cache/manual-probes/e7b4/kind_cache_b3.c --seed build/search-store/sources/bb5eea27a740c0c462b25903212ca389.c --directed-force-phys '0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30' --max-iters 5. The JSON summary reported best_byte_score=2036, but directed_telemetry includes seed#1 and seed#2 with byte_score=2006. best_directed_meta correctly points at a 2/6 directed candidate with byte_score=2036, so the summary appears to be copying the directed-best candidate's byte score into best_byte_score instead of reporting the byte-best score. This is misleading for operators because byte-best and directed-best intentionally diverge on E7B4.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1222e8d38: debug search run now computes best_byte_score independently from directed-best ordering and includes a directed summary regression test.

summary: gricemt.c grIceMt_801F8CDC 99.94%: data-layout/literal-pool ceiling — maintainer-help candidate
body: Ref #228. grIceMt_801F8CDC .text is byte-identical at 99.94%; sole residual = 3 HSD_ASSERTMSG message-string offsets (+0x20) driven by .data pooled-literal emission order. Completing grIm_803E40B0 (real 195 S16Vec3, was placeholder) + defining grIm_803E4544 (s16[218], was undefined extern) + named grIm_803E46F8/47F4 + pointing the file string at grIm_803E46F8 closes the FILE string and lands all named .data symbols at correct offsets with NO sibling regression (proven). StageData does NOT need extending (its 0xC8 symbol includes trailing literals; 0x34 type is correct). Remaining 3 message-string literals (block_num<=BLOCK_COLL_JOBJ_MAX/coll_jobj/block_jobj) need exact literal-tail emission order not reproducible from C without full source/literal-order reconstruction. Data defs preserved for a future dedicated pass. Likely needs a maintainer who knows the original gricemt.c .data/string layout.
resolution_note: fixed in 7e5a27807: applied retained gricemt function-shape/data references on master; grIceMt_801F8CDC checkdiff is match=true/100.00 with only relocation annotation differences, and grIceMt_801F96E0 plus grIceMt_801F993C remain 100.00.

summary: un_803147C4 follow-up: remote+targeted permuter run, 96.67->99.38, swap residual UNCRACKED (sibling ref #298)
body: Followed up on #298 after #158/#159 resolved. RESULTS: (1) Remote stock permuter on coder3 (5263 iters, 16 threads) found ONE valid win = a call-site temp 'new_var = (u8)HSD_GObj_804D784A' that fixes the arg-load SCHEDULING diff; triaged 99.38% real-tree, committed f75c0230c. It then PLATEAUED — best valid score 45 (=the 99.38 candidate), could not crack the gobj/archive callee-save swap. (2) Targeted LOCAL simplify-order scorer (--want-first 32, ig32=gobj wants r29 / ig38=archive wants r28) found low-simplify-order candidates (score 25->18->1) BUT triage proved score does NOT correlate with match%: output-1-1 (best simplify score) triaged to only 92.42% (junk: added 'if(GObj_804D784A){}' + duplicate GObj_Create); ALL targeted candidates triaged WORSE than 99.38% or were CORRUPT/UNSAFE. The only 'fixes' that move the swap require changing memzero's RETURN TYPE (void->int, an external-TU prototype change) — invalid. CONCLUSION: un_803147C4 banked at 99.38% (3 commits). The gobj/archive callee-save swap is a genuine MWCC ig-order ceiling unreachable from VALID in-function C; neither stock byte-distance nor directed simplify-order permutation closes it. Remote permuter infra now WORKS (doctor all-pass on coder3 after clean stock settings.toml + removing the /Users path-leaking yaml). Worktree agent-ad61c0e5c2d3b28af.
resolution_note: banked in e1ee9e463, 2e0e86620, and 63f2b50b4 on master: applied the retained un_803147C4 worktree commits and verified checkdiff restored 99.38%; remaining residual is the documented r28/r29 callee-save register-allocation ceiling.

summary: remote permuter coder1 preflight missing python toml module
body: After #309, the full coder1 preflight failure is visible. Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --remotes coder1 --max-iters 1 --directed-from-diff --verify. Producer failure detail: remote preflight failed for coder1: remote python3 toml: Traceback (most recent call last): File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>; ModuleNotFoundError: No module named toml. This prevents coder1 from contributing remote permuter/search capacity for directed E7B4 runs. Requested fix: install/provision the toml module in coder1 remote python environment or update remote preflight/tooling to use a dependency already present on the host.
resolution_note: fixed in 7b65c2fac3249861819661632f98f52b5cc73a3d: remote permuter submit now auto-repairs bootstrap/tooling preflight failures such as missing remote python toml before retrying doctor; coder1 was repaired and validated via doctor plus one-iteration search producer smoke.

summary: debug search directed still aborts on r0 case_abstained instead of assignment fallback telemetry
body: After dd15f5d15, debug search run is fixed for E7B4: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-remote --max-iters 2 --directed-from-diff --verify returns directed.preflight=fallback:case_abstained and emits force_phys_assignment telemetry. However the dedicated directed entrypoint still gates out with no telemetry: melee-agent debug search directed -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --max-iters 2 --directed-force-phys 0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30 returns gate passed=false reason=not_preflight preflight_reason=case_abstained and directed_telemetry=[]. The seed/source-file worktree fix from #307 is present, but the assignment fallback from #306 appears wired only into debug search run, not debug search directed. Requested behavior: debug search directed should use the same force-phys assignment fallback scoring/telemetry when first-divergence preflight abstains, so operators can inspect directed candidates from either entrypoint.
resolution_note: fixed in 139922e66: debug search directed now continues after case_abstained when a force-phys proof is present, scores the seed through the same assignment fallback path, and records preflight fallback accounting.

summary: debug search remote producer preflight failure for coder1 hides actionable traceback
body: Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --max-iters 5 --directed-from-diff --verify after dd15f5d15. The run successfully started coder2/coder3 and harvested/scored two candidates, but coder1 failed preflight: [search] producer-start-failed producer=permuter-job remote=coder1 detail=remote preflight failed for coder1: remote python3 toml: Traceback (most recent call last):. Final accounting only preserved the same truncated detail string. This makes the remote failure hard to act on and silently reduces available remote capacity. Requested behavior: preserve the full remote preflight traceback/stderr in producer_failures or write it to a linked log path, and ideally classify missing python module/config/env failures separately from candidate/tool failures.
resolution_note: fixed in 139922e66: remote doctor parsing preserves multiline preflight failure details, so remote producer failures retain full tracebacks instead of truncating after the first line.

summary: debug search force-vector objective aborts on r0 preflight abstain instead of scoring candidates
body: After the directed search fixes landed, the force-vector CLI is present but still cannot drive E7B4 candidate ranking. Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-remote --max-iters 2 --directed-from-diff --verify. The derived vector verifies and the union byte-matches: 0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30. The search then exits 4 before scoring: directed objective preflight failed: case_abstained. The same happens with explicit --directed-force-phys. debug inspect first-divergence reports the cause as divergence involves r0, a model boundary the replay cannot predict. For E7B4 this r0 case is the first known cluster, but the full six-node force-vector proof is still valid and useful. Requested behavior: do not make first-divergence preflight abstention fatal for debug search run. Fall back to assignment-aware force-vector objective scoring and emit directed metadata for seed/local/remote candidates, including which proof assignments are satisfied, which are blocked or abstained, byte score, and checkdiff gate result.
resolution_note: fixed in dd15f5d15: debug search run now continues through case_abstained directed preflight using force-phys assignment fallback metadata instead of exiting before scoring candidates

summary: mismatch-db register-keyword pattern is misleading for this MWCC
body: The mismatch-db pattern register-keyword-respected-by-mwcc says MWCC 1.2.5n honors the C register keyword as an allocation hint. User clarified that the MWCC version used by this decomp does not respect register keywords at all. In the ftCo_8009E7B4 manual probe batch, register_flag_reload and register_i_tree_success both scored neutral at the unchanged 99.44976 and did not alter the relevant allocator result, consistent with register being a no-op here. This entry should be corrected, removed, or version-qualified so agents do not waste time trying register-keyword probes for allocator mismatches in this repo/compiler.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2e3bc05bd: mismatch-db now corrects the stale register-keyword-respected-by-mwcc row in persisted databases, updates the historical seed entry, and warns that register is not a reliable allocation hint for this repo/compiler. Verified with tests/test_mismatch_db_search.py and mismatch get smoke.

summary: lifetime-layout marks worse untargeted candidates as improved
body: While probing ftCo_8009E7B4 in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, I ran manual staged candidates through: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --candidate manual=<candidate.c> --max-probes 0 --json. Several candidates that reduced final_match_percent were reported with objective.actionability='improved' solely because interference_removed_count was nonzero, even though target_pairs was empty and interference_added_count was comparable. Examples: flag_direct_u8 dropped from 99.44976 to 99.37799 but reported improved with 32 removed / 29 added; outer_i_tree_success dropped to 98.97129 but reported improved with 64 removed / 64 added; explicit_tree_cursor dropped to 98.10048 but reported improved with 86 removed / 83 added. For operator triage, untargeted interference removals should not be reported as improved when match percent regresses or when there is no directed target/pair objective. Suggested behavior: if target_pairs is empty and no explicit directed objective is provided, classify these as neutral/regressed, or separate 'topology changed' from 'actionability improved'.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2e3bc05bd: lifetime-layout objective now treats untargeted interference removals as topology changes instead of positive actionability, and stops using them to outrank better real scores when --pairs is empty. Verified with tests/test_pressure_explorer.py and lifetime-layout CLI help smoke.

summary: debug search run needs operator-facing directed objective from force-phys proof
body: After merging master through 98b694ed1, the directed search primitives are present (DirectedObjective, DirectedScorePipeline, PcdumpLocalBackend, scheduler directed-mode), but the public CLI path still has no way to use them for the live ftCo_8009E7B4 blocker. Observed in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers: - `melee-agent debug search run --help` exposes only --function/--unit/--seed/remote/max-iters/dry-compiler knobs. - `tools/melee-agent/src/search/cli.py` constructs ByteScorePipeline + DefaultScheduler and never accepts/builds a DirectedObjective. - The verified proof vector for this function is `0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30`; `debug target force-phys-from-diff -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --verify` confirms the full union byte-matches while singleton/prefix probes do not. - `debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-remote --max-iters 1` compiled and scored one candidate, but only by byte score (`best_byte_score`: 2006), not by the allocator proof target. - Existing target-specific commands (`mutate lifetime-layout`, `select-order-search`, `coalesce-search`) can score bounded generated probes, but they are separate from the remote/search substrate and do not let us run remote permuter/search against this exact six-node objective. Needed functionality: 1. Add CLI flags to `debug search run` such as `--directed-force-phys 0:58:4,...` or `--directed-from-diff --verify`, plus `--directed-class 0`, so the CLI builds a DirectedObjective using the current TU/source and pcdump backend. 2. Wire the CLI to use DirectedScorePipeline and scheduler directed mode when that objective is present, while preserving byte-score/checkdiff as the final gate. 3. Emit directed metadata in the JSON summary and artifact manifests: validity/invalid reason, case, reanchor coverage, order_distance, displacement, displacement_delta, applied mutator/producer, byte score, and checkdiff result. 4. Support both local seeds and remote producer candidates under the same directed objective; for this function we need remote fan-out ranked by the allocator proof vector, not just byte score. 5. The objective should not be order-only. For ftCo_8009E7B4, the same-source force-phys pcdump has the same select order as natural but different physical assignments; a useful directed score needs assignment/role closeness for the proof vector (r58/r44/r42 early volatile cluster plus r35/r56/r34 late loop cluster), or else order_distance/displacement may be flat even when the force proof is valid. Acceptance test idea: on ftCo_8009E7B4, `debug search run --directed-from-diff --verify --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-remote --max-iters 1` should derive the six-entry proof vector, preflight non-void, score the seed with directed_meta, and report that the full forced vector matches while the natural seed remains 99.45%. A remote-enabled run should launch/harvest candidates and rank by directed score before byte-score/checkdiff tie-breaks.
resolution_note: fixed in c5ce4f43b: debug search run now accepts explicit/from-diff directed proof vectors, builds/preflights DirectedObjective from seed/current source, emits directed metadata/manifests, and ranks local/remote candidates by directed score before byte score. Verified explicit E7B4 proof-vector smoke with decomp-permuter seed.

summary: directed search lacks force-vector input and returns no_roles on E7B4
body: Clean replacement for mangled issue 304. After merging master into codex/plbonuslib-blockers at 03a478404 and running python tools/worktree-doctor.py --fix, the new public command is melee-agent debug search directed. Command tested: melee-agent debug search directed -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --max-iters 2 --store build/directed-store/e7b4-smoke. Output: gate passed false, reason not_preflight, preflight_reason no_roles, directed_telemetry empty, accounting preflight_failed true. The known force-vector proof for this same source remains valid: melee-agent debug target force-phys-from-diff -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --verify derives CSV 0:58:4,0:44:4,0:42:3,0:35:30,0:56:29,0:34:30; the union force vector byte-matches, while singleton and prefix probes do not. The directed command help exposes only --function, --unit, --store, --dry, and --max-iters, so there is still no operator-facing way to pass or derive the proof vector. Needed: wire directed search to accept the force-vector proof or derive it from checkdiff, then score/rank seed and generated candidates with directed metadata. Acceptance for E7B4: this command should not preflight as no_roles when a verified force-vector proof exists; it should emit directed telemetry for the retained 99.45 percent seed and rank candidates against the six-node assignment objective.
resolution_note: fixed in c5ce4f43b: debug search directed now accepts --directed-force-phys/--force-phys and --directed-from-diff proof setup; DirectedScorePipeline byte-scores live candidates and the directed runner always escalates to pcdump scoring. Installed dry proof-vector smoke emits directed telemetry.
