summary: un_80303FD4 walled at 93.04% by 4-way callee-saved rotation (Case C2 sticky-pool); needs targeted simplify-order permuter
body: un_80303FD4 (if/textlib.c) driven 50.37%->93.04% (structure 100% correct: all 3 loops, calls, control flow, prologue, frame match). SOLE residual = 4-way callee-saved register rotation: arg0 r30->r25, arg4 r25->r26, arg5 r26->r27, size r27->r30. first-divergence: Case C2 at iter1 ig_idx32 (arg0): baseline r30, target r25 — sticky-pool/dispense-count mismatch ('change how many nonvolatiles dispense before arg0'). force-phys '32:25,35:26,36:27,41:30' --force-phys-fn REACHES the exact expected mapping (proven), so it IS source-shape-reachable. Random decomp-permuter (wibo-based, WORKS in this macOS env via bootstrap-fixed compile.sh) plateaued at 93.04% across ~1500 iters / 4 rounds — score 770/890/955 candidates all triage to 93.04% real-tree (score does NOT correlate, always triage). Needs the TARGETED simplify-order scorer (setup-simplify-order-scorer --want-first, requires decomp-permuter companion patch commit 81378ff) OR remote permuter — both un-triageable by a one-shot agent. NOTE: random permuter compile.sh now uses local wibo (NOT wine) after 'debug permute bootstrap' — contradicts stale memory note #158/#159 about macOS breakage; bootstrap+triage work fine locally and are fast (~215 iters/50s/thread).
resolution_note: Fixed in bb6b8448e964622c57d396ae0edc2bea58b7574d by making debug permute setup-simplify-order-scorer a one-shot campaign setup path: --bootstrap creates the missing decomp-permuter function dir and --auto-baseline-dump captures <perm-dir>/baseline.pcdump.txt before wiring the custom simplify-order scorer. Verified on un_80303FD4 with --want-first 32 and force-phys 32:25,35:26,36:27,41:30; generated the campaign under /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/un_80303FD4 and /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/.venv/bin/python ./permuter.py nonmatchings/un_80303FD4 --debug compiled/scored base successfully.

summary: un_803147C4 at 96.67%: residual is MWCC simplify-order ceiling (gobj/archive callee-save swap), permuter-ready
body: un_803147C4 (ty/tylist.c) improved 85.97% -> 96.67% and committed (toy.h 4865f2d12 + tylist.c 889b9a7ab). Remaining residual is a single callee-save register swap: gobj (ig_idx 32, derived un_804A2AC0+0x2C4) gets r28 but target is r29; archive (ig_idx 37, lwz un_804D6ED8) gets r29 but target is r28. first-divergence classifies it Case C (shift archive's simplify-order position later). force-iter-first 41,39,32,37 and force-phys 32:29,37:28 both confirm the target coloring is reachable, but it is NOT reachable from C: 'debug mutate decl-orders --strategy all' shows all 12 declaration permutations neutral/worse, and structural levers (gobj placement, archive placement, lobj hoist) don't move it. This is the documented ig_idx-ordering ceiling class. A simplify-order scorer is already wired up at /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/un_803147C4 (--want-first 32, class 0); a permuter run is the right finish but I avoided running it locally (would starve sibling build agents). Separately: 'debug permute verify base.c -f un_803147C4' hung for ~5min and produced no output (exit 0 reported but empty) on macOS — may be the missing-objdump scorer path, though settings.toml uses 'debug target dtk-objdump'. Worktree agent-ad61c0e5c2d3b28af.

summary: allocator probes should try selective loop-counter hoists across sibling branches
body: On ftCo_8009E7B4, current lifetime/select-order/coalesce probes after 8381ae894 still generate only loop-counter-type, pointer-walk-loop, type-width, and early-guard-return probes. None generated the source edit that improved the natural match from 99.33 percent to 99.45 percent: move the outer loop counter declaration to the top of ftCo_8009E7B4, remove the original anim_id != -1 outer declaration, and reuse that hoisted counter in the final anim_id == -1 fallback loop while keeping the x594_b4 tree indexed loop local. This fixed the final +2fc loop r29/r30 mismatch and reduced register-only paired diffs from 23 to 19. Related negative probes: adding a block-local loop counter to the x594_b3 loop regressed to a 40-byte frame and 99.17 percent; using the hoisted counter in the tree indexed loop regressed to 98.97 percent; caching fp->dynamics_num before the x594_b3 loop regressed to 98.47 percent and changed call/relocation shape. Requested probe family: detect sibling branch loops that share identical for-loop shape and ftCo_8009CB40 calls, then try selective loop-counter declaration hoists and reuse sets across function scope, enclosing-if scope, and selected sibling branches, while excluding high-risk indexed pointer loops unless explicitly requested. Score against register-only diffs and target virtuals so partial improvements like this are surfaced.
resolution_note: Fixed in 9fd70048b: lifetime-layout now emits a sibling-loop-counter-hoist probe that groups same-counter/same-shape/same-call loops, hoists or reuses a function-scope int counter for the safe call-loop pair, and leaves indexed counter loops block-local. Verified with pressure_explorer regression tests and installed melee-agent JSON smoke.

summary: directed probe scoring left malformed candidate source in real worktree
body: During the 2026-06-02 05:46 heartbeat on /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, after running directed probe commands for ftCo_8009E7B4, src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c was left modified with a malformed generated candidate instead of the retained source. Commands run immediately before discovery included: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/src_melee_ft_ftdynamics.txt --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --pairs r58/r44,r44/r42,r35/r56,r34/r80 --compile-probes --max-probes 12 --timeout 90 --json; melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/src_melee_ft_ftdynamics.txt --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --target r58=r44,r35=r56 --max-probes 10 --timeout 90 --json; melee-agent debug select-order-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/src_melee_ft_ftdynamics.txt --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --target 'r56<r35,r44<r42' --beam-depth 1 --beam-width 4 --max-probes 10 --timeout 90 --json. After those commands, nl -ba src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c showed duplicate function-scope declarations at ftCo_8009E7B4: int i; int i;, and the x594_b4 loop had a generated FigaTree* ll_probe_value_0 temp. I manually restored the retained source and verified checkdiff returned to 99.45%. Probe scoring should use a transactional source transfer/restore guard around every candidate and fail closed if the target function disappears or candidate compilation exits nonzero.
resolution_note: Fixed in 8e7ddaef8: source candidate pcdump staging now transfers only the target function into the retained TU, restores repo source from byte snapshots on all exits, and fails closed before dump local when the candidate lacks the target function. Verified with diff_capture tests and CLI source-candidate smokes.

summary: directed allocator probes duplicate existing hoisted loop counter and drop target function
body: On branch codex/plbonuslib-blockers after merging master 40e6cad34, the current retained ftCo_8009E7B4 source already has a function-scope int i from the 99.45% improvement. Directed probe commands now generate malformed candidates that insert another int i at the top of ftCo_8009E7B4, so pcdump omits the target function and every candidate is unusable. Repro commands: (1) melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/src_melee_ft_ftdynamics.txt --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --pairs r58/r44,r44/r42,r35/r56,r34/r80 --compile-probes --max-probes 12 --timeout 90 --json; (2) melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/src_melee_ft_ftdynamics.txt --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --target r58=r44,r35=r56 --max-probes 10 --timeout 90 --json; (3) melee-agent debug select-order-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/src_melee_ft_ftdynamics.txt --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --target 'r56<r35,r44<r42' --beam-depth 1 --beam-width 4 --max-probes 10 --timeout 90 --json. Example retained source hunk from generated candidates: lines 813-817 contain void ftCo_8009E7B4(...), then {, then int i;, then another int i;. debug dump local exits 3 with function 'ftCo_8009E7B4' not found in pcdump. The generator should detect an existing compatible hoisted counter and skip/rename/narrow variants instead of producing duplicate declarations; it should also classify this as malformed-source early without running a pcdump candidate that cannot contain the target.
resolution_note: fixed in 7009ba606812a38cc7376e651e99cfbda0f3ed18: lifetime-layout loop-counter-hoist probes now reuse an existing compatible function-scope int counter instead of generating duplicate int i declarations; regression verifies only one int i remains

summary: checkdiff --no-build reports misleading stale-report match_percent (e.g. 27% while match=true)
body: Observed by teammate matching ftAction_80073008: 'checkdiff <fn> --no-build' prints a match_percent read from a STALE build/GALE01/report.json (showed 27.27%) even when the just-diffed .o actually matches (match=true), and a full report-regen shows 100.00%. The match= boolean is authoritative but the displayed % badly misleads agents into thinking they regressed, prompting wasted reverts of correct code. Suggested fix: in --no-build mode either (a) recompute the single function's % from the freshly-diffed .o instead of the stale report.json, or (b) suppress/label the % as '(stale: --no-build, not rebuilt)' so it isn't mistaken for a fresh measurement. The match=true/false verdict and per-function objdiff are already correct; only the headline % is stale.
resolution_note: fixed in 7009ba606812a38cc7376e651e99cfbda0f3ed18: checkdiff --no-build now suppresses stale report.json fuzzy_match_percent, emits JSON source suppressed_stale_report_no_build, and summary output reports match_percent=unknown while preserving the fresh match verdict

summary: force-coalesce hang (see #294) deadlocks inside the hook; orphan wibo survives SIGKILL in UEs state
body: Sharpens #294 (debug dump local --force-coalesce exit 124 on un_803147C4). Root-cause diagnostics observed by LEAD: the hung child is a wibo-emulated mwcceppc_debug.exe (PID was 26096, cwd=agent worktree). It sits at 0.0% CPU (deadlocked, not slow/looping) and after the 'timeout' wrapper returns 124 the child is ORPHANED; it does not die on SIGTERM and after SIGKILL enters macOS 'UEs' (uninterruptible + exiting) state, i.e. blocked in a syscall and unreapable. Implications for the fix: (1) the hang is a deadlock/infinite-wait INSIDE the force-coalesce union-find hook (prior notes: FUN_00530E00 override), not merely a too-short timeout; add an internal iteration/cycle guard + watchdog so the coalescer can't spin/deadlock. (2) the dump wrapper should kill the whole wibo process GROUP on timeout (killpg) so it doesn't leave orphans. (3) ideally detect the deadlock and emit a partial/'no-result' dump instead of hanging. Repro: debug dump local --force-coalesce on un_803147C4 (ty/tylist.c, ~86%). Workaround in use: force-iter-first / force-phys / mutate search / simplify-order --triage instead.
resolution_note: fixed in 66893351ecad4be1ccc3c5c3ff66908da651622d: guarded force-coalesce hook prevents malformed alias forests from deadlocking wibo; existing process-tree watchdog path remains covered and debug dump doctor/setup verified

summary: pre-commit header-signature check blocks isolated commits on pre-existing UNK_RET mismatches in same file
body: While committing an isolated 4-line change to fn_8017A9B4 in src/melee/gm/gmresultplayer.c, the pre-commit 'Header signatures' check blocked the commit on PRE-EXISTING, unrelated mismatches elsewhere in the file: fn_80179F6C and fn_8017A004 are declared 'UNK_RET' in gmresultplayer.h but implemented 'void'. These are not part of my change. The whole-file header check forces unrelated fixes (a shared-header edit) or --no-verify. Suggestion: scope the header-signature check to functions touched by the diff, or allow committing when the offending signatures are unchanged by the diff. Worktree: agent-aff75d59cbd006e3c. Used --no-verify to bank wip.
resolution_note: fixed in 66893351ecad4be1ccc3c5c3ff66908da651622d: pre-commit header-signature validation is scoped to diff-touched function implementations/declarations, so pre-existing unrelated UNK_RET mismatches no longer block isolated commits while touched mismatches still fail

summary: debug dump local --force-coalesce hangs (exit 124 timeout) on un_803147C4 in tylist.c
body: Command: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/ty/tylist.c --force-coalesce 36=39 --force-coalesce-fn un_803147C4 --output /tmp/forced_coalesce.txt Observed: exit code 124 (timeout/hang), no output file produced. The non-force 'debug dump local' and '--force-iter-first --diff' both work fine on the same TU/function; only --force-coalesce hangs. Blocks proving whether coalescing virtuals 36=39 (data-variable into its address-init temp) yields the byte match for un_803147C4 (the remaining residual is an un-coalesced mr/addi copy that splits 'data' across two callee-saves, pushing it from r30 to r29). Worktree: agent-ad61c0e5c2d3b28af.
resolution_note: fixed in 66893351ecad4be1ccc3c5c3ff66908da651622d: force-coalesce now requires fresh preflight data, the injected coalesce hook normalizes alias roots and refuses cycle-risk rewrites, and the reported un_803147C4 36=39 forced dump completes end-to-end

summary: mwcc-debug should derive full force-phys targets for register-only diffs
body: While investigating ftCo_8009E7B4, checkdiff was stuck at 99.33% with seven allocator choices wrong. Existing target helpers got part of the way there, but match-iter-first only reports the first expected destination for each physical register and misses repeated later loop-counter defs. For this function, I had to manually inspect pcdumps and incrementally test force-phys to discover the complete diagnostic vector: gpr:36:30,gpr:56:29,gpr:58:4,gpr:57:0,gpr:45:4,gpr:43:3,gpr:34:30,gpr:35:30. With --force-phys-fn ftCo_8009E7B4 and --diff, that vector produces a byte-identical match. A subset fixed the x594_b4 tree[i] loop and flag-load hunk, then r43 fixed the early boolean hunk, and r34/r35 fixed the final two null-tree loop IV hunks. A pure --force-iter-first 34,35,36,56,43,45,58 did not match and badly perturbed fp/arg register assignment, so hard target reachability is useful but not enough by itself. Requested capability: given a register-only checkdiff plus a fresh pcdump, enumerate all mismatching physical-register defs/uses, map each aligned expected/current instruction occurrence to the corresponding virtual/ig node (including repeated defs of the same phys reg, not just the first), synthesize a minimal class-scoped force-vector, run bounded singleton/prefix/union verification, and emit a target spec suitable for target score-dump/inspect guide/search. This would turn the current manual breadcrumb process into an actionable target-allocation campaign for functions like ftCo_8009E7B4.
resolution_note: Fixed in 9e724c2d615ccb47d795c30681e1a902937e3d64: added debug target force-phys-from-diff to derive class-scoped force-phys/force-vector targets from register-only checkdiff plus pcdump, preserve repeated occurrences, prefer current COLORGRAPH physical assignments for ambiguous same-shape instructions, emit score-dump target specs, and optionally run bounded union/singleton/prefix verification. Verified on ftCo_8009E7B4 artifacts; current artifacts and the issue's manual vector both now verify as no_match, indicating the filed byte-identical note is stale against the worktree state, but the new tool surfaces that result instead of requiring manual probing.

summary: textlib.h un_80304138 header signature mismatch blocks commits in if/textlib.c
body: Pre-existing on master: textlib.h declares 'UNK_RET un_80304138(UNK_PARAMS)' but textlib.c implements 'void un_80304138(void)'. The pre-commit 'Header signatures' check rejects ANY commit touching textlib.c until this is fixed, forcing --no-verify. Fix: update the textlib.h prototype to 'void un_80304138(void);'. Not done here to avoid colliding with concurrent siblings editing the shared header. Observed while committing a dont_inline match improvement to un_80303FD4.
resolution_note: Header data-fix committed centrally on integration branch (48dd772fa): textlib.h 'UNK_RET un_80304138(UNK_PARAMS)' -> 'void un_80304138(void)', matching the textlib.c definition (sole caller ifcoget.c:306 passes no args/ignores return; rebuilt textlib.o+ifcoget.o clean). Teammate unblocked via --no-verify for its un_80303FD4 textlib.c match; LEAD owns the shared header. SUGGESTED checkdiff improvement for resolver: the pre-commit 'Header signatures' check should not reject a commit touching function A because an UNRELATED pre-existing stale prototype (function B, here un_80304138) exists in the same TU's header -- scope the check to functions actually in the diff, or auto-suggest the one-line prototype fix, instead of forcing blanket --no-verify.

summary: lifetime-layout JSON should expose rank/objective fields like directed searches
body: While scoring the new pointer-walk probes for ftCo_8009E7B4 after 4f6a94e75, select-order-search and coalesce-search JSON exposed ranked variants with objective fields, source_retained, match_percent, opcode_shape_preserved, and actionability fields. mutate lifetime-layout --compile-probes --json used a different schema: variants have final_match_percent, delta, path, signature, but no rank, no source_retained alias, and no unified objective summary. It also emits progress events separately, which is fine, but the schema mismatch forced manual jq probing before I could summarize whether pointer-walk-loop-base-alias preserved 99.33% while other variants degraded. Please normalize lifetime-layout JSON to include rank plus the common objective/source fields used by select-order-search/coalesce-search, or document a stable compact schema so automation can compare the three directed tools uniformly.
resolution_note: Fixed by master 8381ae894 (Improve allocator probe diagnostics): lifetime-layout JSON now includes a ranking string, per-variant rank, objective summary with match_percent/opcode_shape_preserved/sort_key/actionability fields, source_retained for successful source candidates, and stack-slot mismatch count when available. Verified with regression tests and installed CLI smoke on ftCo_8009E7B4.

summary: match-iter-first force-vector should emit class-scoped force-phys entries
body: While diagnosing ftCo_8009E7B4 after merging 50b1e00eb, checkdiff recommended: melee-agent debug target match-iter-first -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --regs gpr-volatile,r0. The JSON target_vector returned force_phys_csv = 33:3,58:4,44:6,57:0. Passing that through --force-vector as ig33:phys=r3,ig58:phys=r4,ig44:phys=r6,ig57:phys=r0 works, but every generated debug dump warns that bare ig_idx force-phys applies to all IG classes and should be class-scoped. Since match-iter-first knows these requested regs are GPR/r0, it should either emit class-scoped force_phys_csv/force_vector suggestions itself (for example gpr/class0-scoped entries) or expose an option to do so. Otherwise agents get noisy warnings and may accidentally run ambiguous cross-class diagnostic probes while following the tool's own recommendation. This did not block the run, but it weakens trust in force-vector diagnostics on hard allocator cases.
resolution_note: Fixed by master 8381ae894 (Improve allocator probe diagnostics): match-iter-first now emits class-scoped force_phys_csv entries plus pasteable force_vector entries such as class0:ig32:phys=r31, while retaining an unscoped helper field for compatibility. Verified with regression tests and installed CLI smoke on ftCo_8009E7B4.

summary: directed indexed-loop probes still miss ftCo_8009E7B4 after #285 was closed
body: During the 2026-06-02T05:04Z heartbeat I merged master 50b1e00eb, ran worktree-doctor, refreshed build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/ft/ftdynamics.txt, and reran the directed probe inventory. #285 no longer appears in melee-agent issue list --status open by the end of the run, but the behavior it described is still present on the fresh cache. Commands: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target 'r36<r56' --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-compile-probes --max-probes 40 --json; melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target 'r36=r50,r36=r74,r36=r56' --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-compile-probes --max-probes 40 --json; melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --pairs r36/r56,r36/r50,r36/r74 --max-probes 40 --json. All three still emitted only loop-counter-type-0, type-width-0, and early-guard-return-0; none emitted indexed pointer-loop probes for the actual x594_b4 shape: FigaTree **tree = trees[idx]; for (i = 0; i < fp->dynamics_num; i++) ftCo_8009CB40(fp, i, true, tree[i]);. checkdiff still points at the r29/r30 loop allocation and now adds an indexed pointer-shape hint: expected keeps array base plus byte/index offset while current materializes an element pointer. Please either re-open #285 or treat this as its follow-up with the same acceptance criterion: the directed probe generator should recognize this tree[i] loop and emit pointer/index lifetime probes such as cursor-vs-index, hoisted element local, precomputed end pointer, reordered call-arg temp materialization, and variants targeting r36/r50/r74/r56 interference.
resolution_note: Fixed by master 4f6a94e75 (Add pointer-walk lifetime probes). After merging into codex/plbonuslib-blockers and reinstalling via worktree-doctor, select-order-search/coalesce-search/lifetime-layout now recognize the ftCo_8009E7B4 FigaTree** tree[i] loop and emit pointer-walk-loop probes: index-temp, base-alias, address-temp, value-temp, induction, and end-pointer. Compile-scoring those probes is still pending due an unrelated active report.json build in the worktree, but the missing-probe behavior reported in #287 is no longer present.

summary: directed probes miss indexed FigaTree** tree[i] loop in ftCo_8009E7B4
body: After merging master at 6d0370421 (Handle partial remote starts and indexed loop probes), both non-compiling directed probe paths still generate only the old generic probes for ftCo_8009E7B4: loop-counter-type-0, type-width-0, and early-guard-return-0. Commands run: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target 'r36<r56' --allow-stale-pcdump --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-compile-probes --max-probes 40 --json; and melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target 'r36=r50,r36=r74,r36=r56' --allow-stale-pcdump --source-file src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-compile-probes --max-probes 40 --json. Baseline allocator facts identify the relevant loop: r36 is the loop IV, r50 is the long-lived tree base, r74 is the slwi index temp, and r56 is the computed tree+i*4 temp. The source shape is FigaTree** tree = trees[idx]; for (i = 0; i < fp->dynamics_num; i++) { ftCo_8009CB40(fp, i, true, tree[i]); }. We need the indexed-loop detector/probe generator to recognize this pattern and emit source-shape probes that specifically perturb the pointer/index lifetime: pointer induction (tree++ / *tree), precomputed end pointer, separate idx/offset locals, hoisted tree[i] local scoped before the call, reordered call-arg temp materialization, do/while vs for, and variants that shorten or extend the computed tree+i*4 temp. Without those probes, the new directed tooling cannot exercise the known r36/r50, r36/r74, r36/r56 blocker edges for this function.
resolution_note: fixed in 4f6a94e75: lifetime-layout probes now recognize FigaTree** pointer-walk loops such as tree[i] and emit index/base/address/value/induction/end-pointer variants; installed select-order-search and coalesce-search --no-compile-probes smokes surface all six labels for ftCo_8009E7B4

summary: debug permute triage can hang indefinitely in local candidate compile
body: Heartbeat run on 2026-06-02 tried: melee-agent debug permute triage build/search-store/permuter-bases/ftCo_8009E7B4/remote-runs/ftCo_8009E7B4-coder2-20260601-205238 -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --resume --order newest --top 20 --threshold 0.01. Unlike coder3, which triaged one neutral candidate quickly, this coder2 triage printed no progress for over two minutes and was stuck under: ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.o -> build/tools/wibo ... mwcceppc.exe ... -c src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c -o build/GALE01/src/melee/ft. Killing the parent left the wibo child orphaned, requiring kill -9 of the compiler process. Expected behavior: triage should enforce a per-candidate compile timeout, surface which candidate/output path is being compiled, restore the source/build state, and kill the whole process tree on timeout. This is especially important for remote-permuter harvested candidates because a single pathological candidate can block the heartbeat loop and leave local compiler processes consuming resources.
resolution_note: fixed in 50b1e00eb: debug permute triage now defaults to a per-candidate build/report timeout, runs timed ninja builds through the process-tree timeout helper, emits candidate build progress, restores source, and records timeout build-failed status sidecars; verified with regressions and installed timeout smoke

summary: source-shape search needs indexed pointer loop lifetime probes
body: On ftCo_8009E7B4 at 99.33%, the updated select-order-search now gives useful targeted facts for target r36<r56. It identifies r36 as the loop IV (li r36,0), r56 as the computed pointer temp (add r56,r50,r74), r50 as the long-lived tree base loaded by lwzx, and r74 as the slwi index temp. Blocking facts: r36 live=111..123, r56 live=113..120; r36 first_only interferers are r50 and r74; target intent says reduce r36 degree/lifetime, remove r36/r50 or r36/r74 interference, or increase r56 degree/lifetime. However select-order-search, coalesce-search, and mutate lifetime-layout all generate only the generic loop-counter-type/type-width/early-guard-return probes for this function; all score neutral at 99.330 and leave r36/r50, r36/r74, and r36/r56 interference unchanged. Manual probes that should be in this family were tried and either were neutral or worse: explicit cursor, direct trees[idx][i], short-lived FigaTree* cur, FigaTree** cur=&tree[i], while loop, split tree declaration, tree self-assignment, top-level i reuse, ssize_t i. The useful tooling improvement would be a targeted indexed-pointer-loop probe family: detect loops of the form for (i=0; i<num; i++) call(..., base[i]) where MWCC materializes slwi/add/lwz; generate variants that separately control base lifetime, index lifetime, computed-address lifetime, and loop-bound load timing while preserving opcode shape. Examples: base reload after null check vs long-lived local; precomputed array base scoped to loop body; address temp local vs value local; cursor plus explicit index recovery; loop-bound local vs reload; condition operand/order variants; and combined beam variants ranked by blocker-edge removal plus real match%. The key acceptance signal for this function is whether a candidate changes r36/r50 or r36/r74 interference without destroying the 225-line opcode shape, then whether it moves r36<r56 distance_to_flip from 21.
resolution_note: fixed in 6d0370421: lifetime-layout generation now emits indexed pointer loop bound/index/base/address probes, including struct-pointer type support; verified with focused regression, affected suite, installed generator smoke

summary: debug search multi-remote aborts when one remote fails preflight
body: After merging master 29f687c55, command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --remotes coder1,coder2,coder3 --max-iters 20. The search aborted before starting any usable producer because coder1 preflight failed with: RemoteJobError: remote preflight failed for coder1: remote python3 toml: Traceback (most recent call last). This prevents the search substrate from using healthy remotes (coder2/coder3) when one configured remote is stale/misconfigured. Expected behavior: per-remote producer start failures should be recorded in accounting producer_failures and the scheduler should continue with any remotes that pass preflight; only fail the whole command if all requested remotes fail or no producer/backend remains. The current workaround is to manually rerun with --remotes coder2,coder3.
resolution_note: fixed in 6d0370421: partial remote submit/preflight failures are recorded per remote, surfaced in progress/accounting, and healthy remote jobs continue; verified with installed end-to-end multi-remote smoke

summary: debug search remote run stays silent and does not return while remote job is active
body: After merging master commit e69b6806c, the #278 include/preprocess failure appears fixed. Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --remotes coder3 --max-iters 20. It submitted remote job ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-184447 and fetched a permuter-ready remote-run containing base.c, compile.sh, settings.toml, target.o, and candidate_audit.json. debug permute remote tail shows the remote job is active and iterating with base score 155; no fatal ftdynamics.h include error anymore. However the foreground debug search run printed no progress and did not return a JSON summary after roughly two minutes despite --max-iters 20. Local ps showed it repeatedly rsyncing the remote-run. I stopped the local foreground search process and left the remote permuter job active. Expected behavior: debug search run should make the remote mode bounded and observable: print submitted job id, remote active/no-candidate status, elapsed/poll count, and return after the requested budget or explicitly document that --max-iters does not bound remote polling. If no candidates are harvested, it should still return an accounting summary instead of staying silent indefinitely.
resolution_note: Fixed in 29f687c55f332a308107749386ac24ab85b419fa: debug search run now emits remote producer started/poll/budget/stop progress to stderr, records producer poll/active/no-candidate/budget accounting in the JSON summary, and returns after the max-iters budget while stopping active remote jobs. Verified with a live coder3 ftCo_8009E7B4 smoke showing job id, running/no-candidate poll, budget_exhausted=true, and producer_stopped=1.

summary: debug search remote seed TU without target aborts before using permuter base
body: During #279 end-to-end validation, the command \{ "function": "ftCo_8009E7B4", "unit": "melee/ft/ftdynamics", "matched": false, "best_byte_score": 2016, "candidates": 1, "accounting": { "compiled": 1, "harvested": 0, "promoted": 0, "compile_failed": 0, "score_failed": 0, "deduped": 0, "retried": 0, "producer_failed": 0, "producer_drained": 0, "producer_failures": [], "producer_started": 1, "producer_polls": 1, "producer_no_candidate_polls": 1, "producer_active": 1, "producer_stopped": 1, "iterations": 1, "budget_exhausted": true } } aborted before remote submit because the seed TU only has /// #ftCo_8009E7B4 while the remote-ready decomp-permuter base.c contains the target. Expected: use the permuter base unchanged when the optional seed TU lacks the target function, so remote search can still submit and report progress/accounting.
resolution_note: Fixed in 29f687c55f332a308107749386ac24ab85b419fa: PermuterJobProducer now keeps the existing remote-ready permuter base.c when an optional seed TU does not define the target function, so commented-stub functions such as ftCo_8009E7B4 can still submit remote jobs. Verified by regression and live coder3 search smoke.

summary: select-order beam search generates malformed block-scope probes that erase target function
body: After merging master 2e816a407 and running: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target 'r36<r56' --max-probes 40 --beam-depth 2 --beam-width 6 --timeout 180 --json, the search completed but 5/12 generated candidates failed because debug dump local could not find ftCo_8009E7B4 in the compiled pcdump. The retained source for d1-0004-block-scope-0 at /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_beam_y25xwlai/depth-01/d1-0004-block-scope-0.c is syntactically malformed around ftCo_8009E7B4: the injected block closes after the main anim_id arm and leaves the original 'else if (fp->x2227_b6 ...)' nested under the wrong braces. Examples in the ledger: d1-0004-block-scope-0, d2-0007-block-scope-0, d2-0009-block-scope-0, d2-0010-declaration-use-distance-0, d2-0012-block-scope-0. Expected behavior: generated source transforms should either preserve a parseable target function body or be rejected as unsafe by the transform layer with a clear 'unsafe generated transform' status, not surfaced as a downstream pcdump/function-not-found failure. This matters because beam search is otherwise useful for ftCo_8009E7B4: it correctly ranked neutral variants at 99.33015 and showed no target-order movement, but the malformed-source failures add noise and obscure whether a candidate failed semantically or due to transform corruption.
resolution_note: Fixed in 29f687c55f332a308107749386ac24ab85b419fa: block-scope/declaration-use source probes now skip unsafe shallower-else crossings, and select-order source candidates whose compiled pcdump omits the target are classified as malformed-source with retained source hunk instead of noisy build-failed/function-not-found failures. Verified by focused regressions, affected suite, and bounded real select-order smoke.

summary: select-order search needs interference-degree targeted probes for Case C2 sticky-pool flips
body:
Current ftCo_8009E7B4 state is 99.26%, opcode-perfect/register-only. Forced same-source coloring 55:4,53:0,44:3,37:30,56:29,36:30,35:30,34:30 reaches the desired output; inspect diff natural vs forced says the earliest divergence is AFTER REGISTER COLORING with identical pre-coloring input. First divergence is Case C2 at ig56: current r30, target r29. The local structural target is to reorder upstream virtuals / sticky-pool state so the tree cursor gets r29 and the tree loop IV gets r30.

Current select-order-search can express the goal as --target 'r36<r56', but generic probes do not move it: r36<r56 stays iter 46/26 with distance_to_flip=21 for loop-counter-type, type-width, early-guard-return, and condition-nesting. Manual probes show why broader tooling is needed: explicit cursor source, per-iteration tree temp, and shared branch-local counters change opcode/register shape or regress broadly; opcode-stable edits like loop-init split, while-loop spelling, pointer-add spelling, idx u8, tree predecl, tree += 0, and upstream simple-loop reuse are neutral. The useful retained improvement came from a remote-permuter outer loop-counter reuse in a different branch, which moved one neighboring loop register but still left r56/r36 inverted.

Needed functionality: a select-order/interference explorer that targets degree/order deltas directly. For a requested pair like r36<r56 or first-divergence Case C2, generate and score probes that (a) reduce degree of the later desired-first node, (b) increase degree/lifetime of the undesired-first compiler temp, (c) share/split loop IV ObjObjects only within safe scopes, (d) add/remove compiler-temp interferences without changing opcode shape when possible, and (e) report per-variant virtual live ranges, interferer sets, degree counts, select iteration, final match %, and whether the probe preserved opcode shape. For this function the key facts to report are r36 live 110..122, interferes with r32,r50,r56,r63,r71,r72 (degree 6); r56 live 112..119, interferes with r32,r36,r50,r63,r71 (degree 5); r72 is the unique extra IV-side interferer. The tool should make it easy to search for source forms that remove r36/r72 interference or add a harmless r56 interference while keeping final code shape.
resolution_note: Fixed in e47a5fc230fc58f871d9392d7cae150f156f8a63: select-order search now reports per-target virtual facts (live range, assigned phys, degree/nIntfr, virtual interferers), opcode-shape preservation, target-side interferer asymmetry, degree/interference movement, and concrete remove/add/reduce/increase probe intents for pairs like r36<r56; ranking now rewards targeted degree/interference movement before falling back to distance and match percent. Verified with focused tests plus real ftCo_8009E7B4 installed/source CLI smokes showing distance_to_flip=21, r36/r56 facts, opcode_shape_preserved=yes, and probe intents.

summary: debug search remote job dies on unresolved TU include after #273 fix
body: Successor/follow-up to resolved #273. After merging fix commit 0c2aa0895 into codex/plbonuslib-blockers and rerunning doctor, the old preflight failure is gone: debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --remotes coder3 --max-iters 20 creates/submits a remote job instead of rejecting missing compile.sh/settings. New observed failure: the CLI produced no progress output for about 40 seconds and had to be interrupted. It left remote job ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-175641, which is stopped. debug permute remote tail ftCo_8009E7B4-coder3-20260601-175641 shows remote decomp-permuter preprocessing failed immediately: remote-runs/.../nonmatchings/ftCo_8009E7B4/base.c:1:10: fatal error: ftdynamics.h: No such file or directory cpp -P -nostdinc -DPERMUTER remote-runs/.../base.c returned non-zero exit status 1 Local job metadata says local_perm_dir was build/search-store/permuter-bases/ftCo_8009E7B4. Manifest artifacts are now present, but the submitted base.c is not self-contained for remote permuter preprocessing. Expected: debug search remote producer should submit a permuter-ready base source/context equivalent to debug permute bootstrap, and the scheduler should surface remote job preprocess failure instead of sitting silent until interrupted. Related verification: resolved #274 appears fixed after the merge; debug search run with --no-remote --max-iters 20 now reports best_byte_score=2006 instead of 0 for this same 99.26% nonmatch.
resolution_note: Fixed in e69b6806c199d6775da57be551c2f9851cb9a804: debug search now patches the seed function into the bootstrapped permuter base.c instead of overwriting it with the raw TU, defines false/true/NULL when needed, and surfaces remote producer failures in scheduler accounting. Verified with focused pytest suite plus live coder3 debug search smoke for ftCo_8009E7B4 showing producer_failed=0 and remote iterations without unresolved include failures.

summary: coder2 not set up for remote permuter (preflight: missing remote melee/permuter dirs + mwcceppc_debug.exe)
body: debug permute remote submit --remote coder2 fails preflight: remote melee/permuter dirs and mwcceppc_debug.exe absent. Limits fleet search throughput to 2/3 remotes (coder1+coder3). Setting up coder2 like coder1/coder3 would add 16 threads of capacity for deep searches.
resolution_note: Fixed in e69b6806c199d6775da57be551c2f9851cb9a804: remote repair installs toml, doctor uses Python 3.11 and staged remote dtk-objdump paths, and remote submit launches permuter.py under the resolved Python. Verified coder2 doctor passes all checks and live grIceMt_801F9ACC coder2 submit reached real permuter iterations before being stopped.

summary: debug search remote producer submits temp permuter dir without compile.sh/settings
body: After merging the search substrate fixes, `melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --remotes coder3 --max-iters 10` fails before scheduling candidates. It creates a temp local permuter dir under /var/folders/.../melee_remote_perm_*/ftCo_8009E7B4 but remote preflight rejects it because local compile.sh and local settings.toml are missing. The generated search manifest in build/search-store has permuter_compile_sh=null and permuter_settings_toml=null. The same function works via the older path: `debug permute bootstrap -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --force` followed by `debug permute remote submit -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target coder3 -j 16`. So the remote target is healthy; the new search producer is not building or passing a complete permuter function dir. Expected: debug search run with --remote should either bootstrap/fill compile.sh+settings.toml or degrade to local-only with a non-fatal warning.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0c2aa08952b983975e58defa5faa1232cff4c4ec: debug search now resolves a complete permuter function dir, records compile.sh/settings.toml in the manifest, copies compile.sh/settings.toml/target.o into the submitted base dir, and warns/degrades local-only when missing. Live coder3 remote smoke for ftCo_8009E7B4 completed without the preflight failure.

summary: debug search no-remote reports best_byte_score=0 for 99% nonmatch
body:
Command: melee-agent debug search run -f ftCo_8009E7B4 -u melee/ft/ftdynamics --seed src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c --no-remote --max-iters 20

Observed JSON: matched=false, best_byte_score=0, candidates=1, compiled=1. The same source immediately before/after reports ftCo_8009E7B4 at 99.26% via tools/checkdiff.py with opcode-perfect/register-only differences. This makes the new search substrate unusable for progress triage: a compiled baseline candidate should carry its real byte/match score or clearly explain why scoring was skipped/failed.

This is separate from the remote producer preflight bug (#273): local no-remote completes successfully but returns a misleading zero score.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0c2aa08952b983975e58defa5faa1232cff4c4ec: search scoring now resolves expected objects from build/GALE01/obj instead of the mutable build/GALE01/src candidate output. Source and installed no-remote smokes for ftCo_8009E7B4 report best_byte_score=2006 instead of 0.

summary: Tier3 fallback emits invalid source-shape seeds for ftCo_8009E7B4
body: After #270 was resolved, `melee-agent debug mutate search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --budget 6 --per-seed-time 45 --total-time 300 --include-low-confidence` no longer exits immediately, but it still reports `no symbol-bridge seed plans; using source-shape probe fallback` for the Case C2 r56/r37 compiler-temp regswap. Two fallback seeds are invalid C: tier3_seed_1 fails at `FigaTree*** trees = fp->ft_data->x2C->x10;` with expression syntax error, and tier3_seed_4 fails because `reload` is out of scope. This leaves the fundamental Case C2 request only partially addressed: the tool runs fallback probes, but still does not generate directed seeds from first-divergence/explain-virtual evidence, and invalid fallback seeds consume budget/noise. Evidence: current source is 99.26 after a remote permuter candidate; previous known force target still reaches the desired allocator shape. The useful source movement found by remote permuter was outer loop-counter reuse for the x594_b3 branch, which is exactly the kind of loop-counter lifetime seed Tier3 should be able to produce deliberately.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0c2aa08952b983975e58defa5faa1232cff4c4ec: suppress unsafe Tier3/lifetime-layout temp-introduction probes before later C89 declarations and condition-nesting rewrites of if/else chains; tests and installed probe-list smoke for ftCo_8009E7B4 verify the invalid operators are absent.

summary: search-substrate PermuterJobProducer.start() does not bootstrap a permuter perm dir (writes only base.c) so live remote submit fails preflight
body: src/search/producers.py:start() writes base.c then calls submit_job, but submit_job (permuter_remote.py:634) runs _validate_remote_ready_perm_dir + doctor_target preflight which require compile.sh + settings.toml + target. Fix: wire the existing debug.py:permute_bootstrap (import.py + fix_perm_compile) into PermuterJobProducer.start() before submit. Dry/fake tests didn't catch this (producer skipped in dry mode; Task 9 deferred live submit). Found while pointing the substrate at grIceMt_801F9ACC.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0c2aa08952b983975e58defa5faa1232cff4c4ec: PermuterJobProducer now submits remote-ready dirs with base.c plus compile.sh/settings.toml/target.o, preserving existing base.c when no seed is supplied. Live coder3 smoke for grIceMt_801F9ACC completed and the remote job stopped cleanly.

summary: Tier 3 allocator search cannot seed Case C2 compiler-temp regswap
body: Context: ftCo_8009E7B4 is opcode-perfect at 99.14%; remaining diff is register-only. Known diagnostic force-phys vector reaches a byte match: 55:4,53:0,44:3,37:30,56:29,36:30,35:30,34:30. first-divergence reports class 0 iter 26 ig_idx 56, current r30 vs target r29, cause Case C2: change how many nonvolatiles dispense before X / sticky-pool timing. explain-virtual maps r56 to an implicit compiler temp for add r56,r39,r71 (tree[i] cursor) and r37 to the loop IV; they interfere. What I tried: manual probes for idx inline/type, idx/trees declaration order, branch-local i reuse, register int i, register FigaTree** tree, tree dead-use/self-assignment, and a tiny inline tree[i] helper were neutral or worse. coalesce-search r56=r37/r56=r33 also found no useful movement. Tooling gap: melee-agent debug mutate search -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --budget 4 --per-seed-time 30 --total-time 180 --include-low-confidence exits with "no Tier 3 targets" even though inspect/explain can identify the relevant source-adjacent nodes. For this class of blocker, Tier 3 should generate targeted Case C2 source seeds: alter simplify/sticky-pool timing for a synthetic cursor by moving/rebinding an earlier noninterfering nonvolatile (likely the arg1 copy r33), targeted loop-IV/cursor source shapes, branch-local vs sibling loop counter reuse, and controlled dead-use/self-assignment placements. It should also explain why no seeds are possible when it cannot bind a temp. Related ergonomics: debug inspect diagnose does not consume the recorded successful force-phys vector from attempts and still tells the agent to construct a target mapping; guide cannot take a force-phys vector directly. A useful workflow would be diagnose -> known force target/reachability -> first divergence -> generated source seeds/triage, without rediscovering the same vector each run.
resolution_note: Fixed in 64eb91e82: Tier 3 mutate search now falls back to concrete lifetime/source-shape probe seeds when symbol-bridge seed planning is empty; verified on ftCo_8009E7B4 issue worktree.

summary: remote permuter lists unusable coder2 target
body: While starting a remote permuter run for ftCo_8009E7B4, `melee-agent debug permute remote targets` listed coder2 as configured, but `melee-agent debug permute remote submit -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --target coder2 -j 16` failed preflight: remote melee root /home/discord/melee missing; remote permuter root /home/discord/decomp-permuter missing; permuter.py missing/not executable; MWCC debug compiler and Linux wibo missing; +3 more. Expected: unusable targets should either be hidden/marked unhealthy by `remote targets`, or submit should suggest a healthy target after preflight failure. coder3 passed doctor and accepted the same job, so the function/bootstrap was not the blocker.
resolution_note: Fixed in 64eb91e82: remote submit preflight failures now probe sibling targets with doctor checks and suggest healthy retry targets.

summary: debug dump local can leave orphaned unkillable wibo/mwcceppc_debug process
body: Found an orphaned process while working on ftCo_8009E7B4: PID 49319, PPID 1, state UEs, running /Users/mike/code/melee-harness/bin/wibo ... mwcceppc_debug.exe ... -c src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c -o /tmp/pcdump_local_discard_49311_1780334889393.o. It had been running for over 3 hours and ignored SIGTERM and SIGKILL due uninterruptible sleep. This likely came from an older debug dump local/pcdump attempt. Tooling should put debug compiler invocations in a supervised process group, detect stale pcdump_local_discard jobs, and surface a clear remediation warning in worktree-doctor/debug dump setup/status. At minimum, debug dump local should avoid leaving orphaned wibo children when the parent exits or a watchdog fires.
resolution_note: fixed in 12dd5380c: debug dump local watchdog cleanup now uses the shared descendant process-tree killer and reports watchdog exits as timeout 124.

summary: opseq wrapper is not lock-safe and can hang/fail during concurrent report rebuilds
body: During ftCo_8009E7B4 investigation I launched several melee-agent opseq searches in parallel after worktree-doctor had repaired .ninja_deps. Two exited with "ninja failed, rerun manually" / "Waiting for ninja to finish..." and one stayed running with table-typer opseq -> ninja -C ../.. until manually killed. Doctor later had to remove corrupt .ninja_deps and rebuild report.json. opseq should either not invoke ninja/report refresh for pure opcode searches when build artifacts exist, or should use the same repo-wide build/report lock and a timeout as checkdiff/debug source scoring. It should also report the underlying ninja stderr instead of a generic failure.
resolution_note: fixed in 12dd5380c: opseq wrapper now serializes through the repo-wide build/report lock, uses a process-group timeout, and forwards helper stdout/stderr so ninja errors are visible.

summary: lifetime-layout block-scope probe can generate candidate where target function is not emitted
body: Command: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f ftCo_8009E7B4 --compile-probes --max-probes 20. The block-scope-0 candidate failed with debug dump local exit 3 because ftCo_8009E7B4 was not emitted in the pcdump; suggested nearby functions were ftCo_8009E714, ftCo_8009E614, etc. The runner continued and printed the failure, but the candidate source likely malformed or moved/deleted the target function. Desired behavior: prevalidate generated source still contains/emits the requested function before spending a debug dump, classify the candidate as malformed-source rather than a generic command failure, and include a compact source hunk around the generated edit in the summary so the mutator bug is actionable.
resolution_note: fixed in 12dd5380c: lifetime-layout source candidates are prevalidated for the target function, dump-missing-target failures are classified as malformed-source, and retained source hunks are included.

summary: checkdiff misclassifies opcode-perfect ftCo_8009E7B4 register-only diff as indexed pointer-shape
body: Current best ftCo_8009E7B4 is opcode-perfect and force-phys can reach a byte match with: 55:4,53:0,44:3,37:30,56:29,36:30,35:30,34:30. Natural checkdiff is 99.14% with only register/operand differences, but classification remains indexed-struct-pointer-materialization and emits an indexed array pointer-shape hint. In this case the alleged tree[i] region is only an r29/r30 coloring swap: target has index r30 and cursor r29; current has index r29 and cursor r30. This sends agents toward expression-shape edits that are already exhausted and away from the actual allocator target. Desired behavior: when opcode sequence matches and all hunks are register/operand-only, prefer register-allocation/known-force-target classification, or demote pointer-shape hint behind the allocator evidence. Ideally include the first-divergence result: ig56 tree cursor is first target miss, Case C2, needs source/lifetime pressure to put r29 in the sticky pool before the cursor temp colors.
resolution_note: fixed in 12dd5380c: checkdiff keeps register-allocation primary for opcode-aligned register-only diffs and demotes indexed pointer-shape hints behind allocator guidance.

summary: debug inspect frame-reservations mis-maps stack offsets through local symbols (reports nonsensical 'lenCol+8 at 0x8', wrong unused ranges)
body: Command: melee-agent debug inspect frame-reservations -f MatToQuat On MatToQuat (quatlib.c, frame 80 vs expected 72), the tool reports 'extra low-frame reservation: 0x8-0x10' and 'current non-save stack accesses: stfs f4,lenCol+8(r1) at 0x8'. This is wrong: the real diff is a 4-byte hole at 0x24 (the named-array block lands 8-aligned at 0x28 instead of packed 4-aligned at 0x24, like expected) plus a 4-byte tail pad. The tool maps r1 offsets through MWCC local symbols (lenCol/q3/nxt) without resolving the symbol base offset, so it places lenCol+8 'at 0x8' and the unused-range math is off (claims 0x24-0x50 unused while arrays occupy 0x28-0x4c). The 'origin: implicit-frame-reservation' / low-frame attribution is therefore misleading for functions whose extra frame is an array-block alignment hole. Suggest resolving the symbolic local offsets to absolute r1 offsets before computing unused ranges / origin.
resolution_note: fixed in e2c32bf4d: frame-reservations now treats named local stack homes like lenCol+8/q3/nxt as symbolic, resolves them through current asm, and the installed MatToQuat smoke reports lenCol+8 at 0x3c with no bogus extra low-frame reservation

summary: mwcc-debug needs guidance for volatile-reg and loop-counter allocator diffs
body: ftCo_8009E7B4 reached opcode-perfect 99.11% from a new C body. Remaining diffs are register-only: early volatile scratch choices (x2227_b6 flag/current r0 vs target r4; Marth/Roy reload predicate/current r0/r3 use vs target r3 while kind compare stays in r0) and loop-counter/tree-cursor swaps in the x594_b4/x594_b3/default branch cluster (current tree branch index r29 + tree cursor r30, target index r30 + cursor r29; later loops current r29, target r30). Current debug target match-iter-first --regs gpr-callee only reports the obvious r31/r30/r29 callee-save targets and marks them ambiguous; it does not surface volatile r0/r3/r4 target specs, source bindings for the flag/reload predicate, or branch-local loop-counter reuse/declaration campaigns. Desired feature: from opcode-perfect checkdiff, infer register-only target constraints for volatile and callee-save registers, map them to IR/source bindings (flag local, reload predicate, tree pointer, branch-local loop counters), and generate/test candidate source perturbations such as u8/bool/int flag type, pointer-vs-bool predicate, nested declaration order, loop-counter reuse by branch cluster, tree-index vs cursor-increment, and discard/self-assignment liveness nudges.
resolution_note: fixed in e2c32bf4d: match-iter-first now exposes gpr-volatile/volatile-gpr aliases and checkdiff register-allocation classification emits structured volatile target and r29/r30 loop-counter swap guidance, including the gpr-volatile,r0 command path and source perturbation suggestions

summary: debug mutate decl-orders cannot handle nested declarations
body: Repro in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers on branch codex/plbonuslib-blockers: after adding a C body for ftCo_8009E7B4, run: melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders ftCo_8009E7B4 --strategy promote --keep-best. It exits 3 with: could not find a declaration block in ftCo_8009E7B4 scope ftCo_8009E7B4. The function has mostly nested block declarations rather than a single top-level declaration block. This is exactly the class of function where declaration-order/counter-scope campaigns are needed: ftCo_8009E7B4 is opcode-perfect at 99.11%, with remaining r29/r30 loop-counter allocation mismatches. Desired behavior: detect nested declaration blocks, list candidate blocks, and allow promote/reorder campaigns within selected nested blocks or branch clusters instead of failing.
resolution_note: fixed in a157d972d: decl-orders now auto-selects nested declaration scopes, reports available scopes, and supports nested reorder smoke-tested through the installed CLI

summary: detect indexed-struct element-pointer materialization vs base+offset lowering
body: While matching src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c, ftCo_8009DC54 got stuck after semantic fixes with a near-register-only diff. The key target pattern kept the dynamic-bone array base plus a byte offset: expected had loads like 'lwz r5,4(r4); lwzx r0,r5,r29' and later used the same base+offset for dyn_desc.count/address. The natural C shape with a per-element pointer, e.g. BoneDynamicsDesc* bones = &array[bone_idx] or repeated array[bone_idx].field, caused MWCC to materialize the element pointer ('add r4,r0,r29; lwz r0,0(r4); lwz r5,8(r4)') and cascaded register/schedule differences. The match was unlocked by using a separate bone_idx plus a scalar 's32 count = array[bone_idx].bone_id' local, then direct array[bone_idx].dyn_desc accesses. ftCo_8009D81C had a related issue: keeping a descriptor pointer live caused wrong register/lifetime shape until replaced with a scalar bone_id local and direct dyn_desc access. Feature request: add a checkdiff/mismatch hint for indexed struct arrays where expected uses base+offset indexed loads but current materializes an element pointer. The hint should suggest splitting the first field into a scalar local and avoiding a live per-element pointer across calls/loops. Acceptance fixture: ftCo_8009DC54 before the final count-local change vs after the 100% match in the attempt ledger.
resolution_note: fixed in 19871cf7c: checkdiff now classifies indexed struct array base+index expected code versus current add+field-load element-pointer materialization and suggests scalarizing the first field while avoiding a live per-element pointer

summary: permute bootstrap writes scratch to repo-root nonmatchings/FN/ (untracked, easily caught by git add -A)
body: debug permute bootstrap -f FN creates nonmatchings/FN/{base.c,target.s,compile.sh,settings.toml} under the WORKTREE ROOT, not gitignored. A routine 'git add -A' (common in decomp commit flow) sweeps these ~2600-line scratch files into the commit. Had to git rm --cached + soft-reset to clean 4 commits this session. Suggest: bootstrap into build/ or a gitignored dir, or auto-append nonmatchings/ to .gitignore on first bootstrap. Sibling: #259 (same command, stale base.c).
resolution_note: fixed in 19871cf7c: permute bootstrap normalizes import.py output into the decomp-permuter checkout and the tracked root .gitignore now ignores /nonmatchings/ so repo-root scratch dirs are not swept into git add -A

summary: permute bootstrap reuses stale nonmatchings/FN/base.c when source edited after a prior bootstrap
body: debug permute bootstrap -f FN does not refresh nonmatchings/FN/base.c if the dir already exists from a prior bootstrap — it silently keeps the OLD preprocessed source, so the permuter explores from a stale baseline (floor stuck at the old score). Workaround: rm -rf nonmatchings/FN before re-bootstrapping. Verify with: grep -c <recent-edit-token> nonmatchings/FN/base.c. Cost: ~2 wasted remote runs this session (permuter floored at 1765 while live source was already at the equivalent of 1405). Suggest: bootstrap should overwrite base.c (or warn) when the live source mtime is newer than base.c.
resolution_note: fixed in 19871cf7c: permute bootstrap now detects the fresh import.py output dir (including FN-2), promotes refreshed generated files into <perm-root>/nonmatchings/FN, and preserves existing settings/output dirs instead of resolving stale repo-root FN

summary: candidate source scoring drops helper definitions outside target function
body: While testing the source shape that ultimately matched fn_8003F654, I generated candidate source files that added a file-local inline helper before fn_8003F654 and called it from the target function. Command shape: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f fn_8003F654 --candidate inline-sqrt-array-slot1:manual=/tmp/f654-manual-candidates/inline-sqrt-array-slot1.c --json. The scorer reported a compile failure at the helper call () because the helper definition outside the target function was not transferred into the real TU. Applying the same helper directly to src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c compiled and led to the final 100% match. Requested fixes: either compile whole-file source candidates as provided, or explicitly detect/reject candidates with external helper definitions and explain that only the target function is transferred. Also include the full MWCC diagnostic text after the caret; current match_percent_error stopped before the actual message, which made this harder to diagnose.
resolution_note: fixed in 2fe082cbc: lifetime-layout source scoring now detects new helper definitions outside the target function before transfer, reports that only the target function is transferred, and preserves adjacent MWCC diagnostic context after file:line errors

summary: permute bootstrap imports committed source not working-tree edits; blocks remote permuter on directed-probe variants
body: debug permute bootstrap -f grIceMt_801F9ACC does not carry local source edits into the permuter base.c. Repro: edited src/melee/gr/gricemt.c to add PAD_STACK(64) (the directed frame-reservation probe), confirmed it builds + checkdiffs at the new state, then ran `debug permute bootstrap -f grIceMt_801F9ACC --perm-root <worktree>` and `debug permute remote submit --target coder3`. The generated nonmatchings/grIceMt_801F9ACC/base.c has PAD_STACK count = 0 (grep), i.e. the bootstrap imported the COMMITTED/pre-edit source, not the current working tree. Effect: cannot test a source variant (e.g. PAD_STACK-reserved-frame base) on the remote permuter — both remote submits ran on the no-PAD source. The bootstrap's import.py step likely re-derives the function body from a cached context or HEAD rather than the live edited file (or --perm-root is being treated as the decomp-permuter clone, which here equals the worktree, so import.py path resolution is ambiguous). ASK: bootstrap should import the CURRENT working-tree source for the function (or expose --source-file to point at the edited .c), so directed-probe variants (PAD_STACK frame reservation, named-float, tempization) can be carried into a remote permuter sweep. Without this, the remote permuter can only explore mutations of the committed body, not a hand-applied directed-probe base. (Context: testing whether reserving the 64-byte frame via PAD_STACK lets the register-aware permuter resolve the ev/did + r3/r4 swaps; had to fall back to a LOCAL permute run to get the PAD base, which pegs the box.)
resolution_note: Fixed in bb324d9465f011387e9077e2c49a9f27af57c594: debug permute bootstrap now accepts --source-file, temporarily stages that edited source over the real repo TU for import.py, restores the working tree afterward, and passes --preserve-macros with PAD_STACK/FORCE_PAD_STACK by default so directed PAD_STACK variants survive into base.c. Verified with a command smoke using a fake decomp-permuter import.py: source_staged=true, import argv includes --preserve-macros PAD_STACK|FORCE_PAD_STACK(?:_[0-9]+)?, and generated base.c contains PAD_STACK(64).

summary: lifetime-layout candidate timeout can leave orphaned wibo debug compiler
body: After #251, I ran: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f fn_8003F654 --timeout 180 --json with candidate addr-taken-pad-guard:manual=/tmp/f654-manual-candidates/addr-taken-pad-guard.c plus other candidates. The first candidate invoked debug dump local, then wibo/mwcceppc_debug hung beyond the 180s timeout. Killing the parent melee-agent and dump-local child exited the wrapper with rc 143, but the wibo process survived as an orphan under pid 1 in STAT UEs and ignored TERM/KILL from my shell. This blocks local resources and forced me to abandon the batch. The candidate source shape changed the top guard to if ((&pad_slot != 0) && pl_Verify_gm_8016AEDC() && (index != 1)) with a local f32 pad_slot. Requested fixes: enforce timeout around the whole subprocess tree, put wibo in a killable process group, surface which candidate is running in progress output/JSONL, and record a failed-candidate entry instead of leaving the batch opaque.
resolution_note: Fixed in bb324d9465f011387e9077e2c49a9f27af57c594: lifetime-layout candidate compiles now set MWCC_DEBUG_HANG_TIMEOUT earlier than the outer candidate timeout, timeout cleanup recursively kills descendant process groups before killing the wrapper, and lifetime-layout emits per-candidate JSONL progress to stderr plus records failed variants with retained source paths on timeout. Verified with diff_capture timeout regressions and installed lifetime-layout JSON progress smoke.

summary: directed source-variant search gaps: no variant family for implicit-no-access frame reservation + select-swap with small distance_to_flip yields no semantically-valid probe (9ACC)
body: DIRECTED SOURCE-VARIANT SEARCH (#247) fully exercised on grIceMt_801F9ACC (committed fa3ea766d, 96.89%). Per-factor result: (A) 64-byte implicit frame: `debug inspect stack-homes --score-sqrt-array-variants` does NOT apply — it requires a checkdiff stack_slot_localizer (FPR sqrtf-spill-home class). 9ACC's gap is an implicit GPR-AREA reservation at 0x28-0x68 with NO pcode/stack accesses (frame-reservations tool confirms origin=implicit-frame-reservation, no origin). The sqrt-array variant generator targets a different frame-gap class; it emitted no variant ("checkdiff did not report a stack_slot_localizer"). GAP: no directed-search variant family for implicit no-access frame reservations (vs FPR spill-homes). (B) ev/did r27/r29 select swap: `select-order-search --target r36<r40` (ev=v36, did=v40 from #242 readout) and `coalesce-search --target r36=r40` BOTH ran all probe families (adjacent-decl-swap, type-width, block-scope, declaration-use-distance, temp-introduction, call-arg-tempization). RESULT: only call-arg-tempization is actionable (select-order: distance_to_flip 101->5; coalesce: target_coalesced=yes, live_overlap_removed=yes) but it REGRESSES to 94.189% AND its actual ASSEMBLY does not reach the target (did lands r28, not r29; ev stays r29) — the IR "coalesced" metric does not correspond to the expected register assignment. NO probe satisfied the target order or improved match% above 96.887. The only order-moving effect came from an int-truncation the probe inserted (semantically invalid; the f32-correct form has no effect). CONCLUSION: ev/did select swap is not C-expressible even with the directed search — the search confirms distance_to_flip=5 but no probe family bridges it without a semantics-breaking truncation. (C) r3/r4 flag-write: unaffected by all probes. (D) @N floats: exact-named extern const f32 grIm_804DB574/578/5BC DO emit named SDA21 relocs (recovered 5 of 12 float lines, 90->89 normalized); shippable in principle but needs full ABS-internal 0.0f coverage + the symbols DEFINED as TU globals at .sdata2 0x574/578/5BC (data-layout class). TERMINAL: #247's directed searches correctly score and confirm each factor, but for 9ACC they find NO shippable variant for (A) or (B). The remaining gaps: (i) a directed-variant family for implicit no-access frame reservations [extend stack-homes beyond FPR spill-homes]; (ii) for an interfering-callee-save SELECT swap with small distance_to_flip (=5) but no satisfying probe, a richer probe family or a 'forced-select-order -> source' synthesizer that respects semantics (the int-truncation hack the current search relies on is invalid). 9ACC stays banked 96.89% (fa3ea766d); 5+ tool fixes shipped from it but it remains a genuine multi-factor diagnostic-reachable-but-not-C-expressible wall.
resolution_note: RETESTED after #254 fix (f32 tempization + --frame-reservation-bytes). Per-factor verdict: (A) FRAME — NOW SOLVABLE. `select-order-search --frame-reservation-bytes 64` generates a frame-reservation-pad-stack-64 probe that inserts PAD_STACK(64) right after the locals. This reproduces stwu -0x98 EXACTLY *and* — unlike my earlier MANUAL PAD_STACK placement — KEEPS the 13 __assert relocs RESOLVED (addi r3,r30,0x690; li r3,grIm_804D4720@sda21; li r5,grIm_804D4728@sda21). So the string-pool regression I reported before was a PLACEMENT artifact; the directed probe's placement avoids it. Result: 96.92%, normalized diff 90->78 (frame factor gone). PAD_STACK(64) is the shippable lever for (A). [Correction to my prior #244/#254 claim that PAD broke the strings — it was placement, now fixed.] (B) ev/did select swap — PROVEN TERMINAL. The new f32-aware call-arg-tempization is semantically correct (f32 ll = cur - f2;) and produces distance_to_flip=101 (NO movement) — confirming the earlier distance_to_flip=5 was ENTIRELY the invalid int-truncation's fctiwz artifact, not a real lever. With the corrected mover, NO probe family (decl-swap/type-width/block-scope/decl-use-distance/temp-introduction/call-arg-tempization) moves ev/did at all; all stay iter 104/4, distance_to_flip=101, satisfied=no. Re-running select-order-search WITH PAD_STACK already in source (frame reserved) ALSO gives distance_to_flip=101. Local register-aware blended permuter on the PAD_STACK base (base score 1755, blend 0.5-0.6, 9090 iters) wrote ZERO improving candidates. So (B) needs did's selection moved 101 positions and there is no semantics-valid C lever or permuter mutation that does it. TERMINAL. (C) r3/r4 flag-write — same coloring class as (B); discover shows it's not even a separate coalesce pair (transient temps), and it persists under PAD_STACK + every probe. No lever. (D) @N floats — exact-named extern const f32 grIm_804DB574/578/5BC recover 5/12 float lines but introduce other reordering (composed with PAD_STACK: 78->89 normalized, net worse); full clean coverage needs the symbols DEFINED as TU .sdata2 globals at 0x574/578/5BC (data-layout class). NET: #254 made (A) shippable (directed PAD_STACK, strings intact) and PROVED (B) terminal (f32 mover -> distance_to_flip=101). 9ACC's remaining blocker is (B)+(C) = an interfering-callee-save SELECTION that requires a 101-position iteration move with no C/permuter expression. Even composing (A)+(D) cannot reach 100% while (B) stands. 9ACC stays banked ~96.9% (committed fa3ea766d, clean, no PAD). The terminal gap is now sharp: a select-order mover that can shift a callee-save's color-iteration by a LARGE distance (101), which neither the probe families nor the permuter provide.

summary: translate forced allocator/select/frame state into a shippable C source transform (cluster-closer meta-capability)
body: The discrete force-* overrides (#240 select-order, #242 class-scoped force-phys, #244 frame-reservations diag) all SHIPPED and PROVE reachability, but none SHIPS a match — force-* are diagnostic-only; the committed source must compile to 100% on stock mwcc. grIceMt_801F9ACC is the proof case: 4 independently-diagnosed factors, NONE C-expressible. (A) 64B implicit frame: PAD_STACK(64) reproduces the prologue byte-identically but regresses 13 assert-string relocs (string-pool shift) and does NOT couple-resolve the register swaps (proven). (B) ev/did interfering-callee-save select swap: --force-select-order pins did->r29 but needs the simultaneous ev->r27 vector + ev's ig_idx (#242 still blocks the readout). (C) r3/r4 swap. (D) @N floats need exact .sdata2 names/offsets. THE GAP: a capability that takes a proven-reachable forced allocator/select/frame STATE and emits (or searches for) the C SOURCE TRANSFORM that makes stock mwcc reproduce it — i.e. invert the diagnostic into a shippable lever. This is the single highest-leverage gr/ tooling gap: it would close the ENTIRE stage-cluster reg-cascade class (grGreatBay/grInishie2/grFourside/grStadium/grIceMt, 11+ fns, all reachability-proven but C-unexpressible per memory gr_stage_cluster_register_walls). Without it, this whole class is permanently capped just below 100%. Concrete first targets: grIceMt_801F9ACC (96.89%, ev/did+frame), grStadium_801D435C, grGreatBay_801F5460.
resolution_note: Implemented forced-state source-search capability on branch codex/forced-state-source-search. Added target parsing/scoring, source transform adapters, retained candidate ledgers, stock MWCC compile/checkdiff verification, apply/dry/keep flow with sibling-regression guard, docs, and tests. Final focused suites passed (42 forced-state tests; 114 broader mwcc-debug focused tests). Live validation produced ledgers for grIceMt_801F9ACC, grStadium_801D435C, and grGreatBay_801F5460; no shippable production source diff was found in the current transform space, so follow-up work should track transform/search coverage rather than the base capability.

summary: stack-homes variant ranking omits named-local stack deltas after target slot is fixed
body: After #250 shipped, `melee-agent debug inspect stack-homes` correctly seeds/ranks local-array sqrt variants for fn_8003F654 and identifies that they fix the target final-only FPR stack home. However, the variant report currently omits the named-local stack movement that becomes the next blocker. Command run in plbonuslib-blockers after merging master 7092a23a6: melee-agent debug inspect stack-homes -f fn_8003F654 --score-sqrt-array-variants --max-variants 12 --variant-timeout 180 --json Observed top ranked variant: - variant_id: `local-array-sqrt-slot-1-index-0` - target_objective.fixed_count: 2 / 2 - target_objective.overall_match_percent: 99.866425 - remaining_stack_slot_deltas: [] - named_local_deltas: [] - summary: `target fixed` The target result is correct: the original early sqrt stfs/lfs 0x30->0x34 mismatch disappears. But full checkdiff for the same source patch still has stack-layout diffs on vector locals. The local-array variant moves: - other_pos x load / Player_LoadPlayerCoords target: expected 0x44, current 0x48 (+4) - cam_pos x load / Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset target: expected 0x38, current 0x3c (+4) This was visible in manual checkdiff output for the variant and is the actual next problem after the target FPR home is fixed. The #250 issue explicitly requested summaries like `target sqrt 0x30->0x34 fixed; cam_pos +4; other_pos +4`, but the current JSON leaves named_local_deltas empty, so an agent sees only `target fixed` and no guidance for the compensation search. Requested fix: - When scoring a generated variant, parse the full checkdiff stack-slot/localizer output, not only target homes. - Detect non-target stack references that moved by paired expected/current offsets after the target fixed. - Attribute common named locals from source/call context where possible: - `Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset(&cam_pos)` / `lfs ..., cam_pos.x` should report cam_pos delta - `Player_LoadPlayerCoords(i, &other_pos)` / `lfs ..., other_pos.x` should report other_pos delta - At minimum, report raw non-target stack deltas if name attribution is uncertain. - Rank text should distinguish `target fixed, new local layout deltas remain` from a clean target/local layout win. Acceptance test: On fn_8003F654 current WIP, the top local-array variant should still rank first for target movement, but its JSON should include named or raw local deltas showing 0x44->0x48 and 0x38->0x3c.
resolution_note: Fixed in f0952cbfcf144d7e6b89b68f11d1c6fa774eb7f5: stack-home variant scoring keeps full checkdiff JSON, derives non-target raw stack deltas, and attributes source-backed named locals from relocation call context. Installed fn_8003F654 smoke ranks local-array variants first and reports other_pos/cam_pos deltas after the target slot fix.

summary: melee-agent wrapper stale after source-search implementation
body: In /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/0004/melee on branch codex/forced-state-source-search, python -m src.cli debug target --help from tools/melee-agent showed the new source-search command, but /opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent debug target source-search returned: No such command source-search. tools/worktree-doctor.py --fix had reported that melee-agent imports repo-local src.cli. Workaround was to run python -m src.cli from tools/melee-agent for live validation.
resolution_note: Resolved operationally during f0952cbfcf144d7e6b89b68f11d1c6fa774eb7f5 validation: refreshed the editable install from /Users/mike/code/melee/tools/melee-agent. /opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent now imports /Users/mike/code/melee/tools/melee-agent/src/cli rather than /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/0004.
