summary: stack-home explorer should seed local-array sqrt variants and rank target-slot movement
body: Follow-up from the F654 cross-tool pass after #248. Using opseq plus Discord/mismatch-db knowledge turned up the historical local float array trick for sqrt stack layout: f32 sqrt_slot[1]; // or [2] sqrt_slot[0/1] = sqrtf(...); dist = sqrt_slot[0/1]; For fn_8003F654 this is a meaningful partial result: - baseline: 99.99278%, opcode-identical, remaining material mismatch is early sqrt final-only spill/reload current 0x30(r1), expected 0x34(r1) - local-array variant: 99.86643%, opcode sequence still 1.0, and the early sqrt spill/reload no longer appears in the diff, meaning the target stack home moved to expected 0x34 - new problem introduced by best placement: vector locals shift by +4: - other_pos expected 0x44, current 0x48 - cam_pos expected 0x38, current 0x3c - branch-local array variants can shift the vectors by +0xC; aggregate struct attempts lose the sqrt-slot fix and cause broad source-shape changes This is exactly why the requested #248 explorer should rank target-slot movement independently from whole-function match percent. A normal match-percent sort would discard this as worse, but structurally it solves the original FPR stack-home mismatch and exposes a smaller follow-up layout problem. Requested addition to #248/tooling: - Seed final-only FPR stack-home searches with known source patterns from mismatch-db/Discord/opseq, especially local float arrays and out-of-bounds local array forms used around sqrtf. - For each variant, explicitly report whether the target stack slot moved to the expected offset even if overall match percent drops. - When target movement succeeds but named locals shift, summarize the new named-local offset deltas so the next search can focus on compensating layout, e.g. `target sqrt 0x30->0x34 fixed; cam_pos +4; other_pos +4`. - Consider an opseq-backed reference lookup step that finds matched functions with `frsp,stfs,lfs` and surfaces nearby source idioms (`volatile f32 y`, `u8 _[N]`, `float foo[1/2]`, pointer-store helpers). Acceptance test candidate: Run against the current fn_8003F654 source and confirm the explorer surfaces the local-array variant as a promising partial, despite its lower 99.86643 score, because it fixes the original 0x30->0x34 final-only FPR slot.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7092a23a64cf179239cb9f5031d51fb7fed92e2e: stack-home explorer seeds local-array sqrt variants, scores them through inspect stack-homes, and ranks target stack-slot movement before match percent. Verified on fn_8003F654: local-array variants fix 2/2 target slots at 99.866425.

summary: frame-reservations should resolve MWCC symbolic stack homes before gap analysis
body: While using the new frame-reservations inspector on fn_8003F654, the expected side is decoded into concrete frame offsets, but the current pcdump side leaves MWCC symbolic homes such as @810/@853/@854 unresolved. Command: melee-agent debug inspect frame-reservations -f fn_8003F654 --json Observed current-side excerpt: - stfs operands `f0,@810(r1)` reports offset 810 - lfs operands `f31,@810(r1)` reports offset 810 - several integer/lfd conversion homes report offsets 853/854/855/856 - current unused_ranges reports `{ start: 8, end: 124, size: 116 }`, implying the whole low frame is unused apart from callee saves But checkdiff's stack-slot localizer with the pcdump bridge can resolve the same final-only FPR home correctly: - stfs current offset 48, expected 52 - lfs current offset 48, expected 52 - pcdump evidence: `FINAL CODE AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING B24:17 stfs f0,@810(r1)` Why this matters: For F654, the remaining mismatch is specifically a final-only FPR stack-home placement issue: current 0x30(r1), expected 0x34(r1). To reason about neighboring homes, the frame-reservations view needs current @-symbol homes lowered to concrete offsets before it builds access ranges and unused ranges. Otherwise it cannot answer which current locals/temps occupy 0x30/0x34/0x38/0x44, and it incorrectly suggests that the low frame is unused. Requested fix: - Reuse the same symbolic stack-home resolution path that checkdiff/pcdump_bridge uses, or expose a shared helper. - Resolve @NNN operands to concrete frame offsets before access_ranges/unused_ranges are computed. - Preserve the original symbolic operand in an evidence/original_operands field if useful. - Add a regression test with fn_8003F654-style final pcdump lines where @810 resolves to 48, so frame-reservations reports the stfs/lfs access at 48 instead of 810. - If an @ home cannot be resolved, report it under unresolved_symbolic_homes instead of treating it as a real frame offset.
resolution_note: Fixed in 3b5afdf56: frame-reservations now resolves MWCC @ stack homes from current checkdiff asm before access/unused range analysis, preserves original_operands/symbolic_home, and reports unresolved symbolic homes instead of treating @NNN as real offsets.

summary: add targeted FPR stack-home/lifetime explorer for final-only spills
body: Context fn_8003F654 in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c is currently a useful hard-case for the new final-only stack-home diagnostics. After the fixes from #245/#246, checkdiff/mwcc-debug can now identify the remaining mismatch precisely instead of losing the FPR roots. Current state from the plbonuslib-blockers worktree: - match is ~99.99278% - remaining material diff is a single FPR stack-home offset pair: - expected: stfs f0,52(r1); lfs f31,52(r1) - current: stfs f0,48(r1); lfs f31,48(r1) - checkdiff pcdump bridge now reports two final-only-stack-home candidates: - stfs candidate: class 1/FPR, virtual f41, spill root r41, assigned f0, source expression `fmr f41,f48`, natural alias f48 -> f41 - lfs candidate: class 1/FPR, virtual f50, spill root r50, assigned f31, source expression `fadds f50,f60,f63`, natural aliases f55/f57 -> f50 - explain-virtual/virtual-to-var can now attribute these as FPR temps instead of bogus GPR/source-line mappings, which is a clear improvement. The remaining gap is that the tools can tell us which final-only FPR temps own the bad stack home, but they do not yet help turn that diagnosis into source-level search. We need tooling that can answer: "what source-shape or lifetime perturbation would move this final-only FPR stack home from 0x30 to 0x34 while keeping the rest of the function stable?" Manual attempts tried so far include PAD_STACK variants, splitting sqrt operands, naming intermediate float temps, block-scoping temps, helper inline variants, final-average expression reshapes, and force/allocator experiments. Most either leave the 0x30/0x34 issue unchanged or cause broader allocation/source-shape cascades. The current tools make the root clearer, but the search space is still too manual. Requested capability Add a targeted FPR stack-home/lifetime explorer for final-only stack-home mismatches. This can live under mwcc-debug, checkdiff, or a wrapper that composes them, but it should be function-oriented and source-edit aware. Desired workflow Given a function and a current pcdump/checkdiff result, the tool should: 1. Detect final-only stack-home mismatches, especially FPR slots where current and expected differ only by stack offset. 2. Build a small "lifetime cluster" around each candidate: - virtual/root id and class - assigned physical register - first/last pcode occurrence - source expression from explain-virtual/virtual-to-var - copy/coalesce aliases - nearby final-code blocks and source-line spans when available - neighboring stack homes / reservation order around the current and expected offsets 3. Generate or guide a small set of source-shape variants targeted at that cluster, rather than whole-function random permutation. Examples: - introduce/remove a named float temporary around the candidate expression - move a temporary into/out of a narrower block - reorder independent float expression evaluation around the target cluster - split or merge a binary float expression - alter assignment expression form without changing semantics - try equivalent forms that change whether a temp has a reusable stack home - test small lifetime-extension/lifetime-shortening transformations around the candidate expression 4. Compile and score each variant with special attention to the target stack-home offset, not just full-function match percent. A variant that temporarily lowers the full score but moves the target from 0x30 to 0x34 should be surfaced as promising. 5. Report a compact table showing: - variant description - whether the target stack-home moved - new current/expected offset for the same candidate if still present - full match percent - new mismatch classification - whether unrelated diffs increased - source patch or minimal edit sketch for the variant Useful output for F654 would look roughly like: - target: final-only FPR stack home f41/f50 current 0x30 expected 0x34 - cluster: f50 from `fadds f50,f60,f63` in sqrt/final-average region; f41 from `fmr f41,f48`; aliases f48/f55/f57 - tried N local source-shape variants - variants that moved the slot: [...] - variants that kept slot but improved/worsened other diffs: [...] - suggested next manual edit, if any Acceptance criteria - Works on fn_8003F654 using the current plbonuslib-blockers source state. - Consumes an existing pcdump/checkdiff result or regenerates one itself. - Correctly treats the candidate as FPR/class 1 and does not fall back to line-only or GPR-only attribution. - Can identify and report neighboring final-only FPR stack homes around 0x30/0x34. - Provides at least one of: - automated local source-shape variant generation for the target expression cluster, or - a ranked set of concrete edit suggestions derived from lifetime/coalesce evidence. - Ranks candidates by target-offset movement separately from overall match percent. - Emits enough detail for an agent to continue manually without re-reading the whole pcdump. Why this matters #245/#246 are enough to diagnose the last F654 mismatch, but not enough to break through it. The next bottleneck is not "which virtual is wrong?"; it is "which small, natural C source-shape change will alter MWCC's final-only FPR stack-home assignment in exactly the desired way?" A focused explorer for that case would also help other late-stage functions where the remaining mismatch is a spill/reload stack-home offset, especially when random permutation wastes most of its budget on unrelated source changes.
resolution_note: Fixed in 47e131800: added debug inspect stack-homes FPR final-only stack-home explorer with lifetime clusters, neighbors, and ranked source-shape suggestions; verified on fn_8003F654 using existing checkdiff localizer + pcdump.

summary: FPR virtual attribution needs class-aware inverse mapping
body: For fn_8003F654, debug inspect explain-virtual -f fn_8003F654 --virtuals r50,r42,r41 --class fpr identifies class 1 simplify-only roots, but virtual-to-var has no --class option and reports misleading GPR-style attribution. Example: virtual-to-var -f fn_8003F654 r42 reports Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2(var_r29) at source line 52, unrelated to the F654 FPR root; r50 reports found false. This makes stack-slot and spill-root diagnosis hard after checkdiff points at class 1 roots. Needed: add --class to virtual-to-var or make it infer f/r tokens, and ensure FPR simplify-only roots report first FPR defining op/source region rather than GPR copy-chain guesses.
resolution_note: Fixed in 56837dcc6: virtual-to-var now accepts --class plus f* tokens, routes FPR lookups through class-aware virtual attribution, and reports FPR first-def provenance instead of GPR source-binding/call-return guesses; real fn_8003F654 f42 smoke reports fpr class, f6, fpr-temp fmr f42,f47.

summary: checkdiff stack-slot bridge returns no-candidates for final-only frsp temp
body: After passing --pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/pl/plbonuslib.txt, checkdiff attaches stack_slot_localizer.pcdump_bridge but reports status no-candidates for the only real F654 mismatch: expected stfs/lfs at 0x34(r1), current 0x30(r1). The pcdump has the relevant FPR simplify roots around class 1 r50/r42/r41 and final scheduled stfs/lfs, but the bridge appears to search precolor stack sites and cannot explain final-only frsp temporary stack homes. Needed output: identify this as a final-only compiler temp and bridge it to the FPR root or stack-home order evidence, or explicitly say final-only temp stack home not currently attributable.
resolution_note: Fixed in 56837dcc6: checkdiff stack-slot pcdump bridge now handles final-only symbolic @stack homes, parses [ROOT] colorgraph rows, maps final physical FPR stack ops back to spilled FPR roots, and real fn_8003F654 smoke now reports stfs f41/r41 and lfs f50/r50 candidates instead of no-candidates.

summary: force select-order / per-node color-iteration-priority override for interfering callee-saves
body: GAP: no mwcc-debug override reorders the color-SELECTION of two mutually-INTERFERING callee-saves from C. Existing overrides are insufficient: --force-coalesce only helps non-interfering pairs (union-find); --force-phys/-iter set a node's physreg by ig_idx but (a) the DLL ignores the class: prefix so it can't scope GPR-only, (b) ig_idx!=virtual so targeting is guesswork, and (c) forcing one node doesn't re-derive the SELECTION ORDER of the interfering set — it just pins one and lets the rest fall out, which doesn't reproduce the original's selection sequence. What's needed: an override that sets the color-iteration PRIORITY (selection order) among a named interfering set, so a node that currently loses the lower callee-save can be selected first. CONCRETE CASE 2 (case 1 = grGreatBay_801F5460, memory gr_stage_cluster_register_walls line 37): grIceMt_801F9ACC at 96.89% (committed 6b2fbd291). The sole semantic residual is a 2-register SWAP of param 'ev' (GrIceMtSegmentLookup, incoming r4) and local 'did' (s32): expected: addi r27,r4(ev); li r29,0(did) [ev->r27, did->r29] mine: addi r29,r4(ev); li r27,0(did) [ev->r29, did->r27] gobj->r31, arg3->r28 in BOTH (those are correct). coalesce-search proof: did=v40, gobj=v32 are colorgraph INTERFERERS (live r40=(7,423) r32=(0,374), same_assigned_reg=False, 'cannot coalesce: colorgraph lists the pair as interferers'); did assigned r27, want r29. The ev/did pair is the same — they interfere and just need their selection order flipped. EXHAUSTED from C (all kept 96.89% or worsened the frame): (1) alias gobj through fresh local at first use [memory line 34 grStadium recipe] -> frame -0x58->-0x60, no flip; (2) tempize ev into a local cb used at both ev(arg3) sites -> frame worse, no flip; (3) did declared last + separate init -> no change; (4) did at highest decl index -> no change; (5) decl-order enum all 9 promote variants -> +0.02%. RAW remote permuter: plateaued 1560 (blind to reg swaps per memory line 33). REGISTER-AWARE blended permuter (debug permute run --blend 0.5, 6055 iters): base 1805 best 1790, candidates only nudge the cleanup ptrs temp, NEVER flip ev/did. This pair is below C control; the override is the only path.
resolution_note: COUPLING CONFIRMED INDEPENDENT (follow-up to prior note): tested whether reproducing the 64-byte frame resolves the ev/did select swap. It does NOT. PAD_STACK(64) reproduces stwu -0x98 exactly but ev/did stay swapped (addi r29,r4 / li r27,0); PAD_STACK(64)+--force-phys 0:40:29 still MISMATCH. So even with the frame reserved AND did forced to r29, ev does not move to r27 from any C input, and r3/r4 flag-write stays swapped. The #240 select-order override pins did->r29 (verified via --diff) but reproducing the 2-node interfering ev<->did SWAP needs the simultaneous vector (ev->r27 AND did->r29) and ev's ig_idx is still unreadable (#242). Net: #240 is the correct mechanism for the (B) class but 9ACC needs the vector form + the ig_idx map (#242) to even test reachability, and a 'translate forced-select-state to source' capability (#244) to ship it. Factor (D) named-floats is mechanically fixable (volatile f32 grIm_804DB574 etc. emit named SDA21) but requires the exact TU-canonical symbol names at exact .sdata2 offsets = data-layout class. 9ACC stays banked 96.89%.

summary: frame-reservation origin tool + translate-forced-state-to-source: 9ACC has register-independent reserved frame with no C expression
body: grIceMt_801F9ACC (committed 6b2fbd291, 96.89%) has a register-INDEPENDENT reserved-frame residual that no current tool or C lever resolves. Evidence: expected stwu r1,-152 vs mine -88 (64 bytes); forcing did->r29 via --force-phys 0:40:29 leaves the frame at -88 (PROVEN independent of register allocation); the reserved region 0x08-0x68 is NEVER written (only r1 stores in expected are the reg-save area + LR); the bl call list is BYTE-IDENTICAL to expected (no inline-boundary difference — same 11x Ground_801C2BA4 / 13x __assert / 2x HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub / etc.); a u8[56] dummy local reproduces -0x98 exactly, but no real local in the function expresses 56-64 bytes (every actual local is a register-resident scalar: seg/did/mgobj/jobj/gp/ptrs/cur/f/f2/id). The unused 'float y' param is not spilled. This is the dominant residual (the ev/did select-order #240 is secondary). HYPOTHESES for the reserved frame: (a) a debug-vs-retail frame-size toggle (cf memory unused-stack-var-debug-retail-toggle); (b) an inlined helper that declares a local aggregate MWCC reserves space for but register-allocates all accesses; (c) a param-save-area sizing from a wider original signature. ASK: a tool to identify WHICH inline/IR construct reserves the frame slot (e.g. 'debug inspect frame-reservations -f <fn>' mapping each reserved stack range to its IR origin / source construct), and/or a 'translate forced allocator state into a source transform' capability so a diagnostic-reachable allocator state (#240) can be turned into shippable C. Currently 9ACC is diagnostic-characterizable but neither factor (frame, select-order) is C-expressible.
resolution_note: COUPLING TEST RESULT (the key hypothesis — frame reproduction does NOT resolve the register swaps): - debug inspect frame-reservations confirms: expected 152 vs current 88, extra 64 bytes reserved at 0x28-0x68, origin=implicit-frame-reservation, no current pcode stack-access origin. - PAD_STACK(60) and PAD_STACK(64) reproduce stwu -0x98 EXACTLY (prologue stwu/stfd 0x90/88/80/stmw 0x68 all byte-match expected). - BUT with PAD_STACK(64): (i) ev/did STAYS swapped (addi r29,r4 / li r27,0 — unchanged); (ii) r3/r4 flag-write swap UNCHANGED; (iii) NEW HARM — the PAD .rodata entry shifts the string pool so the 13 __assert cond-string SDA21 relocs REGRESS from resolved grIm_804D4718/4720/4728 to anonymous @460/@292/@293. Net diff WORSE: 114 lines vs 90 baseline. - COMBINED PAD_STACK(64) + --force-phys 0:40:29 (force did->r29) + --diff: STILL MISMATCH. So even frame-reproduced AND did-forced does not match. CONCLUSION: factors are INDEPENDENT and the coupling hypothesis is FALSE. Reproducing the frame does not couple-resolve the ev/did (B) or r3/r4 (C) selection swaps. PAD_STACK is not shippable here (breaks assert strings) and the frame-reservations tool itself says replace it with natural C — but no natural 64-byte local exists (identical call list, no stack stores, all locals register-resident scalars). TERMINAL: 9ACC's frame is reproducible-but-not-shippable, and the ev/did interfering-callee-save SELECTION remains C-unexpressible even with the frame reserved. This is the diagnostic-reachable-but-not-C-expressible terminal state. The #240 select-order override + #244 frame tooling correctly DIAGNOSE every factor, but none of the 4 factors (frame / ev-did select / r3-r4 / named-floats) has a shippable stock-mwcc C lever for this function. Banked 96.89% (fa3ea766d).

summary: decomp-permuter mutation crashes 'struct deref of non-struct' on ->user_data / synthetic inline temps (blended permuter degraded)
body: While running the register-aware blended permuter (melee-agent debug permute run -f grIceMt_801F9ACC --blend 0.5 -j 6), decomp-permuter's randomizer throws repeated 'Traceback ... AssertionError: struct deref of non-struct <name>' (~12 in 6055 iters), where <name> is user_data, new_var65, new_var81, inline_fn2345678..., etc. These are the permuter's own synthesized temps/inlines being dereferenced as structs when their type is void*/HSD_JObj**. Each crash kills that candidate (rejected), so the mutation budget on the productive register-shaping families is wasted on invalid C — likely why the blended permuter plateaus on this function (the cleanup block uses HSD_JObj** ptrs = &gp->gv.icemt2.xC8 and gp = X->user_data where user_data is void*). Two issues: (1) the type-aware mutation should not emit ->field on a void*/non-struct temp; (2) the aggressive 'widen-u8-to-u32' config (debug permute config wrote perm_randomize_internal_type=50.0) made an EARLIER run degenerate completely (errors climbed monotonically 914->1225, score=inf, never recovered) — that weight profile should be capped or gated. Repro: debug permute config -f grIceMt_801F9ACC then debug permute run --blend 0.5. Workaround used: reset settings.toml to stock (drop weight_overrides). Sibling to #240 (the real wall is the select-order gap; this bug just makes the permuter a weaker witness for it).
resolution_note: Fixed in melee 72bf0a64859f04c5457ee44e939c3f0cb2dc0023 and local decomp-permuter ef4d3910c10bb9a70cf62a2bc99dbe1e70cf65de. melee-agent now caps perm_randomize_internal_type overrides at stock 10.0 when rendering/merging settings.toml, and decomp-permuter converts invalid type-resolution assertions from randomizer passes into RandomizationFailure so void*/non-struct StructRef candidates are skipped instead of counted as internal failures. Verified renderer tests, decomp-permuter randomizer unittest, CLI config smoke, and a bounded blended permuter launch for grIceMt_801F9ACC.

summary: force-phys class prefix ignored + ig_idx-vs-virtual ambiguity blocks targeted reachability test
body: Trying to prove the #240 select-order target is reachable (force did to r29, observe if ev moves to r27), --force-phys is hard to aim: (1) numeric class form 1:40:29 is rejected (expects ig_idx:physReg or class:ig_idx:physReg, class in gpr/fp/fpr/int); (2) gpr:40:29 is accepted but warns the DLL does not support class filtering, so it applies to ALL IG classes at that ig_idx and a GPR-only force can clobber an FP node sharing the ig_idx; (3) the override key is ig_idx, but coalesce-search/discover report VIRTUAL numbers (did=v40, gobj=v32) with no surfaced ig_idx-to-virtual map, so gpr:40:29 is a guess; (4) the forced /tmp/pcdump_forced_*.txt shows IR virtuals, not a clean before/after physreg diff, so confirming did landed r29 and ev moved to r27 needs manual cross-ref. ASK: (a) accept virtual-register targeting, or print the ig_idx/virtual/source-var map in the dump header; (b) implement the class filter; (c) emit a one-line forced-node summary (node X rOLD->rNEW; cascade ev rA->rB) so reachability is readable. This blocks the reachability half of #240.
resolution_note: CONFIRMED BLOCKING this session (was hypothetical, now proven). With the new override I could force did=ig40->r29 (class0:ig40:phys=r29 works, --diff shows did's li/mr move to r29), but to complete the ev/did reachability test I ALSO need ev->r27, and I cannot get ev's ig_idx: (1) the COLORGRAPH DECISIONS (class=1) table in the pcdump truncates to only the first non-trivial iter row (e.g. '0 32 r31 14 14') and elides the rest of the GPR nodes incl. ev — so ev's ig_idx is not readable; (2) discover/coalesce-search report ev as VIRTUAL r36 (from 'mr r12,r36; blrl' the ev(arg3) indirect call) but there's no virtual<->ig_idx map, and '--force-select-order 36,40' / 'gpr:36:...' guesses did NOT reproduce ev->r27 (did/ev landed r29/r29 collision). ASKS (sharpened): (a) print the FULL colorgraph DECISIONS table (all nodes, not just non-trivial), OR (b) emit a virtual<->ig_idx<->source-var map in the dump/`debug inspect` so force-phys/force-select-order can be aimed from the virtual numbers that discover already reports, OR (c) add a `--force-vector` worked example in --help that forces a 2-node swap (igA->rLOW, igB->rHIGH) by source-var name. Without one of these, the override (#240) can pin ONE node but can't reproduce a 2-node interfering SWAP, which is the actual 9ACC case.

summary: #240 is the single highest-leverage gr/ tooling gap — unblocks the whole stage cluster, not just 9ACC
body: Priority elevation for #240 (force select-order / color-iteration-priority override for interfering callee-saves). This same param-vs-local callee-save SELECTION-order swap is the documented terminal wall for the ENTIRE gr/ stage cluster, not just grIceMt_801F9ACC + grGreatBay_801F5460. Per memory gr_stage_cluster_register_walls: grGreatBay_801F5460 (97.30), grGreatBay_801F63F4 (98.32), grInishie2 801FD4F0/801FD9EC, grFourside 801F3274/801F3CC8/801F3894, grStadium 801D3BBC/801D435C/801D4548, grIceMt_801F9ACC (96.89) — 11+ functions blocked on this one missing override. Reachability is PROVEN on these (force-iter-first reproduces the expected reg map) but no shippable C lever or permuter (raw OR register-aware --blend) reorders the color-selection of two mutually-interfering callee-saves. Fixing #240 (+ its support papercut #242) would convert this cluster from 'permuter-territory walls' to closeable. Highest-leverage fix in the gr/ backlog.
resolution_note: resolved by 66aca3804 via #240/#242 allocator-control fix; grIceMt_801F9ACC smoke shows class-scoped force-phys and selection-order controls apply in the target function

body: checkdiff now correctly classifies fn_8003F654 as stack-slot-layout and reports the exact mismatch: stfs/lfs expected 0x34(r1), current 0x30(r1), frame already 168 bytes. To make this actionable, bridge the hunk to the pcdump: identify the likely FPR spill root or IG node, its coalesced aliases, stack-home order, and nearest source expression. Current evidence points at class 1 spill root r50 for the sqrtf frsp temp. This would turn stack-slot-layout from a generic hint into a concrete source-shape or force-order target.

summary: force-iter-first needs register-class scoping for FP spill investigations
body: The remaining fn_8003F654 mismatch is an FP compiler-temp spill slot: expected stfs/lfs at 0x34(r1), current at 0x30(r1). I tried a diagnostic forced order with force-iter-first 41,42 scoped to fn_8003F654 to see whether changing class 1 spill-root order moves the stack home. The forced run also perturbed GPR allocation heavily, shifting slot/entity/table registers, so it did not isolate the FP-class hypothesis. Please add class-scoped force-iter-first, for example class plus ig or class plus iter forms, and report whether only the intended register class changed.
resolution_note: Fixed in 55c903e90: debug dump local/remote and force-vector now support class-scoped --force-iter-first plus class:iter --force-iter-first-iter, and mwcc_debug honors MWCC_DEBUG_FORCE_ITER_FIRST_CLASS / _ITER so FPR probes avoid disturbing GPR allocation.

summary: lifetime-layout probes need real byte-diff scoring and stack-slot output
body: While investigating fn_8003F654 at 99.99 percent, debug mutate lifetime-layout generated useful call-return and lifetime probes and reported pressure deltas, but not final match percent or checkdiff stack-slot-localizer output for each probe. Its generated sources also default to temporary paths that debug dump local refuses because they are outside the repo. select-order-search and coalesce-search already have source scoring that temporarily transfers candidates into the real tree, refreshes report.json, and restores the source. Please add the same score-match-percent path to lifetime-layout, including final_match_percent and stack-slot deltas for each probe, or make generated variant paths directly diffable.
resolution_note: Fixed in 55c903e90: lifetime-layout source candidates now run real-tree match-percent scoring and include checkdiff stack-slot localizer output; checkdiff --no-build also skips the build/report lock so nested stack-slot scoring does not deadlock under the source-scoring lock.

summary: grIceMt_801F9ACC 96.89% allocator/frame wall — structure correct, needs permuter or allocator intervention
body: Committed 4138e8ac1 at 96.89%. Structure m2c-confirmed correct. Applied: (1) void->int return type (early li r3,0 + final mr r3 return of did; resolves signature-type-mismatch; builds clean, no conflicting_types — earlier 451-regression report was from a transient broken index state, NOT the int change); (2) file-local HSD_ASSERT override routing __FILE__ through (char*)&grIm_803E4068+0x690 so the 8 own-asserts emit addi r3,r30,1680 matching expected (siblings 96E0/FA0BC need the same; they are NOT yet 100% — 99.96%/92.78%). RESIDUAL = pure allocator interference: frame 0x40 too small (stwu -152 vs -88; CONFIRMED reservable via a u8[64] probe that overshot to -0xa0, so it is reserved space with no real local), plus r27/r29 (did) and r3/r4 (xC4 flag-write) callee-save swaps. Levers exhausted: decl-order enum all 9 = +0.02%; ported permuter inline helpers (ptrs=&gp->...xC8, Ground_801C2BA4 wrapper) = marginal/no frame fix; mwcc-debug 'inspect ceiling' verdict NO FAST TRANSFORM (spilled call-return copies need lifetime probes). Remote permuter coder3 job grIceMt_801F9ACC-coder3-20260531-214201 plateaued at score 1560 (baseline 1780), no match after ~8min. Same MWCC callee-save-ordering class as the gr/ stage cluster walls. Next: longer permuter run or force-phys with ig_idx mapping. No regressions (7D94/865C stay 100%).
resolution_note: FINAL BANK: committed 6b2fbd291 at 96.89%. Longer permuter (coder3, 19819 iters/~27min) plateaued at 1560 vs 1780 baseline, no score-0. Lowest-score-1560 candidate hand-ported (inline_fn ptrs + do/while wrap) -> checkdiff still 96.89% (frame+cascade intact, NOT a hidden match). coalesce-search verdict: did(r40)/gobj(r32) are genuine colorgraph interferers (live 7-423 vs 0-374), all source-shape probes <=96.89%. Banked as gr/ stage-cluster callee-save-ordering wall. Two correct levers landed (void->int return, assert +0x690 offset-hack). No regressions.

summary: dtk dol split and dtk --version hang in uninterruptible I/O, blocking normal ninja rebuild
body: During #228 validation in /Users/mike/code/melee on master, python tools/worktree-doctor.py --fix hung in ninja build/GALE01/report.json. summary: lifetime-layout probes need real byte-diff scoring and stack-slot output
body: While investigating fn_8003F654 at 99.99%, lifetime-layout pressure explorer - fn_8003F654 baseline: frame=168 saved=r25,r26,r27,r28,r29,r30,r31,f30,f31 spills=41,42,50 Probes: - call-return-compare-switch-0 [call-return-compare-chain]: Rewrite `b34` call-result compares as a switch. - call-return-compare-inverted-0 [call-return-compare-chain]: Try the `0` compare before `1`. - call-return-compare-copy-in-else-0 [call-return-compare-chain]: Compare `b34_result` first and copy into `b34` only in the else arm. - call-return-compare-split-direct-0 [call-return-compare-chain]: Use `b34_result` for the first compare, then copy for the second compare after the first branch. - call-return-compare-narrow-pointer-0 [call-return-compare-chain]: Narrow dependent pointer `teammate_table` inside the `b34 == 1` branch. - adjacent-decl-swap-0 [declaration-order]: Swap adjacent function-scope locals `new_var` and `b34_result` to test allocator role order. - loop-counter-type-0 [loop-counter-type]: Change loop counter `i` type int -> s32. - temp-introduction-0 [temp-introduction]: Introduce a named temp for one compound assignment RHS. - type-width-0 [type-width]: Change local `b34_result` type s32 -> int. - declaration-use-distance-0 [declaration-use-distance]: Move `b34_result` declaration down next to its first use. - early-guard-return-0 [early-guard-return]: Invert a top-level guard and unwrap its body after return. - block-scope-0 [block-scope]: Tighten one local declaration and following statement into a block. Variants: - call-return-compare-switch-0 [call-return-compare-chain] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +135 -135 coalesce: +3 -3 - call-return-compare-inverted-0 [call-return-compare-chain] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +44,45,49 -41,42,50 interference: +4 -4 coalesce: +1 -1 - call-return-compare-copy-in-else-0 [call-return-compare-chain] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +135 -135 coalesce: +3 -3 - call-return-compare-split-direct-0 [call-return-compare-chain] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +159 -135 coalesce: +3 -3 - call-return-compare-narrow-pointer-0 [call-return-compare-chain] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +0 -0 coalesce: +0 -0 - adjacent-decl-swap-0 [declaration-order] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +0 -0 coalesce: +0 -0 - loop-counter-type-0 [loop-counter-type] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +0 -0 coalesce: +0 -0 - temp-introduction-0 [temp-introduction] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +98 -68 coalesce: +1 -1 - type-width-0 [type-width] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +135 -135 coalesce: +3 -3 - declaration-use-distance-0 [declaration-use-distance] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +44 -44 coalesce: +1 -1 - early-guard-return-0 [early-guard-return] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +0 -0 coalesce: +0 -0 - block-scope-0 [block-scope] frame: 168 -> 168 (0) saved: +- -- spill: +- -- interference: +0 -0 coalesce: +0 -0 generated useful call-return/lifetime probes and pressure deltas, but did not report final match percent or the checkdiff stack-slot localizer for each probe. The generated sources defaulted to /tmp; then refused them because they are outside the repo, so I had to switch to select-order/coalesce search just to get real byte scores. Please add select-order/coalesce-style support to lifetime-layout, including , stack-slot-localizer deltas, and source path/restore safety, or make the generated variant paths directly diffable.

summary: stack-slot localizer should map mismatched r1 offsets to MWCC spill roots
