To move from diagnosis to action, the tool should bridge this hunk to the pcdump: identify the likely FPR spill root/IG node (current evidence points at class=1 spill root around r50), its coalesced aliases, stack-home order, and nearest source expression ( frsp temp). That would turn stack-slot-layout from a generic hint into a concrete source-shape/force-order target.
resolution_note: Superseded by a cleanly quoted report; original body lost the sqrtf expression because of shell expansion.

summary: gricemt.c data tables grIm_803E40B0/grIm_803E4544 truncated — blocks 4 funcs (9ACC/FA0BC/F993C/8CDC)
body: ROOT CAUSE for the gricemt 'data-layout wall': the committed source defines grIm_803E40B0 as a 5-element S16Vec3[] (0x1E=30 bytes) but the original object has it at 0x494=1172 bytes; grIm_803E4544 is 'extern s16[218]' in source but the original DEFINES it in this TU at 0x1B4=436 bytes immediately after grIm_803E40B0. With both truncated, the __assert(__FILE__,...) string lands at ...data.0+0x184 instead of the original +0x690, producing ~94 data/relocation diff lines in EVERY function that references the assert string at that offset. CONFIRMED affected (all show addi rX,r30,0x690 in expected vs 0x184 in mine): grIceMt_801F9ACC (18.88% local, 96.34% on decomp.me with complete-data context), grIceMt_801FA0BC (0%), grIceMt_801F993C, grIceMt_801F8CDC (99.45%, the banked WIP 'data-layout wall'). FIX = complete both tables with correct symbol names/types/declaration-order (4068, 40B0[full], 4544[full defined], 4718). Extracted byte content available from dtk elf disasm of build/GALE01/obj/melee/gr/gricemt.o (293 u32 words for 40B0, 109 for 4544). This is the single data fix that unblocks all 4 functions. Per brief's HARD EXCLUSION this is reserved for the 8CDC owner — flagging so it's done ONCE and the other 3 funcs are re-scored after.
resolution_note: WITHDRAWN: misdiagnosis from a BROKEN build. My void->int signature change caused a conflicting_types error producing garbage 18.88% output where the assert string showed at +0x184. On the CLEAN committed state 9ACC builds at 96.87% and the data tables are only a 16-line sideshow, NOT the blocker. Dup of #228/#231 (8CDC data-layout wall). 9ACC's real residual is the 64-byte frame + assert offset-hack, both tractable without the data tables.

summary: gricemt.c assert string-pool offset capped by truncated grIm_803E40B0 / undefined grIm_803E4544 data tables
body: grIceMt_801F8CDC (and siblings grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C) reach 99.94-99.96% with the SOLE residual being assert file/message strings referenced at the wrong offset from grIm_803E4068 (mine +388 / +0x184, expected +1680 / +0x690). Root cause is NOT in the function .text (registers/stack/floats/opcodes all match) — it is TU-wide static data truncation: (1) grIm_803E40B0 at gricemt.c:98 is defined with only 5 S16Vec3 elements (0x1e bytes) but symbols.txt says size 0x494 (1172 bytes); (2) grIm_803E4544 (gricemt.c:473) is declared 'extern s16[218]' and never defined, but symbols.txt says it is a 0x1B4 (436-byte) .data object owned by this TU. Original layout: 0x48 + 0x494 + 0x1B4 = 0x690 puts gricemt.c string at +1680; truncated build puts it at +388. Completing both tables from orig DOL (.data vaddr 0x803B9840 -> file 0x3B6840) should close all three functions. Filed spawn_task to recover the data.
resolution_note: fixed in c33a4b54a: recovered the full grIm_803E40B0 stage point blob, defined grIm_803E4544, modeled grIm_803E46F8 assert strings, and reused the named file/assert strings so gricemt .data/string offsets match the original DOL; grIceMt_801F96E0 and grIceMt_801F993C are instruction-identical under checkdiff --no-build, and grIceMt_801F8CDC no longer has the assert string offset residual.

summary: source-shape probe generator can bind function prototypes instead of definitions
body: While validating #225/#226 against the F654 scratch source, debug mutate lifetime-layout generated probes from an unrelated span because _find_function_body_span matched the fn_8003F654 prototype and then the next brace. Fixed in this pass by making the span resolver skip prototypes and require the matching signature's next non-space token to be '{'.
resolution_note: self-filed during validation and fixed in 3909eedf8: source-shape function span lookup now skips prototypes/declarations and binds only actual function definitions before generating probes.

summary: stack-slot localizer should target compiler temp spill slots independently of named locals
body: At 99.99 F654 opcode/control-flow matches, but the sqrtf frsp spill uses 0x30(r1) while expected uses 0x34(r1); cam_pos must remain at 0x38. PAD_STACK(4), FORCE_PAD_STACK_4, branch-scope PAD_STACK, unused f32 array, address-taken f32, volatile f32, named sqrt temp, and decl-order search either leave the sqrt temp at 0x30 or move cam_pos to 0x3c. We need tooling that can explain and search MWCC compiler-temp stack slot assignment, not only frame size or named-local offsets.
resolution_note: fixed in 3909eedf8: checkdiff now classifies same-frame paired r1 temp spill offset mismatches as stack-slot-layout with compiler-temp guidance and hex/decimal slot parsing.

summary: source-shape search should try case-0/default switch for boolean guard long-branch lowering
body: After the y_delta improvement, F654 was stuck at 99.60 because an early guard compiled as compact bne epilogue. Rewriting if (ftLib_8008732C(entity)) return; as switch (ftLib_8008732C(entity)) { case 0: break; default: return; } produced the expected beq body; b epilogue shape and moved to 99.99. Existing early-guard-return and empty-then/else/goto source variants normalized back to compact bne.
resolution_note: fixed in 3909eedf8: lifetime-layout source-shape probes now include boolean-guard-switch candidates for case-0/default long-branch lowering; verified against the fn_8003F654 scratch source/pcdump.

summary: source-shape search should try split expression/local compare temps for float abs branches
body: F654 moved 97.72 -> 99.60 when the Y-axis branch was changed from testing the same abs local to a separate y_delta local: y_delta = pos->y - prevPos->y; abs = y_delta; if (y_delta > 0.0f) { if (abs < 0.0f) ... else abs = abs; } else { ... }. Existing ABS/self-assign/temp variants did not find this; the useful transform is specifically splitting the branch discriminator expression from the value local used by the redundant abs materialization.
resolution_note: fixed in 3909eedf8: lifetime-layout source-shape probes now include abs-branch-discriminator-split candidates for float abs branch temp splitting; verified against the fn_8003F654 scratch source/pcdump.

summary: force-coalesce / force-phys lacks a multi-node vector composition mode with cascade auto-verify
body: When a residual needs MULTIPLE coupled register choices to swap together (e.g. for grIceMt_801FA0BC, r28->r30 for `ptrs` plus any upstream node displacements that the swap forces), single `--force-coalesce A=B` or `--force-phys class:iter:phys` overrides don't compose: `match-iter-first --auto-verify` proves reachability for ONE node at a time but the cascade of downstream displacements is invisible. Repro: same setup as the sibling discover-non-copy-edge feature request — grIceMt_801FA0BC at 58eda3697, 99.85% with 5-instruction r28/r30 swap. Request: a composition mode that accepts a VECTOR of forces (e.g. `--force-vector 'ig40:phys=r30,ig??:phys=r??,...'`), runs cascade auto-verify on the union (rebuild + checkdiff after applying all forces together, ALSO probe each intermediate force individually for incompatibility-detection), and reports whether the full vector reaches match=true. This is what F654's plbonuslib_blockers_rematch ROUND 3 also needed (3-way coupled GPR+FPR+anon-const; forcing dim-by-dim each gave +0.00%); both this gap and the discover-no-copy-edge gap (sibling issue) together would close the residuals on register-swap-class walls without needing permuter.
resolution_note: fixed in f1f61c6bd: debug target match-iter-first now accepts --force-vector, composes force-phys/force-phys-iter/force-coalesce/force-iter-first overrides into an integrated checkdiff union run, and probes singleton/prefix diagnostic steps

summary: fresh agent worktree missing orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol blocks build
body: In agent worktree .claude/worktrees/agent-aba0094eec49c9fe8, orig/GALE01/sys/ contained only .gitkeep (no main.dol). 'python configure.py && ninja' failed at SPLIT step: "orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol not found". checkdiff/build are impossible until the DOL is present. Workaround: cp /Users/mike/code/melee/orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol into the worktree's orig/GALE01/sys/. Suggest the worktree-setup script symlink or copy orig/ (and any other gitignored build prerequisites) into new agent worktrees automatically.
resolution_note: Fixed in c229334a6: session startup and pr-worktree setup now link/copy orig/GALE01/sys/main.dol from the shared checkout/config into fresh worktrees, and worktree-doctor --fix repairs broken DOL symlinks; covered by regression tests and hook smoke.

summary: inspect diff source-to-source path times out on F654 candidates
body: Command: melee-agent debug inspect diff -f fn_8003F654 src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c /tmp/f654_y_abs_temp.c. I expected a pcdump/source variant diff, but it invoked tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh for source A and timed out after 90s, then started source B. I killed it. For F654 micro-campaigns this path is too heavy/unclear; it should either use the pcdump-local diff path documented in mwcc-debug, expose a timeout/pcdump-only mode, or fail with guidance.
resolution_note: Fixed in c229334a6: debug inspect diff now compares .c inputs through local pcdumps by default and only runs mwcc-inspect when --source-inspect or explicit --inspect-a/--inspect-b snapshots are supplied; verified source and installed source-to-source diff smokes.

summary: coalesce-search serial real-score run can go silent after scoring child exits
body: After the 5d966a304 fixes, I ran a single serialized command: melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r38=r41 --max-probes 8 --timeout 180. It started a ninja build child, then the ninja child exited, but the parent melee-agent process stayed alive/silent for several more polls with no active child and no progress output. I killed it after about 4 minutes. This is no longer a concurrency issue; the serial real-score path still needs progress/status or to exit after child completion.
resolution_note: fixed in 6ddef98fa: real-score source scoring now emits non-JSON stderr status callbacks through lock, apply, build, report refresh, restore, and cleanup stages so serial coalesce-search runs remain visibly alive after child completion

summary: coalesce-search tracebacks when baseline pcdump omits requested function
body: While smoke-checking issue #220, \ auto-resolved a cached pcdump for the unit that contained plBonusLib_8003D514 but not fn_8003F654. The command raised ValueError from pressure_signature_from_pcdump and printed a Python traceback. Consumer commands should surface the standard function-not-in-pcdump diagnostic instead.
resolution_note: fixed in 6ddef98fa: coalesce/select-order/lifetime baseline pcdump consumers now convert missing requested functions into the standard function-not-in-pcdump diagnostic instead of raising ValueError tracebacks

summary: match-iter-first auto-verify restore aborts after forced F654 check
body: Command: melee-agent debug target match-iter-first -f fn_8003F654 --auto-verify. The useful diagnostic completed: baseline -> with override stayed 97.72% (+0.00), actionability no_improvement. But cleanup then failed with exit 125 because restore dry-run planned 399 ninja steps, above MWCC_DEBUG_RESTORE_MAX_STEPS=64. For forced checks that only touch one TU/object/report, restore should avoid a broad rebuild or provide a safe targeted cleanup path.
resolution_note: verified fixed on current master: protective restore refusal return 125 is treated as diagnostic state rather than a command failure; targeted tests cover the expensive-restore guard and non-failing auto-verify exit path

summary: debug suggest coalesce --discover doesn't surface non-copy-edge register-swap candidates
body: On grIceMt_801FA0BC the residual is a callee-save register swap: `HSD_JObj** ptrs` (= `&gp->gv.icemt2.xC8`) lands on r28 in mine, expected reuses r30 (which had been holding the grIm_803E4068 base reg from the assert preamble). `debug suggest coalesce --discover` ran but only surfaced a NON-actionable r33=r35 cross-block pair with no copy edge between them — i.e. discover's enumeration is gated on copy-edge presence in the IR, so the actual residual (r28<->r30 reuse, also no copy edge) was invisible. Expected: discover should ALSO enumerate register-reuse opportunities for callee-save nodes whose live-range ends BEFORE another node's live-range begins (the dead-after-use base reg pattern), even when no copy edge exists, since that's the exact shape of the residual checkdiff classifies as register-allocation. Verified the swap isn't coalesce-fixable via `debug intervene coalesce r51=r36` — confirms it's a re-coloring opportunity, not a coalescing one. Repro: build claude/nervous-hermann-d4c9f9 at 58eda3697 (grIceMt_801FA0BC at 99.85%), run `melee-agent debug suggest coalesce --discover -f grIceMt_801FA0BC` and observe r28/r30 absent from the report despite being the documented residual.
resolution_note: fixed in 6ddef98fa: suggest coalesce discover now adds register-reuse candidates for non-overlapping callee-save live ranges even when there is no direct copy edge; calibrated cascade ordering is preserved

summary: real-score source searches need cross-process locking or clear concurrency failure
body: I accidentally ran three real-score source searches in parallel: coalesce-search --target r38=r41, coalesce-search --target r37=r33, and select-order-search --target 'r54<r53,r53<r35' --beam-depth 1. All spawned local ninja/report scoring, produced no progress output, were still sleeping/silent after >6 minutes, and had to be killed. Since --score-match-percent temporarily transfers candidates into the real tree, these commands should probably take a shared lock, reject concurrent source-scoring runs in the same worktree, or serialize with visible status. This matters for automation because parallel tool use can corrupt or stall candidate scoring.
resolution_note: fixed in 5d966a304: real-score source candidate scoring now uses a repo-wide source-scoring lock around transfer, build, report refresh, restore, and cleanup, with visible wait/acquired status

summary: interrupted real-score search can leave generated candidate applied
body: After killing three long-running source-scoring searches, src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c was left with a generated call-return-compare-switch candidate applied to fn_8003F654. The candidate scored 95.52% versus the retained 97.72% source. This should be restored in a finally/cleanup path even on SIGTERM, or the tool should operate on an isolated copy/worktree when scoring real match percent.
resolution_note: fixed in 5d966a304: source-scoring restore now runs under the same repo-wide lock through cleanup, preserving the active source restore/signal path without cross-process restore races

summary: lifetime-layout JSON reports cleaned temp candidate paths
body: Command: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f fn_8003F654 --compile-probes --max-probes 16 --json > /tmp/f654_lifetime_layout.json. The JSON variants include path values such as /var/folders/.../T/melee_lifetime_layout_mnk1tufj/call-return-compare-inverted-0.c, but the temp directory is removed by the time the command exits. Follow-up scoring via coalesce-search --candidate ... fails with 'candidate path not found'. Either persist candidates when paths are emitted, add an output-dir option, or omit unusable paths from JSON.
resolution_note: fixed in 5d966a304: lifetime-layout --compile-probes JSON now keeps generated candidate paths live, exposes generated_source_dir, and adds --output-dir for explicit candidate retention

summary: debug dump local succeeds when requested function is absent from pcdump
body: While validating issue #207, debug dump local /Users/mike/code/melee/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --function fn_8003F654 --output /tmp/fn_8003F654_issue207.pcdump.txt --no-cache-sync exited successfully, but the generated pcdump contained other plbonuslib functions and did not contain fn_8003F654. Downstream debug inspect virtual-to-var then failed with function-not-found. The dump command should fail clearly when --function is requested and the emitted pcdump omits that function.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0e460f96f4f0bc40b69da300df2bbd2c44712fd6: debug dump local --function now validates the emitted pcdump contains the requested function, prints suggestions/hint, preserves the dump, skips cache sync, and exits nonzero when absent.

summary: virtual-to-var omits call-return copy-chain context that diagnose can identify
body: For fn_8003F654, debug inspect diagnose reports a useful source bridge for the spilled call-return virtual: r40: ft_80087B34(entity) -> b34_result (call-return/copy-chain), chain r40 <- r43 <- r59 <- r3, use B33 cmpi cr0,r40,0. But the command it recommends for details, melee-agent debug inspect virtual-to-var -f fn_8003F654 r40, only prints 'no source variable bound to r40 (likely compiler-introduced temp)'. This loses the most actionable context when the problematic value is a call-return temp rather than a declared local. virtual-to-var should surface the same call-return/copy-chain attribution, call site, chain, and first compare/use that diagnose already found.
resolution_note: Fixed in a0575e28b8264ecdb8b62ea5f38757a6d1bb3e5f: virtual-to-var now reuses virtual attribution call-return/copy-chain detection when no direct source variable binds, and renders source expression, call site, chain, and use sites.

summary: debug dump local --diff exits 0 after force-coalesce produces no object
body: Diagnostic command: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --force-coalesce 37=33 --force-coalesce-fn '' --function fn_8003F654 --diff. Output started with '--diff requested but .o not produced at /tmp/pcdump_local_keep_42828_1780263136460.o; compile likely failed (see error above).' There was no earlier compiler error in stdout/stderr, the command exited 0, and it wrote /tmp/pcdump_forced_42828_1780263136981.txt. Running debug intervene coalesce against that pcdump then failed with 'intervention pcdump has no hook events for fn_8003F654'. If --diff cannot run because no object was produced, debug dump local should exit nonzero and include the actual compiler/DLL failure tail, or mark the pcdump unusable for the requested function.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1fea906ee: debug dump local --diff now exits nonzero after preserving the pcdump when the compile produces no .o for checkdiff.

summary: intervene coalesce resolves relative --source-file under tools/melee-agent/src
body: After merging #192, running debug intervene coalesce from the repo root with a normal relative source path failed before MWCC: melee-agent debug intervene coalesce -f fn_8003F654 --source-file src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --baseline-pcdump build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/pl/plbonuslib.txt --force r37=r33 --output-dir /tmp/f654_intervene_192/force-r37-r33. The intervention compile failed with 'Invalid value: file not found: /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers/tools/melee-agent/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c'. Re-running the same command with an absolute --source-file path reached the expected force-coalesce preflight. The command should resolve relative --source-file values against the caller repo root, like other melee-agent debug commands.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1fea906ee: intervene coalesce resolves relative --source-file to an absolute existing path before invoking child debug dump.

summary: coalesce-search advertises final match percent tiebreaker but reports n/a for compiled probes
body: debug coalesce-search help/output says it ranks by target coalesce objective with final match percent tiebreaker, and --compile-probes is enabled by default. On fn_8003F654 after a fresh pcdump, command: melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r38=r41 --max-probes 12. It compiled and ranked 11 source variants, but every row printed final_match_percent: n/a. JSON had objective.match_percent null for each variant. For decomp use, this makes it hard to tell whether a target-objective-neutral variant is still a real match win/loss; either compute/checkdiff real match percent for compiled probes, expose an option to do so, or change the text so users do not think score is part of ranking.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1fea906ee: coalesce-search now scores source candidates with real report.json match percent by default and keeps --no-score-match-percent for pcdump-only speed.

summary: coalesce-search proceeds after stale cached pcdump warning
body: While exercising #192 workflows, I edited src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c and started debug coalesce-search before the fresh pcdump cache finished. Command: melee-agent debug coalesce-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r37=r33 --max-probes 24 --json. It printed '[mwcc_debug] using stale cached pcdump (plbonuslib.c modified since cache; src=14:30:05.694 cache=14:28:11.254). Re-run debug dump local to refresh.' but continued and generated/ranked variants. For a target-coalesce objective, stale ig_idx mappings can make the target pair meaningless. This should fail closed or auto-refresh, matching the stale-cache guard added to select-order-search.
resolution_note: Fixed in 1fea906ee: coalesce-search now requires a fresh auto-resolved cached pcdump by default and exposes --allow-stale-pcdump as an explicit opt-out.

summary: mwcc-inspect candidate source defaults to unpushed local branch and fails checkout
body: When inspecting a candidate source from this worktree, tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh defaulted MWCC_INSPECT_REMOTE_REF to the local branch name codex/plbonuslib-blockers. That branch is local-only on the Windows host, so the wrapper failed during checkout and produced no structured output. Repro: tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh --function fn_8003F654 --output /tmp/f654_inspect_seed_after_merge.txt /tmp/f654_after_merge_select_20260531_140320/seed.c. Output: remote command failed exit 1, stage=checkout ref='codex/plbonuslib-blockers', no structured output was produced. Workaround: MWCC_INSPECT_REMOTE_REF=master tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh --function fn_8003F654 --output /tmp/f654_inspect_seed_masterref.txt /tmp/f654_after_merge_select_20260531_140320/seed.c succeeds. The wrapper should choose a remote-resolvable ref, use a detached commit when available, or fail with a clearer hint to set MWCC_INSPECT_REMOTE_REF.
resolution_note: fixed in e332fc0c0: mwcc-inspect uploaded candidate sources now default to upstream or master instead of an unpushed local branch, with regression coverage for a codex/local-only branch

summary: checkdiff classifies F654 control-flow line-delta mismatch as stack-layout
body: On the current retained fn_8003F654 baseline, python tools/checkdiff.py fn_8003F654 --format summary reports classification=stack-layout. The full diff, however, shows line count differs expected 314/current 308 and the first meaningful code mismatch is control-flow shape: expected emits beq fallthrough + unconditional branch after ftLib_8008732C, while current emits a direct bne to the epilogue. The summary also says call shape differs and many stack/data differences are downstream offset/relocation noise after the shortened code shape. This classification can mislead agents into stack/padding work; it should prefer a category such as control-flow/line-count/source-shape when early branch structure changes dominate before stack slot offsets cascade.
resolution_note: fixed in e332fc0c0: checkdiff now detects branch/control-flow source-shape divergence before downstream stack-slot noise can classify the mismatch as stack-layout

summary: checkdiff repeat-attempt warning can interleave into disassembly diff output
body: While running python tools/checkdiff.py fn_8003F654 and piping a line range through sed during F654 experiments, the repeat-attempt warning was printed into the middle of a disassembly line: a current line beginning '+230: 7f 83 ...' was interrupted by '[REPEAT] this source matches attempt #136...' before the rest of the instruction. Repeat detection is useful, but its diagnostics should be emitted before/after the diff or on a separate stream in a way that cannot corrupt the paired asm output, especially because agents and scripts often pipe the full diff into further analysis.
resolution_note: fixed in e332fc0c0: checkdiff repeat/divergent-repeat diagnostics now flush stdout before writing the banner to stderr, preventing merged streams from corrupting disassembly lines

summary: select-order-search can leave a candidate applied to the real source after beam run
body: After merging 5629790dd, I refreshed pcdump and ran `melee-agent debug select-order-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r40<r33,r54<r41 --beam-depth 2 --beam-width 6 --max-probes 24 --campaign-dir /tmp/f654_unwrapped_select_depth2_...`. The command produced no output in the exec session and no visible process remained, but src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c was left mutated to an early-guard-return/block-scope candidate. `python tools/checkdiff.py fn_8003F654 --format summary` dropped from the retained 97.72 baseline to 70.75. I restored fn_8003F654 from `/tmp/f654_after_merge_select_20260531_140320/seed.c`, which returned to 97.72. Desired behavior: all source-scoring/search commands must restore the exact original source on every exit path, including exceptions, timeout, killed child build, and beam composition failures; also consider writing a before/after source hash check and failing loudly if restoration did not happen.
resolution_note: fixed in e332fc0c0: select-order source scoring now registers active source snapshots, restores on SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP, verifies restored content, bounds compile/report/cleanup timeouts, and was smoke-tested with a real plbonuslib candidate source hash restore

summary: match-iter-first proceeds with stale cached pcdump during force-target derivation
body: After editing src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c, running melee-agent debug target match-iter-first -f fn_8003F654 --regs gpr-callee --auto-verify printed '[mwcc_debug] using stale cached pcdump (plbonuslib.c modified since cache; src=14:21:48.102 cache=14:16:32.443). Re-run debug dump local to refresh.' but then continued to derive ambiguous iter-first targets and run auto-verify. For target derivation this can produce stale ig_idx mappings and misleading force-iter/force-phys suggestions. Commands that derive allocator targets should either refresh the pcdump, fail closed like select-order-search's fresh-cache guard, or require an explicit --allow-stale flag.
resolution_note: fixed in e332fc0c0: match-iter-first now requires fresh auto-resolved pcdumps by default and only proceeds with stale allocator facts when --allow-stale-pcdump is explicit

summary: Add granular allocator intervention hooks
body: force-phys helps test final physical-register assignments, but F654 needs more granular allocator experiments. We need hooks that can perturb one allocator decision without rewriting C source blindly. Requested hooks: - Force or block a specific coalesce/copy pair, e.g. prevent r43 -> r40 or force it. - Force simplify/select order for a small virtual set, e.g. require r33 before r40 or r37 before r40. - Force or forbid spilling one virtual. - Scope all interventions to one function. - Produce a first-divergence report explaining whether the intervention reaches the target or which later decision fails. Why this matters: In fn_8003F654, call-return-compare-inverted removes the r40 spill marker but tanks the real score. We need to know whether the desired allocation is unreachable from current allocator inputs, or whether a single coalesce/select/spill decision is the real blocker. Acceptance test idea: Run an intervention on fn_8003F654 that blocks or forces the r43/r40 call-return copy/coalesce and report whether r33<r40/r37<r40 or the final match-relevant allocation becomes reachable.
resolution_note: Shipped in local master commit 7a6b835cc (Add coalesce allocator intervention report): adds debug intervene coalesce plus backend force-coalesce reporting.

summary: Add lowering-phase diff from front-end IR to PCode
body: We can inspect front-end IR with mwcc-inspect and back-end PCode/register allocation with mwcc-debug, but there is no direct tool that explains when two source variants first become meaningfully different across the lowering pipeline. Requested behavior: - Given baseline source and candidate source, produce a staged lowering diff from front-end statement/ENode shape through generated PCode passes. - Identify the earliest stage where the variants diverge in a way relevant to codegen: branch lowering, temp creation, copy insertion, value numbering, copy propagation, block layout, interference graph input, simplify/coalesce/coloring. - Summarize what changed in source terms and PCode terms. - Allow comparing two generated candidate source files from select-order-search or permuter triage. F654 example: call-return compare-chain variants often look plausible. One variant removes the r40 spill marker but regresses to about 70.477%, while several others stay neutral at 97.375. The tooling should answer where the bad variant goes bad instead of requiring manual pcdump reading. Acceptance test idea: Compare fn_8003F654 baseline with call-return-compare-inverted and call-return-compare-copy-in-else. The report should explain why inverted destroys the real match while copy-in-else is neutral, including earliest meaningful pass divergence.
resolution_note: Fixed in 5629790dd: debug inspect diff now renders a LOWERING SUMMARY that classifies the earliest divergence across front-end inspect snapshots, mid-end optimized IR, backend PCode, and RA input/output, and accepts explicit --inspect-a/--inspect-b snapshots so generated candidate pcdumps/sources can be compared with pre-captured mwcc-inspect output. Verified with focused diff report/CLI regressions, broader affected suites, compileall, diff-check, and command-level staged-lowering smoke.

summary: expression-shape search should detect artificial CSE assignments that worsen load order
body: Manual F654 exploration found another improvement that current tooling did not point at. The previous source used `prevPos->x - (avg_sum = pos->x)` inside the sqrt distance expression, apparently to force a temp. Replacing it with the natural squared-distance expression `((prevPos->x - pos->x) * (prevPos->x - pos->x)) + ...` raised checkdiff from 97.64621 to 97.718/97.72 and aligned the first distance load/subtract order better. Desired extension: add an expression-shape/load-order probe class for compound float expressions: remove redundant assignment-in-expression temps, try natural repeated operand forms, introduce named dx/dy temps, and score the earliest float instruction/load-order hunk. The diagnostic should describe when a source-level temp is causing worse scheduling/load order instead of treating the remaining diff as only register allocation or stack layout.
resolution_note: Fixed in b84260ab0: lifetime-layout/source-shape generation now detects assignment-in-expression CSE temps and emits a natural repeated-operand probe, plus dx/dy named temp probes for repeated squared-distance expressions; CLI probe listing smoke verified the new labels.

summary: decl-order search should try adjacent swaps and loop-counter hoist/swap variants
body: Manual F654 exploration found a real improvement that current diagnose/mutate did not discover. Changing the loop counter from a nested `s32 i;` inside the loop block to a function-scope `int i;` declared immediately before `int count;` raised checkdiff from 97.37545 to 97.64621 by fixing the loop-index/count callee-save assignment. `debug inspect diagnose` only tried promote-style decl-order variants and reported no win; after the manual change it sees promoting i/count as regressions. Desired extension: add source-shape probes for adjacent local declaration swaps, nested loop-counter hoist to function scope, loop counter int-vs-s32 changes, and combined hoist+swap variants. The report should call out register role swaps like count/i r25/r26 so agents know this is a declaration-order problem rather than a generic stack-layout plateau.
resolution_note: Fixed in b84260ab0: lifetime-layout/source-shape generation now includes adjacent declaration swaps, nested loop-counter hoist-before/hoist-after variants, and loop-counter int/s32 type probes; CLI probe listing smoke verified the new labels.

summary: build-audit: dtk dol split orphans on worktree-remove and wedges in UE state (unkillable), poisoning concurrent builds
body: Running per-branch report.json builds (cold worktrees, and state diff-remotes --build does the same) and removing the worktree with 'git worktree remove --force' does NOT kill the in-flight 'dtk dol split' child. It reparents to launchd (ppid=1) and keeps running with a now-deleted cwd, eventually wedging in macOS 'UE' state (uninterruptible, trying-to-exit, 0% CPU, kill -9 ineffective). ~28 accumulated across a 30-branch sweep over hours, saturating disk I/O so every subsequent build either ran ~10x slow or timed out (1100s) — a self-inflicted cascade. Repro: build a ref in a worktree, kill ninja or remove the worktree mid-'dtk dol split'. Fix ideas: launch each build in its own process group (setsid) and killpg the WHOLE tree before 'worktree remove'; or have the orchestrator wait for dtk to exit; or add a 'dtk' timeout. Affects state diff-remotes --build robustness under any interruption. Workaround: reboot to clear wedged UE procs.
resolution_note: Fixed in b84260ab0: state diff-remotes --build now runs configure/ninja in owned process groups, kills the group on timeout/KeyboardInterrupt/SIGTERM, and regression tests cover process-group launch/cleanup plus the build path.

summary: select-order-search continues with stale baseline pcdump while scoring current source
body: During the F654 new-baseline campaign, after source changes raised checkdiff to 97.718, select-order-search printed a stale cached pcdump warning because plbonuslib.c was newer than build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/pl/plbonuslib.txt, but it continued to emit allocator-objective rankings and final_match_percent values. That can mix stale select-order/spill objective facts with current source scores, so agents may trust outdated objective results. Desired behavior: auto-refresh cache for the requested TU when source is newer, or fail unless an explicit allow-stale-pcdump flag is passed. Include source/cache timestamps in JSON/ledger metadata.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7294f77f98f4accc07ff660c613c1031bb976795: select-order-search now rejects stale auto-resolved pcdumps by default, supports explicit --allow-stale-pcdump, and records source/cache mtimes in JSON and beam ledger metadata; regression tests added.

summary: mwcc-inspect candidate run exits with empty output and no diagnostics
body: After merging c89f0ce2a, nzxt-local is reachable (`ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 nzxt-local 'echo ok'` prints ok), but candidate-source mwcc-inspect still fails after printing `Running on nzxt-local…`. Commands tried: - `tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh --function fn_8003F654 --output /tmp/f654_inspect_seed_retry2.txt /tmp/f654_select_beam_20260531_003210/seed.c` - `tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh --function fn_8003F654 --output /tmp/f654_inspect_copy_in_else_retry2.txt /tmp/f654_select_beam_20260531_003210/depth-01/d1-0003-call-return-compare-copy-in-else-0.c` Observed: - both commands exit code 1 - output files are created but 0 bytes - no remote compiler/inspector stderr is shown beyond `Running on nzxt-local…` Requested behavior: - preserve/show remote stderr and failing command when inspector exits nonzero - distinguish upload/checkout/compiler/inspector failures - avoid leaving an empty output file without explanation This blocks using issue #193's candidate-source inspection path on fn_8003F654 even when the host is reachable.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7294f77f98f4accc07ff660c613c1031bb976795: mwcc-inspect now captures remote stderr via temp files, reports the failing ssh/stage, preserves partial output, and removes empty final output on remote failure; regression test added.

summary: remote permuter tail follows forever by default with no non-follow option shown
body: After submitting fn_8003F654-coder1-20260531-024100, I ran `melee-agent debug permute remote tail JOB | tail -n 40` expecting a bounded log snapshot. The command invoked remote `tail -n 80 -f .../permuter.log` and kept the local session open until I manually killed the local melee-agent, tail, and ssh processes. For automation/heartbeat use this is risky because it leaves sessions running. Desired behavior: expose a documented non-follow/snapshot mode, e.g. --lines 80 without -f by default, or --follow opt-in; if follow remains default, show that in --help and support a timeout.
resolution_note: Fixed in 7294f77f98f4accc07ff660c613c1031bb976795: remote permuter tail is a bounded snapshot by default, --follow is opt-in and documented in help, and tests cover both command forms.

summary: Support uncommitted/candidate source in mwcc-inspect
body: mwcc-inspect is valuable for ObjObject / ENode / statement structure, but the current workflow is too heavy for decomp micro-campaigns because it requires committed/pushed source on the remote Windows host. F654 needs inspection of many temporary candidates generated by select-order-search and manual probes. Requested behavior: - Inspect uncommitted local source without requiring a commit/push. - Support one of: upload a patch/source snapshot to the remote, run inspector against a temporary worktree, or provide a local inspector path if feasible. - Accept arbitrary candidate source paths, including /tmp select-order-search outputs. - Write outputs next to the normal build/mwcc_inspect location or a caller-provided output path. - Make it safe for multiple agents by using unique remote temp directories. F654 example: We need to compare ObjObject/statement ordering for baseline vs call-return compare-chain variants and composed candidates. The current commit/push requirement makes that too expensive for a 10-50 candidate campaign. Acceptance test idea: Run inspector against fn_8003F654 baseline and a /tmp call-return-compare-copy-in-else candidate without committing either, then diff local variable ObjObject order and statement IR.
resolution_note: Fixed in c89f0ce2a8cd471dd7dc6b5caaae198ac9d3e583 and c6398c811ce53aa981eaa30cf897f685315d7d00: mwcc-inspect accepts candidate source paths, uploads candidate source to unique remote temp trees, copies remote sibling headers, uploads changed local sibling headers, and prepends temp include roots. Verified live on nzxt-local with /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c, producing /tmp/f654-inspect-plbonuslib-blockers.txt with FUNCTION: fn_8003F654, STATEMENTS, and ObjObject sections without committing the worktree.

summary: debug dump remote --no-pull still runs remote git pull
body: While validating #196 end-to-end, `python -m src.cli debug dump remote /Users/mike/code/melee/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --timeout 120 --no-pull` reached nzxt-local but run_pcdump.ps1 still attempted git pull and failed because C:\Users\mikes\code\melee was detached. Root cause appears to be cmd.exe `set VAR=1 && ...` assigning a trailing space before &&, so `$env:MWCC_DEBUG_NO_PULL -ne "1"`. The wrapper should use quoted `set "VAR=value"` env assignments and validate with remote dump smoke.
resolution_note: Fixed in 4edb4a3899dbd3b18404f29b1468291603ff612a: remote dump now emits quoted cmd.exe env assignments (set "VAR=value") so --no-pull reaches run_pcdump.ps1 exactly as value 1. Verified with command-construction regression and live nzxt-local dump using --no-pull.

summary: diagnose decl-order build failures lack actionable details
body: After merging aec4cac6f, `melee-agent debug inspect diagnose fn_8003F654` now reports build failures for simple decl-order promotions that previously scored cleanly/neutral: - promote b34_result: build failed - promote table: build failed - promote avg_sum: build failed The output does not include the generated candidate path or the first compiler error, so the matching agent cannot tell whether this is an invalid source mutation, a transfer problem, or a real source constraint. Earlier runs before this tooling update reported these same promotions as 97.38% neutral rather than build failures. Requested behavior: - If decl-order probes fail to compile inside diagnose, include first compiler error and candidate path. - If the generated promotion is invalid for this source shape, classify it as invalid-probe rather than just build failed. - Preserve previous neutral scoring when the probe is valid. Command: `/opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent debug inspect diagnose fn_8003F654` Worktree: /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers HEAD: aec4cac6f
resolution_note: Fixed in 4edb4a3899dbd3b18404f29b1468291603ff612a: diagnose decl-order build failures now preserve failed candidate source paths, surface first compiler diagnostics, and classify compiler-diagnostic failures as invalid-probe while retaining normal scoring for valid probes. Verified with focused pytest, lint/py_compile, diagnose help, remote pcdump cache generation, and bounded diagnose smoke.

summary: Add general front-end-to-PCode virtual provenance
body: F654 shows the current gap: trace-copy can attribute r40 to the ft_80087B34(entity) call-return chain, but that required a special-case helper. We need a general provenance bridge from front-end constructs to back-end PCode virtuals. Requested behavior: - For any PCode virtual in a function, report the source statement/expression that introduced it when available. - Include the originating ObjObject / ENode / statement ID if mwcc-inspect data is available. - Classify the virtual source: local object, argument, call return, implicit temp, compare temp, address temp, copy/coalesce product, load/store address, etc. - Show first/last PCode use and source span or statement context. - Work for unresolved/spilled virtuals, not only colored/mapped ones. Why this matters: In fn_8003F654, diagnose is now able to name r40 only because call-return copy tracing was added. The same provenance should work for non-call-return temps so agents can stop guessing which source expression affects an allocator node. Acceptance test idea: Run on fn_8003F654 and show r40 -> ft_80087B34(entity) / b34_result, r54 -> Player_GetEntityAtIndex(slot,index), plus useful provenance for r38/r39 or an explicit abstain reason.
resolution_note: Fixed in aec4cac6f: explain-virtual now has --all over all pre-coloring PCode passes and classifies first-def provenance for implicit temps, compare temps, copy/coalesce products, and load/store address forms. Verified against fn_8003F654: --all reports r40 as ft_80087B34(entity) and r54 as Player_GetEntityAtIndex(arg0, arg1).

summary: audit duplicates: redundant/safe detection never fires (always 0); doesn't compare against fresh upstream
body: audit duplicates --all reports redundant=0/safe=0 even though backup/master-pre-pull-rebase carries dozens of 2022-2025 commits long-since merged upstream, and its function-name extraction uses the first commit-message token (yields module prefixes like 'HSD','grstory' instead of real symbols). For PR-assembly the needed signal is: function has a col-0 C body in branch X but only a '/// #name' placeholder or INCLUDE_ASM in fresh upstream/master AND upstream has NO col-0 body (the matched-but-unnamed '/// #' annotation must NOT count as unmatched). state diff-remotes --origin <branch> is closer but its fast source-parse counts any C body as 'matched' (so it flags 99.x% WIP bodies as matches and missed ifStatus_802F6EA4/grGreatBay_801F60C4). Request: an 'audit net-new --origin <branch>' that buckets per-function {net-new 100, net-new <100 WIP, dup-upstream-has-body, dup-matched-but-unnamed} using checkdiff/report.json for the %.
resolution_note: Fixed in ec3cf26fec336c3097d8df93dce1e3d449df415a: added audit net-new --origin/--upstream classification using col-0 C bodies, report.json percentages, placeholder/include-asm detection, and matched-marker duplicate bucketing.

summary: Add composed probe beam search with real-score ranking
body: The current select-order and lifetime probes are useful one at a time, but F654 appears to sit in a basin where many single probes are neutral at 97.375 or obvious regressions. We need a composed search that chains neutral/promising transforms and ranks by real match first. Requested behavior: - Beam search over generated source probes from select-order-search / lifetime-layout / mutate operators. - Keep neutral candidates when they improve secondary diagnostics such as select-order, spill set, coalesce shape, or provenance clarity. - Score every candidate by real checkdiff/match percent first, then allocator objectives. - De-duplicate by function-body fingerprint and source diff fingerprint. - Emit a resumable campaign directory and summary ledger. - Prefer remote execution or generate a remote-permuter seed bundle when candidate count grows. F654 example: call-return-compare-copy-in-else, declaration-use-distance, type-width, block-scope, and narrow-pointer variants can be neutral; inverted removes r40 spill but regresses badly. The useful next search is combinations of neutral transforms plus allocator objectives, not more isolated single probes. Acceptance test idea: Run on fn_8003F654 with targets r33<r40,r37<r40 and produce a ranked list of composed candidates, showing real match percent and allocator deltas for each step in the chain.
resolution_note: fixed in d98b85af1d688c10bf1020aa31afdad0402b40c3: select-order-search now supports --beam-depth/--beam-width composed generated-probe search, real-match-first ranking, function/diff fingerprint dedupe, and a campaign ledger
