satisfied=no actionable=no distance_to_flip=6 missing=- r37<r40: iter 51/49 -> iter 51/49; satisfied=no actionable=no distance_to_flip=3 missing=- final_match_percent: n/a source: /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_b1udvnfy/early-guard-return-0.c 10. block-scope-0 [block-scope] objective: target_order_satisfied=no target_order_improved=no satisfied=0 actionable=0 missing=0 target_spill_removed: - r33<r40: iter 54/49 -> iter 54/49; satisfied=no actionable=no distance_to_flip=6 missing=- r37<r40: iter 51/49 -> iter 51/49; satisfied=no actionable=no distance_to_flip=3 missing=- final_match_percent: n/a source: /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_b1udvnfy/block-scope-0.c still prints for generated probes even though it compiles them and can score the emitted source paths. Example: call-return-compare-inverted-0 reports target_spill_removed r40, but verify shows it is score-neutral at 97.37545. Having final match percent in the ranking would avoid manual verification and prevent agents from overvaluing spill/select-order movement without real-tree improvement.
resolution_note: fixed in a89743b385a9dd53589c45a804d11bfca0b1dd8c: select-order-search now scores source/generated probe final match percent by default, with --no-score-match-percent for faster pcdump-only runs

summary: Add call-return compare-chain source probes to select-order tooling
body: For fn_8003F654, trace-copy/explain-virtual can now attribute r40/r43/r59 to the ft_80087B34(entity) call return at src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c:974. The remaining plateau needs source actuators for a call result used in multiple branch compares (== 1 then == 0) plus nearby lifetime pressure from teammate_table. select-order-search currently reports r40 disappearing/missing as actionable, but it does not generate the relevant source family: switch vs if/else, inverted compare order, copy only in else arm, direct first compare plus copied second compare, and narrowed dependent pointer lifetime. Please add a targeted call-return compare-chain probe family, include provenance in the ranking output, and score generated candidates with final checkdiff/match percent when possible so agents can seed remote permuter campaigns instead of manually writing each variant.
resolution_note: fixed in 85fc8a5905aa93ebcda5d4931f9f091cb4e0b5a2: select-order now generates call-return compare-chain probes, carries provenance into rankings, and can score final match percent with --score-match-percent

summary: debug inspect diagnose should reuse call-return copy-chain provenance
body: For fn_8003F654, debug inspect diagnose reports spilled r40 as '(no source binding or first-def found)', but debug inspect trace-copy --involving r40 can already attribute r40/r43/r59 to the ft_80087B34(entity) call return at src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c:974 with assigned_local=b34_result and use site cmpi cr0,r40,0. Diagnose should reuse that call-return provenance in its spilled-virtual summary and next-step guidance so agents do not treat r40 as an anonymous temp or chase unrelated locals. This complements issue #187's request for select-order probe generation/provenance.
resolution_note: fixed in 85fc8a5905aa93ebcda5d4931f9f091cb4e0b5a2: debug inspect diagnose now reuses explain-virtual call-return copy-chain provenance for spilled virtual hints and next-step guidance

summary: explain-virtual should collapse call-return copy chains to source call expressions
body: After merging 3c5f6f2e4, #185 is fixed: explain-virtual no longer treats colorgraph interferers metadata as an instruction. For the current F654 select-order signal, r40 now traces to real IR: BEFORE GLOBAL OPTIMIZATION B20 has mr r59,r3; mr r43,r59; mr r40,r43 after bl ft_80087B34 in B19, and B33 compares r40 against zero. Commands: melee-agent debug inspect explain-virtual -f fn_8003F654 --virtuals r59,r43,r40 --json and melee-agent debug inspect trace-copy -f fn_8003F654 --involving r40 --list-copies --json. The tools correctly report copy chains r59->r43->r40 and r43->r40, but all three have source=null and virtual-to-var reports found=false, so the actionable select-order signal still has no source handle. In source, this is the ft_80087B34(entity) return assigned through b34_result/b34 and used by the b34==1 / b34==0 branch split. Desired: when a temp chain originates from a call return (r3 after a bl), explain-virtual/trace-copy should collapse the chain to source provenance: call symbol, source line/expression, assigned local if known, and branch/use sites. That would make missing-r40 actionable as a source-level b34_result/b34 lifetime/branch-shape problem instead of another anonymous compiler-temp chain.
resolution_note: fixed in 59bd7c2f01d842fd739852be0f5e4302f9cd5281: mwcc-debug now traces call-return copy chains through mr virtuals to source call expressions and exposes them in explain-virtual/trace-copy.

summary: explain-virtual treats colorgraph interferers text as a virtual first occurrence
body: While following the current F654 select-order signal, debug select-order-search ranks declaration-use-distance as actionable because r40 disappears from r33<r40/r37<r40. To map r40 back to source, I ran: melee-agent debug inspect explain-virtual -f fn_8003F654 --virtuals r37,r40,r33 --json. The r40 record has source=null, live_range=[309,309], and first_occurrence/last_occurrence set to pass AFTER PEEPHOLE FORWARD, block_idx 91, instr_idx 39, opcode="interferers:", operands="40=r6 45=r40 120=r-1 121=r-1". That is not a real defining/use instruction; it is parsed colorgraph/interference text being treated as an instruction occurrence. r37 similarly has no live range/use count despite appearing in the select-order objective. This leaves the only actionable select-order signal (missing r40) without source attribution. Desired: explain-virtual should exclude metadata/interferers lines from occurrence scans and, when an ig has no source binding, report the nearest real IR instruction/def-use context or explicitly say no real occurrence was found. It should also make clear whether the requested id is an ig_idx, virtual register, or class-specific alias when multiple register classes have the same index.
resolution_note: fixed in 3c5f6f2e4: parser stops treating mwcc_debug hook table rows as pcode instructions, and explain-virtual notes allocator-only IG nodes with no real pcode occurrence

summary: declaration-use-distance reapplication can generate invalid nested else block but still rank as actionable
body: After merging d66709ed6, I used the new select-order actionable ranking on F654. First command: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r33<r40,r37<r40 --max-probes 16. It ranks declaration-use-distance-0 as actionable because r40 disappears. Then I used that generated source as a second-step base: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f fn_8003F654 --source-file /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_d8877dfx/declaration-use-distance-0.c --target r33<r40,r37<r40 --max-probes 16. The new declaration-use-distance-0 candidate is reported in the ranking with target-order data (r33<r40 54/48, r37<r40 51/48), but direct verify fails to compile: melee-agent debug permute verify /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_6g5awzln/declaration-use-distance-0.c -f fn_8003F654 --json. MWCC diagnostic points at line 995: } else if (1) {. The generated source nests a second declaration-use-distance block around teammate_slot inside the b34 block, then leaves the else-if paired across the inserted block boundary, producing invalid C. Desired: declaration-use-distance should avoid reapplying across an if/else chain in a way that breaks else binding, and select-order-search should mark generated probes that fail compile/pcdump as build-failed rather than ranking them with target-order iter values.
resolution_note: Fixed in aedc89b2f68e5f4a460bd313e1c0812bc116f789: declaration-use-distance now skips moves whose paragraph crosses a shallower else, extends moved blocks through later uses with balanced brace scope, and select-order source candidates whose compiled pcdump omits the function are marked build-failed. Verified with F654 reapplication and direct verify smokes.

summary: permute verify rejects generated probes for unchanged baseline scalar self-assignment
body: In the plbonuslib-blockers diagnostic worktree, the retained F654 baseline intentionally contains the no-op source-shape clue abs = abs in the xD74 absolute-value else branch. After 34c8c6d91, select-order-search generated probes under /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_bqcv1kiy. Running melee-agent debug permute verify /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_select_order_bqcv1kiy/early-guard-return-0.c -f fn_8003F654 --json exits 7 unsafe-candidate with reason scalar-self-assignment solely because the candidate still contains the unchanged baseline abs = abs. That makes verify unusable for generated probe candidates derived from a diagnostic baseline containing an already-known no-op. Desired: verify should compare candidate text against the current/base function text and only reject newly introduced scalar self-assignments, or select-order-search should emit a base.c/sidecar so verify can distinguish unchanged baseline risks from candidate-introduced risks. The goal is not to bless abs = abs for PR code; it is to keep generated-probe verification actionable during diagnostic iterations.
resolution_note: Fixed in d66709ed6a61c633f648ac82b9ed5f7fe7b8ec4f: permute verify now audits generated candidates against base.c or the current target source and scalar self-assignment risks are compared against the base, so unchanged diagnostic baseline no-ops do not reject; live F654 verify smoke returned status ok with source_risks [].

summary: select-order follow-up: post-34c8 probes still cannot separate F654 r33/r37 from r40
body: After merging 34c8c6d91, issue #181 is resolved and select-order/lifetime-layout now generate early-guard-return and declaration-use-distance probes for F654. They compile, but they still do not move the relevant target order. Repro: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r33<r40,r37<r40 --max-probes 16. Baseline remains r33<r40 iter 54/49 and r37<r40 iter 51/49 unsatisfied. New early-guard-return leaves both unchanged at 54/49 and 51/49. New declaration-use-distance moves b34 down but makes r40 missing in the target comparison (r33<r40 54/? and r37<r40 51/?), not a satisfied/improved order. lifetime-layout with --pairs r37/r40,r43/r33 shows declaration-use-distance changes interference +24/-24 but no frame/spill change and no target pair live/interference movement; early-guard-return and block-scope are zero movement. Need next actuator families that can change this specific allocator order, e.g. b34 path restructuring beyond a local declaration move, loop-local lifetime splitting around the player scan, helper extraction/inlining around the movement accumulation or average update, and/or ranking probes by distance-to-flip when one side disappears so generated changes can be refined instead of treated as n/a.
resolution_note: Fixed in d66709ed6a61c633f648ac82b9ed5f7fe7b8ec4f: select-order objectives now expose missing target virtuals, actionable_movement, and distance_to_flip, and rank missing-side actionable probes above unchanged candidates; live F654 select-order-search ranks declaration-use-distance first with missing r40 actionable for both target pairs.

summary: select-order search needs stronger actuators for coupled virtual-order moves
body: After 6f7203558, debug select-order-search can measure the F654 residual, but current probes do not move the relevant order independently. Repro: melee-agent debug select-order-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r33<r40,r37<r40 --max-probes 8. Baseline has r33<r40 at iter 54/49 unsatisfied and r37<r40 at iter 51/49 unsatisfied. temp-introduction, type-width, and block-scope variants only shift both sides together (for example 60/55, 53/48, 55/50) and never flip the relationship. lifetime-layout likewise changes interference/coalesce counts but leaves target pair live/interference unchanged. Remote permuter candidates from fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-200602 also found no winners over the 97.38 baseline. Request: add actuator families targeted at separating allocator select order for interfering/spilled virtuals, such as declaration/use distance rewrites, helper-inline extraction/insertion, condition/body reshaping around early guard and b34 paths, loop-local lifetime splitting, and source transformations ranked by per-target iter movement rather than only final score. This would turn the select-order diagnostic from measurement into an actionable search path for F654-like allocator residuals.
resolution_note: Fixed in 34c8c6d91: select-order/lifetime-layout probes now include declaration-use-distance and early-guard-return actuators; F654 smoke generated and compiled both probes successfully.

summary: wibo build inlines roll2upvec at one call site where native mwcc does not -> false 33.9% on HSD_CObjGetLeftVector (already matches upstream)
body: REPRO: src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c is byte-identical to upstream/master (verified by diff; no asm/NONMATCHING override, so it MUST compile-match; upstream ships it matched at size 0x27C, frame -0x78). Local checkdiff reports HSD_CObjGetLeftVector match=false 33.92% classification=stack-layout. ROOT CAUSE: MWCC -inline auto makes a per-call-site budget decision for the static helper roll2upvec(). The TARGET object inlines roll2upvec at 2 of 3 sites (HSD_CObjGetUpVector @ line 689, HSD_CObjSetRoll @ line 817 -> frsqrte present) but NOT at the third (HSD_CObjGetLeftVector @ line 736 -> emits 'bl roll2upvec', frame -0x78). The LOCAL macOS wibo build inlines roll2upvec at ALL THREE sites, inflating LeftVector's frame to -0xd8 (+96 bytes) and adding an f31 spill + the whole frsqrte/PSMTXRotAxisRad math, collapsing the score to 33.9%. EVIDENCE the divergence is environmental, not source: - Confirmed with BOTH bundled compilers: mwcceppc.exe (Ninji-patched, md5 079ef43b) and mwcceppc_old.exe (md5 acef694b) -- both inline roll2upvec into LeftVector locally. (mwcceppc_debug.exe also present, md5 c46b3994 -- the mwcc-debug instrumented build.) - Same source + same compiler version (GC/1.2.5n) + identical cflags (-inline auto, -O4,p, ...) as upstream configure.py. - 62 of 68 cobj functions still match 100% under wibo; the 6 near-misses (LeftVector 33.9, SetRoll 99.75, GetUpVector 99.79, SetUpVector 99.80, GetViewingMtxPtr 99.94, GetInvViewingMtxPtr 99.95) are exactly the inlined-helper-frame-slot family -- consistent with wibo computing inline-budget / frame-slot allocation slightly differently at the margins. LEVERS TRIED (none give a clean TU-wide match locally): - #pragma dont_inline on/reset around roll2upvec: LeftVector->100% but roll2upvec->40% (kills its internal GetEyeVector inline), GetUpVector->27%, SetRoll->20%. - #pragma auto_inline off/on around roll2upvec: LeftVector->100%, roll2upvec preserved 99.95% (good), but GetUpVector->27%, SetRoll->20% (target inlines roll2upvec there). - Manual inline of roll2upvec body into GetUpVector: 99.79 -> 56.86% (auto-inlined codegen != hand-inlined). - #pragma inline_max_auto_size(0) around LeftVector: IGNORED (no effect). - Mid-function #pragma dont_inline toggle around the call statement: IGNORED (pragma state read at fn entry, not call site). IMPACT: checkdiff can report a large false-negative for functions whose match hinges on an auto-inline-budget decision that wibo evaluates differently than native mwcc. Suggest: (a) a way to run/spot-check the native compiler for inline-budget-sensitive TUs, or (b) detect/flag 'wibo inlined a callee the reference keeps out-of-line' as a tooling-artifact class rather than a source mismatch.
resolution_note: Fixed in 34c8c6d91: checkdiff now classifies local/wibo inline-boundary divergence as inline-boundary-toolchain-artifact; verified on HSD_CObjGetLeftVector summary smoke.

summary: directed search needs a SELECT-ORDER objective for INTERFERING virtuals — coalesce-search (#153) is the wrong objective for ig-ordering swaps (5460 gobj/gp interfere, cannot coalesce)
body: A whole class of last-mile residuals is a COLORING SELECT-ORDER swap between two virtuals that INTERFERE (live simultaneously), which the current register-aware tooling cannot address. EVIDENCE (grGreatBay_801F5460, this session): the residual is gobj(ig32/r32) wants the callee-save gp(ig33/r33) gets, and vice-versa. coalesce-search (#153) reports they INTERFERE ("r32/r33: coalesced=no; interference yes->yes; live yes->yes") — live across the whole fn, so they CANNOT be coalesced, and `suggest coalesce --discover` correctly reports "no candidate coalesces". So the coalesce objective is the WRONG objective here. The diagnostic `debug target match-iter-first --force-iter-first 34,32,33` PROVES the target is reachable (reproduces the exact register map) — the colorgraph simply selects ig33 before ig32 and the match needs the reverse. `debug permute run` (register-aware) plateaus (4 runs across blend 0.5/1.0) because neither the byte nor IGNode score captures select-ORDER. This is the documented MWCC ig_idx-ordering ceiling class (param-vs-local: the param loses the top callee-save to competing locals) and very likely F654 wall too. ASK: a directed search with a SELECT-ORDER objective — analogous to #153 (coalesce-directed) but ranking candidate source-shapes by whether the colorgraph SELECT-ORDER of the two interfering virtuals flipped (ig32 selected before ig33), using force-iter-first as the oracle for the target order. The coalesce objective only fits NON-interfering virtuals; interfering-virtual ordering needs its own objective. OUTCOME: either finds the C lever for the whole interfering-virtual select-order class (5460, F654, the gr cascades) or proves the source space is empty (a real characterization, not a guess). Complements #153 (coalesce objective), #177 (scorer de-noise), #152 (lifetime).
resolution_note: Fixed in 6f7203558: added debug select-order-search with COLORGRAPH select-order scoring, regression tests, and real pcdump/probe CLI smokes for grGreatBay_801F5460 r32<r33.

summary: lifetime-layout loop-init probe emits unsupported for-init declaration
body: After merging af4bb5dc3, F654 lifetime-layout now compiles block-scope probes, but loop-init still fails. Command: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f fn_8003F654 --pairs r37/r40,r43/r33 --compile-probes --max-probes 8. Output reports: loop-init-0 failed: fn_8003F654 not found in pcdump. The retained source at /var/folders/lz/3mtg0ctx6wb8crp5w9j51hrh0000gn/T/melee_lifetime_layout_7bkoiycc/loop-init-0.c changes s32 i; ... for (i = 0; 6 > i; i++) into for (int i = 0; 6 > i; i++). That is a C99-style for-init declaration and is not valid for the MWCC C mode used by the project, so the probe cannot produce a useful pcdump. Desired: loop-init should use a source shape MWCC accepts, such as moving the declaration to a tight enclosing block before the loop rather than inside the for initializer, or explicitly report the compiler diagnostic rather than only target-omitted.
resolution_note: Fixed in de7566d4ef5e9579bc3a22fda777979eba74aa65: lifetime-layout loop-init probes now use a C89-compatible tight block (decl before for inside an enclosing block) instead of unsupported for-init declarations. Verified with regression tests and the reported fn_8003F654 command in the plbonuslib-blockers worktree: temp-introduction/type-width/block-scope/loop-init all returned status ok.

summary: permuter byte-scorer is dominated by @N reloc noise (HSD_ASSERT strings) that name-magic resolves — should normalize like checkdiff so the real residual is not drowned
body: On HSD_ASSERT-heavy functions the permuter byte-scorer is dominated by anonymous @N relocation-name noise that does NOT block a real match (checkdiff name-magic resolves these at link), drowning the actual residual so the permuter cannot find the real lever. EVIDENCE (this session): HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtr base byte-score = 8521, almost entirely from @N HSD_ASSERT-string relocs; the REAL residual is just a 24-byte frame-slot ORDERING (the only thing blocking match=true). The byte-scorer chases the assert-reloc noise instead of the frame diff, so the permuter cannot find the frame-decl-position lever (the same class of lever that closed grGreatBay_801F60C4 pos). Identical on HSD_CObjGetInvViewingMtxPtr and the rest of the assert-heavy cobj cluster; and a factor on grGreatBay_801F5460 (byte-score 1345, noise-dominated, plateaus). ASK: make the permuter byte-scorer apply the SAME name-magic normalization checkdiff already does — treat anonymous @N reloc names that resolve to the correct symbol as MATCHED, not as diffs — so the score reflects the real residual. Then the register-aware debug permute run can actually target the frame/register residual on assert-heavy functions instead of chasing noise. IMPACT: unblocks the permuter on the entire cobj.c near-match cluster (6 fns at 99.7-99.95%, all frame/reg walls) plus the deferred gr reg-cascade batch (5460, grStadium_801D435C, grfourside). It is the scorer-side complement to the register-aware blend (#152/#153): blend made the scorer see REGISTER distance; this makes the byte component ignore reloc NOISE. VALIDATION (cheap, optional): an HSD_ASSERT-override on one cobj fn de-noises the relocs manually; if the permuter then finds the frame lever, that proves this fix unblocks the cluster.
resolution_note: Fixed in a4b48b1ef3f1da2b06c36e2594eb021e15509129: debug target dtk-objdump now applies checkdiff-style name-magic to a temp copy of candidate/base objects using sibling target.o or build/GALE01/obj before emitting scorer disassembly. Auto name-magic now includes HSD_ASSERT .sdata strings as well as .sdata2 constants. Verified with dtk/o_rewriter/checkdiff regression tests and real cobj.o smoke: default dtk-objdump rewrote anonymous @N assert relocs to HSD_CObj_804D5D40/HSD_CObj_804D5D48 and reduced anonymous reloc names from 24 to 14.

summary: debug mutate lifetime-layout block-scope probe generates invalid C (scopes decls out of body)
body: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f grGreatBay_801F5460 --compile-probes: the block-scope-0 probe emits invalid C — it wraps the local DECLARATIONS in a bare block { HSD_JObj* jobj=...; Ground* gp=...; } and then uses jobj/gp AFTER the block (out of scope), so the function fails to compile and the probe reports 'grGreatBay_801F5460 not found in pcdump'. The 'tighten one local decl AND following statement into a block' should include the USES in the block, not just the decls. Source retained at /tmp/claude-501/melee_lifetime_layout_*/block-scope-0.c. (call-arg-tempization-0 also failed the same compile-omitted-function way.) The probe IDEAS (block-scope, call-arg-tempization) are good and directly relevant to gr/ register-swap functions; just the codegen of the block-scope variant is wrong.
resolution_note: Fixed in af4bb5dc3c598cec049771dc529563d2c4465d1b: lifetime-layout block-scope probes now wrap leading declarations through their last in-block use, and call-argument-tempization no longer treats pointer member access (->) as arithmetic or collapses multi-arg calls. Verified with red/green regressions, focused pressure explorer tests/static checks, and grGreatBay_801F5460 --compile-probes smoke using a valid cached pcdump: block-scope-0 status ok.

summary: debug permute verify does not pass base.c to semantic-risk audit
body: After merging #174 fix commit 331c684a6, direct audit of nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-195201/output-1330-2/source.c with base_text from nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/base.c correctly returns unsafe-candidate / semantic-risk-high / manual-abs-sign-flip. But 'melee-agent debug permute verify <that source.c> -f fn_8003F654 --json' still reports status=ok, semantic_risk_bucket=plausible-C-shape, source_risks=[]. Code inspection shows verify_perm calls candidate_audit.audit_candidate_source(candidate_text) without passing base_text, while triage passes base_text = read_candidate_base_text(perm_dir). This leaves direct verify and any workflows built on it able to present known sign-breaking candidates as clean. Expected: verify should resolve/read candidate base.c the same way triage does and pass it into audit_candidate_source.
resolution_note: Fixed in 5b86b826de08f09bbbf3c912a875a2f3f821e7c3: debug permute verify now reads nearby base.c and passes it into candidate_audit.audit_candidate_source, so base-dependent semantic risks such as manual-abs-sign-flip are rejected. Verified with regression test, focused audit/verify tests, static checks, and temp-copy smoke of the reported fn_8003F654 candidate returning unsafe-candidate/manual-abs-sign-flip.

summary: semantic-risk classifier misses sign-breaking abs candidate
body: Remote run fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-195201 produced output-1330-2/source.c. Real-tree triage reported it as a WIN at 96.89% over the then-current 96.74% baseline, and its sidecar says semantic_risk_bucket=plausible-C-shape with source_risks=[]. But the candidate changes the xD74 horizontal abs path from behavior-preserving abs(pos->x - prevPos->x) to an unconditional sign flip after the conditional abs: if (abs < 0.0f) abs = -abs; abs = -abs; table->xD74 += abs;. This makes positive deltas negative and should be semantic-risk-high (or unsafe), not plausible-C. The useful retainable clue was the explicit else/no-op source shape, but the scored candidate itself is behavior-changing and should not be presented as clean.
resolution_note: fixed in 331c684a6: candidate audit now rejects manual absolute-value guard followed by unconditional negation as manual-abs-sign-flip; verified against reported F654 output-1330-2 and triage smoke

summary: remote repair function sync copies nested remote-runs and stale outputs
body: End-to-end validation after #170/#172: remote doctor --repair with a matcher function dir reached the function-dir rsync and started copying nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs plus prior output-* history into the persistent remote function dir. Repair only needs current base/target/settings/compile artifacts for preflight/fresh submit; copying remote-runs is slow and can pollute the persistent permuter dir.
resolution_note: fixed in ceeb4b870: repair function sync and submit staging now exclude remote-runs/ and output-* history; coder1 repair showed filtered rsync and fresh submit still produced candidates

summary: remote permuter scorer: dtk-objdump given RELATIVE target.o path, fails 'object file not found' (residual after #165)
body: Progress: #165 fix added '--melee-root' to the staged objdump_command. But remote jobs STILL die. permuter.log: subprocess.CalledProcessError on ['melee-agent','debug','target','dtk-objdump','--melee-root','/home/discord/melee','remote-runs/<job>/nonmatchings/<fn>/target.o'] exit 2, message 'object file not found: remote-runs/<job>/.../target.o'. Root cause: the scorer (scorer.py _objdump) invokes objdump_command with cwd=/home/discord/decomp-permuter and passes the target.o as a path RELATIVE to that cwd ('remote-runs/.../target.o'). But 'debug target dtk-objdump --melee-root X' resolves the O_FILE relative to the melee root (or otherwise mis-resolves), so it can't find the file. PROOF on coder3: the same command with an ABSOLUTE O_FILE path (/home/discord/decomp-permuter/remote-runs/.../target.o) works and disassembles correctly; the relative one fails with 'object file not found'. Fix: dtk-objdump should resolve a relative O_FILE against the process CWD (not melee-root), OR remote submit should stage an objdump_command that passes the target.o as an absolute path. This is the last blocker for remote permuting on the gr/ reachability-proven cases (grGreatBay_801F5460 etc.). LOCAL permuter works (relative path resolves against the worktree cwd which IS the melee root).
resolution_note: fixed in ceeb4b870: dtk-objdump resolves relative target.o paths against --object-root; verified on coder1 from a remote function cwd and with a fresh remote submit

summary: remote doctor repair overloads perm-root and can sync matcher worktree as decomp-permuter
body: End-to-end validation after #170: running remote doctor --repair with --perm-root pointing at a matcher worktree resolved the function dir correctly but passed that same root as local_perm_root to repair_target, starting an rsync of the matcher tree into the remote decomp-permuter root. The CLI needs to resolve the decomp-permuter code checkout separately from the function artifact root, like debug permute run already does.
resolution_note: fixed in ceeb4b870: remote doctor --repair now resolves decomp-permuter code root separately from matcher/candidate --perm-root; coder1 repair passed with matcher artifact root

summary: remote permuter submit omits target.o from staged remote-run function dir
body: After merging 325602e07 (#167) and running remote doctor --repair, coder1 preflight passes with a Linux dtk and a real target.o smoke command. I submitted a fresh dtk-backed F654 job: melee-agent debug permute remote submit -f fn_8003F654 --target coder1 --threads 16, job fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-193805. The job immediately stopped. Remote log shows dtk-objdump now has the right command shape, but fails with: object file not found: remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-193805/nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o. Local function dir does have target.o (nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o, 6200 bytes), and doctor preflight had checked /home/discord/permuter-work/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o, not the remote-run copy. Fetching the job shows output dirs and remote-run/permuter.log but no staged nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o in the fetched remote-run. This blocks fresh remote permuter runs after the dtk fixes. Submit/staging should copy target.o (and likely settings/compile/base as needed) into the remote-run function dir, or the scorer objdump path should point at the preflighted persistent target.o path.
resolution_note: fixed in ceeb4b870: remote submit now stages a dtk-objdump command with --object-root, live coder1 F654 job produced candidates, and temp fetch includes remote-run/nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o

summary: Note for #165: the #159 dtk-objdump switch likely REGRESSES the F654 stream's working coder1 — the #165 fix must verify coder1, not just coder2/coder3
body: ADDENDUM to #165 (remote permuter dtk-objdump cannot resolve melee-root from the run dir). A cross-stream REGRESSION risk the #165 fix must account for: TIMELINE: - BEFORE the #159 fix, the F654 plbonuslib stream pulled SCORED remote candidates from coder1 all session (remote-run dir fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111, with best_pct/delta values). So coder1 was WORKING on the OLD scorer path (devkitPPC powerpc-eabi-objdump, present on coder1 provisioned tree /home/discord/permuter-work/melee). - The #159 fix switched the remote scorer to "melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump". - #165: that new dtk-objdump path cannot find a melee-root from cwd = the permuter run dir under /home/discord/decomp-permuter, so it dies ("could not find a melee root"). IMPLICATION: once the F654 stream merges the #159 change into its branch, its previously-working coder1 remote permuter switches to the broken dtk-objdump path and hits #165 — silently breaking the ONE remote node that was working. So #165 is not merely "coder2/coder3 never worked"; it can REGRESS coder1. #165 FIX ACCEPTANCE should therefore include: 1. Verify dtk-objdump root resolution works on coder1 specifically (the provisioned /home/discord/permuter-work/melee tree), not only coder2/coder3. 2. Bake an explicit --root (or env) into the staged objdump_command so the scorer resolves the melee-root regardless of cwd (the run dir under decomp-permuter). 3. Ideally confirm an in-flight F654 coder1 run still scores after the change. Flagged from cross-stream observation (I manage the gr/ stream; the F654 stream is separate and may not see this). Worth verifying BEFORE the F654 stream merges the #159 commit.
resolution_note: Overtaken/subsumed by #171. This note flagged the RISK that the #159 dtk-objdump switch would regress remote scoring (incl. coder1). That risk is now a PROVEN concrete blocker: #171 shows the #165 fix passes target.o as a RELATIVE path that dtk-objdump --melee-root resolves against the melee root (not the scorer cwd) -> object-not-found on coder3 (and by the same mechanism all nodes incl coder1). #171 is the actionable fix (resolve relative O_FILE against cwd, or stage absolute path). Nothing further needed here.

summary: remote dtk repair syncs non-Linux dtk causing Exec format error
body: After merging 01f433da5 (#166), melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f fn_8003F654 --repair now emits useful failure detail. The repaired remote preflight fails the real dtk-objdump smoke test with: rc=1 command=/home/discord/.local/bin/melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump --melee-root /home/discord/permuter-work/melee target=/home/discord/permuter-work/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o. The captured traceback shows dtk_objdump.py calling /home/discord/permuter-work/melee/build/tools/dtk elf disasm ..., and subprocess raises OSError(8, 'Exec format error'). This means the repair path has successfully synced a dtk file, but it is not executable on the Linux remote (likely the local macOS build/tools/dtk). This blocks fresh dtk-backed remote permuter runs for F654. Remote repair should provision a Linux-compatible dtk binary on coder targets, or dtk-objdump/remote submit should use a remote-native project tool path instead of copying the host binary.
resolution_note: Fixed in 325602e07: remote repair no longer syncs host build/tools/dtk; it downloads a Linux dtk release on the remote and coder1 repair/doctor now passes the dtk-objdump smoke.

summary: debug permute run fails: ModuleNotFoundError 'src.compiler'
body: melee-agent debug permute run -f grGreatBay_801F5460 --perm-root <worktree> -j 4 --blend 0.3 fails immediately with 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named src.compiler'. The Tier-2 blended-scoring runner (which is exactly what's needed for register-cascade functions where the raw permuter's byte-scorer is misled by anonymous @N reloc-name noise) can't import decomp-permuter's src.compiler — likely a sys.path / cwd issue (the permuter modules resolve relative to /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter, but the runner invokes from elsewhere). Raw ./permuter.py from the decomp-permuter dir works fine. Repro: the exact command above after + bootstrap. Impact: can't use mwcc-debug-blended scoring to target register diffs; stuck with byte-only scoring that plateaus on reloc noise for grGreatBay_801F5460 (gobj/gp callee-save swap) and grGreatBay_801F63F4 (loop-counter cascade).
resolution_note: Fixed in 325602e07: debug permute run now resolves a real decomp-permuter code root separately from matcher nonmatchings roots, avoiding ModuleNotFoundError: src.compiler.

summary: remote dtk-objdump smoke preflight fails with empty diagnostic after #164
body: After merging 03455c3f8 (#164 fix) and running worktree-doctor, melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f fn_8003F654 --repair now syncs the project dtk and repaired remote tooling, but exits 2 with: FAIL remote objdump command required (empty detail). Re-running without --repair reproduces the same blank failure. This is distinct from #164's root-discovery error: #164 is resolved in the issue DB, and the preflight now tries to smoke-test target.o, but when that smoke test fails it emits no stderr/stdout detail. Current settings.toml still has objdump_command = "melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump"; remote doctor reports local objdump command OK and remote root/tooling OK, then fails only the remote objdump command with blank detail. This blocks submitting a fresh dtk-backed F654 remote job, and the empty detail gives no way to distinguish missing target.o, dtk invocation failure, quoting/env issue, or a wrapper crash. Please surface the exact remote command, rc, stdout/stderr, and target.o path, and ensure doctor uses the same rewritten command that submit will stage (including MELEE_ROOT/--melee-root handling).
resolution_note: Fixed in 01f433da5: remote doctor uses the same --melee-root dtk-objdump command as submit and failure details now include rc, command, target.o path, and captured stdout/stderr.

summary: remote permuter scorer: dtk-objdump can't find melee root from remote-run dir (regression after #159 fix)
body: After #159 fix (settings.toml uses 'melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump') and 'remote doctor --repair' syncs dtk to /home/discord/melee/build/tools/dtk, remote permuter jobs STILL fail. permuter.log: subprocess.CalledProcessError on ['melee-agent','debug','target','dtk-objdump','remote-runs/.../target.o'] exit 2. Root cause: the scorer runs objdump_command with cwd = the permuter run dir (/home/discord/decomp-permuter/remote-runs/<job>/nonmatchings/<fn>), and 'debug target dtk-objdump' searches UPWARD from cwd for a melee root containing build/tools/dtk — that dir is under /home/discord/decomp-permuter, NOT under /home/discord/melee, so the search fails: 'could not find a melee root with build/tools/dtk; checked: /home/discord/decomp-permuter/... , /home/discord, /'. dtk DOES exist at /home/discord/melee/build/tools/dtk. Fix options: make 'debug target dtk-objdump' accept/resolve an explicit melee-root (e.g. MELEE_ROOT env or --root) and have remote submit set it to the remote melee root; OR bake an absolute dtk path into the staged objdump_command for remote runs. Repro: submit any fn to coder3 after repair; tail the log. LOCAL permuter works (dtk found via cwd in the worktree).
resolution_note: Duplicate root cause fixed in 03455c3f8: dtk-objdump honors MELEE_ROOT/--melee-root and remote submit stages objdump_command with --melee-root <remote melee root>.

summary: mwcc-debug: coalesce-directed source search -- probe source shapes to make target virtuals coalesce, rank by virtual-relationship improvement not match %
body: CONTEXT: debug suggest coalesce (suggest-coalesce-source) finds useful leads but only as passive WARNINGS. The next tool turns them into an active directed search with the RIGHT objective function. ASK: generate source-shape probes specifically aimed at making a TARGET virtual relationship coalesce naturally under STOCK mwcc (e.g. r37=r40, r43=r33), driving the transform operators from #152, and RANK candidates by the INTERMEDIATE OBJECTIVE -- "did the intended virtual relationship improve / did the target pair coalesce / did interference at that pair drop?" -- NOT only by final match percent. WHY THE OBJECTIVE MATTERS (the core point): ranking by final match% is exactly the blind-volume trap that wasted 38k+ iterations and that keeps surfacing attractive-but-non-retainable candidates. An intermediate objective (the target coalesce happened, or the spill set shrank at the right virtual) gives the search a GRADIENT even when match% is flat, and it rejects "score went up for the wrong reason" candidates. This closes the feedback loop the matcher actually needs: "this source expression caused this virtual/lifetime/spill behavior" -> search source shapes against THAT objective, not against the terminal reward. DEPENDS ON: #151 (attribution: which source produces the target virtuals and why they interfere) and #152 (the transform operators + pressure diagnostics). PRECEDENT: the resolved #141 suggest-schedule-source is the scheduling analog; extend the same shape to coalescing. ACCEPTANCE: a command like debug coalesce-search -f fn_8003F654 --target r37=r40 emits ranked source-shape probes, each scored by whether the target pair coalesced / interference dropped, with final match% only as a secondary tiebreaker; at least one probe is mechanically applicable to plbonuslib.c. NOTE: capstone of the register-pressure analog of #138-141. Search-narrowing, not a claim F654 is unmatchable; keep F654 in the pool.
resolution_note: Fixed in 03455c3f8: added debug coalesce-search with generated/candidate source-shape probes ranked by target coalescence/interference/spill objective before terminal match percent.

summary: remote dtk-objdump preflight passes help but scorer fails target.o root discovery
body: After merging 70ccb7b99, melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f fn_8003F654 --repair passes all checks, including REPAIR synced project dtk and PASS remote objdump command: /home/discord/.local/bin/melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump --help. I stopped the stale F654 job and submitted a fresh one with: melee-agent debug permute remote submit -f fn_8003F654 --target coder1 --threads 16. It started as fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-184029 but immediately stopped. The remote log shows permuter invoking ['melee-agent', 'debug', 'target', 'dtk-objdump', 'remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-184029/nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/target.o']; dtk-objdump exits 2 with: could not find a melee root with build/tools/dtk; checked /home/discord/permuter-work/melee/tools/melee-agent, /home/discord/permuter-work/melee/tools, /home/discord/permuter-work/melee, ... . This means the doctor only validates --help and misses the real scorer path. The remote settings rewrite should probably include an explicit --melee-root /home/discord/permuter-work/melee for dtk-objdump, or dtk-objdump should honor MELEE_ROOT, and doctor should smoke-test the actual target.o command rather than --help only. Fresh job produced only 5 outputs before stopping; triage found 0 winners, best 96.38 below 96.74 baseline.
resolution_note: Fixed in 03455c3f8: remote dtk-objdump now honors MELEE_ROOT/--melee-root and remote doctor probes target.o root discovery instead of only --help.

summary: checkdiff: offset-0 ...data.N anchor not normalized against named global at same .data:0x0 (ADDR16_HA/LO)
body: checkdiff reports a relocation mismatch when the expected .o references a named global (e.g. R_PPC_ADDR16_HA/LO grI2_803E4A60) at .data:0x0 but my .o references the synthetic section anchor (...data.0) at the SAME .data:0x0. dtk's elf disasm shows both '.sym ...data.0, local' (size 0x0) and '.obj grI2_803E4A60, global' (size 0x54) co-located at .data:0x0; dtk picks the zero-size local for the reloc display. Since both resolve to the identical link-time address (.data+0+addend), these should be treated as matched for ADDR16_HA/LO the same way --normalize-reloc handles the +2 SDA21 quirk. Currently this adds 2 spurious diff lines per affected function (HA + LO) and can block match=true even when codegen is byte-identical. Affects grInishie2_801FD9EC, grStadium_801D435C, grInishie2_801FD4F0 (grI2_804D6A00 / grPs_803E1248 anchors). Repro: python tools/checkdiff.py grInishie2_801FD9EC --no-tty --format plain (see lines +004/+01c). Request: normalize an offset-0 section anchor (...data.N) to the named global occupying the same section offset, or count them equivalent when final addresses match.
resolution_note: Fixed in 70ccb7b99: checkdiff now maps zero-size local section anchors such as ...data.0 to co-located global objects before diffing, covering ADDR16_HA/LO anchor aliases.

summary: remote permuter repair does not provision project dtk for dtk-objdump scorer
body: After merging 42a424d24 (#162 fix), the previous remote doctor failures are gone: local compile runner now passes as staged remote Linux wibo and remote melee-agent wrapper resolves. The F654 permuter dir has settings.toml objdump_command = "melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump". New blocker: melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f fn_8003F654 exits 2 with FAIL remote objdump command: /home/discord/permuter-work/melee/build/tools/dtk missing or not executable. Running the same command with --repair performs the standard sync steps (tools/melee-agent, tools/mwcc_debug, MWCC debug compiler, decomp-permuter, function dir, python deps, Linux wibo, wrapper) but still ends with the same missing remote build/tools/dtk failure. This blocks submitting a fresh F654 remote job using the new project dtk-objdump scoring path. Repair/preflight likely needs to sync or build the project dtk binary under the remote melee root, or dtk-objdump needs an alternate remote dtk location.
resolution_note: Fixed in 70ccb7b99: remote permuter repair now requires local build/tools/dtk, creates remote build/tools, and rsyncs the project dtk before doctor checks.

summary: remote permuter doctor rejects fresh dtk-objdump bootstrap on coder1
body: After merging 0fce9d658 and running worktree-doctor, I regenerated F654 with: melee-agent debug permute bootstrap -f fn_8003F654 --force --json. The regenerated settings.toml correctly contains objdump_command = "melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump". But remote readiness still fails: melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f fn_8003F654 exits 2 with FAIL local compile runner: compile.sh still requires wine after remote rewrite, and FAIL remote objdump command: melee-agent not found or not executable. This is on coder1, where the same doctor output reports PASS remote melee-agent: /home/discord/.local/bin/melee-agent and PASS remote Linux wibo. Running --repair syncs tools, resets/syncs the function dir, refreshes the wrapper, then reports the same failures. This blocks submitting a fresh F654 remote job using the new project dtk-objdump scoring path. The doctor/rewrite path probably needs to use the discovered absolute remote melee-agent wrapper for objdump/scorer commands and rewrite the fixed compile.sh to force remote wibo instead of treating the local wine fallback block as remote-ready.

summary: remote permuter #159 is coder3/coder2-specific, NOT all-nodes: coder1 is provisioned + in active use; provision coder2/coder3 or add an objdump preflight
body: SHARPENS #159 (do not re-file it; this corrects its SCOPE). #159 verified coder3 lacks /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump and concluded remote scoring is broken for ALL functions / both modes / gates the entire effort. That conclusion is an overgeneralization from one node. EVIDENCE: the F654 plbonuslib stream has been pulling SCORED remote candidates all session from coder1 (remote-run dir fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111, with best_pct / delta scores). Scoring requires objdump+dtk, so coder1 IS provisioned and working RIGHT NOW. ROOT CAUSE (config-level): permuter-remotes.toml shows coder1 uses a DISTINCT provisioned tree (remote_perm_root=/home/discord/permuter-work/decomp-permuter, remote_melee_root=/home/discord/permuter-work/melee), while coder2 AND coder3 share /home/discord/decomp-permuter + /home/discord/melee -- which on coder3 has no built dtk/objdump (grstream verified). coder2 shares coder3 config paths, so is almost certainly also unprovisioned (resolver should verify coder2). REAL FIX (one or both): 1. Provision the built toolchain (dtk + devkitPPC powerpc-eabi-objdump) on coder2 and coder3 so all three nodes are usable in parallel -- lets multiple streams permute without contending on coder1. 2. Make "debug permute remote submit" PREFLIGHT-check for the scorer binary on the chosen target and either refuse with a clear message (e.g. "target coder3 missing objdump/dtk -- use coder1") or auto-failover to a healthy node, instead of dying mid-run with a bare FileNotFoundError. IMPACT CORRECTION: the permuter is NOT unusable for all functions. coder1 works and is in active use by F654. The gr/ stream simply pointed at an unprovisioned node. Until coder2/coder3 are provisioned, route gr/ permuter runs to coder1 (sharing throughput with F654) or wait. The reachability-proven grGreatBay_801F5460 is the priority re-run once a healthy node is free.
resolution_note: Fixed in 4ebabb32d9c5c8ef7fd8db7466bfef671d7b5c72: remote submit now runs remote doctor preflight on the staged permuter dir before writing job metadata, rsync, or tmux; dtk-objdump settings require the remote melee build/tools/dtk to exist, so coder2/coder3 fail fast instead of starting broken jobs.

summary: local permuter: bootstrap compile.sh uses wine (fails on macOS), not wibo
body: After 'melee-agent debug permute bootstrap -f grGreatBay_801F5460', running the local permuter (./permuter.py nonmatchings/grGreatBay_801F5460) fails to compile base.c with: 'wineserver: Can't check in server_mach_port / wine: the wine server failed to run / Unable to compile base.c'. The patched compile.sh stages the source but the underlying mwcc invocation still calls wine rather than the wibo path used by 'debug dump local'. On macOS this makes the LOCAL permuter unusable for short smoke/triage runs (manager guidance is to triage locally before spending remote cores). Expected: bootstrap should emit a compile.sh that uses build/tools/wibo (or melee-harness wibo) the same way checkdiff/dump local do. Repro: bootstrap then run permuter.py on the dir. Workaround used: submitted to remote (coder3) instead, which works.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0fce9d658c6abaa653e75fcc528d43e5256e7054: permute bootstrap/fix-compile now rewrites import.py compile.sh to stage source and invoke local wibo via "" build/compilers/.../mwcceppc.exe instead of wine.

summary: remote permuter: scorer dies with FileNotFoundError on /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump (missing on coder3)
body: After 'melee-agent debug permute remote submit -f grGreatBay_801F5460 --target coder3', the remote permuter.log shows the scorer crashes immediately: 'FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump' (scorer.py _objdump). Verified via ssh that coder3 has NO powerpc-eabi-objdump (not at that path, not in PATH) and no built dtk under /home/discord/melee/build/tools. The bootstrap-generated settings.toml hardcodes objdump_command='/opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump -dr -EB -mpowerpc -M broadway'; older remote-runs settings (gm_80173EEC, lbDvd) use the same dead path, so remote permuter scoring appears broken for ALL functions on coder3. Combined with #158 (local permuter wine failure on macOS), the permuter is currently UNUSABLE in this environment for the gr/ register-cascade walls. Request: bootstrap/remote-submit should set objdump_command to a tool that exists on the remote (build dtk on remote and use 'dtk elf disasm', or ship/point to a powerpc-eabi-objdump), and remote doctor should verify the objdump_command resolves on the target before submit.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0fce9d658c6abaa653e75fcc528d43e5256e7054: generated/staged settings.toml now uses melee-agent debug target dtk-objdump, remote submit stages that objdump command, and remote doctor executes the full command help path instead of only checking /opt/devkitpro powerpc-eabi-objdump.

summary: match-iter-first: param-copy (addi rX,rN,0) targets show 'no structural match in pre-coloring'
body: debug target match-iter-first emits 'no structural match in pre-coloring for addi r31, r6, 0x0' for parameter-copy moves. The expected .s uses 'addi rX, rN, 0' (a register copy of a param) but pre-coloring represents these as 'mr rX, rN' virtuals, so the structural matcher fails to find them and drops those targets from the force-iter-first vector (only the 'mr rX,r5' target survives). For grGreatBay_801F60C4 only 1 of 3 callee-save targets was recovered (r30<-ig34), making the tool unusable for the common param-vs-local ordering case. Expected: treat 'addi rX,rN,0' in expected .s as equivalent to 'mr rX,rN' when matching against pre-coloring virtuals. Repro: melee-agent debug target match-iter-first -f grGreatBay_801F60C4
resolution_note: Fixed in 68f2e6ba99dc1da8202cda186d37b6e85ccb2ae8: match-iter-first normalizes expected addi rD,rS,0 copies to pre-color mr signatures, so grGreatBay_801F60C4 now recovers force_iter_first 33,34,32.

summary: lifetime-layout pressure explorer drops SPILLED markers and masks generated-probe failures
body: After merging 98d5caccf, ran: melee-agent debug mutate lifetime-layout -f fn_8003F654 --pairs r37/r40,r43/r33 --max-probes 4 --compile-probes. Baseline prints 'spills=-', but the same current pcdump via 'melee-agent debug inspect stuck fn_8003F654' reports unexpected SPILLED markers r37, r38, r39, r40, r54. Since #152 acceptance specifically asks for spill-set deltas, lifetime-layout should either use the same spill marker model as inspect stuck or label that it is reporting only a narrower allocator spill_set. The same run generated block-scope-0 and loop-init-0, both failing only with 'fn_8003F654 not found in pcdump'; if those probes failed to compile or changed preprocessing enough to omit the function, the output should surface the real compiler/variant diagnostic and keep the generated source path or diff for triage. Current output makes it look like the target disappeared rather than explaining why the mechanically generated probe is unusable.
resolution_note: Fixed in 27bd198d567c831f3e9e056b41f4a4f4e010d6d8: lifetime-layout pressure signatures now report all simplifygraph SPILLED markers like inspect stuck, and failed source probes retain/report the generated .c path with a target-omitted diagnostic.

summary: mwcc-debug: lifetime/layout perturbation operators with spill-set / frame / saved-reg / interference attribution
body: CONTEXT: F654 is a register/stack-PRESSURE problem, so the useful source operators are NOT scheduler nudges -- they are controlled LIFETIME/LAYOUT transforms. The matcher needs a library of these plus per-transform pressure attribution. ASK: a perturbation explorer that applies controlled operators to the target function and, for EACH, reports the MWCC pressure-effect diagnostics. Operators to include: - temp introduction / removal - narrowing / widening locals (type width changes) - block scoping (tighten/loosen a local lifetime) - loop-init variants - condition nesting / un-nesting - call-argument tempization (hoist an arg into a named temp) For each applied transform, report which of these CHANGED: the spill set, the frame size, the set of saved (callee) registers, or the virtual interference graph. The deliverable is the ATTRIBUTION (transform -> pressure delta), not merely a new match percent. DEPENDS ON: #151 (virtual -> source attribution) to interpret WHICH virtuals each transform moved (e.g. did this temp removal relieve interference at r37/r40?). ACCEPTANCE: for fn_8003F654, the explorer lists each applied transform with its spill-set delta, frame-size delta, saved-reg delta, and interference change, so the operator that relieves pressure at r37/r40 or r43/r33 is identifiable. NOTE: these are the "actuators" that the coalesce-directed search (filed next) drives. Part of the register-pressure analog of the scheduling layer #138-141. Search-narrowing; keep F654 in the pool.
resolution_note: Fixed in 98d5caccf: added debug mutate lifetime-layout pressure explorer. It generates lifetime/layout probe families and scores candidate pcdumps/sources by frame-size, saved-reg, spill-set, coalesce, interference-edge, and target-pair deltas; verified on fn_8003F654 artifacts.

summary: mwcc-debug residual 'ceiling' terminology in command/help/guidance should be taxonomy-neutral
body: During the F654 heartbeat after master 788d3bd18, 'melee-agent debug inspect --help' still lists 'ceiling' as a command and describes it as 'Current-tooling diagnosis', while 'debug inspect guide -f fn_8003F654' tells agents to run 'debug inspect ceiling fn_8003F654'. The user explicitly wants to remove/replace language like 'ceiling'/'C-source unmatchable' because agents infer impossibility and defer work. Ask: keep backwards-compatible aliases if needed, but change user-facing command descriptions, guide/stuck output, skill docs, and diagnostics to a taxonomy-neutral category such as 'current-tooling diagnosis', 'source-shape search needed', 'allocator/scheduler attribution gap', or another explicit non-impossibility classification. Acceptance: normal help/guidance paths for F654 no longer tell agents they hit a 'ceiling' except maybe in hidden/backcompat alias text, and the output explains what evidence is missing or which tooling layer is needed next.
resolution_note: Fixed in 98d5caccf: visible inspect command is now debug inspect diagnose, guide/stuck/docs point at diagnose, and debug inspect ceiling remains only as hidden compatibility alias.

summary: mwcc-debug: virtual-register -> source attribution (vreg -> pcdump block -> source stmt/local -> lifetime/interference reason)
body: CONTEXT: F654 is a STACK/REGISTER-PRESSURE problem -- not a scheduler problem, and not a raw-permutation-volume problem. Reliable state is 96.74%; the only higher candidate seen was a false lead that mutated an external prototype (rejected by the resolved #150). The bottleneck is ATTRIBUTION: diagnostics name the pressure points as virtuals (r37/r40, r43/r33) but leave the matcher manually guessing which C statement/lifetime creates each one. ASK: a tool that maps, for a named function: virtual register -> the pcdump block(s) where it is live -> the source statement / local / expression that defines and uses it -> the lifetime/interference REASON (which other virtuals it interferes with, and why it cannot share a color or coalesce). This is the REGISTER-ALLOCATION analog of the resolved #139 (which mapped scheduled instructions back to IR node + source). Reuse the #139 instruction->source plumbing; add the virtual-register-centric view plus the interference/lifetime attribution. WHY: this is the FOUNDATION for any directed source search. The coalesce-directed search and the lifetime/layout explorer (filed as siblings) both need to know WHICH source construct produces a given virtual and WHY it interferes. Without it, every register-pressure fix is blind guessing. ACCEPTANCE: for fn_8003F654, a command prints, for r37/r40 and r43/r33: the defining source statement/local (file:line + expression text), the live range (pcdump blocks), and the interference reason (what forces them apart so they cannot coalesce). NOTE: Tier 1 of a register-pressure attribution layer that parallels the scheduling explanation layer (#138-141). Search-narrowing for F654, not a claim F654 is unmatchable; keep F654 in the pool.
resolution_note: Fixed in 788d3bd18: added debug inspect explain-virtual to map requested virtuals/pairs to pre-color live blocks, source locals/field expressions, colorgraph interferers, and pair live/interference reasons; verified on fn_8003F654 r37/r40 and r43/r33.

summary: decomp-permuter triage: semantic-risk classifier -- bucket candidates (repo-invalid / semantic-risk-high / plausible-C) + add sign-correctness/abs detection
body: CONTEXT: permuter candidates often improve score by source-hostile means: sign-breaking abs() changes, impossible prototype changes, unnatural no-op perturbations. Two specific cases are already POINT-rejected -- the resolved #150 (external prototype/declaration mutations) and the resolved #136 (compound no-op memory writes: field += 0, field -= 0, field *= 1). But those are ad-hoc per-pattern rejections, and the matcher still has to eyeball the rest. ASK: generalize the point-rejections into a semantic-risk CLASSIFIER that outputs an explicit bucket per candidate, instead of a binary ok/reject: - repo-invalid: will not survive a real repo build (external decl/prototype/header/out-of-target-function changes). Already covered by #150 -- fold it in. - semantic-risk-high: builds, but source-hostile. Generalize #136 (no-op perturbations) and ADD sign-correctness / abs-mutation detection (e.g. swapping a signed compare or wrapping a value in abs to gain bytes), unnatural casts, and self-assignment-class no-ops. - plausible-C-shape: a legitimate source reshape worth retaining. Report "score improved but semantic/source risk high" SEPARATELY from "plausible C shape improved" so the matcher spends attention only on plausible-C candidates. WHY: this directly reduces the blind candidate VOLUME the matcher is drowning in -- attractive-but-non-retainable noise gets labeled, not hand-triaged. PRECEDENTS TO GENERALIZE (do NOT re-file): #150 (resolved), #136 (resolved). This issue is the generalization into a bucketed classifier plus the new sign-correctness dimension. ACCEPTANCE: triage --json emits a semantic_risk_bucket per candidate; a sign-breaking abs change and a no-op perturbation both land in semantic-risk-high (not ok); a genuine source reshape lands in plausible-C; #150/#136 cases still rejected via the unified classifier. NOTE: triage-side companion to the attribution/search layer (#151/#152/#153). Reduces volume; keep F654 in the pool.
resolution_note: fixed in c8e3718c97a5678d44964935551beb61c085a189: candidate audit/triage now emit semantic_risk_bucket repo-invalid/semantic-risk-high/plausible-C-shape, classify external prototype and build/read failures as repo-invalid, no-op and abs/sign-cast mutations as semantic-risk-high, and include buckets in sidecars and triage JSON

summary: permuter triage misses unsafe external prototype mutations
body: While triaging remote job fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111 with: melee-agent debug permute triage nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111 --function fn_8003F654 --resume --order newest --max-candidates 10 --json, candidate output-1250-90/source.c was reported ok with source_risks: [] and best_pct 96.888084. The candidate diff mutates the known external declaration from void Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset(Vec3 *) to volatile unsigned int Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset(Vec3 *), plus semantic-breaking local changes. Applying the equivalent prototype mutation in the real worktree makes report regeneration/full build fail when src/melee/gr/stage.c redeclares the function as void. Triage should flag candidates that alter declarations/prototypes outside the target function or otherwise require TU context changes that cannot survive a repo build, instead of presenting them as clean ok/best candidates.
resolution_note: fixed in 4dd81f12aa33c120b491485ff56c33f62229bc34: candidate audit now compares top-level prototypes against base.c (including ancestor permuter roots for remote-runs) and triage rejects candidates that mutate external declarations

summary: decomp-permuter randomizer intermittently crashes with typemap KeyError on local block variables
body: With remote job fn_8003F294-coder1-20260530-143111 running from the current plbonuslib nonmatching source, the permuter repeatedly logs internal permuter failure tracebacks but continues. Examples: randomizer perm_mult_zero / perm_add_sub / perm_temp_for_expr call ast_types.expr_type, then KeyError: 't2' or KeyError: 'table'. The source uses local block variables such as pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t* t2 inside an inner block and table at function scope. Repro seed printed in the log: --seed 50823758196696320101. This is not currently killing the job, but it consumes iterations and hides whether a pass family is systematically unusable on common decomp C shapes. Desired: randomizer/typemap should either model these local block variables or skip the mutation cleanly without traceback spam.
resolution_note: fixed in decomp-permuter d372cac: Candidate now refreshes the current FuncDef before/after randomization so typemap builds over the same function object the randomizer walks; added stale-function regression and verified reported F294 seed

summary: mwcc-debug suggest-schedule-source: emit candidate C reshapes from a divergent scheduler decision (extend suggest-coalesce-source)
body: PRECEDENT: "debug suggest-coalesce-source" already does exactly this for COALESCING -- IR-grounded checkers that map a forced coalesce to candidate C-source patterns plus hints. It works and is in the tree. This issue is the SCHEDULING analog. ASK: given a divergent scheduler decision (#140) plus its IR/source provenance (#138 + #139), emit ranked candidate C-source reshapes likely to flip the decision under STOCK mwcc. Reshape families to check: reorder the two field reads; split or merge the enclosing statement; route one read through a separate pointer or temp (force a distinct load path); change subexpression evaluation order; reorder struct fields when that changes natural emission order. Each suggestion cites the divergent decision it targets. WHY: these become DIRECTED permuter seeds / hand edits instead of blind shuffle -- blind shuffle has already failed 38k+ iterations on F294. Narrowing "shuffle everything" to "these two reads, this property" is the whole point. HONEST CAVEAT (please implement): if #140 reports the divergence is a large GAP (not a TIE), the suggester must SAY the lever is structural / large rather than emit a cosmetic shuffle. That yields a principled "not found via <these reshapes>, needs structural change" -- a real characterization, not more wasted cycles, and never a claim the function is unmatchable. DEPENDS ON: #138, #139, #140. ACCEPTANCE: "debug suggest-schedule-source -f fn_8003F294" lists ranked source-reshape candidates with rationale tied to the divergent decision; at least one is mechanically applicable to src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c. This is Tier 4 (capstone) of the explanation-hook layer.
resolution_note: fixed in bf63a3ba7: added debug suggest schedule plus hidden suggest-schedule-source alias, ranked scheduler source reshapes, JSON/text output, and regression tests

summary: remote permuter triage needs resume/skip-known candidates for active jobs
body: While focusing on fn_8003F654 in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, the active remote job fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111 fetched 414 candidates. debug permute triage starts from output-* lexicographic order every run, so each heartbeat re-real-compiles candidates already seen in previous triage/fetch cycles. I stopped the batch after ~182/414 with no transferring improvement; most results were known classes: unsafe memory no-ops/self-assignments, inline_fn placeholder leaks, or real-tree build failures from redeclared locals like abs/dist/b34/teammate_slot. The command has --max-candidates but no --resume, --offset, --since-output, --skip-status-json, or persisted triage ledger. This blocks efficient use of active remote permuter jobs because fresh candidates are buried behind old work and local resources are spent repeating old compiles. Desired behavior: persist per-candidate triage status under the remote run directory and support skipping already-triaged candidates plus optionally sorting newest/highest-score first.
resolution_note: fixed in 08e5db10b: debug permute triage now supports --resume/--skip-status-json to skip terminal triage sidecars before --max-candidates, records skipped candidates in JSON/text summaries, and supports --order name|newest|score-desc|score-asc so active remote jobs can prioritize fresh outputs

summary: explain-schedule (#138) reports a positional proxy + hardcoded priority, not the scheduler decision margin -- degenerate on F294 adjacent loads
body: SHARPENS #138 (and gates #139-#141, which build on its margin). The in-progress Tier 1 implementation in tools/melee-agent/src/mwcc_debug/schedule_explain.py satisfies the LETTER of #138 (prints contested pairs + TIE/GAP verdict + margin) but not the INTENT, and as written it is misleading for the exact function it targets. CONCRETE DEFECTS (as built): 1. The "priority" field is a HARDCODED CONSTANT. _explain_rule_in_pass passes priority=0 / priority=1 literally into the candidate records (the _candidate(...) calls and the inline ScheduleCandidate), then render_text prints "priority=N". No scheduler priority is read anywhere; parse_pcdump does not carry one. An agent reading "priority=0" will reasonably believe it is a real scheduler priority. It is not. 2. The "margin" is OUTPUT ADJACENCY DISTANCE, not the scheduler decision margin. Classification is purely positional over the FINAL CODE AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING pass: adjacent load pair (either order) -> verdict=TIE margin=0; one intervening instruction -> verdict=GAP margin=1. This measures the OUTCOME, not the DECISION. Two loads can be emitted adjacent yet have had a large internal priority gap, or be one-apart yet be a pure tie the scheduler broke by slotting an unrelated ready instruction between them. The proxy can be backwards from the truth. WHY THIS BREAKS THE KEYSTONE ON F294: F294 contested loads are ADJACENT same-base lwz. So this command ALWAYS returns "TIE margin=0, small source-order nudges may flip this" for them -- a constant, non-discriminating verdict. That verdict is directly contradicted by 38k+ permuter iterations that already failed to flip the order with nudges. As built, #138 would hand the matching agent false confidence and send it back to the blind nudging that has already failed -- the opposite of its purpose. The whole reason #138 is the gate is to tell TIE (cheap C lever likely) from GAP (structural) for ADJACENT pairs; an adjacency proxy gives zero signal for the adjacent case. REQUIRED ACCEPTANCE (hard tests the rebuild must pass): - The margin MUST derive from the scheduler real per-candidate priority/cost at the DECISION point (the ready-list pick), not from output adjacency. A hardcoded priority field is disallowed. - For an ADJACENT contested pair (the F294 case) the verdict MUST NOT be a constant; it must distinguish a true near-tie from a decisive preference using real decision data. If the data cannot currently distinguish them, the command must SAY "priority data unavailable" rather than assert TIE. - Running it on fn_8003F294 must NOT emit "small source-order nudges may flip this" unless grounded in a real near-tie margin (it is empirically false for F294 after 38k+ failed nudges). OPEN QUESTION THE REBUILD MUST RESOLVE: Are the per-candidate scheduler priorities extractable from any existing dump pass, or do they require new instrumentation at the scheduler hook site (the heavier thing #138 originally asked for: log candidate priority/cost + the heuristic field that broke the tie, in observe-only mode)? - If extractable from a dump: parse the real values; drop the hardcoded field. - If NOT extractable: the honest Tier 1 cut is to RELABEL the current command as an adjacency HEURISTIC (rename the field away from "priority"; state the limitation in output) and file/own the hook-site priority-logging as the real #138 core. Do not present an adjacency proxy as a decision margin. EPISTEMIC NOTE: the failure mode here is shipping a confident-looking TIE/GAP verdict that is not grounded in the mechanism. That is exactly what the explanation layer exists to avoid; the tooling must not manufacture a false "cheap lever exists" signal.
resolution_note: Overtaken by events + converges with #143. (1) #143 already relabeled the misleading priority field as a heuristic, addressing defect 1 (the priority=N label that implied real scheduler data). (2) F294 schedule half is now SOLVED naturally in shippable C (assignment-expression lever in plbonuslib.c), found empirically WITHOUT needing a true decision-margin verdict, so the F294 adjacent-load degeneracy (defect 2) no longer blocks anything. (3) The deeper ask -- a TRUE TIE/GAP margin read from real scheduler priorities at the hook site rather than output adjacency -- remains technically valid but has NO current consumer: F294 schedule is done, F654 is blocked on frame/register-allocation not scheduling. Per the case-driven principle, defer building the real hook-site decision-margin until a specific function is actually blocked on schedule guidance, rather than speculatively. Reopen/refile at that point. This is an honest deferral, NOT a claim the adjacency proxy is correct.

summary: explain-schedule provenance misses global field expressions
body: After merging fd9265099, debug inspect explain-schedule for fn_8003F294 annotates the six solved schedule loads with ir node ids and field_offset values, but source_confidence remains ir-node and no source=file:line/expr is emitted even with --source-file src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c. These loads correspond to obvious global field expressions in the current source, e.g. pl_804D6470->x94, pl_804D6470->x90, pl_804D6470->xAC, etc. The provenance bridge appears to depend on a source variable binding for the base virtual; global/SDA pointer bases like pl_804D6470 do not get a base_var, so _find_source_expression is never called. Desired: when base binding is absent but the load offset matches a global struct field access in the provided source, report source=file:line:col and expr=pl_804D6470->xNN instead of only field_offset/source_confidence=ir-node. This would complete the source-actionable output promised by #139 for global config tables.
resolution_note: fixed in 61372b437: explain-schedule now falls back to global-looking source field expressions such as pl_804D6470->x94 when IR base virtuals have no local binding, emitting source file/line/expr/base_var provenance for scheduler load candidates

summary: mwcc-debug: differential decision-trace -- diff real-source vs forced-path scheduler decisions, report first divergence + margin
body: PROBLEM: we already have BYTE-level first-divergence. The missing level is DECISION-level. We can force the target schedule and we can name the loads (#138/#139), but we still do not see the exact decision where the real source parts ways from the target, nor how decisively. ASK: compile the real source and the forced path, capture the scheduler decision trace for BOTH (from the #138 logging), and diff the TRACES rather than the bytes. Report the FIRST decision where real-path and forced-path diverge -- that decision IS the mechanism. Pair it with the TIE/GAP margin (#138) and the source expression (#139). OUTPUT shape: "at scheduling step K, real source picked load X (priority P_x); forced path picked load Y (priority P_y); margin = P_x - P_y; divergence traces to source expr E at file:line." That single line collapses the whole blind search into one actionable fact per function. DEPENDS ON: #138 (decision logging) and #139 (source annotation). ACCEPTANCE: a command like "debug diff-schedule -f fn_8003F294 --against <forced.o>" prints the first divergent decision, its margin, and the source expression. This is Tier 3 of the 4-part explanation-hook layer.
resolution_note: fixed in 09ffd8f30df513f416a7ddefc27a8f05a007aa01: added debug diff-schedule to compare real vs forced pcdumps through schedule explanation records, report the first divergent scheduler-window decision, real/forced picked loads, heuristic margin/window gap, and source provenance from #139

summary: checkdiff should flag manual unused stack padding arrays like PAD_STACK
body: After c49fed5c2, replacing diagnostic PAD_STACK(24) in fn_8003F294 with an equivalent manual local `UNUSED unsigned char natural_stack_probe[24];` still reports match=true 100.00 instruction-identical, but checkdiff no longer emits diagnostic_pad_stack/source_guidance. This is the same non-semantic stack reservation as PAD_STACK and can let agents hide diagnostic padding behind a named unused local. Desired: detect simple unused byte arrays / local padding-shaped declarations that only affect frame size, or at least flag suspicious UNUSED char arrays whose size equals a known stack-frame delta. The pre-commit hook warns about manual padding, but checkdiff is where agents decide whether a source variant is a real win.
resolution_note: fixed in fd92650990988bb65b8a4bd50a53553db33fe5ea: checkdiff diagnostic stack-padding detection now treats simple UNUSED char/u8/s8 arrays such as natural_stack_probe[24] like PAD_STACK probes, preserving diagnostic_pad_stack/source_guidance on matching runs

summary: mwcc-debug: map scheduled/forced instructions back to IR node + source expression (file/line/col + field/offset)
body: PROBLEM: even once we know WHICH loads to reorder (issue #138 / force-schedule), "swap loads 3 and 4" is not actionable in C, because we do not know which source expression each load came from. The agent and permuter are reordering anonymous instructions blindly. OPPORTUNITY: the source-line / position tables we need are ALREADY present in the debug DLL we hook. MWDBG326 exists precisely to map object back to source; we are simply not reading it out. ASK: extend the explain/dump output (built in #138) so each instruction of interest (the contested loads, the forced ops) is annotated with: IR node id, source (file, line, col), and where available the expression / lvalue text or the struct field + numeric offset being loaded. RESULT: "swap the two lwz" becomes "reorder the reads of obj->field_a (line N) and obj->field_b (line N+1)" -- a lever an agent or human can act on, and a directed target the permuter can be steered toward instead of shuffling the whole function. DEPENDS ON: #138 (reuse its hook plumbing and per-instruction record). ACCEPTANCE: explain output for fn_8003F294 annotates each contested lwz with its source line and the field/offset it loads, so the 6 swaps can be named as specific C reads. This is Tier 2 of the 4-part explanation-hook layer.
resolution_note: fixed in 7b546011f030739609b648b6d04260d252eab206: debug inspect explain-schedule now annotates load candidates with advisory source provenance by mapping final scheduled loads back to pre-color IR nodes, base virtual/source variable, source file:line:col where the field access is found, expression, and numeric field offset; --source-file can override the auto-resolved repo source

summary: explain-schedule misses already-target straddled load windows
body: After merging 62877276e and applying the F294 source probe that makes checkdiff report 100.00 with PAD_STACK(24), reran melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c and then melee-agent debug inspect explain-schedule -f fn_8003F294 --force-schedule lwz:0x94>0x90,lwz:0xAC>0xA8,lwz:0xC8>0xC4,lwz:0x1C>0x18,lwz:0x30>0x2C,lwz:0x134>0x130. The last three adjacent swaps report already-target, but the first three report status=missing. These first three are the GAP/straddled form where target order is high load, intervening addi r9, low load. The implementation recognizes observed low, middle, high as matched GAP but does not recognize already-target high, middle, low. Desired: report these as already-target with heuristic_verdict=GAP/window_gap=1 instead of missing, so solved source does not look unexplained.
resolution_note: fixed in c49fed5c2774aed3c153e2d858c1dc66e85b7a19: explain-schedule now recognizes already-target one-instruction-straddled load windows as status=already-target heuristic_verdict=GAP window_gap=1

summary: need source-level guidance for stack reservation deltas after PAD_STACK probe
body: After merging 2a2d09706 and using explain-schedule, fn_8003F294 scheduler mismatches are solved naturally by assigning the high threshold arguments inside the pl_8003906C calls. With the retained diagnostic PAD_STACK(24), python tools/checkdiff.py fn_8003F294 --summary reports match=true 100.00. Removing PAD_STACK(24) drops to 99.95 stack-layout only: expected frame 56 bytes, current frame 32 bytes. Current checkdiff correctly diagnoses the frame delta and suggests a PAD_STACK probe, but the remaining task is finding natural C that makes MWCC reserve those 24 bytes.
