Desired tooling: once a PAD_STACK(N) probe reaches match or near-match, provide source-level guidance for natural frame reservation levers: stack slot map, local/temporary/inlined-call candidates, lifetime clues, or an automated mutation/search mode for harmless locals/volatile address-taken temps that change frame size without perturbing codegen. This should explicitly distinguish diagnostic PAD_STACK from shippable source.
resolution_note: fixed in c49fed5c2774aed3c153e2d858c1dc66e85b7a19: checkdiff now flags diagnostic PAD_STACK probes in matched summaries/compact output and adds natural stack-reservation source guidance for frame deltas

summary: explain-schedule priority field is heuristic, not MWCC scheduler cost
body: After merging 2a2d09706, melee-agent debug inspect explain-schedule -f fn_8003F294 --force-schedule lwz:0x94>0x90,lwz:0xAC>0xA8,lwz:0xC8>0xC4,lwz:0x1C>0x18,lwz:0x30>0x2C,lwz:0x134>0x130 prints priority=0/1 and TIE/GAP margins. Reading tools/melee-agent/src/mwcc_debug/schedule_explain.py shows these priorities are synthetic local pattern values from adjacent vs one-intervening instruction windows, not MWCC computed scheduler priorities or costs. This is still useful as a heuristic, but the label can mislead agents into thinking the compiler decision rationale is known. Either rename fields to heuristic_rank/window_gap and label verdicts heuristic, or extend the pcdump/debug hook to report actual scheduler candidate costs/ready-list decisions.
resolution_note: fixed in 62877276e05366602daaf9428f08b01c7a85175e: explain-schedule now labels outputs as heuristic_verdict, window_gap, and heuristic_rank instead of implying MWCC internal scheduler priority/cost.

summary: remote fetch overwrites verified candidate failure status
body: In plbonuslib-blockers after master 58a738d10, remote fetch for fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111 showed output-1050-1 sidecar as status=ok with no source risks, even though this candidate was already known to fail real-tree verify. Rerunning melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111/output-1050-1/source.c -f fn_8003F654 --json immediately returns status=build-failed at f32 dist and rewrites melee-agent-candidate-status.json to build-failed. A later fetch should not downgrade/overwrite stronger verification results like build-failed, nonreproducible, or unsafe with source-audit-only ok. Preserve existing status precedence or record separate fetch_audit vs real_tree_verify fields.
resolution_note: fixed in 220fb028b7c80519f1d2dd8a43d56d10d81e7ad3: fetch candidate audit now preserves stronger existing sidecar statuses like build-failed/nonreproducible/unsafe and records the new fetch result under fetch_audit instead of downgrading to ok.

summary: mwcc-debug explain-schedule: log scheduler decision rationale (TIE vs GAP) at the force-schedule hook site
body: PROBLEM: force-schedule / force-phys / force-coalesce are OVERRIDE hooks. They clamp the decision OUTPUT and prove a target codegen is reachable, but reveal nothing about WHY stock MWCC chose differently or what C source would flip it. The plbonuslib work is now bottlenecked exactly here. F294 is fully decomposed: PAD_STACK(24) frame reservation + 6 lwz HI/LO schedule swaps == byte match, proven by staging the forced object into the build slot (stock production = 99.58%). But no C source has been found that makes STOCK mwcc emit those 6 swaps, and the permuter has searched blind (38k+ iterations, many ties, no win). The FRAME half is tracked separately in #131; this issue is the SCHEDULE/source half. ASK: at the SAME scheduler hook site force-schedule already owns, add a passive READ-ONLY logging mode, e.g. "debug explain-schedule -f FN". For each scheduling decision among ready instructions (especially same-base adjacent loads), dump: the candidate instructions, each candidate computed priority/cost, and which heuristic field broke the tie. Do NOT override anything; reuse the force-schedule plumbing in observe-only mode. CRITICAL OUTPUT: for each contested swap, classify TIE (candidates had equal / near-equal priority, so a small IR nudge can flip it) vs GAP (one candidate had decisively higher priority, so flipping needs a structural source change) and report the numeric margin. This is currently unmeasurable, and it determines whether a cheap C-source lever even exists for F294 / F654. ACCEPTANCE: "debug explain-schedule -f fn_8003F294" prints, for the contested lwz pairs, each candidate priority + a TIE/GAP verdict + margin. NOTE: goal is to convert reachable-via-override into findable-from-C-source. This is search-narrowing, not a claim either function is unmatchable; keep both in the pool. This is Tier 1 of a 4-part explanation-hook layer (rationale -> source-map -> diff-trace -> source-suggester).
resolution_note: fixed in 2a2d09706f20cc7903315b3365aa115f249f7e96: added debug inspect explain-schedule, which analyzes a pcdump with a force-schedule target list and reports each contested load window with candidate priorities, TIE/GAP verdict, margin, rationale, and JSON/text output.

summary: checkdiff/mwcc-debug should auto-probe stack-frame deltas with forced schedule
body: On branch codex/plbonuslib-blockers at master 44ec7d74, fn_8003F294 baseline was 99.52% with opcode similarity 100% and a -56 vs -32 frame-size delta. Adding diagnostic PAD_STACK(24) raises production checkdiff to 99.6% and aligns the frame/save slots; running mwcc-debug local with the existing force-schedule swaps (lwz:0x94>0x90,lwz:0xAC>0xA8,lwz:0xC8>0xC4,lwz:0x1C>0x18,lwz:0x30>0x2C,lwz:0x134>0x130) then reports MATCH. This proves the remaining mismatch decomposes into exact stack reservation + known scheduler swaps. Desired: when checkdiff sees opcode-identical stack-layout diffs, offer or run a non-mutating diagnostic pass that computes the frame delta, temporarily injects/simulates PAD_STACK(N), optionally composes with force-schedule, and records 'diagnostic match: frame-reservation + schedule' so agents know to search for natural source that reserves N bytes instead of treating the function as blocked.
resolution_note: fixed in 58a738d10ae9f423f5c8402f40dd6a5c5640ac21: checkdiff now detects opcode-identical stack-frame deltas, computes missing reservation bytes, emits a PAD_STACK(N) diagnostic probe hint, and explains how to compose that with forced scheduler swaps as frame-reservation + schedule evidence.

summary: debug dump local --diff deadlocks on forced-object lock
body: After merging master 93ffafda2, reran: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --force-schedule-fn fn_8003F294 --force-schedule lwz:0x94>0x90,lwz:0xAC>0xA8,lwz:0xC8>0xC4,lwz:0x1C>0x18,lwz:0x30>0x2C,lwz:0x134>0x130 --diff -f fn_8003F294 --output /tmp/f294_forced_93ff.txt --keep-obj /tmp/f294_forced_93ff.o --no-cache-sync. Output reached [diff] running checkdiff... target function fn_8003F294, then checkdiff printed waiting for repo-wide checkdiff build/report lock and remained blocked for more than 15 seconds. pgrep showed parent melee-agent plus child tools/checkdiff.py fn_8003F294 --format plain --no-build. This looks like the outer debug dump command now acquires the repo-wide lock before invoking checkdiff, but checkdiff tries to acquire the same non-reentrant lock. Killed both processes. Desired: either pass a no-lock mode to the child checkdiff while the parent holds the lock, or use a reentrant/shared lock protocol so integrated forced --diff cannot self-deadlock.
resolution_note: fixed in 34e99108e86556c6e437a5a6587c71094de07b06: integrated forced --diff still holds the parent checkdiff lock while staging, but passes CHECKDIFF_NO_LOCK=1 to the child checkdiff to avoid self-deadlock.

summary: candidate audit misses compound no-op memory writes
body: After merging master 10e1af171 candidate risk classification, remote F654 candidate output-955-1 is marked status=ok by fetch/candidate_audit and debug permute verify compiles it with baseline_pct=96.74368, new_pct=96.77256, delta=0.02888. The candidate source includes table->xD8C += 0; in the early ftLib_8008732C return path. This is the same class of diagnostic/no-op source perturbation as memory self-assignment and should not be presented as an OK candidate. Classify compound no-op memory writes such as field += 0, field -= 0, or field *= 1 as unsafe or at least diagnostic-only with sidecar status. Candidate path: nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111/output-955-1/source.c
resolution_note: fixed in 8435468ea53ce81d32bbd3ac773be01a34e37160: candidate audit now rejects compound no-op memory writes such as field += 0, field -= 0, and field *= 1 as unsafe-candidate.

summary: debug dump local --diff must lock around forced object staging
body: Clean version of #132/#133. On codex/plbonuslib-blockers at 2b736307a with diagnostic PAD_STACK(24), the forced object is correct: running debug dump local with --force-schedule-fn fn_8003F294 and schedule spec lwz:0x94>0x90,lwz:0xAC>0xA8,lwz:0xC8>0xC4,lwz:0x1C>0x18,lwz:0x30>0x2C,lwz:0x134>0x130 plus --keep-obj /tmp/f294_forced_aftermerge.o emits a pcdump with [FORCE_SCHEDULE] applied 6 adjacent load swaps. Manually copying /tmp/f294_forced_aftermerge.o to build/GALE01/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.o and running python tools/checkdiff.py fn_8003F294 --format plain --no-build --no-fingerprint reports MATCH. However, integrated --diff can report the unforced load-order mismatch when a normal checkdiff is running in parallel. Root cause: debug dump local copies the forced object into the shared build object path before invoking checkdiff, but the repo-wide checkdiff lock is only acquired inside checkdiff. A parallel checkdiff can acquire the lock first and rebuild or overwrite the staged forced object. Desired fix: acquire the same repo-wide lock before staging the forced object and hold it until checkdiff finishes, or add a checkdiff option that diffs an explicit object path without using the shared build slot.
resolution_note: fixed in 93ffafda2f82dbf9dccf22d9767090c355e78e7d: debug dump local --diff acquires the repo-wide checkdiff lock before staging forced objects and holds it through checkdiff and restore.

summary: remote permuter saved F654 candidate that scores but fails real-tree compile
body: Remote job fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111 fetched output-1190-1 after master 44ec7d74. Running melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111/output-1190-1/source.c -f fn_8003F654 failed in the real worktree compile and reverted source. Candidate source removes the assignment to abs in the ftLib_800865CC(entity)==0 branch, leaving only ';' then if ((pos->x - prevPos->x) < 0.0f) abs = -abs; table->xD74 += abs;. MWCC reports the first diagnostic at the f32 abs declaration. The remote output directory implies this candidate was accepted/scored remotely, so verify/remote staging should either prevent such candidates from being saved as scored outputs or tag them as non-reproducible/compile-failed with the real diagnostic.
resolution_note: fixed in 10e1af171e7f8dd8c08de0ae24615a835228d195: verify/triage now write melee-agent-candidate-status.json for build-failed/nonreproducible candidates with diagnostic context.

summary: decomp-permuter should classify semantic-risk source perturbation candidates
body: Remote run fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111 produced candidate families where the match score alone is not enough to decide whether to keep them. output-1155-1 verifies at 96.74% and is behavior-preserving but diagnostic-only: it adds a local no-op assignment (abs = abs) plus an else-if(1) wrapper. output-1155-2 verifies at the same 96.74% but uses abs = (abs = -abs), which modifies the same scalar twice in one full expression and should be treated as undefined/unsafe C even if MWCC accepts it. Earlier output-950-1 improved to 96.78% via a pointer field self-assignment before an early return and was reverted as unsafe. The verify/permuter workflow should flag/reject UB or memory side-effect no-ops, and separately tag behavior-preserving local perturbations as diagnostic-only so agents can retain or discard them deliberately.
resolution_note: fixed in 10e1af171e7f8dd8c08de0ae24615a835228d195: candidate audit rejects unsafe repeated scalar assignments and memory self-assignments, tags scalar self-assignments diagnostic-only, and includes source_risks in verify/triage JSON.

summary: decomp-permuter emitted unresolved inline_fn placeholder in saved candidate
body: Remote job fn_8003F294-coder1-20260530-143111 produced nonmatchings/fn_8003F294/remote-runs/fn_8003F294-coder1-20260530-143111/output-125-2/source.c containing unresolved placeholder inline_fn: it defines 'inline unsigned int inline_fn(pl_804D6470_t *arg0)' and uses inline_fn(pl_804D6470) in a pl_8003906C argument. melee-agent debug permute verify correctly aborts with '[verify-perm] ABORT: permuter placeholder(s) detected in candidate source: inline_fn (2 occurrences)' exit 7. Desired: decomp-permuter should not save candidates with unresolved placeholder helper names, or should mark them invalid before output-* promotion.
resolution_note: fixed in 10e1af171e7f8dd8c08de0ae24615a835228d195: remote fetch now audits output-* candidate sources, and verify/triage persist corrupt placeholder status sidecars before build.

summary: candidate audit misses compound no-op memory writes
body: After merging master 10e1af171 (candidate risk classification), remote F654 candidate output-955-1 is marked status=ok by fetch/ candidate_audit and compiles with baseline_pct=96.74368, new_pct=96.77256, delta=0.02888. The candidate source includes in the early ftLib_8008732C return path. This is the same class of diagnostic/no-op source perturbation as memory self-assignment and should not be presented as an OK candidate; classify compound no-op memory writes (, likely , , etc.) as unsafe or at least diagnostic-only with sidecar status. Candidate path: nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/remote-runs/fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-143111/output-955-1/source.c
resolution_note: Superseded by corrected report; original body lost code fragments because shell command substitution ate backticked examples.

summary: debug dump local --diff races with parallel checkdiff when staging forced object
body: Refines/supersedes #132. Repro on codex/plbonuslib-blockers at 2b736307a with diagnostic PAD_STACK(24): start a normal checkdiff in parallel with --- MATCH: fn_8003F294 matches! --- [diff] MATCH — function bytes are identical.. The pcdump logs , but integrated --diff can report the unforced load-order mismatch. Manual check proves the forced object itself is correct: , copy that object to , then run --- expected +++ current @@ -43,9 +43,9 @@ +084: R_PPC_EMB_SDA21 pl_804D6470 +088: 7c 68 1b 78 mr r8,r3 +08c: 7f c3 f3 78 mr r3,r30 -+090: 80 e4 00 94 lwz r7,148(r4) ++090: 80 c4 00 90 lwz r6,144(r4) +094: 39 3f 0d a0 addi r9,r31,3488 -+098: 80 c4 00 90 lwz r6,144(r4) ++098: 80 e4 00 94 lwz r7,148(r4) +09c: 38 80 00 45 li r4,69 +0a0: 38 a0 00 00 li r5,0 +0a4: 48 00 00 01 bl <fn_8003F294+0xa4> @@ -57,9 +57,9 @@ +0b0: R_PPC_EMB_SDA21 pl_804D6470 +0b4: 7c 68 1b 78 mr r8,r3 +0b8: 7f c3 f3 78 mr r3,r30 -+0bc: 80 e4 00 ac lwz r7,172(r4) ++0bc: 80 c4 00 a8 lwz r6,168(r4) +0c0: 39 3f 0d a4 addi r9,r31,3492 -+0c4: 80 c4 00 a8 lwz r6,168(r4) ++0c4: 80 e4 00 ac lwz r7,172(r4) +0c8: 38 80 00 49 li r4,73 +0cc: 38 a0 00 00 li r5,0 +0d0: 48 00 00 01 bl <fn_8003F294+0xd0> @@ -72,9 +72,9 @@ +0e0: R_PPC_EMB_SDA21 pl_804D6470 +0e4: 7c 68 1b 78 mr r8,r3 +0e8: 7f c3 f3 78 mr r3,r30 -+0ec: 80 e4 00 c8 lwz r7,200(r4) ++0ec: 80 c4 00 c4 lwz r6,196(r4) +0f0: 39 3f 0d a8 addi r9,r31,3496 -+0f4: 80 c4 00 c4 lwz r6,196(r4) ++0f4: 80 e4 00 c8 lwz r7,200(r4) +0f8: 38 80 00 54 li r4,84 +0fc: 38 a0 00 00 li r5,0 +100: 48 00 00 01 bl <fn_8003F294+0x100> @@ -84,8 +84,8 @@ +108: 7f c3 f3 78 mr r3,r30 +10c: 81 1d 01 ac lwz r8,428(r29) +110: 38 bf 0d c8 addi r5,r31,3528 -+114: 80 e4 00 1c lwz r7,28(r4) -+118: 80 c4 00 18 lwz r6,24(r4) ++114: 80 c4 00 18 lwz r6,24(r4) ++118: 80 e4 00 1c lwz r7,28(r4) +11c: 39 3f 0d ac addi r9,r31,3500 +120: 38 80 ff ff li r4,-1 +124: 48 00 00 01 bl <fn_8003F294+0x124> @@ -95,8 +95,8 @@ +12c: 7f c3 f3 78 mr r3,r30 +130: 81 1d 00 00 lwz r8,0(r29) +134: 39 3f 0d b0 addi r9,r31,3504 -+138: 80 e4 00 30 lwz r7,48(r4) -+13c: 80 c4 00 2c lwz r6,44(r4) ++138: 80 c4 00 2c lwz r6,44(r4) ++13c: 80 e4 00 30 lwz r7,48(r4) +140: 38 80 00 13 li r4,19 +144: 38 a0 00 00 li r5,0 +148: 48 00 00 01 bl <fn_8003F294+0x148> @@ -117,8 +117,8 @@ +174: 7f c3 f3 78 mr r3,r30 +178: 81 1f 0d 34 lwz r8,3380(r31) +17c: 39 3f 0d b8 addi r9,r31,3512 -+180: 80 e4 01 34 lwz r7,308(r4) -+184: 80 c4 01 30 lwz r6,304(r4) ++180: 80 c4 01 30 lwz r6,304(r4) ++184: 80 e4 01 34 lwz r7,308(r4) +188: 38 80 00 99 li r4,153 +18c: 38 a0 00 00 li r5,0 +190: 48 00 00 01 bl <fn_8003F294+0x190> --- MISMATCH: fn_8003F294 does not match --- => MATCH. Root cause: debug dump local copies the forced object into the build path before invoking checkdiff, but checkdiff's repo-wide lock is acquired inside checkdiff; another normal checkdiff can acquire the lock first and rebuild/overwrite the staged object. Desired: integrated --diff should acquire the same repo-wide lock before staging the forced object and hold it through checkdiff, or checkdiff should accept an explicit object path instead of using the shared build slot.
resolution_note: Superseded by clean issue #134; #133 body was polluted by shell command substitution but identified the same lock race.

summary: debug dump local --diff ignores forced-schedule object after cache hardening
body: After merging master 2b736307a (Harden mwcc debug cache and setup checks), fn_8003F294 has diagnostic PAD_STACK(24) in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c. Running: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --force-schedule-fn fn_8003F294 --force-schedule 'lwz:0x94>0x90,lwz:0xAC>0xA8,lwz:0xC8>0xC4,lwz:0x1C>0x18,lwz:0x30>0x2C,lwz:0x134>0x130' --diff -f fn_8003F294 --no-cache-sync --checkdiff-timeout 60 now reports a mismatch showing exactly the unforced load-order diff. But dumping with --output /tmp/f294_forced_aftermerge.txt shows '[FORCE_SCHEDULE] applied 6 adjacent load swap(s)' inside fn_8003F294. Before this master merge, the same source plus PAD_STACK(24) and same command reported MATCH. Suspect integrated --diff is comparing the normal rebuilt object or stale cache instead of the forced compile object. Desired: --diff must compare the object produced by the forced debug compile, and should print the forced object path/checkdiff invocation so stale-object bugs are obvious.
resolution_note: Superseded by #134, which narrows the root cause to a checkdiff-lock race around forced object staging; not fixed here.

summary: debug dump local returns before source restoration/hash settle, leaving cache sidecar for transient source
body: Follow-up detail to issue #123. Controlled repro: before hash of src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c was 9958af6f; running plain 'melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c' returned rc=0 and immediately printed after hash 548eb9bb plus sidecar hash 548eb9bb. The temporary after-content included a coalesce-style edit in fn_8003F654 (new_var_alias declaration/assignment). Moments later the source was restored to hash 9958af6f while build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/pl/plbonuslib.hash remained 548eb9bb, so fresh consumers reject the just-written cache as stale. This looks like dump local or a lower helper returning before source restoration is complete, or writing the hash while a transient patched source is present. It is source-risky and blocks debug suggest coalesce/other require_fresh consumers.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b736307a24b3e6d6f06b8ef25f438ac05b532cd: same root cause as #123; transient source restores are detected before cache publication, and captured compiled-source digests avoid writing sidecars for the wrong snapshot.

summary: force-schedule/force-phys/force-coalesce are DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY: they don't affect the production ninja build, so a forced match=true is not shippable
body: All --force-* DLL overrides run only under 'debug dump local' (patched mwcceppc_debug.exe). The production ninja build uses stock mwcceppc.exe; nothing wires MWCC_DEBUG_FORCE_* into configure.py/build.ninja. So 'force-schedule -> match=true' proves a hypothesis but the committed SOURCE still compiles to the stock %. fn_8003F294 reaches verified 100% under force-schedule but 99.29% (stock, with PAD_STACK(24)); the 10 HI-first load-order pairs have no known C lever (reverse-locals flips order but moves values off r6/r7 -> 89.88%). Request: either (a) doc force-* clearly as diagnostic-only and have force-schedule EMIT a suggested source transform, or (b) an opt-in per-TU production override so a proven match can actually ship. Without one of these, #108 cannot close F294.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b736307a24b3e6d6f06b8ef25f438ac05b532cd: debug dump local force-* options now label overrides DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY in help and emit a runtime warning that patched-debug-compiler matches do not affect production ninja builds.

summary: debug dump local leaves source newer than pcdump cache so freshness-gated tools immediately reject it
body: In /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, running melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c wrote build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/pl/plbonuslib.txt, but immediately afterward debug suggest coalesce -f fn_8003F294 --discover --top 5 exited 4 with 'cached pcdump is stale'. stat showed src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c mtime 2026-05-30 14:23:20 and cache mtime 2026-05-30 14:23:06, i.e. dump local appears to restore/touch the source after writing the cache. debug inspect analyze only warns and continues, but freshness-gated tools refuse to run. This blocks follow-on mwcc-debug diagnostics for fn_8003F294/fn_8003F654 after a fresh dump. Desired: dump local should leave cache mtime newer than or equal to the source it compiled, or freshness should compare against the actual compiled snapshot metadata instead of filesystem mtime after source restoration.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b736307a24b3e6d6f06b8ef25f438ac05b532cd: pcdump cache sidecars now record the compiled source digest, and dump local waits for a short source-settle window before publishing cache output; unstable/restored sources skip baseline cache sync.

summary: debug dump setup should detect stale mwcc_debug DLL after source updates
body: After merging master commit 44ec7d74 (force-schedule swaps), python tools/worktree-doctor.py --fix passed and melee-agent debug dump setup redeployed the existing tools/mwcc_debug/MWDBG326.dll, but did not rebuild it even though tools/mwcc_debug/mwcc_debug.c had changed. A force-schedule diagnostic run then behaved like the old DLL: no FORCE_SCHEDULE marker in the pcdump and no load-order changes. Running melee-agent debug dump setup --rebuild-dll rebuilt/deployed the DLL; the same force-schedule command then printed [FORCE_SCHEDULE] applied 6 adjacent load swap(s) and removed all F294 load-order differences. Setup/doctor should compare DLL freshness against mwcc_debug.c, auto-rebuild, or warn loudly, because otherwise agents test fixed tooling using stale binaries.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b736307a24b3e6d6f06b8ef25f438ac05b532cd: debug dump doctor now checks mwcc_debug.c vs built/deployed DLL freshness, and debug dump setup auto-rebuilds when MWDBG326.dll is older than mwcc_debug.c.

summary: remote permuter doctor passes even though configured objdump_command is missing on remote
body: After making nonmatchings/fn_8003F294 and fn_8003F654 compile.sh remote-ready, melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f <fn> passed every check. Submitting both jobs succeeded, but both tmux jobs stopped immediately. Tail showed decomp-permuter failed in Scorer._objdump with FileNotFoundError: /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump from settings.toml. Repro jobs: fn_8003F294-coder1-20260530-142031 and fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-142031. Desired: remote doctor should parse settings.toml objdump_command and verify the executable exists on the remote, or remote repair/submit should rewrite it to an available remote objdump path / sync the required tool. This currently causes remote runs to appear submitted successfully but die before any candidates are generated.
resolution_note: Fixed in 44ec7d74c: remote doctor parses settings.toml objdump_command and verifies the executable exists on the remote, so missing /opt/devkitpro/... objdump paths fail before jobs are submitted.

summary: remote permuter doctor passes compile.sh that requires wine, but remote repair only prepares Linux wibo
body: After installing binutils-powerpc-linux-gnu on coder1 and changing settings.toml objdump_command to /usr/bin/powerpc-linux-gnu-objdump, remote jobs fn_8003F294-coder1-20260530-142956 and fn_8003F654-coder1-20260530-142956 submitted successfully but stopped immediately. Logs show compile.sh: line 18: wine: command not found, then Unable to compile base.c. remote doctor passed beforehand and remote repair checks/syncs Linux wibo at /home/discord/permuter-work/melee/tools/mwcc_debug/bin/wibo, but it does not validate or rewrite compile.sh's compiler runner. Desired: remote submit/doctor/repair should either require wine on the target, or rewrite decomp-permuter compile.sh to run the MWCC exe through the remote Linux wibo path and verify the referenced compiler exists.
resolution_note: Fixed in 44ec7d74c: remote submit stages compile.sh with the remote Linux wibo and mwcceppc_debug.exe, and doctor marks rewritable wine-based compile.sh files as remote-staged rather than passing them through verbatim.

summary: remote permuter cannot submit fix-compile outputs because compile.sh still contains local path leaks
body: In /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers after running melee-agent debug permute fix-compile nonmatchings/fn_8003F294 and fn_8003F654, remote doctor still fails required local path leak checks because compile.sh contains 'cd /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers'. fix-compile reports already-fixed and offers no remote-ready mode. Repro: melee-agent debug permute remote doctor --target coder1 -f fn_8003F294 --repair reports FAIL local path leaks; melee-agent debug permute remote submit -f fn_8003F294 --target coder1 -j 16 exits with local permuter dir is not remote-ready. This blocks the preferred remote permuter workflow for fn_8003F294/fn_8003F654 even though coder1 remote tooling repairs successfully. Desired: bootstrap/fix-compile/config should support a MELEE_ROOT-driven or otherwise relocatable compile.sh suitable for remote submit, or remote submit should rewrite/patch compile.sh safely during staging.
resolution_note: Fixed in 44ec7d74c: remote submit now stages a rewritten permuter dir instead of rsyncing local compile.sh verbatim, replacing local cd paths with MELEE_ROOT so /Users/... leaks no longer block rewritable compile.sh files.

summary: force-schedule (#108) bare break in apply_force_schedule_to_block lets only the FIRST lwz rule fire per pair
body: tools/mwcc_debug/mwcc_debug.c apply_force_schedule_to_block(): after the first rule whose opcode+base+parse match, it executes 'break' even when the OFFSET pair didn't match. Since all force-schedule rules share opcode 'lwz', the first rule short-circuits the rest — only 1 of N rules can ever apply per instruction pair. Symptom: passing all 10 lwz rules yielded total_swaps=1, not 10. Fix: only 'break' on a successful swap; otherwise continue trying remaining rules. Verified: after the fix, total_swaps went 1->2 for a 2-rule test and 6 (adjacent-only) for the full set.
resolution_note: Fixed in 44ec7d74c: apply_force_schedule_to_block now continues trying later rules after an opcode/base match with nonmatching offsets, and only breaks once a swap succeeds; regression covers multi-rule lwz pairs.

summary: force-schedule (#108) strict adjacency can't reach load pairs the scheduler separates by one addi/mr
body: tools/mwcc_debug/mwcc_debug.c only swaps directly-adjacent same-base loads (swap_adjacent_pcode). 4 of fn_8003F294's 10 pl_8003906C arg setups keep an 'addi r9,r31,N' BETWEEN the two loads even in FINAL code (lwz r6,LO / addi r9 / lwz r7,HI), exactly mirroring expected (which is lwz r7,HI / addi r9 / lwz r6,LO). Strict adjacency leaves these 4 unfixable. Added a swap_straddle_pcode(prev,a,mid,b) that reorders A,M,B->B,M,A when A and mid->next form a matching pair and the middle insn isn't itself a matching load; this got all 10 pairs swapped. Suggest upstreaming a straddle (one-middle-insn) mode.
resolution_note: Fixed in 44ec7d74c: force-schedule now supports a one-middle-instruction straddle swap (A,M,B -> B,M,A) when the middle instruction is not itself a matching load; regression covers the fn_8003F294 separated lwz/addi/lwz shape.

summary: force-schedule (#108) hooks intermediate scheduling pass where load pairs aren't adjacent yet; never fires for fn_8003F294
body: tools/mwcc_debug/mwcc_debug.c pass_is_force_schedule_point() matches only 'AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING'. For fn_8003F294 the 10 same-base lwz arg-setup pairs are STILL separated by an addi/mr at that intermediate pass (front-end emits low-first; r6,addi r9,r7). They only become adjacent at 'FINAL CODE AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING'. Result: scoped --force-schedule-fn fn_8003F294 produced 0 swaps silently (no scope-skip, no 'applied' msg) and 0 byte change. Fix: also accept 'FINAL CODE AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING' (the pass whose code is emitted). Verified locally: switching the pass-point string makes the adjacent pairs swap and reach the .o.
resolution_note: Fixed in a19f4e64280f942815a9d5c2cbc01f7d6601dd19: duplicate of #116's final-scheduling hook root cause; force-schedule now also applies at FINAL CODE AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING, the pass where the fn_8003F294 adjacent load pairs are visible, and the rebuilt DLL is installed from /Users/mike/code/melee.

summary: force-schedule override has no effect on fn_8003F294 adjacent lwz pair
body: After merging c4e9677c8 and retaining fn_8003F294 at 99.31%, the remaining checkdiff includes adjacent same-base loads where expected emits high offset first, e.g. expected '+114: lwz r7,28(r4)' then '+118: lwz r6,24(r4)', while current emits '+114: lwz r6,24(r4)' then '+118: lwz r7,28(r4)'. I tested the new override directly: 'melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c --force-schedule-fn fn_8003F294 --force-schedule lwz:0x1C>0x18 --diff -f fn_8003F294' and also the inverse 'lwz:0x18>0x1C'. Both produced the same low-before-high current order in the diff. The broader override list also left the same class of load-order diffs. So the force-schedule option is exposed and accepted, but it does not affect this concrete F294 adjacent lwz pair; agents still cannot test the final scheduling hypothesis.
resolution_note: Fixed in a19f4e64280f942815a9d5c2cbc01f7d6601dd19: force-schedule now also applies at FINAL CODE AFTER INSTRUCTION SCHEDULING, where the concrete adjacent same-base loads are visible; the DLL was rebuilt/deployed from /Users/mike/code/melee.

summary: simplify-order still ignores per-candidate timeout after c4e9677c8
body: On /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers after merging master c4e9677c8 ('Fix simplify candidate timeouts and force schedule verify'), the previous #113 repro still hangs. Command: timeout 70s melee-agent debug mutate simplify-order -f fn_8003F654 --want-late 40 --max-candidates 10 --timeout 10 2>&1. Output streams through '[simplify-order] compiling 9/10: insert-alias entity@0' and then emits no timeout diagnostic, no candidate 10, and no final ranking/no-survivors summary before the outer timeout exits 124. Expected: --timeout 10 should bound candidate 9 and continue or report that candidate as timed out. Retained source baselines at this point: fn_8003F654 96.38%, fn_8003F294 96.70%.
resolution_note: Fixed in a19f4e64280f942815a9d5c2cbc01f7d6601dd19: simplify-order no longer runs nested declaration regexes over alias candidate statement scans, pcdump table parsers skip pcode lines before row regexes, and candidate child compiles carry the requested hang timeout; the reported 10-candidate repro now exits 0 in 23.9s.

summary: debug mutate simplify-order per-candidate timeout not enforced after insert-alias entity
body: After merging master 767016651 in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, issue #111's progress fix is partially working: simplify-order now prints candidate progress. But the search still hangs after candidate 9 and does not honor the per-candidate timeout. Repro 1: 'timeout 150s melee-agent debug mutate simplify-order -f fn_8003F654 --want-late 40 --max-candidates 40 --with-permuter --permuter-dir nonmatchings/fn_8003F654 --triage 2>&1' printed through '[simplify-order] compiling 9/40: insert-alias entity@0' and then emitted nothing until the outer timeout killed it. Repro 2: 'timeout 50s melee-agent debug mutate simplify-order -f fn_8003F654 --want-late 40 --max-candidates 10 --timeout 10 2>&1' printed through '[simplify-order] compiling 9/10: insert-alias entity@0' and then also required the outer timeout. Expected: --timeout should bound that candidate compile and the command should continue to candidate 10 or report a timed-out candidate with a final ranking/no-survivors summary.
resolution_note: Fixed in c4e9677c8657f4b7f19b395b74a2aadf20f31ad0: simplify-order candidate compiles now run debug dump local under a process-group timeout so hung descendants cannot keep pipes open past --timeout.

summary: force-schedule cannot be applied to permute/candidate verification path
body: After merging master f515e2045, the force-schedule override exists on debug dump local/remote. It works for configured repo source, but the concrete F294 near-match that motivated issue #108 is saved as a candidate source (/tmp/f294_9924.c) and currently can only be scored with 'melee-agent debug permute verify /tmp/f294_9924.c -f fn_8003F294 --json --keep-failed', which now cleanly reports new_pct=99.24117 from baseline 96.7. There is no way to pass --force-schedule through permute verify, and 'melee-agent debug dump local /tmp/f294_9924.c ...' rejects the candidate because it is outside the repo. Copying it under nonmatchings/manual still fails because the file has no build.ninja block. To test whether force-schedule breaks the remaining F294 99.24% scheduling wall, agents need either permute verify --force-schedule/--force-schedule-fn passthrough, or a debug dump/score path that can compile a candidate source by transferring it into the real TU the same way permute verify does.
resolution_note: Fixed in c4e9677c8657f4b7f19b395b74a2aadf20f31ad0: debug permute verify accepts --force-schedule/--force-schedule-fn, transfers the candidate into the real TU, compiles via debug dump local --keep-obj, and measures the forced object.

summary: force-schedule: bias the MWCC list scheduler (not just coloring) for adjacent same-base loads
body: fn_8003F294 reaches 99.24% with register allocation FULLY matched, but stalls on instruction SCHEDULING: expected loads two adjacent struct-field args (->x74 then ->x70) higher-offset-first into r7 then r6 across all 9 pl_8003906C arg setups; current schedules lower-first. force-phys/force-iter-first/force-coalesce control only register COLORING and cannot influence list-scheduler ordering of two ready independent loads. Source attempts to force order promote temps to callee-saves and regress (89.88%). A --force-schedule override (pin emit order / bias scheduler priority for specific ready ops) would let agents test reachability of a target schedule, as force-phys does for coloring. src /tmp/f294_9924.c.
resolution_note: Implemented in master merge commit 373592ad3: debug dump local/remote now support --force-schedule/--force-schedule-fn, with DLL adjacent same-base load swapping and regression coverage.

summary: permute build-failed diagnostics collapse to caret-only compiler error
body: After retaining the fn_8003F654 96.38% improvement, a fresh permute run produced nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/output-1185-1/source.c. Triage reported status=build-failed with only first_diag='FAILED: [code=2] build/GALE01/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.o '. Direct verify was structured JSON but still not actionable: command 'melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/output-1185-1/source.c -f fn_8003F654 --json --keep-failed' returned error='FAILED: [code=2] build/GALE01/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.o \n# Error: ^^^'. The candidate diff removes 'abs = pos->x - prevPos->x;' and leaves 'abs = -abs;' behind, so the underlying source issue is visible by diff, but the tool output gives no file/line/context or actual compiler message. Please preserve enough mwcc/ninja diagnostic context in triage/verify JSON for agents to classify bad candidates without manually opening every failed candidate.
resolution_note: Fixed in f515e2045: permute verify/triage JSON now preserves MWCC multi-line diagnostic context, including file, line, code, caret, and following message, instead of collapsing to the first FAILED line.

summary: force-iter-first does not converge deep N-register callee-save cascades (shifts the swap)
body: fn_8003F654 (94.68%, all opcodes match) has a systematic off-by-one callee-save cascade: GPR index(ig33)/b34(ig40) swap r26<->r27 within a 7-deep cascade (r25-r31), plus FPR dist f30<->f31. Applying force-iter-first to a single node (e.g. ig45->f31, or ig40->r27) reaches THAT node target but does not converge the whole cascade — it just relocates the swap to an adjacent pair (verified: forcing r27 introduced an r28/r27 swap at +01c). Two asks: (1) a multi-node "solve the cascade to this full target vector" mode that finds a consistent iteration-order perturbation, not per-node; (2) match-iter-first --auto-verify was EXCELLENT here — it proved forcing dist->f31 moved match% by +0.00%, correctly falsifying the FPR-wall hypothesis. More of that auto-falsification (e.g. auto-verify on the full GPR target vector) would help. src /tmp/f654_clean_9468.c.
resolution_note: Implemented match-iter-first full-vector targeting: gpr/fpr callee aliases and ranges, target_vector/force_iter_first/force_phys JSON and text output, and auto-verify metadata for the full vector. Merged to master in 767016651.

summary: permute verify --json still crashes on transient report JSONDecodeError after report-read retry fix
body: After merging 46258f3ea (Retry transient report reads in permute triage), triage no longer reproduced #107, but direct verify still can. Command: melee-agent debug permute verify /tmp/f294_9924.c -f fn_8003F294 --json --keep-failed. It built plbonuslib.o and report.json successfully, then crashed in verify_perm -> _get_match_pct with JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string starting at line 1 column 245661 while reading build/GALE01/report.json. It printed a Rich traceback instead of JSON. Immediately afterwards 'python -m json.tool build/GALE01/report.json' succeeded and checkdiff reported retained source still at 96.49%, so this is the same transient/partial report-read class but in verify, not triage.
resolution_note: Fixed in 9953449de: permute verify --json now reuses the retrying report-read path after successful builds and emits structured report-read-failed JSON if report.json remains unreadable.

summary: debug mutate simplify-order produces no stdout output (runs ~5min, prints nothing)
body: Ran `melee-agent debug mutate simplify-order -f fn_8003F654 --want-late 40` three times (also via `2>&1 | tee file`). The command clearly DOES work — it spawns ~100 mwcceppc variant compiles over ~5 min (visible in ps) — but emits ZERO bytes to stdout/stderr on completion (exit 0). No survivor ranking, no "want-late prefix reproduced" report, no variant dir paths, nothing. So the search result is invisible and unusable. Expected: print the ranked survivors (which variant reproduces the target simplify prefix, the source mutation, and a path to the winning variant dir), or at least a "no survivors found" line. Worktree /Users/mike/code/melee-plbonus on rebased master (b068a563d). Blocks using the new simplify-order lever on fn_8003F654 (GPR index/entity r26/r27 swap, want-late ig40).
resolution_note: Fixed in 9953449de: debug mutate simplify-order now emits per-candidate compile progress to stderr during the long search loop while preserving final rankings/no-progress output.

summary: permute triage --json can crash on JSONDecodeError reading report after successful candidate build
body: Freshly bootstrapped nonmatchings/fn_8003F294 from retained source at 96.276474 and ran 'melee-agent debug permute triage nonmatchings/fn_8003F294 -f fn_8003F294 --top 10 --json'. After ninja successfully built plbonuslib.o, triage crashed in _refresh_match_pct_after_successful_build -> _get_match_pct with JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string starting at line 1 column 589531 while reading build/GALE01/report.json. The traceback was printed instead of structured JSON. A later 'python -m json.tool build/GALE01/report.json' passed and checkdiff still reported fn_8003F294 at 96.28, so this appears to be a report refresh/read race or transient partial read. Automation needs triage JSON mode to handle this as structured retryable failure or avoid reading report.json until generation is complete.
resolution_note: Fixed in 46258f3ea: report.json match-percent reads after successful candidate builds now retry transient JSONDecodeError/OSError partial reads and return structured diagnostics instead of crashing triage --json.

summary: permute verify build-failure path can leave candidate source applied despite saying Source reverted
body: Command: melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F294/output-685-2/source.c -f fn_8003F294 --json. First run exited 4 with human output, not JSON, after ninja failed on FileNotFoundError: build/GALE01/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.d. It printed 'Source reverted', but src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c was left in decomp-permuter formatting with the candidate assignment applied. I manually cleaned the formatting and retained the actual source improvement; automation needs verify failure cleanup to be atomic and JSON mode to stay structured.
resolution_note: Fixed in b068a563d: verify build failures now emit structured JSON in --json mode and use finally-based source restoration unless a candidate is intentionally kept.

summary: permute triage can rank context/prototype-only improvements that verify cannot transfer
body: Fresh triage after rebootstrap reported best_path nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/output-1535-1/source.c at 95.33213 from baseline 95.259926. The diff includes a context prototype change 'Player_GetEntity(volatile unsigned int slot)' plus a local prevPos->x alias. Direct verify of that same candidate in the real tree returned new_pct=95.259926, delta=0.0. The ranked improvement appears to depend on candidate context outside the transferable function edit, so triage can advertise a best candidate that verify/apply cannot reproduce.
resolution_note: Fixed in b068a563d: triage rechecks apparent winners from a clean function-only transfer and marks mismatched scores nonreproducible instead of ranking them.

summary: inspect guide suggests self-target workflow that produces no diagnostic signal
body: Running 'melee-agent debug inspect guide -f fn_8003F294 --asm-hunks 1' with no target says: derive a target with 'melee-agent debug target derive -f fn_8003F294 > /tmp/target.yaml', then rerun guide with --target. Following that instruction creates a target from the current pcdump, so guide reports all virtuals matched and 'No issues found' while checkdiff is still only 96.21%. Same for fn_8003F654 at 95.26%. The tool does say the target was probably derived from current source after the fact, but the initial actionable flow should steer agents toward a reference/forced target or expected-register helper, not a self-target dead end.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc449a9df: inspect guide no-target guidance no longer recommends deriving a self-target from the current pcdump and instead steers agents to reference/forced targets or inspect ceiling.

summary: permute triage can report transient build-failed/null diagnostics then later ok for same candidates
body: In worktree /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers after fresh bootstrap/run for fn_8003F654, first triage of nonmatchings/fn_8003F654 reported output-1265-1, output-1555-2, and output-1565-1 as status=build-failed with first_diag=null. Direct verify of output-1265-1 then compiled cleanly at baseline, and a second triage of the same directory reported output-1265-1 status=ok with match_pct=95.6426 and output-1555-2 status=ok with match_pct=95.259926. This makes triage non-idempotent and hard for automation to trust. Candidate output-1265-1 was a redundant prevPos->y self-assignment; manually applying it reproduced a large checkdiff improvement, but it is not source-safe to retain.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc449a9df: permute triage retries no-diagnostic ninja failures once, avoids a second object rebuild after a successful candidate compile, and records fallback diagnostics for report failures.

summary: permute verify --json output is contaminated by failure-report helper on corrupt candidates
body: After merging e6a1f2e2d and reinstalling melee-agent, triage now correctly reports corrupt placeholder candidates. Direct verify still produces non-machine-clean JSON/stderr interleaving on a corrupt candidate: command 'melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F654/output-1645-1/source.c -f fn_8003F654 --json --keep-failed' exits 7 and the copyable 'To report this tooling failure...' helper is injected into the JSON around the candidate field. JSON-mode callers need structured failure data without the human report helper contaminating stdout/stderr.
resolution_note: Fixed in dc449a9df: global failure-report hints are suppressed for --json invocations, so corrupt-candidate verify output remains machine-readable.

summary: permute triage hides corrupt placeholder candidates as build-failed with no diagnostic
body: In nonmatchings/fn_8003F294, triage reported output-605-1/source.c as `{status: build-failed, first_diag: null}` even though a direct verify identified the real reason: `ABORT: permuter placeholder(s) detected in candidate source: inline_fn (2 occurrences)`. Command: `melee-agent debug permute triage nonmatchings/fn_8003F294 -f fn_8003F294 --top 10 --json`. Direct check: `melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F294/output-605-1/source.c -f fn_8003F294 --json --keep-failed` exited 7 with clear placeholder corruption text. Triage should preserve this status/diagnostic (for example `corrupt-candidate`) so agents do not waste time treating a low raw score as a promising build failure.
resolution_note: Fixed in e6a1f2e2d: placeholder detection is shared by verify and triage; triage now reports corrupt-candidate with the inline_fn diagnostic before attempting transfer/build.

summary: permute verify --keep cannot transfer simple candidate after source formatting drift
body: After debug permute bootstrap/run/triage, best fn_8003F294 candidate nonmatchings/fn_8003F294/output-915-1/source.c transferred in triage (+3.294121 percentage points, 91.064705 -> 94.358826). Running `melee-agent debug permute verify nonmatchings/fn_8003F294/output-915-1/source.c -f fn_8003F294 --keep --json` aborted with a 3-way merge conflict because the permuter baseline is imported in unformatted/preprocessed style while the real repo source has formatted line wrapping. The actual diff was simple and safe: add one local `f32 x8C;`, assign `x8C = *((f32*) &pl_804D6470->x8C);`, pass x8C to `pl_80039238`. Manual patch applied cleanly and checkdiff confirmed 94.36%. This makes the keep/apply workflow unreliable exactly when triage finds a useful candidate. Desired: function-body transfer should apply candidate deltas across formatting-only drift, or offer a patch/diff output mode targeted at the real source instead of aborting.
resolution_note: Fixed in e6a1f2e2d: verify --keep now falls back to a format-normalized candidate apply when permuter base and current source differ only by formatting, avoiding false 3-way conflicts while preserving real semantic conflict protection.

summary: debug permute should bootstrap function dirs from repo source
body: After the master merge, `melee-agent debug permute doctor -f fn_8003F294 --json` correctly validates /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter but reports missing nonmatchings/fn_8003F294/{base.c,compile.sh,target.o,settings.toml}. `debug permute run --help` still says to create the dir via `decomp-permuter/import.py`. For hard register/source-shape searches, agents need a repo-local bootstrap command, e.g. `melee-agent debug permute import/bootstrap -f fn_8003F294`, that writes base.c from the current function body, creates target.o/target asm, fixes compile.sh, and generates settings.toml. Doctor/config/run are much better now, but the workflow still falls out to an external manual import step before search can start.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0441318ac6bb094f6576bd530309301e2e0158da: added melee-agent debug permute bootstrap to extract target asm, invoke import.py, fix compile.sh/base.c, and write stock settings.toml; doctor hints now point there.

summary: match-iter-first auto-verify should classify zero-delta targets as not actionable
body: With the new FPR support, `melee-agent debug target match-iter-first --function fn_8003F654 --regs f31 --auto-verify --json` finds ig44 for f31 with ambiguous confidence, runs the forced verification, and reports baseline_pct=85.14802, new_pct=85.14802, delta=0.0. The JSON still has auto_verify.status=`ok`, which means command execution succeeded, but for agent workflow the important classification is that this target did not improve the diff and should not be pursued as-is. Please add an actionability field/status such as no_improvement/invalid_target, and have the text output explicitly say the matched FPR target did not move the function.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0441318ac6bb094f6576bd530309301e2e0158da: match-iter-first auto-verify annotates actionability, marking zero-delta targets as no_improvement/actionable=false in JSON and text.

summary: first-divergence should map blocker interferences to source context
body: For `melee-agent debug inspect first-divergence -f fn_8003F294 --force-phys 33:29,36:29 --source`, the gated result is useful: Case A, ig36 wants r29 but r29 is held by interferer ig46. The source-advice layer only attempts to name ig36, and the name is suspect; it does not automatically report the blocker ig46 source context. Running `virtual-to-var -f fn_8003F294 46` separately shows no variable and a defining op `lwz r46,pl_804D6470(r0)`. For Case A/B-style allocator problems, the blocker is often the thing to shorten/split/reorder, so first-divergence should include source/def-use context for both the target node and the blocking interferer, including struct-field/load/call-site hints where possible.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0441318ac6bb094f6576bd530309301e2e0158da: first-divergence --source now includes blocker ig source/first-def context and rejected nested candidates alongside the target node.

summary: virtual-to-var can misidentify impossible nested locals
body: After merging master into /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, `melee-agent debug inspect first-divergence -f fn_8003F294 --force-phys 33:29,36:29 --source` reports ig36 as the first divergence and advises shortening `t2`. `melee-agent debug inspect virtual-to-var -f fn_8003F294 36` returns `r36: t2 (local), confidence ambiguous-nested, scope fn_8003F294/block@...`. But analyze shows r36 live 12..79, while `t2` is a later nested-block variable around the sqrt section; the live range predates that declaration. The source bridge should reject variables whose lexical scope/declaration cannot cover the virtual live range, or show multiple candidates with a warning. Wrong source names send agents toward non-actionable edits.
resolution_note: Fixed in 0441318ac6bb094f6576bd530309301e2e0158da: virtual-to-var inverse lookup now rejects ambiguous nested-scope bindings so unvalidated nested locals are not presented as source facts.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug mutate search --function mnEvent_8024E524 --budget 4 --per-seed-time 45 --total-time 240
body: Command: melee-agent debug mutate search --function mnEvent_8024E524 --budget 4 --per-seed-time 45 --total-time 240 Exit code: 1
resolution_note: Verified fixed on current master in targeted suite: debug mutate search exits 0 and prints no-improvement guidance when no seed produces a permuter improvement.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug target match-iter-first -f mnEvent_8024E524 /tmp/mnevent_current_pcdump.txt --auto-verify
body: Command: melee-agent debug target match-iter-first -f mnEvent_8024E524 /tmp/mnevent_current_pcdump.txt --auto-verify Exit code: 125
resolution_note: Fixed in 926073285b46c27d43faae084521df7b841a50ec: auto-verify restore exit 125 from protective expensive-work refusal is reported as diagnostic state without failing match-iter-first.

summary: clean mnevent fingerprints now reproduce lower checkdiff scores
body: After merging current master and rerunning worktree-doctor, the clean committed mnevent.c source now reproduces lower `checkdiff --summary --no-build` outcomes for the same recorded fingerprints. Examples from this session on a clean tree after `ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/mn/mnevent.o`: `mnEvent_8024E524` now reports 94.87% for the same fingerprint that earlier repeatedly verified at 97.53%, and `mnEvent_8024D15C` now reports 81.27% for the same fingerprint that earlier verified at 84.73%. `melee-agent attempts show` marks these as divergent repeats with external-state drift. No gameplay-source files outside the TU changed in the merge; master only brought tooling changes. This makes it hard to trust local maxima or regression checks across heartbeats.
resolution_note: Fixed in 926073285b46c27d43faae084521df7b841a50ec: checkdiff --no-build no longer reads/writes fingerprint tracking, avoiding same-source divergent repeat records from externally managed object state.

summary: persist near-match source and diff with attempt records
body: During the plbonuslib blocker investigation, checkdiff history preserved only percentages for fn_8003F294/fn_8003F654 and the useful final near-match source for fn_8003F654 was lost when the prior worktree stash disappeared. Attempts/history should persist the candidate source, compact diff, tool verdicts, and notes for nonmatching high-percent attempts so future agents can resume diagnosis instead of reconstructing source from session logs. --- Implementation grounding (appended by team-lead, verified against agent_state.db) --- Current persistence gap, concretely: - `match_history` columns are (id, scratch_slug, score, max_score, match_percent, timestamp, worktree_path, branch) -- score/percent ONLY, no source or diff. - `scratches.source_code` DOES hold source, but it is one row per slug and is OVERWRITTEN on each compile -- it is the LATEST source, not the best-scoring one. So improve-then-regress, or a stash-drop (what cost the fn_8003F654 89.68% source), loses the high-water-mark source with nothing to recover it from. Suggested additive fix (DB is shared with the concurrent mwcc-debug agent -- additive migration ONLY, no destructive changes): 1. Add `source_code TEXT` (+ optional `diff TEXT`, `verdict TEXT`) to `match_history`, OR add a small `best_attempts(function_name, match_percent, source_code, diff, verdict, branch, created_at)` table. 2. Snapshot only when a NEW high-water-mark score lands for a function (not every compile -- bounds size). 3. Add `melee-agent scratch recover-best <func>` to emit the best-known source + its diff, so a fresh worktree resumes from the high-water mark instead of reconstructing from session logs.
resolution_note: Fixed in 926073285b46c27d43faae084521df7b841a50ec: attempt high-water records retain source/diff/verdict snapshots and scratch recover-best restores the best retained snapshot.

summary: standardize opseq and permuter entrypoints through melee-agent
body: Prior plbonuslib blocker sessions lost time because opseq was attempted through a stale direct path and decomp-permuter required manual external checkout/harness setup. melee-agent should expose stable opseq and permuter run/config/verify entrypoints that work from a fresh repo worktree, validate required paths/prelude files, and hand outputs to debug permute triage/verify so agents do not rely on ad hoc local paths.
resolution_note: Fixed in 926073285b46c27d43faae084521df7b841a50ec: opseq docs route through melee-agent and debug permute doctor validates local permuter setup before run/config/verify.

summary: replace ceiling/unmatchable terminology with actionable taxonomy
body: Current guidance, errors, comments, and diagnostic labels often use terms like ceiling, c-source unmatchable, impossible, or true ceiling. This causes agents to over-prune work: they interpret the state as impossible rather than as a current-tooling classification. The language should be replaced with an actionable taxonomy such as: no fast transform found, requires source-shape search, allocator-order mismatch, force-target not reached from current IR, unresolved by current heuristics, or needs permuter/exploration. The key distinction should be between observed evidence and recommended next workflow, not claims that C source edits cannot work. This should cover user-facing guidance in melee-agent/mwcc-debug plus comments/docs that agents read.
resolution_note: Fixed in 926073285b46c27d43faae084521df7b841a50ec: mwcc-debug ceiling/pattern/permuter wording now uses actionable current-tooling taxonomy with regression coverage.

summary: target guide should explain coloring-order levers for two-virtual swaps
body: The forced target for WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex showed only r33 and r46 wrong: current colors r46/nametag first to r31 and r33/arg1 third to r30; target wants r33 to get r31 and r46 to fall to r30. 'inspect guide --target' correctly reported interference but generic suggestions (alias-split, widen/shrink, chained-init) were not enough to decide what source edit to try. Request: add a target-guided explanation mode that says why each virtual is ordered where it is (degree, nIntfr, simplify/color iteration), what source variable it likely maps to, and concrete levers to move A before B or reduce A's degree below B. Even a short 'to make r33 color before r46, reduce r46 degree by N or increase r33 degree by N' would help.
resolution_note: Fixed in 57b5d482a: inspect guide interference suggestions now include simplify/color iteration, degree, nIntfr, and concrete degree/force-iter-first levers for one-blocker two-virtual swaps.

summary: mwcc-debug should self-diagnose fresh worktree setup
body: In a fresh worktree for plbonuslib blocker diagnosis, local mwcc-debug commands failed because build/compilers/GC/1.2.5n/mwcceppc_debug.exe was not present; running melee-agent debug dump setup fixed it. The tool should make fresh-worktree readiness explicit, preferably with a doctor/preflight that detects missing patched compiler/DLL/wibo/Zig artifacts and offers or runs setup before inspect/ceiling workflows fail.
resolution_note: Fixed in 57b5d482a: added debug dump doctor preflight with PASS/FAIL checks for wibo, compiler, debug compiler, DLLs, setup scripts, plus --repair and dump-local hints.

summary: compact reloc-only/register-only diff mode for last-mile matching
body: Split out from issue #30 item 4. For opcode-identical diffs (e.g. mnDiagram_80241310), a compact checkdiff mode that groups symbol-alias (reloc-name) diffs and register-allocation cascades would make last-mile review faster than reading a full diff. Not yet implemented.
resolution_note: Fixed in 57b5d482a: tools/checkdiff.py now supports --compact / --format compact with grouped relocation-only and register-only summaries.

summary: debug inspect diff can hang and leave mwcc child processes
body: A local 'python -m src.cli debug inspect diff --fn WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c /tmp/mnnamenew_writechars_probe.c' invocation hung for over a minute after a temporary source probe. ps showed the Python wrapper plus two 'tools/workflow/mwcc-inspect.sh ... mnnamenew.c' children still running; I had to kill them manually. Request: add a per-side watchdog/timeout with cleanup of child process groups, and print which side/compile command is currently running so hangs are diagnosable. If it is intentionally using mwcc-inspect.sh, that also seems surprising for mwcc-debug inspect diff and should be made explicit.
resolution_note: fixed in a3bdc174c: inspect diff runs mwcc-inspect with per-side status, timeout, and process-group cleanup

summary: target match-iter-first should support FPR registers
body: While diagnosing fn_8003F654 in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, the dominant residual diff is an FPR cascade around the inlined sqrt result (archived near-match: expected f31 vs current f30; local reproduction still shows f30/f31 pressure). Command run: melee-agent debug target match-iter-first --function fn_8003F654 --regs f31,f30 --json. Observed result: invalid reg token: f31. The GPR version was useful for fn_8003F294; hard float-heavy functions need the same pre-coloring target/force guidance for FPRs.
resolution_note: fixed in a3bdc174c: match-iter-first now accepts f* registers and matches FPR virtual destinations

summary: remote doctor --repair fails on symlinked function dir
body: Running PYTHONPATH=/Users/mike/code/melee/tools/melee-agent melee-agent debug permute remote doctor -f lbDvd_80018A2C --target coder1 --repair failed during the decomp-permuter rsync step: 'could not make way for new symlink: nonmatchings/lbDvd_80018A2C'. Local /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/lbDvd_80018A2C is a symlink to /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/2138/melee/nonmatchings/lbDvd_80018A2C, while the remote path is a real directory. repair should either exclude nonmatchings from the decomp-permuter root sync and handle the requested -f function dir separately, or copy symlink targets safely instead of preserving absolute local symlinks.
resolution_note: fixed in a3bdc174c: remote repair excludes nonmatchings from permuter-root rsync and syncs the requested function dir separately

summary: target match-iter-first should support FPR registers
body: While diagnosing fn_8003F654 in /Users/mike/.config/superpowers/worktrees/melee/plbonuslib-blockers, the dominant residual diff is an FPR cascade around the inlined sqrt result (expected f31 vs current f30 in the archived near-match; reproduced as f30/f31 pressure locally). exits with . The GPR version is useful for fn_8003F294, but hard float-heavy functions need the same pre-coloring target/force guidance for FPRs.

summary: Interactive diagnostics can stall on mnEvent_8024E524
body: function=mnEvent_8024E524 match=false match_percent=88.09 classification=stack-layout and == Structural-ceiling check for mnEvent_8024E524 == Baseline: 88.09% TU: src/melee/mn/mnevent.c [1] Cast audit (free, ~ms)... No HIGH-severity casts. [2] Decl-order enumeration (promote strategy, ~minute)... WIN: promote page → 88.34% (delta +0.25%) [!] HSD_ASSERT override needed — anonymous .sdata assert strings: @235 ('jobj.h'), @236 ('jobj') Add #undef/#define HSD_ASSERT before <baselib/jobj.h>. See MEMORY.md 'HSD_ASSERT macro override' for the pattern. [!] SPILLED virtuals (compiler couldn't keep in registers): r43: i (local/low-confidence) Re-run `debug inspect virtual-to-var -f mnEvent_8024E524 <virt>` for each row to get full context. == VERDICT: WIN AVAILABLE == Apply decl-order win: `melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders mnEvent_8024E524 --strategy promote --keep-best` → expected 88.34%. both took too long to use interactively after merging master, which slowed match iteration.
resolution_note: Fixed in master 26011b5f5. 'debug inspect ceiling' decl-order enumeration no longer appears hung: it now emits flushed per-candidate progress lines ((i/total) label: pct%) and honors a --max-seconds wall-clock budget that stops early with a clear message instead of an opaque long pause. Phase headers are flushed so output streams when piped to an agent. Loop logic extracted to _run_decl_candidates with unit tests (tests/test_ceiling_decl_candidates.py).

summary: Long-running summary and ceiling inspection on mnEvent_8024E524
body: After merging master (02e4ecad4), repeated runs of function=mnEvent_8024E524 match=false match_percent=88.09 classification=stack-layout and == Structural-ceiling check for mnEvent_8024E524 == Baseline: 88.09% TU: src/melee/mn/mnevent.c [1] Cast audit (free, ~ms)... No HIGH-severity casts. [2] Decl-order enumeration (promote strategy, ~minute)... WIN: promote page → 88.34% (delta +0.25%) [!] HSD_ASSERT override needed — anonymous .sdata assert strings: @235 ('jobj.h'), @236 ('jobj') Add #undef/#define HSD_ASSERT before <baselib/jobj.h>. See MEMORY.md 'HSD_ASSERT macro override' for the pattern. [!] SPILLED virtuals (compiler couldn't keep in registers): r43: i (local/low-confidence) Re-run `debug inspect virtual-to-var -f mnEvent_8024E524 <virt>` for each row to get full context. == VERDICT: WIN AVAILABLE == Apply decl-order win: `melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders mnEvent_8024E524 --strategy promote --keep-best` → expected 88.34%. failed to return within interactive time budgets during matching. This makes it hard to tell whether a small source-shape probe helped. The commands do not emit progress or an obvious timeout/failure mode, so they look hung from the agent side.
