summary: mndiagram matching tooling feedback
body: Session feedback from mndiagram.c matching: (1) Previously the CLI suggested `melee-agent issue report` after errors, but the Homebrew-installed `melee-agent` on PATH did not expose that command, which made the suggested feedback path fail. (2) `mismatch search` returns pattern IDs, but the intuitive `mismatch show <id>` fails; `mismatch get <id>` is the actual command. Consider aliasing show to get or printing the retrieval command in search results. (3) `checkdiff.py` reports `[REPEAT]` before the function header for matching functions, so quick summary scripts need special casing to distinguish true 100% matches from repeat notices. (4) For opcode-identical diffs like mnDiagram_80241310, a reloc-only/register-only compact mode that groups symbol aliases and register allocation cascades would make last-mile work faster than reading a very long full diff. (5) worktree-doctor warning for missing table-typer includes a fix command, but --fix does not build it; either auto-build it under --fix or label it optional.
resolution_note: Fixed in master 26011b5f5. mndiagram papercut bundle dispositions: (1) issue report exposed — the editable melee-agent now exposes 'issue report' (stale Homebrew install was the cause; verified). (2) mismatch retrieval — 'mismatch show <id>' alias already exists AND 'mismatch search' now prints a 'Run melee-agent mismatch get <id>' hint. (3) checkdiff [REPEAT] — banner already prints to stderr after the stdout summary line, so summary scripts reading stdout are unaffected. (5) worktree-doctor --fix now actually builds table-typer (go build) instead of only suggesting it; if Go is absent it warns and labels opseq optional rather than failing. (4) compact reloc/register diff mode split out as feature issue #89 (not implemented).

summary: extract get output includes duplicate assembly block and no C decomp output
body: writes the assembly twice in the output file (duplicated full listing) and does not include decompiled C for manual editing context. During this TU matching session this made it harder to inspect call/variable intent from one command run.
resolution_note: Fixed in master eb614f970. The duplicate-assembly defect no longer reproduces on master (extract get -o writes a single ASM listing; verified). The remaining 'no C decomp output' ask is addressed by the new opt-in --decompile-c flag, which runs local m2c (tools/decomp.py) and prints first-pass C without a decomp.me server.

summary: melee-agent command failed: extract get lbDvd_80018A2C --create-scratch
body: Command: melee-agent extract get lbDvd_80018A2C --create-scratch Exit code: 1 Observed while setting up Layer A validation campaign. CLI could not find local decomp.me server at nzxt-discord.local, 10.200.0.1, or localhost:8000 and printed STOP: report this issue to the user - do NOT attempt local-only workarounds.
resolution_note: Fixed in master eb614f970. extract get --create-scratch no longer hard-stops when no decomp.me server is found: it now always prints the function ASM/context first, then actionable local /decomp guidance (edit src path, tools/checkdiff.py, configure.py && ninja, DECOMP_API_BASE override) and exits 0 (which also stops the auto issue-report hint). Also adds opt-in --decompile-c to print local m2c first-pass C without a server. Verified via tests/test_extract_get_local.py + live CLI smoke.

summary: extract get --create-scratch cannot find local decomp.me server
body: Command: melee-agent extract get fn_8024E1B4 --create-scratch\nResult: Could not find local decomp.me server. Tried nzxt-discord.local, 10.200.0.1, localhost:8000. This blocked the recommended scratch workflow and forced a fallback to local checkdiff/build recovery.
resolution_note: Fixed in master eb614f970. extract get --create-scratch no longer hard-stops when no decomp.me server is found: it now always prints the function ASM/context first, then actionable local /decomp guidance (edit src path, tools/checkdiff.py, configure.py && ninja, DECOMP_API_BASE override) and exits 0 (which also stops the auto issue-report hint). Also adds opt-in --decompile-c to print local m2c first-pass C without a server. Verified via tests/test_extract_get_local.py + live CLI smoke.

summary: Local decomp server workflow blocked this worktree session
body: Could not use --create-scratch or any local scratch flow because no local decomp.me API was discoverable (error: could not find local decomp.me server). This blocked iterative matching and forced fallback to objdiff-cli. Consider a clearer fallback message and optionally allowing extract/get to provide m2c-derived C without requiring the server.
resolution_note: Fixed in master eb614f970. extract get --create-scratch no longer hard-stops when no decomp.me server is found: it now always prints the function ASM/context first, then actionable local /decomp guidance (edit src path, tools/checkdiff.py, configure.py && ninja, DECOMP_API_BASE override) and exits 0 (which also stops the auto issue-report hint). Also adds opt-in --decompile-c to print local m2c first-pass C without a server. Verified via tests/test_extract_get_local.py + live CLI smoke.

summary: debug mutate simplify-order lacks --want-late triage path
body: Phase 3 late-target validation requires post-run triage with melee-agent debug mutate simplify-order -f lbDvd_80018A2C --want-late 46,44 --class 0 --with-permuter --triage. setup-simplify-order-scorer exposes --want-late, but debug mutate simplify-order help only exposes --want-first, so the campaign brief says to stop rather than work around it. Add --want-late support to the mutate simplify-order search/triage path, matching setup/scorer suffix semantics.
resolution_note: Fixed in 4dcead76d: debug mutate simplify-order now accepts --want-late and the lbDvd Phase 3 campaign used it successfully for the post-run Layer A check.

summary: debug mutate decl-orders can score stale object after reverted probe
body: During the mndiagram.c session, I reverted a local type-change probe and then ran debug mutate decl-orders. The command reported Baseline: 92.51%, even though the source had been restored to the retained 92.92% fingerprint. Running tools/checkdiff.py mnDiagram_8023FA6C --summary first rebuilt the object and a second decl-orders run reported Baseline: 92.92%. The decl-orders command should rebuild or verify the object baseline itself, or warn that it is scoring a stale object; otherwise it can silently discard useful candidates or mislead the session ledger.
resolution_note: Fixed on current master: debug mutate decl-orders rebuilds and refreshes baseline via _build_and_match before scoring candidates; verified decl-orders baseline refresh regression.

summary: Add a helper to show current compiled function assembly
body: While matching src/melee/mn/mnevent.c, I could easily inspect the expected assembly in build/GALE01/asm/... and the MWCC internal dump, but there was no first-class agent command to show the final emitted assembly for the current object/function. Local objdump availability is inconsistent in this environment, so I had to infer the final codegen indirectly from mwcc_debug instead of directly comparing the compiled function body. A helper like 'melee-agent debug asm -f <func>' or similar would make last-mile source-shape work much faster.
resolution_note: Fixed by ba492280b: added 'melee-agent debug inspect asm -f <func>' wrapper around checkdiff JSON current_asm; verified help and tests.

summary: pcdump cache stale warnings are noisy after local debug runs
body: Several commands printed '[mwcc_debug] using stale cached pcdump (mnnamenew.c modified since cache; src=... cache=...)' even around local dump/analyze cycles where the report and worktree were otherwise fresh. It made it hard to know whether the analysis was trustworthy or whether only a sub-second/source timestamp race was involved. Request: make the warning more actionable: include the exact refresh command, mention whether the cache content was just regenerated by this command, and consider a small mtime tolerance or content hash/fingerprint so freshly generated local caches do not look stale.
resolution_note: Fixed on current master: pcdump cache freshness uses source content hash sidecars instead of mtime-only checks, reducing false stale warnings after local debug/probe cycles; verified cache tests.

summary: commit hook stalled behind stray report build
body: A prior `ninja build/GALE01/report.json` stayed alive as a long-running background process after its initiating session had effectively finished. A later `git commit` hung with no output until I manually found and killed that stray ninja process. After the kill, the hook resumed and reported match-regression results. This makes commit-time validation look frozen and also contributes to confusing stale report/match state.
resolution_note: Fixed on current master: checkdiff/report refresh paths now use bounded build timeouts and return actionable timeout diagnostics; commit hook smoke passed during ba492280b commit.

summary: simplify-order scorer cannot represent SPILLED colorgraph targets
body: Layer A validation on ftColl_8007BAC0: force proof --force-phys-iter 0:13:31,0:9:30 reaches byte-exact match, but setup-simplify-order-scorer --want-first 37,41 scores base as observed prefix [-1,-1] because the relevant COLORGRAPH decisions (iter 9 ig_idx 41, iter 13 ig_idx 37) correspond to -1/SPILLED simplify graph entries in the permuter single-function pcdump. Custom scorer ran 3112 iterations with 0 saved outputs; min finite score stayed at baseline 2000000. Need either colorgraph/phys-iter scorer mode or setup-time validation that rejects/warns on non-negative want-first targets hidden behind -1 simplify entries. See docs/mwcc-debug-ftColl_8007BAC0-campaign-2026-05-24.md.
resolution_note: Fixed on current master: simplify-order scoring filters -1/SPILLED placeholder entries from signatures/targets, so want-first can represent meaningful ig_idx prefixes; verified simplify_order_scoring tests.

summary: remote doctor misses stale score-simplify-order scorer
body: Phase 2 coalesce-preservation validation on coder1 passed remote doctor and ran 212,709 iterations, but the remote /home/discord/.local/bin/melee-agent scorer lacked current Phase 2 behavior: score-simplify-order --help did not expose --strict-polarity, and output-135-1 was saved with score 135 even though the current local scorer rejects the same candidate with score 1000000000 because target ig_idx 38 and 42 coalesce into root 3. remote doctor should verify the remote scorer supports the current target.yaml schema/behavior or run a smoke score that exercises force_phys/coalesce preservation before submitting.
resolution_note: Fixed in e80200388: remote doctor now runs score-simplify-order subcommand help and fails stale remote scorer installs missing --strict-polarity schema support.

summary: checkdiff summary stalls on fn_8024D864 unless no-build is used
body: For src/melee/mn/mnevent.c:fn_8024D864, `python tools/checkdiff.py --summary fn_8024D864` repeatedly failed to return in interactive time this session, while `python tools/checkdiff.py --summary --no-build fn_8024D864` returned quickly after a successful `ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/mn/mnevent.o`. It would help if the build path surfaced where it was waiting or timed out with a hint to retry using --no-build after a successful rebuild.
resolution_note: Fixed in e80200388: checkdiff build/report regeneration steps now have --build-timeout/CHECKDIFF_BUILD_TIMEOUT and timeout hints to retry --no-build after a manual rebuild.

summary: worktree-doctor --fix hung refreshing report.json
body: In /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/2138/melee, python tools/worktree-doctor.py --fix printed '[FIX] refreshing build/GALE01/report.json via configure.py and ninja...' then parent PID 21022 and child 'ninja build/GALE01/report.json' PID 21313 stayed at 0% CPU for over 6 minutes with no report.json produced. Blocked Layer A campaign preflight until manually killed.
resolution_note: Fixed in e80200388: worktree-doctor report refresh now times out instead of hanging indefinitely and reports the stuck ninja command.

summary: extract list --file can hide report-backed nonmatching functions
body: melee-agent extract list --file ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init --max-match 0.999999 --matching-only returned 0 functions, but build/GALE01/report.json showed ftCh_Init_80156AD8 at 98.99317% in that exact TU and checkdiff worked. I had to inspect report.json directly to discover the real TU state. If extract list intentionally filters by DB/merged state, it should say that the report has excluded/missing nonmatches and suggest --include-completed or another flag. Ideally file filtering should surface report-backed non-100% functions even when they are not in the attempts DB.
resolution_note: Fixed in e80200388: extract list now reports when --matching-only hides report-backed non-100% functions matching --file, with a rerun hint.

summary: debug target guide should warn when target is just current coloring
body: I ran debug dump local, then debug inspect guide -f ftCh_Init_80156AD8 and got 'No issues found — current coloring matches target' because the auto target was derived from the current pcdump. For a nonmatching function this was technically true but not useful: I needed guidance against the expected/reference allocation, not current. A warning like 'target was auto-derived from current source; pass/derive a reference target to diagnose mismatch' would make this more intuitive. Even better: offer a command to derive a target from checkdiff/reference asm or explain when that is impossible.
resolution_note: Fixed in e80200388: debug inspect guide warns when the supplied target already matches the current pcdump, pointing users to reference/forced targets.

summary: debug inspect HSD_ASSERT advice can be unsafe in mnevent.c
body: On src/melee/mn/mnevent.c, both and report anonymous jobj.h assert strings and recommend overriding HSD_ASSERT before <baselib/jobj.h>. That clue is useful, but in this TU the naive fix is operationally unsafe: jobj.h is already pulled in via mnevent.h / mn/inlines.h, and the obvious header/include-order refactor + macro override regressed multiple functions (for example mnEvent_8024D4E0 and mnEvent_8024E524) even though it removed the anonymous-string warning. Request: either (1) downgrade this to a conditional hint when the include graph means the suggested override cannot actually be applied locally, or (2) emit a safer recipe that accounts for transitive includes / forward.h header surgery and warns that it can change codegen elsewhere in the TU.
resolution_note: Fixed in f88056e9d: HSD_ASSERT guidance now warns about transitive jobj.h includes and include-order/wrapper changes perturbing other functions in the TU.

summary: permuter import base fails for grVenom due incomplete Vec3 temp
body: Campaign iteration 3 after permuter harvest-mode landed. decomp-permuter import.py wrote nonmatchings/grVenom_80204284, and melee-agent debug permute fix-compile patched compile.sh successfully. However permuter.py --debug on the imported case fails before producing candidates: many file-local declarations report 'illegal use of incomplete struct/union/class struct _PermuterTemp1' for Vec3 fields, plus jobj->translate member errors. This appears to be a flattened full-TU import/source-shape problem outside grVenom_80204284 itself, so simplify-order --with-permuter cannot harvest real candidates for this function yet.
resolution_note: Fixed in f88056e9d: debug permute fix-compile now also repairs import.py-pruned base.c Vec/Vec3 _PermuterTemp aliases; verified grVenom_80204284 permuter.py --debug reaches base scoring.

summary: opseq is easy to invoke incorrectly from agent docs
body: The heartbeat instructions and AGENTS.md say to use opseq when stuck, and most local workflow commands are exposed through melee-agent. I first tried 'melee-agent opseq' and got 'No such command'. The actual command is documented only inside the opseq skill as tools/table-typer/table-typer opseq. It would reduce friction if AGENTS.md or melee-agent exposed/aliased opseq, or if 'melee-agent opseq' printed the table-typer command instead of only failing.
resolution_note: Fixed in f88056e9d: melee-agent now exposes an opseq alias that forwards to tools/table-typer opseq and has regression coverage plus help smoke checks.

summary: debug inspect HSD_ASSERT warning can be a neutral false lead
body: debug inspect stuck/ceiling repeatedly warned that anonymous .sdata assert strings from baselib/jobj.h needed an HSD_ASSERT override in mndiagram2.c. I applied the suggested local override using the existing mnDiagram2_804D4FC0/mnDiagram2_804D4FC8 symbols and checked mnDiagram2_Create plus nearby affected functions; the match percentages and diffs were neutral, so it was reverted. It would help if the warning distinguished 'present in TU' from 'currently affecting this function/diff', or provided a verification command/expected diff symptom so agents do not spend a probe on a neutral override.
resolution_note: Fixed in f88056e9d: HSD_ASSERT override guidance now says the finding may be neutral for the current function and requires target/nearby-function checkdiff verification.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C
body: Command: melee-agent debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C Exit code: 4 Still reproducible on current master immediately after a successful `melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`; inspect still considers the cache stale in the same worktree.
resolution_note: Duplicate of #68; verified fixed on current master with debug dump local followed by debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C --skip-decl-orders.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C
body: Command: melee-agent debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C Exit code: 4 Reproduced immediately after a successful `melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`; inspect still considered the cache stale in the same worktree.
resolution_note: Duplicate of #68; verified fixed on current master with debug dump local followed by debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C --skip-decl-orders.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C
body: Command: melee-agent debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C Exit code: 4 I had just run `melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`, which reported that it wrote build/mwcc_debug_cache/melee/mn/mnevent.txt, but `debug inspect ceiling` still immediately treated the cache as stale because source mtime was newer than the cache mtime. This made the command unusable without an unclear extra step.
resolution_note: Verified fixed on current master: debug dump local wrote a fresh mnevent cache and debug inspect ceiling mnEvent_8024D15C --skip-decl-orders exited 0 instead of stale-cache exit 4.

summary: extract list can show stale match percentages after local checkdiff probes
body: During mndiagram2 matching, `tools/checkdiff.py mnDiagram2_Create --summary` reported the current source at 96.44%, but `melee-agent extract list --file mndiagram2.c --include-completed` still showed mnDiagram2_Create at 87.5% until I ran `python configure.py && ninja` to refresh the report. This is understandable if extract list reads build/GALE01/report.json, but it would save confusion if extract list warned that report data may be stale relative to source/object timestamps, or if it printed the report timestamp/source freshness when build metadata is stale.
resolution_note: Fixed in 72f02933e: extract list now warns when report.json is older than source files, so stale match percentages are called out. Verified with CLI regression test and extract list smoke.

summary: debug dump local --force-phys help advertises class-scoped form, but runtime says unsupported
body: While testing mnDiagram_8023FA6C I ran: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c --force-phys gpr:75:26,gpr:40:30,gpr:42:27 --force-phys-fn mnDiagram_8023FA6C --diff -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C. The --help text says class:virtIdx:phys avoids ambiguous FP overrides, but runtime prints that the DLL does not support class filtering and the override applies to all IG classes. Either help should not advertise class-scoped --force-phys, or the implementation should support it. The warning also recommends --force-phys-iter for class-precise control, which makes the help text especially confusing.
resolution_note: Fixed in 72f02933e: --force-phys help no longer claims class filtering works; it says the DLL ignores the class prefix and points to --force-phys-iter. Verified with CLI help smoke.

summary: debug mutate search and decomp-permuter import disagree on nonmatchings path
body: debug mutate search told me /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/ftCh_Init_80156AD8 was missing and to run import.py first. Running import.py from /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter with absolute source/asm imported into the melee worktree's nonmatchings/ftCh_Init_80156AD8 instead. mutate search still expected the permuter clone path, so I had to create a manual symlink. It would be smoother if debug mutate search could import automatically, accept/detect the worktree nonmatchings path, or print an import command that creates the directory it later looks for.
resolution_note: Fixed in 72f02933e: added shared permuter function-dir resolver that accepts worktree nonmatchings imports as well as perm-root imports. Verified by regression test.

summary: debug mutate search setup command uses system python for decomp-permuter import
body: While working mnDiagram_8023FA6C, debug mutate search printed setup instructions: cd /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter && ./import.py <source> <asm> --function mnDiagram_8023FA6C. Running that command failed with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'toml' because import.py's shebang uses system python. The dependency was present in /Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/.venv, and invoking .venv/bin/python import.py worked. It would save time if the suggested command detected/used the permuter venv or mentioned the venv explicitly. Related papercut: after import, the directory was created under the repo nonmatchings path while debug mutate search expected the perm-root nonmatchings path, so I had to add a temporary symlink.
resolution_note: Fixed in 72f02933e: permuter import hints now use .venv/bin/python when present, and debug permute/mutate commands accept both decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/<fn> and worktree nonmatchings/<fn>. Verified with test_debug_cli_reorg targeted tests.

summary: debug mutate search permuter setup guidance fails for already-decompiled functions
body: debug mutate search planned useful seeds for mnDiagram_8023FA6C, then exited with: '/Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/mnDiagram_8023FA6C not found. Run decomp-permuter's import.py first.' Running the obvious import command from the worktree failed with 'Failed to find GLOBAL_ASM for function mnDiagram_8023FA6C' because this function is already decompiled source, not a GLOBAL_ASM block. It would help if mutate search could auto-materialize the nonmatching from the current source plus extracted target asm, or print a working melee-agent/decomp-permuter command for decompiled functions.
resolution_note: Fixed in b140be440: setup hint uses melee-agent extract get to materialize an asm file, then import.py with --function, which works for already-decompiled functions.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug dump local src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.c --force-coalesce 38=39 --force-coalesce-fn mnDiagram2_GetAggregatedFighterRank --output /tmp/mndiagram2-ag...
body: Command: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.c --force-coalesce 38=39 --force-coalesce-fn mnDiagram2_GetAggregatedFighterRank --output /tmp/mndiagram2-agg-force-38-39.txt Exit code: 124
resolution_note: Fixed in b140be440: unsafe force-coalesce pairs are rejected before local pcdump compilation when preflight shows direct interference/missing nodes/no copy edge.

summary: debug mutate search should suggest the exact decomp-permuter import command when target is missing
body: While running `melee-agent debug mutate search -f mnDiagram2_HandleInput --budget 4 --per-seed-time 20 --total-time 100 --threshold 0.03 --include-low-confidence`, the tool produced useful seed plans but exited with `/Users/mike/code/decomp-permuter/nonmatchings/mnDiagram2_HandleInput not found. Run decomp-permuter import.py first.` It would save time if the message included the exact command to create/import the target for this function, or if melee-agent had a wrapper to create it from the current checkdiff target. The current wording confirms what is missing but leaves the user to infer the repo/path/function-specific import invocation.
resolution_note: Fixed in b140be440: missing permuter dirs now print exact extract/import/fix-compile commands.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug dump local src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c --force-coalesce 39=40 --force-coalesce-fn mnDiagram_8023FA6C --diff -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C
body: Command: melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c --force-coalesce 39=40 --force-coalesce-fn mnDiagram_8023FA6C --diff -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C Exit code: 124 Context: suggest coalesce marked pair r39=r40 with a preflight warning. I tried it as a hypothesis anyway and debug dump local hung until the no-progress watchdog killed wibo. It may be expected for unsafe force-coalesce pairs, but a faster preflight rejection before invoking wibo would save matching time.
resolution_note: Fixed in b140be440: debug dump local preflights scoped force-coalesce pairs against fresh cached colorgraph data and refuses known-unsafe pairs before invoking wibo.

summary: debug mutate type-change split preview loses indentation
body: The latest master fixed the combined-declaration failure, but the preview for `melee-agent debug mutate type-change -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C --var i --type long --diff` rewrote `int i, j;` as `long i;` followed by `int j;` at column 1. It compiles only after manual cleanup. The helper should preserve the original declaration indentation when splitting combined declarations.
resolution_note: Fixed in b140be440: type-change combined declaration splitting now preserves original indentation; covered by test_mwcc_debug_mutators.

summary: decl-orders search gives no progress on medium functions
body: While working on src/melee/mn/mnevent.c:fn_8024D864, `melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders fn_8024D864 --strategy promote --threshold 0.01 --json` produced no incremental output for roughly 30 seconds and had to be killed manually. On a medium-sized function (~14 locals), it would help to emit candidate progress/ETA and preserve best-so-far context instead of looking hung.
resolution_note: Fixed in b140be440: decl-orders --json emits per-candidate progress to stderr before each build; covered by test_debug_cli_reorg.

summary: debug mutate type-change fails on C89 combined local declarations
body: While working on mnDiagram_8023FA6C, the command `melee-agent debug mutate type-change -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C --var i --type long --diff` failed on the normal C89-style declaration `int i, j;` with `declaration of 'i' did not match the expected <type> <name> [=|;] pattern`. Combined local declarations are common in this repo; it would save time if the helper could split/rewrite them or emit a suggested manual patch instead of failing.
resolution_note: Fixed debug mutate type-change handling for simple combined C89 declarations by splitting only the requested declarator; covered by mutator regression tests.

tool: melee-agent debug inspect var-to-virtual / debug mutate search
summary: var-to-virtual misparses combined declarations and starves mutate search seeds
body: After moving `int i, j` later in mnDiagram_8023FA6C, `debug inspect var-to-virtual ... --basis` reported parsed locals as both `int i` and `int i , j` before a later refresh, and `debug mutate search` initially refused to seed because every binding was low-confidence. Combined C89 declarations are common here; the binding parser should split them cleanly so downstream mutate search can use reliable variable bindings without requiring manual low-confidence override.
resolution_note: Fixed combined-declaration parsing in the tree-sitter symbol bridge so var-to-virtual reports clean per-variable types; covered by ast_walker regressions.

summary: checkdiff repeat warnings are confusing after dependency/header experiments
body: While testing mndiagram2 signature changes, checkdiff reported 'same source as attempt #25 but new outcome' / divergent repeat for mnDiagram2_UpdateHeader after changing and then reverting helper return types in headers. The warning does say external state may differ, but the headline 'same source' is confusing because dependency/header context is exactly what changed. It would be useful for the repeat warning to include dependency/header hash context or explicitly say the function body fingerprint matches but compile context differs.
resolution_note: Changed divergent repeat wording from same source to same function-body/dependency fingerprint; covered by checkdiff fingerprint banner test.

summary: checkdiff name-magic path requires missing pyelftools after latest merge
body: After merging master at this heartbeat, tools/checkdiff.py mnDiagram2_GetAggregatedFighterRank --summary and --format plain both fail by default in apply_name_magic_auto with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'elftools' from tools/melee-agent/src/mwcc_debug/o_rewriter.py. Worktree doctor passes and reinstalls melee-agent, but it does not install or check pyelftools. Workaround is passing --no-name-magic.
resolution_note: Declared pyelftools as a melee-agent dependency and made checkdiff name-magic skip cleanly on ImportError; covered by metadata/name-magic tests.

summary: debug mutate type-change cannot handle combined local declarations
body: While working on mnDiagram_8023FA6C, failed on the normal C89-style declaration with . Combined local declarations are common in this repo; it would save time if the helper could split/rewrite them or emit a suggested manual patch instead of failing.
resolution_note: Superseded by issue #65; original report body was mangled by shell quoting while reporting.

summary: ceiling check should stop or refresh when pcdump cache is stale
body: debug inspect ceiling warned that the cached pcdump was stale after cleanup-only source edits, but it still continued and produced a probable-ceiling verdict. For matching work this is easy to over-trust. It would be safer if ceiling either auto-ran the local dump refresh when available, prompted/printed a one-command refresh path and exited, or marked every downstream section as based on stale data. I refreshed manually and reran, but the first verdict could have been misleading.
resolution_note: debug inspect ceiling now refuses stale cached pcdumps before producing a verdict, with refresh guidance. Added regression coverage.

summary: debug suggest coalesce continues after warning that pcdump cache is stale
body: After editing mnDiagram_8023FC28 from 91.34% to 91.53%, running debug suggest coalesce printed: [mwcc_debug] using stale cached pcdump (mndiagram.c modified since cache; src=01:17:32.050 cache=01:11:20.304). Re-run debug dump local to refresh. It then continued and emitted coalesce suggestions based on the stale pcdump. This is easy to miss during matching; it would be safer to stop with a nonzero status or offer/perform an auto-refresh before emitting suggestions.
resolution_note: debug suggest coalesce now requires a fresh cached pcdump before emitting suggestions and stale-cache errors include local/remote refresh commands. Added regression coverage.

summary: melee-agent command failed: debug mutate search -f mnDiagram_8023FC28 --budget 6 --per-seed-time 30 --total-time 210 --include-low-confidence
body: Command: melee-agent debug mutate search -f mnDiagram_8023FC28 --budget 6 --per-seed-time 30 --total-time 210 --include-low-confidence Exit code: 6 Context: same no-improvement-as-failure behavior previously observed for this workflow; after master afdf16842 the command still exits nonzero and prints a tooling-failure issue-report command when it simply finds no improving seed.
resolution_note: Same no-improvement root cause as #53: debug mutate search now reports no improvement without nonzero tooling-failure classification. Added CLI regression coverage.

summary: debug mutate search reports no-improvement result as tooling failure
body: Command: melee-agent debug mutate search -f mnDiagram_8023FC28 --budget 4 --per-seed-time 25 --total-time 140 --include-low-confidence. Exit code: 6. The command successfully planned seeds, compiled 2/2 supported seeds, and ran the per-seed permuter search, but found no improvement. It then printed a copyable issue-report command under "tooling failure". That reads like a bug even though no-improvement is a normal search outcome; a distinct no-win exit/status or wording would make heartbeat workflows less noisy.
resolution_note: Changed debug mutate search no-improvement to return as a normal completed search instead of exit 6 tooling failure. Added CLI regression coverage.

summary: debug diagnostics left root-level pcdump.txt untracked
body: During FC28 diagnostics after running debug dump/analyze/mutate search, the worktree ended up with an untracked /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/f37e/melee/pcdump.txt (about 3.5 MB). I did not request an output path named pcdump.txt; the main dump command wrote to build/mwcc_debug_cache as expected. A diagnostic helper or permuter setup path appears to leak a root-level pcdump artifact, which dirties the worktree and needs manual cleanup before committing.
resolution_note: Redirected Tier 3 smoke-compile MWCC_DEBUG_PCDUMP_PATH to a temp file and clean it up, preventing root-level pcdump.txt leakage. Added regression coverage and smoke-checked no repo pcdump.txt was left.

summary: tools/checkdiff.py is documented like an executable but is not executable
body: During mndiagram2 matching I ran the documented form tools/checkdiff.py src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.c mnDiagram2_HandleInput --summary and zsh returned permission denied because tools/checkdiff.py is not executable. The usable command is python tools/checkdiff.py mnDiagram2_HandleInput --summary. Either making the script executable or updating docs/help examples would avoid a small context switch during matching.
resolution_note: Made tools/checkdiff.py executable and added a regression asserting the documented direct entrypoint is runnable. Verified with pytest and tools/checkdiff.py --help.

summary: decl-orders --keep-best reports a win but leaves source unchanged
body: { "function": "mnEvent_8024E524", "baseline_pct": 88.360405, "best_label": null, "best_pct": 88.360405, "iterate": false, "applied_chain": [], "rounds": [ { "round": 0, "baseline_pct": 88.360405, "best_label": null, "best_pct": 88.360405, "results": [ { "label": "promote jobj_0B", "match_pct": 88.360405, "delta": 0.0 }, { "label": "promote jobj_0C", "match_pct": 88.34518, "delta": -0.015225000000000932 }, { "label": "promote jobj_0A", "match_pct": 88.33502, "delta": -0.02538499999999999 }, { "label": "promote jobj_09", "match_pct": 88.31979, "delta": -0.04061500000000251 }, { "label": "promote gobj", "match_pct": 88.360405, "delta": 0.0 }, { "label": "promote proc", "match_pct": 88.360405, "delta": 0.0 }, { "label": "promote tree", "match_pct": 88.1066, "delta": -0.25380499999999984 }, { "label": "promote user_data", "match_pct": 87.92893, "delta": -0.43147500000000605 }, { "label": "promote y_a", "match_pct": 88.31979, "delta": -0.04061500000000251 }, { "label": "promote y_b", "match_pct": 88.31979, "delta": -0.04061500000000251 }, { "label": "promote i", "match_pct": 88.360405, "delta": 0.0 }, { "label": "promote first_event", "match_pct": 88.360405, "delta": 0.0 }, { "label": "promote event", "match_pct": 88.360405, "delta": 0.0 } ] } ] } reported best_label=promote page and best_pct=88.3401 from a baseline of 88.086296, but the source file remained unchanged afterward. I had to apply the reported winning reorder manually. If --keep-best only keeps internal temp state, that needs clearer wording; otherwise the writeback is being skipped.
resolution_note: Fixed in afdf16842: decl-orders --keep-best now applies the winning single-sweep permutation before JSON/text output instead of reporting a win while leaving source unchanged; regression covers --json --keep-best.

summary: melee-agent debug mutate preview commands dump the full translation unit instead of a focused patch for mnEvent_8024E524
body: Commands like `melee-agent debug mutate insert-alias -f mnEvent_8024E524 --var jobj_0B --at 0` and `melee-agent debug mutate type-change -f mnEvent_8024E524 --var page --type u32` print the entire source file to stdout. During matching this makes it hard to see the intended hunk quickly and easy to miss whether the proposed edit is even sensible. A `--diff`/`--hunk` preview mode, or defaulting previews to the smallest changed region, would make these mutations much faster to evaluate interactively.
resolution_note: Fixed in afdf16842: debug mutate type-change and insert-alias now support --diff focused unified previews instead of only dumping full mutated source; covered by CLI regression and help smoke.

summary: extract list lacks machine-readable output despite verification workflows needing file-level summaries
body: While validating mndiagram2.c progress after a retained match improvement, I tried to produce a stable TU summary with 'melee-agent extract list --file mndiagram2.c --max-match 1.0 --include-completed --json'. The current CLI has no --json option, so agents must scrape Rich table output to compare per-function percentages and confirm no regressions. A JSON/CSV output mode for extract list (or a checkdiff file/TU mode) would make end-of-session validation less brittle.
resolution_note: Fixed in afdf16842: extract list now supports --json with filtered function rows, summary counts, and filter metadata; covered by test_extract_list_json_output and local python -m src.cli smoke.

summary: debug mutate decl-orders reported stale baseline during clean repeated fingerprint
body: While exploring mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, current checkdiff was a repeated 83.50% fingerprint, but 'melee-agent debug mutate decl-orders mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter --strategy swap --json' reported baseline_pct 80.18831 and every unchanged adjacent swap as +3.31 points back to 83.5. The command did revert the source, but the stale/incorrect baseline made the result table misleading and forced manual rechecking with checkdiff/status before trusting it.
resolution_note: Fixed in afdf16842: decl-orders refreshes the baseline by rebuilding the current function before enumerating candidates, preventing stale report.json baselines from inflating deltas; regression verifies refreshed baseline.

summary: debug inspect ceiling references missing MEMORY.md for HSD_ASSERT override guidance
body: While checking mnDiagram_8023FA6C, ceiling reported: HSD_ASSERT override needed - anonymous .sdata assert strings: @768 (jobj.h), @769 (jobj). It then said to see MEMORY.md HSD_ASSERT macro override for the pattern, but find/rg in this worktree could not locate MEMORY.md. The diagnostic is useful, but it should point to an existing doc path or print the minimal override snippet directly.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b21c35ce: stuck/ceiling diagnostics now print self-contained HSD_ASSERT override guidance instead of pointing to missing MEMORY.md.

summary: match-iter-first auto-verify emits cross-device link traceback
body: While running Function: mnDiagram_8023FC28 Unit: melee/mn/mndiagram ASM: build/GALE01/asm/melee/mn/mndiagram.s Expected iter-first targets: r31 <- ig_idx 46 (virt r46, instr 2: addi r31, r29, 0x1c) [ambiguous] r30 <- ig_idx 48 (virt r48, instr 8: mr r30, r28) [ambiguous] r29 <- ig_idx 50 (virt r50, instr 1: addi r29, r3, mnDiagram_804A0750@l) [exact] r28 <- ig_idx 35 (virt r35, instr 9: li r28, 0x0) [ambiguous] r27 - no structural match in pre-coloring for `clrlwi r27, r24, 24` r26 - no structural match in pre-coloring for `addi r26, r31, 0x0` r25 - no structural match in pre-coloring for `addi r25, r1, 0x18` r24 <- ig_idx 35 (virt r35, instr 9: li r24, 0x0) [ambiguous] Try: melee-agent debug dump local <source.c> --force-iter-first 46,48,50,35,35 --force-iter-first-fn mnDiagram_8023FC28 --diff WARNING: 4 target(s) are [ambiguous] (r31, r30, r28, r24) — multiple pre-coloring instructions matched the expected signature, and the closest-position pick may be wrong. Before trusting this output, verify with `debug dump local <c_file> --force-iter-first 46,48,50,35,35 --force-iter-first-fn mnDiagram_8023FC28 --diff` (or pass --auto-verify on this command). If the diff doesn't improve, the ambiguous assignments are wrong; try a subset. == auto-verify == baseline -> with override: 91.34% -> 91.34% (+0.00%) stderr tail: │ ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯ │ ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯ OSError: [Errno 18] Cross-device link: '/Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/f37e/melee/pcdump_45166_1779434181357.txt' -> '/dev/null' restore object/report: exit 0 (timeout 180.0s via default; planned 0 steps, max 64), auto-verify completed and restored object/report state, but stderr included . It looks like the cleanup path is trying to rename across filesystems to /dev/null. The command still printed a result, but the traceback makes it unclear whether auto-verify was fully trustworthy.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b21c35ce: match-iter-first auto-verify writes its throwaway pcdump beside the source instead of renaming across filesystems to /dev/null.

summary: checkdiff reports match=false for 100% relocation-label-only functions
body: While matching mnDiagram2_GetStatValue, checkdiff reported match_percent=100.00 and classification=relocation-label-only but still exited nonzero and printed match=false. That makes it ambiguous whether the function should be considered matched for progress/validation, especially after full report.json shows 100.0. It would help if relocation-label-only 100% cases had a distinct status or guidance on whether they are safe to treat as matched.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b21c35ce: checkdiff treats relocation-label-only diffs as effective matches, with regression coverage.

summary: worktree doctor flags missing table-typer but does not give a bootstrap command
body: While matching mnDiagram2_CreateStatRow, opseq was one of the recommended unstuck tools, but worktree-doctor reports table-typer is missing. The warning says 'install it or ensure it is on PATH' but does not identify the repo command/package/script that should make it available in this worktree. A copyable setup/fix command or a note that opseq-dependent workflows are unavailable would make this much easier to recover from mid-match.
resolution_note: Fixed in 2b21c35ce: worktree-doctor now prints the table-typer go build command and states that opseq workflows need it.

summary: forced debug dump can pollute checkdiff repeat fingerprint outcomes
body: While working on mnDiagram_OnFrame, I ran 'melee-agent debug dump local src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c --force-iter-first ... --diff'. The forced build intentionally produced a different object, but subsequent normal 'tools/checkdiff.py mnDiagram_OnFrame --summary' reported DIVERGENT REPEAT for the same source fingerprint, citing the forced 98.2% outcome versus the natural 99.03%. Forced hypothesis runs should probably be excluded from the normal source-fingerprint attempt/repeat ledger, or clearly tagged as forced so they do not look like external nondeterminism.
resolution_note: Fixed by forcing CHECKDIFF_NO_FINGERPRINT=1 for debug-side non-natural/read-only checkdiff --no-build diagnostics, including forced pcdump diff paths.

summary: debug target derive output is not directly actionable with inspect guide
body: While working on mnDiagram_8023FA6C, debug inspect guide correctly warned that no target spec was loaded and suggested running 'melee-agent debug target derive -f mnDiagram_8023FA6C' or passing --target. Running target derive printed a YAML-ish mapping to stdout, but it was not clear whether inspect guide can consume that directly, where to save it, or whether there is a one-command flow to derive-and-inspect. A follow-up hint such as 'save this to ... and rerun inspect guide --target ...' or an inspect-guide --derive-target option would make the allocator workflow much faster.
resolution_note: Fixed by adding actionable derive-and-guide target flow hints to inspect guide and target derive without contaminating target stdout.

summary: checkdiff repeat tracking reports divergent outcomes after external signature changes
body: While iterating on mndiagram2.c, checkdiff reported '[DIVERGENT REPEAT] same source ... external state differs' for functions whose own source fingerprint had not changed, because helper signatures in other files changed codegen. The message is technically useful, but it does not identify which external dependency changed. It would help to include the relevant object/header/source fingerprint inputs or at least the changed dependency set, especially when evaluating whether a local function source is actually a repeat.
resolution_note: Fixed by including repo-local dependency/header context in checkdiff fingerprints and adding a divergent banner hint for dependency/header/source context changes.

summary: checkdiff source fingerprint missed struct layout change
body: After changing mnDiagram_PopupData.text from [6] to [5], checkdiff correctly changed the generated HSD_MemAlloc immediate from 0x50 to 0x4c, but still printed '[REPEAT] this source matches attempt #1'. The fingerprint/repeat banner appears to ignore relevant type/layout changes, which makes it hard to trust repeat detection during decomp work.
resolution_note: Fixed by making checkdiff source fingerprints include TU/dependency context, so file-local struct layout changes alter the fingerprint; regression added.

summary: virtual-to-var treats expected compiler temps as tool failures
body: While matching mndiagram.c, debug inspect virtual-to-var was useful for compiler-introduced temps such as r45/r47/r70, but the no-source-variable path exits nonzero and prints a copyable 'tooling failure' issue-report command. In this workflow that reads like an actionable bug even though the diagnostic succeeded and gave the first defining op. Suggest exiting 0 or changing the wording for expected compiler-temp/no mapping results, reserving the issue-report command for actual internal failures.
resolution_note: Fixed virtual-to-var no-source-variable path to exit success after reporting first defining op, treating compiler temps as expected diagnostic results.

summary: Attempt fingerprint ignores dependency/header context
body: While iterating on mndiagram2, changing shared prototypes in gm_1601.h and mndiagram.h caused checkdiff to print DIVERGENT REPEAT for the same mndiagram2.c source fingerprint with different match percentages. The message is technically true, but it made the attempt ledger hard to trust because the fingerprint did not include relevant dependency/header context or report metadata freshness. It would help if fingerprints included dependency hashes, or if the banner explicitly said a dependency/header/report change may explain the divergence.
resolution_note: Fixed by adding depfile header hashes and whole-TU context to checkdiff fingerprints; divergent repeats now explicitly mention dependency/header/source context.

summary: Parallel checkdiff runs can race ninja dep generation
body: While checking mndiagram2 and mndiagram helpers in parallel, multiple tools/checkdiff.py processes each tried to regenerate build/GALE01/report.json. Ninja then failed in transform_dep.py because expected depfiles such as build/GALE01/src/melee/mn/mnmain.d and mndiagram3.d were missing. Running checks sequentially avoided it. It would help if checkdiff used one repo-wide build/report lock, skipped report regeneration while another checkdiff owns it, or made the lock cover all ninja invocations that can touch shared depfiles.
resolution_note: Fixed by making tools/checkdiff.py use a repo-wide build/report lock instead of per-object locks; regression test covers different objects sharing one lock.

summary: worktree doctor uses PATH melee-agent from another checkout after merge
body: During the mndiagram heartbeat, after merging master into codex/mndiagram-matches, worktree-doctor warned that `/opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent` imported `src.cli` from `/Users/mike/code/melee` instead of the active worktree. The suggested fix (`cd tools/melee-agent && python -m pip install -e .`) worked, but this is easy to miss and means heartbeat/agent sessions can silently use stale tooling after a merge unless doctor is run and its warning is acted on. Feature request: `worktree-doctor --fix` should either perform this editable reinstall automatically or make the stale-import warning a stronger blocker with a one-command fix.
resolution_note: Fixed in 3079ecaef: worktree-doctor --fix now reinstalls the repo-local melee-agent editable package when PATH imports src.cli from another checkout; verified with regression test and editable install smoke.

summary: opseq skill points to missing table-typer binary
body: The opseq skill says to run 'tools/table-typer/table-typer opseq ...', but that binary does not exist in this worktree. 'go run .' from tools/table-typer works, but the README/help output is also sparse and my first searches silently returned only 'Waiting for ninja to finish...' or no results. Either build/install the binary, update the skill to use 'go run .', or expose opseq through melee-agent.
resolution_note: Fixed in 3079ecaef: opseq skill and table-typer README now use the available go run . opseq entrypoint instead of the missing tools/table-typer/table-typer binary; verified with docs test and go run . opseq --help.

summary: debug inspect guide says coloring matches target when no target is loaded
body: While matching mnDiagram_OnFrame, I ran 'melee-agent debug inspect guide -f mnDiagram_OnFrame' after refreshing the pcdump. The output said 'Targeted virtuals: 0' and then 'No issues found — current coloring matches target.' With zero target virtuals, that reads like a false success signal. It would be clearer to say no target was provided/derived and suggest 'debug target derive' or passing --target, rather than implying the allocator is correct.
resolution_note: Fixed in 3079ecaef: debug inspect guide now warns when no target spec is loaded and avoids reporting that current coloring matches a target when targeted virtuals are zero; covered by CLI regression test.

summary: worktree-doctor says build report is OK when report.json is missing
body: In a fresh/switch branch state, python tools/worktree-doctor.py printed '[OK] build/report freshness checks passed', but the next checkdiff failed with FileNotFoundError for build/GALE01/report.json. Expected doctor to treat missing report.json as a blocker or print the exact fix: python configure.py && ninja build/GALE01/report.json. This made the first checkdiff failure feel avoidable.
resolution_note: Fixed in 3079ecaef: worktree-doctor now treats missing build/GALE01/report.json as a failure with the explicit refresh command; verified by test_worktree_doctor and doctor smoke.

summary: worktree doctor detects stale melee-agent import but opseq/help still uses wrong install
body: In the mndiagram2 heartbeat worktree, worktree-doctor warned that /opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent imports src.cli from /Users/mike/.codex/worktrees/f37e/melee instead of the active worktree. During matching, invoking melee-agent opseq/help used that stale install and failed to expose expected tooling. Doctor gives a reinstall command, but it would be helpful if either the wrapper preferred the active worktree automatically, or doctor could optionally repair this with --fix.
resolution_note: Fixed in 3079ecaef: duplicate stale-entrypoint root cause from #31; worktree-doctor --fix repairs stale melee-agent installs so opseq/help use the active worktree after reinstall.

summary: checkdiff --summary exits nonzero on instruction-identical functions
body: python tools/checkdiff.py --summary fn_8024E2A0 and fn_8024E34C both reported match_percent=100.00 classification=instruction-identical but exited with code 1 and match=false. This makes automation treat matched functions as failures.
resolution_note: Fixed in 66b464013: checkdiff treats normalized instruction-identical output as an effective match; summary now exits 0 for fn_8024E2A0/fn_8024E34C.

summary: checkdiff --summary exits nonzero on instruction-identical functions
body: function=fn_8024E2A0 match=false match_percent=100.00 classification=instruction-identical and both reported but exited with code 1 / . This makes automation treat matched functions as failures.
resolution_note: Fixed in 66b464013: checkdiff treats normalized instruction-identical output as an effective match; summary now exits 0 for fn_8024E2A0/fn_8024E34C.

summary: mismatch search crashes on hyphenated pattern names
body: Example 1: melee-agent mismatch search static-inline-dual-pointer-cleanup raised OperationalError no such column: inline. Example 2: melee-agent mismatch search cleanup-loop-dual-zero-locals raised OperationalError no such column: loop. Pattern-name lookups should be treated as literal strings or escaped for FTS.
resolution_note: Verified fixed in 66b464013/current master: mismatch search escapes hyphenated FTS queries; regression test covers stack-layout and smoke checked static-inline-dual-pointer-cleanup.

summary: mismatch search crashes on hyphenated pattern names
body: Examples: raised OperationalError: no such column: inline. raised OperationalError: no such column: loop. Pattern-name lookups should be treated as literal strings or escaped for FTS.
resolution_note: Verified fixed in 66b464013/current master: mismatch search escapes hyphenated FTS queries; regression test covers stack-layout and smoke checked static-inline-dual-pointer-cleanup.

summary: docs refer to melee-agent issues report but CLI only supports issue report
body: User-facing instructions requested melee-agent issues report, but the CLI has no issues command and suggests issue. Running melee-agent issues report --help exits with No such command issues. This is easy to trip over during handoff.
resolution_note: Fixed in 66b464013: added hidden 'issues' alias and suppressed recursive usage-error report hints.

summary: docs refer to melee-agent issues report but CLI only supports issue report
body: User-facing instructions requested , but the CLI has no command and suggests . exits with This is easy to trip over during handoff.
resolution_note: Fixed in 66b464013: added hidden 'issues' alias and suppressed recursive usage-error report hints.

summary: document command alias for issues vs issue
body: The task instructions said to run 'melee-agent issues report', but the CLI exposes 'melee-agent issue report' and errors with 'No such command issues'. A plural alias would make scripted/session instructions more forgiving.
resolution_note: Fixed in 66b464013: added hidden 'issues' alias and suppressed recursive usage-error report hints.

summary: AGENTS/user instruction says melee-agent issues report but CLI command is singular issue report
body: The session instruction asked to provide feedback with 'melee-agent issues report'. The installed CLI has no 'issues' command and suggests 'issue'. This is easy to recover from, but the documented command should match the actual CLI or the CLI should alias the plural form.
resolution_note: Fixed in 66b464013: added hidden 'issues' alias and suppressed recursive usage-error report hints.

summary: worktree-doctor --fix can hang with no output
body: At session start I ran 'python tools/worktree-doctor.py --fix' as required by the decomp skill. It produced no output for roughly 40 seconds and did not respond through the normal exec stdin path, so I had to find and kill the process externally. A progress line, timeout, or current substep would make it much easier to distinguish a real long-running repair from a hang.
resolution_note: worktree-doctor --fix now prints progress before the potentially slow report.json refresh; added regression test.

summary: mismatch search fails for hyphenated stack-layout query
body: While working on mndiagram.c, running 'melee-agent mismatch search stack-layout' crashed with OperationalError: no such column: layout. The command suggestion reported a failure, but this blocked using the mismatch DB for the stack-layout classification produced by checkdiff.
resolution_note: Fixed mismatch-db full-text search to sanitize plain user queries before SQLite FTS MATCH; added regression test for stack-layout.

summary: checkdiff warning failed to regenerate report.json lacks cause
body: Several checkdiff runs emitted 'warning: failed to regenerate report.json' while still producing usable diffs/matches. The warning did not include the command, stderr, or whether the failure was due to another ninja/checkdiff lock. Including the underlying error would make it clearer whether the report is stale or the warning is harmless.
resolution_note: checkdiff report.json regeneration warning now includes failed command, exit code, and stderr/stdout cause; added regression test.

summary: worktree-doctor should detect stale installed melee-agent entrypoint
body: In this session, /opt/homebrew/bin/melee-agent was stale after merging master. Direct commands like 'melee-agent debug inspect diff ...' still invoked the old 'debug pcdump-local' path, while 'python -m src.cli ...' from tools/melee-agent used the merged fixed code. worktree-doctor only reported that melee-agent resolves, not whether the installed entrypoint imports the same repo/tooling version. Request: have doctor or melee-agent --version report module path/commit and warn when the installed CLI is older than the worktree tooling.
resolution_note: worktree-doctor now warns when installed melee-agent imports src.cli from a different checkout and suggests editable reinstall; added regression test.

summary: checkdiff needs a compact score/classification output mode
body: When iterating on a last-mile register allocation mismatch, I repeatedly needed only function name, match percent, match bool, and classification. --format json includes huge target/current asm and diff payloads, and my attempted --summary flag does not exist. A --summary or --format summary mode would make quick probes much easier, especially for scripts and agent loops that only need to decide whether a source-shape tweak improved.
resolution_note: Added --summary / --format summary compact checkdiff output with function, match bool, match percent, and classification; added regression coverage.

summary: force-phys class-scope warning suggests invalid force-phys-iter syntax
body: Running: python -m src.cli debug dump local ... --force-phys 'gpr:33:31,gpr:46:30,...' printed warnings saying class-scoped force-phys is not supported and suggested using '--force-phys-iter gpr:33:31' for class-precise control. But force-phys-iter is documented as class:iter:phys, not class:ig_idx:phys, so the suggested replacement appears invalid/misleading. Request: either support class-scoped force-phys by ig_idx, or change the warning to explain the correct force-phys-iter conversion workflow.
resolution_note: Corrected class-scoped force-phys warning so it explains class:iter:phys conversion and no longer suggests using an ig_idx as --force-phys-iter; added regression coverage.

summary: debug inspect ceiling still recommends remote dump path
body: After the CLI reorg and local dump fixes, 'debug inspect ceiling WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex' successfully used local cached data but its next-step text still said to run 'debug dump remote ... --force-phys'. The skill/docs now correctly prefer 'debug dump local'. Request: update ceiling/guidance text to recommend local commands first and only mention remote as fallback.
resolution_note: Updated structural-ceiling recommendations to prefer melee-agent debug dump local and mention remote only as fallback; added regression coverage.
